title: Northstar Fintech
body:
Account is very focused on cost governance + evidence trails.

Key personas (from calls):
- FinOps lead: wants route/model breakdown for internal chargeback ("which product team is burning tokens").
- Security/GRC: cares about export auditability + RBAC ("downloads are data exfil events in our policy").
- Eng platform: wants consistency with billing and clear semantics (what counts as retry/fallback; cache hit/miss attribution).

What they asked for (verbatim/paraphrase):
- "We need to reconcile this with invoices at month end; if it’s directionally correct but not tie-out, finance won’t adopt." 
- "Export has to be controlled. Not everyone who can view usage should be able to download cost." 
- "If someone downloads a report, we need an audit record with user + time + what was exported."

Product fit notes:
- Usage Insights aligns well (route + model breakdown; top drivers; CSV expor...

normalized.nextStep: Schedule security/RBAC + audit logging review with their GRC lead; confirm export audit event schema and data residency plan; align on reconciliation tolerance and billing source of truth for beta.
blockers: Needs written confirmation that CSV exports generate audit log events (who/when/what filters, download URL access, file hash optional).
Wants RBAC for cost visibility (finance-only) and separate permission for exports/share links.
Billing reconciliation requirement: totals in console must match invoice line items within defined tolerance; must document timing/late-event behavior.
Data residency: EU workloads must stay in-region; unclear if initial beta includes EU region for telemetry aggregation.

title: Northstar Customer Support
body:
Account summary
- Northstar evaluating Redwood Optimize for cost reduction on LLM-driven customer support workflows (agent-assist + ticket summarization). Current pain: GPU spend growing faster than seat growth; wants predictable unit economics without quality drift.
- Strong requirement: strict quality controls BEFORE any cost-saving config change touches production traffic. They explicitly called out “no silent regressions” and asked how Redwood prevents ‘helpfulness’ drops when changing batching/caching/quantization.

Workload snapshot (as shared)
- Primary: chat-style agent assist + summarization of long ticket threads; some internal KB retrieval (embeddings + reranking) planned but not in initial POC.
- Traffic: spiky weekday peaks; on-call incidents when latency or quality degrades.
- Latency: wants p95 steady under internal SLOs for agent workflows; willing to trade small laten...

title: Northbridge Analytics
body:
Account summary
- Northbridge Analytics is evaluating Redwood Optimize to reduce inference cost on a set of customer-facing summarization + internal doc-search workloads. They like the platform direction, but procurement is currently blocked on change-management controls for optimization configs.

What they’re building / why Optimize
- Workloads:
 - Customer-facing summarization (chat-style generation, high concurrency during business hours)
 - Internal doc-search (embeddings + reranking)
- They have an existing model-serving stack and have been burned by config/optimization tweaks causing performance regressions. They are interested in Redwood’s batching/caching/quantization recommendations, but only if changes are traceable + reversible.

Procurement blockers (verbatim-ish from calls)
- “We can’t let someone flip a batching knob in prod without an audit trail.”
- “Rollback has to be...

title: Aegis Insurance Group
body:
Account summary
- Enterprise insurance carrier evaluating Redwood Dedicated for regulated workloads (customer data, claims docs). Primary motivation: predictable throughput + isolation + “real least-privilege admin controls.”
- Their security team is explicitly asking for: (1) RBAC templates they can adopt without custom policy authoring, (2) SSO/SAML group mapping with clear scoping semantics, and (3) audit logs that are evidence-friendly (SOC2-style) and exportable to SIEM.

Firmographics / org
- HQ: North America (US). Mix of onshore/offshore dev teams.
- Central IAM team owns SSO and group provisioning. Separate Platform Ops team runs internal developer platforms.

Workload / technical requirements
- Initial workload: claims-call summarization + internal adjuster assistant. Inputs include call transcripts + PDF/email attachments.
- Model needs: chat + summarization; some doc-searc...

title: FalconBank
body:
Enterprise account. Very security-sensitive; procurement will not proceed without clear change control story.

Primary thread: change controls around route-level configuration updates (esp. Quantization Profile Library toggles) + audit logging completeness.

Key stakeholders (FalconBank):
- InfoSec reviewer: asked repeatedly for "who changed what, when, and what the previous value was" for route config.
- Risk/Compliance: wants audit log export to Splunk + retention policy documented.
- Platform Eng: wants deterministic behavior + explicit rollback path.

Requirements / asks (captured in 2025-03-12 security review):
- Audit logging: 
 - Must emit events for enable/disable quantization per route AND profile reference changes.
 - Event must include actor identity, auth method, request id, route id/name, model id/variant, previous vs new setting, and resolution outcome (applied vs fallba...

title: Helio Financial
body:
Account: Helio Financial (enterprise).

Primary driver:
- Wants route-level A/B testing, but the buying decision hinges on audit logging + change management maturity.

What they care about (verbatim-ish):
- "We need a clean audit trail for every change that can impact routing."
- "Export to Splunk is mandatory."
- "We can’t have experimentation be a black box—must be explainable and reviewable."

Key requirements captured:
- Audit events for experiment lifecycle: create/update/start/stop/delete.
- Include actor, time, org/workspace, route_id, experiment_id, variant_id(s), config_version, and a human-readable change summary.
- Prefer a diff: before/after values for allocations, eligibility rules, guardrail thresholds.
- RBAC: separate roles for view vs mutate; explicit permission for stop (and break-glass override).
- Retention/export: they’ll accept Redwood retention if >= 1 year OR n...

Legal review: DPA language + subprocessor list
Security: confirmation of audit log retention options + export frequency (near-real-time vs batch) and redaction approach
Procurement requires named SLA and incident notification terms before PO

title: Northstar Customer Support AI
body:
Account context
- Northstar Customer Support AI = multi-tenant support-agent platform (B2B) used by ecommerce + SaaS support orgs.
- Current pain: canary model changes are hard to validate for quality; they only detect issues via downstream support metrics (CSAT drop, escalations) days later.

What they want from Redwood
- Dedicated capacity (VPC) is the end state; will consider starting with Hosted API for a limited-scope POC if we can demonstrate data controls.
- Shadow traffic: mirror production requests to candidate variants while returning control response; score async; surface quality deltas next to latency/cost.
- Quality gates attached to rollouts: show pass/fail + CI bands; support alerting and (eventually) auto-rollback.

Key quotes / call snippets
- 'We’ve had upgrades where everything looked fine on latency and cost, and then our agents started failing tool calls because J...

title: Northbridge Health
body:
Account context
- Regulated healthcare provider network (HIPAA). Security team is heavily involved early; procurement requires security exhibit attachments.
- Primary buyer persona: Security/Compliance + Platform Engineering. Secondary: AI product lead (agent assist + summarization).

Key call notes (high signal)
- Customer quote (1/29): "If we can’t rely on audit logs for every privileged action, we can’t take this to production." They want explicit coverage for: RBAC edits, API key lifecycle (create/rotate/revoke), routing/policy changes, retention/residency settings, SIEM export config changes, and Private install/upgrade operations.
- Customer quote (2/06): "We need contract language that says you have audit logging, what it covers, and how long you retain it." They asked about delivery semantics and duplicates; ok with at-least-once if we provide event_id + guidance to dedupe in ...

blockers: BAA language review (needs confirmation on data handling + audit log retention/residency statements)
Security exhibit requires explicit audit log delivery semantics (at-least-once vs best-effort) and duplicate-event guidance
Private deployment parity concerns: customer wants same RBAC/audit event coverage as Hosted (esp. role edits + key lifecycle)
SSO/SAML integration plan (IdP is Okta; SCIM requested but not required for POC)
Customer requires SIEM export target validation (Splunk HEC primary; Sentinel optional)

- Primary buyer persona: Security/Compliance + Platform Engineering. Secondary: AI product lead (agent assist + summarization).

Key call notes (high signal)
- Customer quote (1/29): "If we can’t rely on audit logs for every privileged action, we can’t take this to production." They want explicit coverage for: RBAC edits, API key lifecycle (create/rotate/revoke), routing/policy changes, retention/residency settings, SIEM export config changes, and Private install/upgrade operations.
- Customer quote (2/06): "We need contract language that says you have audit logging, what it covers, and how long you retain it." They asked about delivery semantics and duplicates; ok with at-least-once if we provide event_id + guidance to dedupe in ...

title: Trident Financial Services
body:
Late-stage regulated finserv account (ABM pilot). They are effectively through technical qualification; the gating factor is procurement/legal language.

Key takeaways from 2025-06-05 procurement/legal call (Fireflies):
- They can proceed with Redwood Private (VPC) if contract includes: US-only data processing/storage (including logs), configurable retention, and clear breach/IR commitments.
- Procurement asked specifically for: service credit schedule, SLA measurement definitions, and whether any third-party subprocessors receive prompt/output content.
- Legal is pushing for uncapped liability for confidentiality/security. Redwood position: standard caps with limited carve-outs; needs business approval if they insist.

- Exact log fields captured by default vs optional (need Security/Eng-confirmed language).

title: EmberFin
body:
Account summary
- Regulated fintech (EU HQ). Building internal + customer-facing GenAI for support + compliance workflows.
- Primary concern is not model quality; it’s governance around rollout/change control for model versions/config.
- Interested in Hosted for speed, but insists on VPC / private networking path and strong audit/change history. Dedicated/Private on table if Hosted governance can’t meet controls.

Current use case
- Support agent assist: summarize customer threads + propose responses (human-in-the-loop).
- Compliance/risk: summarize SAR/transaction investigations; classify issues; retrieve policy excerpts.
- Traffic: initial pilot ~10–30 RPS peak, but expects spikes during incident windows. Latency sensitivity: “p95 can’t jump during rollout; we’ll roll back instantly if it does.”

- Hosted canary rollouts (percentage tr...

title: NorthPeak Health
body:
Account: NorthPeak Health (regulated healthcare provider network). Champion: Security Architecture (platform security) + ML Platform lead.

High-level posture:
- Treats inference as PHI-adjacent (assume PHI in prompts/outputs). They want Private deployment in their AWS VPC with controlled egress.
- Data residency is a hard gate. "US only, including backups and telemetry" (their words). They also asked about where trace spans/logs land.

KMS constraints (detailed):
- Must use customer-managed key (CMK) for envelope encryption; key policy must be least-privilege; no wildcards.
- Prefer separate roles: runtime decrypt role vs rotation/admin role. They asked if Redwood can operate with a split principal model.
- Rotation cadence: quarterly preferred; they will accept annual if we can show strong compensating controls + documented procedure. They want a repeatable runbook and an evidence t...

title: Acme AI
body:
Design partner for Route-level A/B testing (Console > Rollouts > Route experiments). Primary goal is to A/B model variants for their support-agent route with deterministic user assignment and clear guardrails.

Account snapshot
- Product: multi-tenant support automation app (agentic workflows). Heavy chat completions + occasional embeddings for retrieval.
- Current inference posture: mix of 3P APIs + some self-hosted GPU for peak. Wants to consolidate onto Redwood if unit economics + reliability are predictable.
- Deployment: prefers Redwood Dedicated in customer VPC long-term; beta running on Hosted API w/ strict data handling. Initial region: us-east-1; wants eu-west-1 option for EU customers later.

Route experiments requirements (from calls + beta kickoff)
- Needs route-level experiments on a single endpoint (their /agent/respond) with 2-3 variants.
- Variants are primarily differ...

title: HelioFin RegTech
body:
Account summary (regulated fintech prospect)
- HelioFin building RegTech workflows for banks (case triage + investigation summaries + policy Q&A). They are evaluating Redwood primarily for Private (EU VPC) with a path to on-prem later.
- Strong interest in Shadow Traffic quality metrics during rollouts, but only if data handling is “extremely conservative.” Multiple stakeholders repeated: “we can’t create a new copy of regulated customer data that we can’t explain to auditors.”

Primary contacts (per call notes)
- VP Platform Engineering (technical buyer) + Head of Compliance Engineering (risk owner)
- Security officer joined last 20 min of 1/22 call; asked about prompt retention, access logging, and whether Redwood staff can see samples.

Use case / workload
- Mix of chat generation and retrieval-augmented Q&A (embeddings + reranking).
- Workloads include:
 - Investigation summaries ...

title: Acme Banking (Acme Bank)
body:
High-level:
- Acme is treating SIEM export for audit events as a hard gate. Quote from SecOps: "If it’s not in Splunk, it didn’t happen." They’re specifically worried about incident response and access reviews.

SIEM export specifics:
- Target: Splunk HEC (preferred) into Splunk ES. They want control over index/source/sourcetype and a stable event_id for dedup.
- Delivery expectations: they asked for exactly-once semantics; we aligned on at-least-once + downstream dedup keyed by event_id. Need to keep this consistent in writing (FAQ + addendum).
- They asked how we handle downstream outages/429: must not drop events; acceptable to increase lag and backfill when destination recovers.
- Operational needs: status endpoint/console view (last delivered timestamp, lag, last error), plus notification/escalation path during beta.

Audit content / privacy:
- They want actor, action, resource, ...

normalized.nextStep: Provide beta SIEM audit export evidence pack + confirm Splunk HEC delivery semantics (at-least-once + downstream dedup) and finalize security addendum language for procurement.
blockers: SIEM-ready audit export is beta/feature-flagged; Acme wants contractual commitments and clear GA timeline.
They prefer Splunk as system of record; will not accept 'download from console' audit logs for incident response.
Data isolation requirements: Dedicated or equivalent; Hosted multi-tenant requires additional internal approvals.
Backfill limits/retention semantics must be explicit for SOC2 evidence; concern about 'missing events' during downstream throttling.

title: Silverbloom SaaSWorks
body:
Summary:
- Mid-market product analytics SaaS evaluating Redwood for customer-facing features: chat support assistant + internal doc search.
- Starting with Hosted API POC to validate latency & quality, then evaluate Dedicated (VPC) for production.

POC scope / success metrics:
- Hosted POC (2 weeks): integrate chat endpoint + embeddings for KB retrieval.
- Latency target: 95th <= 350ms for short chat (prompt+context <= 512 tokens) on hosted path.
- Throughput: ~120 QPS peak for embeddings ingestion during sync windows, lower concurrent for chat.
- Quality: >85% CSAT on triaged responses in sandbox.

Security / procurement asks (driving Dedicated interest):
- SOC2 Type II report required before procurement sign-off (Lena).
- Must support SSO/SAML (Okta) + SCIM provisioning.
- Audit logs: per-request logging (token/route level) and 365d retention; need exportable logs to their SIEM.
- R...

title: HelioBank
body:
- 2025-04-22: Inbound from HelioBank (security-first posture).
- 2025-04-29: Discovery: initial workload = internal agent for policy + incident summaries; strong need for auditability.
- 2025-05-07: Security review kickoff.
- 2025-05-14: Nate (Security) emailed questionnaire. Quote: "We can't allow prompts or completions to be written to disk anywhere."
- 2025-05-15: S&C confirmed hosted has limited retention for debugging/abuse prevention; strict zero-retention not available on hosted today.
- Open question: will accept Redwood Private (VPC) w/ customer-controlled logging, or need a hosted exception?

Requirements snapshot:
- Must: no prompt/completion content at rest ("no logging / zero retention"), access controls for any debug tooling.
- OK: aggregate metrics (counts/latency), no content.
- Prefer: KMS integration + audit logs for admin access.

Risks:
- If they insist on hosted-o...

title: OrchardLoop Solutions
body:
Founder-led inbound. CEO Aisha Rahman (founder/cto) on call 02/10. Very price-sensitive — comparing Redwood hosted vs OpenAI + Anthropic. Wants a clear per-token example with embeddings + chat split.

Call highlights:
- Use-case: product Q&A + chat-based support for merchants; embed catalog + search, then chat overlay (handoff to human).
- Traffic profile: bursty (Black Friday spikes), typical ~200-300k tokens/day now, expects 2x in 6 months.
- Latency: 150-300ms per token target for chat paths; embeddings can be async.
- Cost sensitivity: founder quote: "If it costs more than OpenAI for same quality we'd struggle to justify."
- Deployment: prefers hosted API (self-serve). No immediate need for VPC.
- Security: wants retention controls (auto-delete logs after 30d), basic audit logging; asking about KMS for API keys but not mandatory now.
- Features: streaming, function-calling for str...

title: Cerulean ML Enterprises
body:
- 2024-11-02: Intro call with CTO Alex Martinez and Head of ML Lina Park. Very interested in latency and cost math.
- 2024-11-20: Demo (streaming + embeddings). AE Jordan did walkthrough; flagged prefetching + KV cache as must-haves.
- 2024-12-05: Security deep dive with Cerulean SecOps (Fireflies FF-20241205-cerulean-security-deepdive). Primary ask: bring-your-own-KMS, audited key rotation, and on-prem option for PII workloads.
- 2025-01-14: Procurement asks for committed pricing. Finance concerned about recurring hosted spend; asked for TCO comparison vs self-host.
- 2025-03-02: Internal infra PoC starts; Cerulean engineering tests vLLM + TGI deployment on mixed Ampere/RTX fleet. Early results show ~40% lower per-token cost vs our hosted quote for their workload profile.
- 2025-09-10: Security pushback remains — they prefer full control over keys and runtime. No champion in product ...

title: Northpeak Bank
body:
Account summary
- Enterprise bank evaluating Redwood Private for internal knowledge + analyst workflows. Very sensitive to security posture, operational controls, and change management.
- Core ask: Private deployment in their AWS VPC (no public egress from inference pods; allowlisted control-plane endpoints only). Secondary: Dedicated capacity as interim bridge if Private lead time slips.

Primary contacts
- Platform Engineering lead ("internal AI platform"), Security Architecture, and SRE manager. Procurement engaged informally (asking for order-of-magnitude pricing + support tier).

Use cases / workload
- Document search + summarization over internal policies and regulatory docs.
- Analyst assistant that may do tool-calling (internal search + retrieval + summarization). Mix of streaming and non-streaming.
- Early sizing shared verbally: 300–800 RPS burst across business hours, but e...

title: IronBank Financial
body:
Account snapshot
- Prospect: IronBank Financial (enterprise FS). Strong security posture; running internal GenAI programs under strict governance.
- Target deployment: Private/VPC (AWS) preferred; Dedicated capacity acceptable if VPC isolation + contractual controls are strong.

Primary asks (security / compliance)
- Least-privilege RBAC w/ separation of duties. Quote from call: "We cannot have 'admin means everything' in prod." They want distinct roles for:
 - Security Admin (identity, RBAC policy, audit log export config)
 - Platform/Deployment Operator (capacity, routing policies, model rollouts)
 - Read-only Auditor (view configs + export audit logs; no mutation)
- Provable enforcement: deny-by-default + consistent permission checks across API + Console + control plane. Asked explicitly whether we enforce only at the gateway or also in downstream services.
- Audit logs to SIEM:
 -...

Security review requires example events + control mapping (SOC2) and answers for emergency/break-glass admin procedure

- Target deployment: Private/VPC (AWS) preferred; Dedicated capacity acceptable if VPC isolation + contractual controls are strong.

Primary asks (security / compliance)
- Least-privilege RBAC w/ separation of duties. Quote from call: "We cannot have 'admin means everything' in prod." They want distinct roles for:
 - Security Admin (identity, RBAC policy, audit log export config)
 - Platform/Deployment Operator (capacity, routing policies, model rollouts)
 - Read-only Auditor (view configs + export audit logs; no mutation)
- Provable enforcement: deny-by-default + consistent permission checks across API + Console + control plane. Asked explicitly whether we enforce only at the gateway or also in downstream services.
- Audit logs to SIEM:
 -...

title: Orbit Analytics
body:
Account context:
- Orbit Analytics is building an LLM layer into their analytics product (analyst assistant + internal support workflows). They’re latency-sensitive and have exec visibility on user-facing responsiveness.
- They explicitly requested Dedicated H100, not shared Hosted API, due to p95 SLO concerns and prior bad experiences with noisy neighbor + config drift.

What they asked for (verbatim-ish from 2/7 call):
- 'Don’t just tell us it’s fast — show p95 under our concurrency and prompt mix.'
- Wants benchmark evidence segmented by sequence-length buckets + concurrency, and wants to know what runtime config/profile was used.

Hardware tuning profiles angle:
- They reacted positively to the idea of Redwood shipping versioned, safe-by-default hardware profiles per GPU SKU (H100 now; B200 later).
- They asked:
 - How profile selection works (auto-detect SKU vs pinned config)
 - ...

title: LegalBeacon Discovery
body:
Summary: evaluation for enterprise RAG search across legal discovery workloads. Primary ask: embeddings + reranking pipeline with strict retention and audit logging. Corpus: estimated 25M docs (mix of scanned PDFs + extracted text), growth ~20%/yr.

Timeline / recent activity:
- 2026-01-14 discovery call (FF-20260114-1532) w/ CTO (Mark Li), Head of Search (Priya Rao), AE Ava Chen. High level requirements captured.
- 2026-02-18 POC kickoff (FF-20260218-1010) — ingested 500K doc sample to test embed pipeline.
- 2026-02-25 security questionnaire submitted; security team asked for KMS/HSM diagram and retention controls.
- 2026-02-27 SE workshop w/ Diego — ran perf tests for rerank at 512-token context; saw p50 token latency ok, p99 for rerank tail ~450ms on dedicated GPU variant.

Requirements / priorities (from multiple stakeholder calls):
- Workload: near-real-time document search + ref...

title: NordicBank Group
body:
Account summary (enterprise / regulated):
- NordicBank is evaluating Redwood for a regulated internal assistant + document search/summarization in EU. Strong emphasis on predictable latency (p95) + provable/defensible perf methodology. Security constraints are non-negotiable.

Current stage / deal shape:
- Considering Redwood Dedicated (reserved capacity) vs Redwood Private (VPC control plane). Initial leaning: Private-in-VPC due to regulatory posture, but they want Dedicated economics if isolation + controls are acceptable.
- Pilot request: EU region only, with rollouts gated (canary cohorts + auto rollback). They asked specifically about FP8/INT8: want the cost/throughput win but are worried about “surprise regressions.”

- Primary: summarization of internal policy + customer comms (mixed prompt sizes; some long-context). Secondary: RAG/...

title: Summit Telecom
body:
High-priority enterprise telecom account (EMEA). The whole evaluation is anchored on EU data residency + predictable Dedicated throughput.

What they care about (verbatim-ish):
- "Residency is not a slide—it's a requirement." They want EU-only processing and storage, plus clarity on human access (support/engineering) and auditability.
- Dedicated capacity: wants to eliminate noisy-neighbor risk and have stable tail latency. They asked for concrete sizing assumptions and how Redwood handles bursts.
- Procurement/legal: will push standard MSA/DPA pack; they want subprocessor change notifications and explicit residency language.

Key technical asks:
- Dedicated: clear throughput guarantees, recommended autoscaling/burst approach (even if narrative), and how quotas/rate limits will be set for multiple teams.
- Routing/fallback: allowed only within EU unless explicit customer opt-in. They ...

title: SentryFolio Compliance Intelligence
body:
Summary: SentryFolio is a mid-enterprise legal/compliance platform serving fintech and marketplace customers. They are evaluating Redwood for a VPC/private RAG deployment focused on large-scale embeddings + reranking for legal discovery and audit workflows. Primary drivers: immutable audit logs, long retention (7+ years), US-only data residency, and deterministic latency/cost.

Key technical requirements: indexing scale to support ~25-50M documents (est. 3-8B tokens), vector store for ~30M embeddings (1536d), dense retrieval + L1 rerank with LLM-based top-K reranking (K=5-10). Target query volume 50-150 QPS peak; monthly active queries ~8M. Latency SLO: p95 end-to-end <=600ms (goal p95 <=450ms) for desktop lawyer experience; p50 <220ms. Cost sensitivity: target cost per query <= $0.02 (embeddings + rerank amortized) for production workload.

Security/compliance: must run in customer V...

title: Northbridge
body:
Account summary
- Northbridge is evaluating Redwood primarily for production inference with experimentation. They currently run a mix of managed APIs (for prototyping) and a small self-hosted cluster (vLLM) for cost control, but they’re hitting operational overhead.
- Key need is route-level A/B testing for model variants with first-class cost attribution in Console (they want to show internal stakeholders cost per request and cost per token by variant).

Primary use case(s)
- /summarize route: customer support ticket + call transcript summarization (high volume, mostly short inputs, occasional long contexts).
- /search route: embeddings + rerank for internal knowledge base (not in POC scope but mentioned as “phase 2”).

- “We need to be able to run experiments safely without building our own bucketing and dashboards. If we can’t attribute cost by variant, finan...

title: Helio Insurance
body:
Stage: PROCUREMENT. Helio Insurance (enterprise, insurance) is in final vendor selection and running a formal legal/compliance checklist. Primary stakeholder group is procurement + security + internal audit.

Key themes from procurement/legal:
- They want retention controls to be productized, not “support-configured.” Specific ask: ability to set TTL by data class and understand purge semantics (scheduled deletion cadence, backfills, and whether deletion is hard-delete vs tombstone).
- They repeatedly asked: “What happens if we set short retention for request logs—does that break incident response?” We discussed documented exceptions/break-glass and that audit logs may have separate minimum retention requirements.
- They asked for legal hold semantics: who can place a hold, how it is audited, and what data classes are in scope.

- VP Product (AI Experiences): owner of provider copilot initiative.
- Director, Data Platform: cares about tenancy boundaries + routing.

- Procurement/Legal: DPA language + incident notification.

Internal team:
- AE: Avery Johnson
- SE: Markus Klein
- Looping in Security/Compliance PM: Naomi Feldman (requested for residency alignment) and Security Eng: Marcus Lin for technical Qs (not yet scheduled).

What they care about (verbatim-ish notes):
- 'We can’t have a surprise failover to another geography. If we pin EU, it must fail closed.'...

body:

- Regulated healthcare provider (US). Strong compliance posture. They are exploring LLM inference for internal workflows (summarization + support agent + “clinical notes assist”), but will not send PHI to a public endpoint.

title: CinderPath Analytics
body:
Account snapshot: mid-market B2B SaaS (user-facing search + chat embedded in product). Product team is pragmatic — wants to move from Hosted API proof to Dedicated reserved capacity to control unit economics and predictable latency. Key asks: deterministic p95/p99 under 100ms for short chat turns, 10k qps/day baseline for embeddings pipeline (batching allowed), predictable cost per 1k tokens, and audit trails for regulated customers.

POC plan (agreed high level): 4-week Dedicated POC. Objectives: demonstrate stable p99 latency under load, quantify tokens-per-second cost with batching/caching, validate canary routing and automated rollback. Canary plan: start with 10% production traffic -> 30% -> 60% -> 100% over 10 days given no regressions. Canary metrics gated: error rate < 0.5%, p99 latency within 1.2x of baseline, quality regression < 1.5% on golden prompt set.

Fallback / routin...

title: Aegis Cortex Solutions
body:
Account summary: Aegis Cortex is a finserv platform offering real-time analytics for trading desks and fraud ops. Evaluating Redwood Dedicated for low-latency inference + private networking. Heavy emphasis on throughput guarantees for sustained 1k TPS across peak windows and 99th p50/p95 tail latency targets. Procurement & legal are the gating items.

Recent timeline:
- 2026-01-20: Intro call (AE Ava) - high-level product fit, AE demo, interested in Dedicated.
- 2026-02-10: Architecture deep-dive (SE Diego) - validated model types (mix of 7B/13B Llama variants + custom reranker), discussed autoscaling and reserved GPU pools.
- 2026-02-24: Capacity planning workshop - provided baseline token/sec per GPU numbers, proposed initial reservation: 8x A100-equivalent GPUs (reserved) + 2x burst.
- 2026-02-27: Workload profiling call (Fireflies ff_2026-02-27_4b9c3d) - customer sent sample logs,...

title: Cedarstone Health
body:
Account context:
- Large healthcare provider w/ centralized infra/security. Team is actively evaluating LLM inference options but has a hard “run it in our environment” posture.
- Champion so far appears to be Infrastructure Platform (Director-level); security team is driving gating questions.

What they care about (verbatim/paraphrased):
- “No patient data leaves our environment.”
- “We need to know exactly what’s logged and where it goes—our SIEM team will ask day 1.”
- “Our PKI is internal; we can’t use public certs for internal services.”

Positioning notes:
- Lead with: deployment flexibility (on-prem) + operational controls (audit logs, RBAC, retention) + predictable performance.
- Avoid over-claiming on healthcare compliance language. Keep it to controls/evidence; do not use ‘HIPAA-certified’ phrasing.

Open questions to resolve in the deep dive:
- On-prem packaging: installer/...

Security team wants audit log export details (schema + transport) before approving any pilot.
Open question: whether Cedarstone will accept “PHI-adjacent only” scope for initial POC.

title: CompassLoop Labs
body:
Short-form notes from calls and internal threads:
- CTO: "need predictable p95 under 300ms for chat; embeddings can be batchy"
- Product: concerned about model drift on user prompts; wants eval hooks and regression alerts
- Ops: wants VPC + KMS + audit logs; whitelist IPs for canary run
- Pricing: mid-market budget, prefers 12-month commit if clear unit economics
- Quote: 'If Redwood can guarantee consistent inference cost per 1M tokens, we'll push to procurement'
- POC acceptance criteria: p95 latency < 300ms (chat), throughput 150 reqs/min for search, error rate < 1%, fallback successful <1% of requests during failover tests

blockers: internal legal review on data residency clause

title: Northstar Bank
body:
Account context
- Northstar Bank (regulated). Platform team building internal GenAI capabilities (ops support + policy summarization + document Q&A). Current approach is a mix of internal GPU clusters + vendor APIs; they want a single inference platform with predictable operations.

Why they care about Runtime LTS (key theme)
- They have strict change management (CAB) and a low tolerance for "surprise" regressions. They explicitly asked for an LTS channel with:
 - Stable kernel + scheduling behavior (no experimental heuristics landing mid-stream)
 - Predictable patch cadence (monthly preferred; out-of-band only for security/Sev1)
 - Explicit backport eligibility rubric (security + Sev1/Sev2 only; minimal diffs)
 - Enterprise-grade release notes (categorized changes + known issues + perf notes)
- Quote from 2/6 call (paraphrase): "We can’t upgrade every few weeks just to keep things ru...

title: Copper Falcon Dynamics
body:
Summary: Mid-market product analytics SaaS evaluating Redwood. Initial onboarding via hosted API to validate latency/quality, scoped move to Dedicated (VPC) for production. Security review underway (SOC2 + SSO + audit logs + KMS).

Discovery highlights:
- Customer profile: B2B SaaS (product analytics SDK + workspace), NA HQ, EU users require residency.
- Primary workloads: chat (customer-facing assistant in-app), embeddings for search across event data, reranking for top-N results.
- Traffic profile: steady baseline ~150 qps embeddings/search, bursty chat peaks ~400 qps during release windows. Cost-sensitive for bursts.
- Latency targets: chat p95 < 200ms for 512-token flows, embedding gen < 80ms single-vector on avg.
- Model preference: prefer Llama-3-class 70B quantized int8 on Dedicated; acceptable fallback to 13B for lower-cost routes. Want routing policy: prefer low-latency (dedi...

title: Helio Finance
body:
2025-12-11 (LM): Inbound via EMEA partner channel. Helio says procurement will require marketplace route for any new spend; prefers GCP Marketplace. Interested in Private (VPC) because of regulatory posture.

2026-01-09 (LM): Discovery call. Platform lead: "We can’t use a public hosted LLM API for regulated case data." Wants Redwood Private with clear residency controls + audit trail. They are comparing Vertex (private service connect) and Bedrock (but worried about limited model control).

2026-01-22 (MK): Security-focused follow-up. Key asks: CMEK/KMS, audit logs export, retention controls (disable prompt/output retention), and confirmation Redwood has SOC 2 Type II + ISO-aligned controls (evidence via portal / on request). They want a marketplace security packet for internal vendor onboarding.

2026-02-03 (MK + Naomi Feldman): Security review walkthrough. Helio asked for: sample au...

title: Tensorlytics
body:
Key contacts (from calls/notes):
- VP Eng (buyer): focused on risk + reliability, wants contractual SLOs and a regression story
- Platform Eng lead (champion): wants deep metrics (queueing vs execution), reproducible baselines, and alert hygiene

What they said (paraphrased / partial quotes):
- “We can’t have a kernel update turn into a 2-week fire drill again.”
- “p95 is what we live and die by. If p50 is great but p95 regresses, our product feels broken.”
- “Show me how you detect regressions before my users do.”

Dedicated interest:
- Strong lean toward Dedicated for predictable throughput + isolation. Hosted API is acceptable for early dev but they expect to migrate production to Dedicated.
- Asked if Redwood can reserve capacity with region-level failover and clear rollback procedures.

SLO expectations (needs to be formalized):
- Wants p95 first-token and p95 end-to-end lat SLOs...

- Strong lean toward Dedicated for predictable throughput + isolation. Hosted API is acceptable for early dev but they expect to migrate production to Dedicated.
- Asked if Redwood can reserve capacity with region-level failover and clear rollback procedures.

SLO expectations (needs to be formalized):
- Wants p95 first-token and p95 end-to-end lat SLOs...

title: Elmwood Reserve Planning
body:
CRM notes from discovery + workshop. Short bullets and call fragments below.

- Company: Elmwood Reserve Planning (payment/settlement platform for mid-size banks). Heavy PII, regulated EU and NA traffic.
- Primary ask: dedicated capacity with throughput guarantees and predictable unit economics for customer-facing chat assistant + background reranking pipeline.

title: Guardian LogWorks, Inc.
body:
Summary: Guardian LogWorks is evaluating Redwood Private (VPC) to replace an aging in-house inference pipeline for customer-facing fraud-detection and support triage. Primary decision levers are auditability and data residency. InfoSec is focused on SIEM integration (Splunk/QRadar), log schema parity with existing telemetry, and BYOK/KMS with HSM attestation.

Key requirements / asks (verbatim & paraphrase):
- "Customer quote" : "We need a 1:1 mapping of inference events into our SIEM with the same fields as app logs (request_id, user_id, model_version, prompt_hash, tokens_in, tokens_out, decision, latency_ms, route, node_id)".
- SIEM export: prefer syslog-over-TLS (CEF/LEEF mapping) and HTTPS push for cloud SIEMs; expect retention to be handled in SIEM but require ability to export raw logs for eDiscovery.
- Log schema: must include stable request_id (traceable to upstream app), time...

title: Crescent BriefSearch
body:
Summary of account and technical requirements:
- Business: midsize eDiscovery/compliance vendor supporting law firms and in-house counsel. Primary product is a searchable discovery index for litigation and regulatory investigations.
- Data scale: currently ~120M documents (30TB raw); anticipate growth to 300M+ over 18 months. Indexing cadence: daily ingestion of new corpora + nightly re-index for metadata updates.
- Architecture preference: private deployment (VPC control plane) with on-prem inference nodes inside customer network for PHI/PII workloads. Must support customer-managed KMS/HSM for key custody.
- Search stack: existing inverted-index (Elastic) + separate vector store for semantic search. Want Redwood to handle embedding generation + persistent vector index + reranking pipeline (dense retrieval -> cross-encoder rerank).
- Performance targets: median end-to-end query latenc...

title: Cypress Canyon Inference Labs
body:
Account: mid-stage enterprise evaluation focused on high-throughput reranking for ad ranking pipeline.
Primary ask: predictable p95 token latency for online reranker + bulk embedding generation for nightly indexing.
Customer quote: "We need guaranteed tail-latency or the business impact is material."
Cost sensitivity: medium-high; wants reserved capacity discounts but needs SLA guarantees before committing.
Security: must run inference inside customer VPC; HSM/KMS integration mandatory for key material; audit logs for token-level usage requested.

title: Ironbridge Medical AI
body:
CRM notes:
- Company: Ironbridge Medical AI (large regional health system running AI for clinician workflow).
- Primary pain: strict regulatory + residency requirements across US & EU, must be able to run inference in customer VPC.
- Key ask: Private VPC deployment with SSO (SAML/Okta), SCIM provisioning for automated user/groups, and fine-grained RBAC exposed in Redwood Console/control plane mapped to AD groups.
- Security stack: customer uses AWS (GovCloud for some workloads) + Splunk for SIEM. They require: customer-managed KMS keys (CMK), audit log streaming to Splunk/CloudWatch, 7-year retention for logs, and SOC2/ISO27001 evidence.
- Quote from CISO: "No console admin user that isn't bound to our AD groups — full RBAC and SCIM mapping is non-negotiable."
- Technical notes from SE call: need SAML metadata template, SCIM provisioning test plan, sample mapping for role -> AD group ...

title: Havenwell Clinical Search
body:
CRM notes (synthesized) - compressed bullets and call paraphrase.

Account background:
- Enterprise healthtech platform, supports ambulatory EHR integrations and a clinical documentation archive with PHI.
- ~120M indexed docs (notes, reports, imaging captions) today; expect 3x growth in 18 months.
- Strong data retention requirements (7+ years) and strict egress controls.

Primary ask / product fit:
- Replace brittle keyword search with RAG: dense embeddings + cross-encoder reranker for high precision at top-K.
- Throughput target: 120 QPS sustained for peak business hours; peak spikes to 400 QPS for surge.
- Latency SLO: p95 end-to-end < 450ms for clinical search queries; soft p99 < 900ms.
- Cost sensitivity: target <$0.015 per user query (inclusive of retrieval + rerank).
- Index scale concerns: embeddings store ~200M vectors, average vector dim 768; expect storage + retrieval cost ...

title: Horizon Vega Compute
body:
2026-02-18 discovery call w/ infra + security. Primary ask: private VPC deployment with strict data residency (US) and KMS integration.
Customer IdP: Okta. Explicit requirement: SAML 2.0 + SCIM provisioning for automated user lifecycle. Quote from Dir. SRE: 'We must not manage users manually in console.'
RBAC expectations: map Okta groups to console roles (admin, engineer, auditor). Need support for role scoping per-project and read-only audit roles.
KMS: prefer AWS KMS CMEK; want HSM-backed keys and key rotation policy tied into internal audit cadence. Must show architecture for how keys are referenced from private control plane.
Audit logging: retention 7 years for security events; logs must be exportable to their Splunk/ELK stack and include token-level request IDs.
Data residency: primary storage in us-east-1; backup replica in us-west-2; no EU/APAC copies permitted by legal.
POC ...

title: Hexagon Support Systems
body:
Account summary
- Enterprise customer running a support platform with heavy weekday seasonality.
- Primary pain: daily ramp causes queue buildup + p95 latency regressions if scaling is purely reactive; previous vendor either over-provisioned all day or had cold starts.
- Secondary pain: finance requires a strict cost ceiling. Quote from discovery: “We need a hard max. If the system decides to scale, it can’t surprise us.”

Current state / evaluation status
- Evaluating Redwood Dedicated (reserved pools) + autoscaling policy controls.
- Pilot scope: 1 Dedicated deployment in us-east-1, targeting support-agent traffic + reranker.
- They are responsive and technical; security + procurement involved early.

Traffic profile (from calls)
- Weekday ramp: roughly 2–3x within ~60–90 minutes in the morning; stable midday; smaller ramp again late afternoon.
- Incidents: irregular spikes; they ac...

Data residency question for EU sub-tenant (possible second region needed)

title: LexForte Compliance Hub
body:
Account summary:
- LexForte is a mid-size legaltech provider focused on enterprise e-discovery and compliance automation. Looking to replace homegrown retrieval stack with RAG + reranking for higher precision on privileged/PII detection.

Requirements:
- Index scale: target initial index 25M docs, long-term 150M+ artifacts (PDFs, ESI, transcripts).
- Query volume: peak ~800 qps across tenants; average ~150 qps.
- Latency targets: embedding nearest-neighbor recall <200ms (interactive), end-to-end rerank <350-450ms for single-query interactive flows.
- Cost sensitivity: target <$0.03/query average across reranking path; willing to trade 10-15% latency for ~20% cost reduction via quantization/batching.
- Models: prefer open weights (Llama-family / Mistral variants) for private deployment; require validated quantized variants and documented quality regressions.
- Routing/fallback: per-cus...

title: Nordic Bank
body:
Account summary
- Large EU-headquartered bank. Exploring LLM inference vendor for internal customer support + knowledge workflows.
- Primary evaluation driver: EU data residency + strong auditability + ability to report/attribute costs by region/business unit.
- They are skeptical of “black box” managed APIs; want clarity on where data flows and how logs are handled.

Current status (as of 2025-02-22)
- Stage: Evaluation (security review in progress, solution architecture being validated).
- Commercial: early pricing alignment pending (Dedicated EU capacity vs Private VPC deployment).

Key contacts (customer)
- Head of AI Platform (technical sponsor)
- Security Architecture lead (controls, audit logging, SIEM)
- Data Protection Officer team (DPIA/DPA review)
- Finance/Procurement (wants region-specific unit economics; chargeback model)

title: Elevaris Bridgeworks
body:
Company snapshot: mid-market B2B productivity SaaS. Building contextual in-app assistant and improved support routing. Heavy focus on latency for US customers.
Primary ask: Start on Hosted API to validate UX, then move to Dedicated for predictable latency and data isolation. Roadmap expects Dedicated by end of Q1 if POC passes.
Workload profile: chat-heavy (coaching assistant) with embeddings for doc search. Average context window ~3k tokens; peak concurrent QPS ~40-60; spike to 120 for quarterly product launches.
Latency SLO: 95p end-to-end <= 150ms for short-turn chat (<=256 tokens). Cost sensitivity: mid; willing to commit for 6-12 month term if predictable pricing reduces TCO by >=30% vs hosted pay-as-you-go.
Security: Okta SSO required, org-managed KMS keys for token encryption, audit logs retention 90 days, US data residency. Need SOC2 + ISO27001 evidence (we provided security F...

title: Kepler Copilot Plugins
body:
2026-02-18 discovery call (Jordan/Priya) - strong interest in low-latency streaming for inline completions in VS Code extension.
Customer profile: VS Code extension publisher with ~120 employees; main product distributes a copilot to enterprise customers (SaaS + self-hosted installs).
Primary ask: sub-100ms median response for inline suggestions (single-line completions) and <200ms for multi-line streaming; aim to feel 'native' inside editor.
Traffic/scale: target POC load = 200 concurrent active devs, peak RPS ~1,200 for completion endpoints, average tokens per completion ~18 tokens.
Cost sensitivity: high. Wants predictable unit economics; open to reserved capacity if it reduces per-token cost for high-traffic customers.
Model constraints: want model/version pinning per workspace and per-customer; support for lightweight 4-bit quantized variants for edge customers; fallback chain to...

title: Northbridge Payments
body:
Account came inbound after public benchmark report (EU region numbers). They forwarded the interactive table internally and asked if we can hold p95 steady under load and how Dedicated/Private maps to their residency requirements.

High-level: payments platform w/ strong EU footprint (merchant + consumer). They are exploring LLM for internal ops + customer support (summarize disputes, chargeback evidence, KYC/AML case notes) and eventually an agent-assisted workflow for risk analysts.

What resonated:
- EU region performance in the benchmark (their comment: "finally someone publishing p95 + cost in the same chart")
- Consistency story (same API across Hosted/Dedicated/Private; ability to start Hosted for pilot then move into VPC)

Key requirements (from discovery + follow-up email):
- Latency SLO: target p95 <= 700ms end-to-end for short prompts ("support macro suggestions") and p95 <...

title: Northpeak AI
body:
Account summary (Northpeak AI)
- Strong tail-latency focus; they evaluate vendors primarily on p95/p99 under mixed prompt lengths.
- Quote from deep dive: "Our short chat requests can’t get stuck behind long-context jobs. Users notice immediately." They described classic head-of-line blocking symptoms.

Workload + requirements
- Primary: chat generation for support agent + internal ops assistant.
- Secondary: summarization + doc search (embeddings), but their gating factor is chat tail latency.
- Traffic pattern: mix of short interactive prompts (typical chat) + periodic long-context prompts (importing large ticket threads / knowledge packs). Long-context can spike during batch workflows.
- Success metrics (as stated by them / aligned in call):
 - Improve or hold p95 TTFT for short prompts during long-context spikes.
 - Keep throughput roughly flat (they’ll tolerate small hit if p95 i...

title: Holloway ProductStack
body:

title: MapleForge Innovations
body:
Mid-market product analytics SaaS; heavy embeddings + conversational help widget across product pages.
Started on Hosted API to validate quality (Feb). Positive quality for embeddings but cost sensitive at scale.
PM quote: 'need predictable unit economics — can't have token surprises mid-quarter.'
Interested in Dedicated for predictable throughput, VPC option, and KMS integration for keys.
Wants canary + fallback routing: primary low-cost quantized model for most traffic, automatic failover to higher-capacity variant when quality or latency thresholds breached.
Security review: SOC2 ok, legal requested added language around residual model caching and data residency.

title: Veridian MedIntel Search
body:
Shard + incremental index required — they expect continuous ingest from EHR + RIS feeds.
PHI constraint: no egress, all raw PHI must stay inside hospital VPC. Quote from CISO: 'No PHI leaves our networks; full audit trail required.'
Current prototype: embeddings generated with open-encoder, 100 dim vectors; local vector store on k-NN. Reranker is BERT-base finetuned on 6k labeled q|d pairs.
Evaluation results: reranker improves clinical relevance (BLEU/NDCG proxies) but doubles latency at top-5. Team asking for quantized re-ranker or offload pattern to reduce p95.
Indexing scale: target scope ~18,000,000 documents (avg doc length 1.8k tokens), ~1.2B tokens total; expected growth 25%/yr.
Traffic profile: 100–200 QPS peak across global sites; most queries require < 250ms server-side latency for interactive clinician workflows.
Cost target: internal benchmark <$0.005 per query (embedding...

normalized.notes:
Shard + incremental index required — they expect continuous ingest from EHR + RIS feeds.
PHI constraint: no egress, all raw PHI must stay inside hospital VPC. Quote from CISO: 'No PHI leaves our networks; full audit trail required.'
Current prototype: embeddings generated with open-encoder, 100 dim vectors; local vector store on k-NN. Reranker is BERT-base finetuned on 6k labeled q|d pairs.
Evaluation results: reranker improves clinical relevance (BLEU/NDCG proxies) but doubles latency at top-5. Team asking for quantized re-ranker or offload pattern to reduce p95.
Indexing scale: target scope ~18,000,000 documents (avg doc length 1.8k tokens), ~1.2B tokens total; expected growth 25%/yr.
Traffic profile: 100–200 QPS peak across global sites; most queries require < 250ms server-side latency for interactive clinician workflows.
Cost target: internal benchmark <$0.005 per query (embedding...
normalized.nextStep: 2-week reranker latency POC on Dedicated GPU pool; security pack review with InfoSec (SOW draft)
blockers: PHI ingestion pipeline sign-off from legal
per-query cost > target with current reranker

title: SummitForge Technologies
body:
Overview: Enterprise evaluation for Dedicated capacity to support customer-facing chat + high-throughput reranking pipelines. Primary ask: predictable throughput guarantees and latency SLOs for multi-region deployments behind VPC.

Timeline / recent activity:
- 2026-02-20: Discovery call (Marissa, Diego, CTO Rajiv Mehta). Collected baseline QPS and latency targets.
- 2026-02-25: Security intake submitted; asked for SOC2 report, KMS integration options, and data residency controls.
- 2026-02-28: Kickoff POC meeting (fireflies: ff_20260228_01_summitforge_kickoff). Agreed on model candidates and sizing approach.
- 2026-03-01: Baseline throughput run against in-cloud hosted endpoint. 95th pctl latency observed ~210ms for 512-token context on llama2-70b-chat-v1 in FP16.
- 2026-03-03: Dedicated test #1: reserved 4xA100-80GB pool, quantized weights (int8/awq prototyping). Throughput improved...

title: Ironbark Assurance Technologies
body:
Account summary: Large regulated fintech focused on automated compliance tooling; evaluating an enterprise-grade IDE copilot for internal engineer productivity. Primary constraints: code never leaves VPC or on-prem boundary; strict retention and audit requirements; must integrate with customer KMS and SSO (SAML + SCIM).

Recent calls: CTO emphasized "cannot have dev code exfiltrated — full on‑prem/VPC deployment or air‑gapped option required". Security team requested example audit logs and retention policy (7 years preferred for certain records). Procurement asked for cost model for 2000 devs with steady-state 200 concurrent low-latency sessions.

title: Nordbank
body:
Account: Nordbank (EU bank). This is an RFP-driven deal with a strict security gate.

Primary stakeholders (customer):
- Procurement lead: "We need SOC 2 + clear EU residency statement before we can shortlist"
- Security architect: cares about audit log export + SIEM integration, and retention controls
- Data Protection: wants a crisp data flow + residency + deletion narrative by deployment mode

What they asked repeatedly (verbatim-ish):
- "Can you guarantee all data stays in the EU, including logs?" -> Must answer by mode; do not over-commit.
- "Are you ISO 27001 certified?" -> Use approved ISO alignment language only.
- "Do you train on our data?" -> Use standard definition (no training on customer prompts/outputs); reference FAQ wording.
- "How do we get SOC 2?" -> Must follow evidence request playbook; NDA required.

Product fit:
- Strong pull for Private/VPC in EU (Frankfurt lik...

title: Brighton Mosaic Software
body:
Short CRM notes / call snippets:
- Product/CTO: Maya Chen; Platform lead: Aaron Holt.
- Primary pain: current in-house embeddings + search costs high; support volume spiky -> need predictable capacity.
- Latency targets: p50 <= 200ms, p95 <= 550ms for user-facing assistant; throughput target ~40-60 RPS steady-state; peak bursting to 150 RPS acceptable with burst capacity.
- Cost sensitivity: wants to keep annual inference spend < $150k if possible, open to reserved commit for Dedicated.
- Model preferences: prefer curated open models; want 2-tier model strategy: high-quality 13B for premium customers + quantized 7B for fallback/bulk.
- Routing/fallback: plan to route by customer tier -> premium -> primary region/model, default -> cheaper quantized variant; region failover US->EU if US capacity degraded.
- \"Canary\" approach agreed: 2-week canary within POC (10% traffic -> 30% -> 100%...

title: Verdant VPC Guardians
body:
Summary notes and call fragments:
- 'We cannot have logs leave our account' — security lead on 2026-02-10 call.
- Primary ask: SIEM export to Splunk (HEC) and Datadog (HTTP JSON). Sumo/CEF optional.
- Retention: default 90d fine for telemetry; compliance requires 7y cold archive for audit logs. Legal wants WORM/immutable option.
- Log schema: want one-line JSON events, schema fields below. Must include request_id, model, model_version, timestamp, tenant_id, user_id_hash, prompt_hash, token_count, response_token_count, latency_ms, node_id, batch_id, kv_cache_hit(boolean), decision (served/fallback), error_code/null, redaction_flags.
- PII policy: customer will send prompt samples for review; Redwood must provide redaction pipeline or clear instructions for client-side hashing/redaction before sending.
- KMS: customer-managed keys (AWS KMS) required for both storage at rest and for encr...

title: SableBridge Technologies
body:
Account: SableBridge - risk/treasury platform for institutional customers.
Primary ask: enterprise-grade code assistant integrated into internal IDE (JetBrains + VS Code) with streaming completions and sub-50ms char latency for local agents.
Must support model/version pinning per team and rollback strategy for regressions.
Data controls: strict retention policies, ability to purge customer prompts, audit trail for all model calls.
Security: SSO/SAML SSO via Okta, SCIM required for provisioning, HSM-backed KMS, SOC2/ISO27001 expected.
Network: prefer Private deployment (VPC) with optional on-prem runner for secret clearance teams. No external internet access from on-prem agents.
Integration: want IDE plugin with streaming token-by-token update, ability to disable streaming per policy, and local latency telemetry exposed to Redwood Console.
Scale: initial pilot ~120 devs, ramp to ~1,200...

title: Radiant Cove Platforms
body:
Account snapshot: mid-market SaaS (product analytics platform) evaluating Redwood for customer-facing features (chat support and semantic search + reranking).

POC summary:
- Use: embedding-based doc search for KB + reranking for support answers; chat assistant for power users.
- Volume: initial scope ~300 QPS at peak for search requests, burstable; expected growth to 1k QPS over 12 months.
- Latency targets: p50 <= 150ms for short-context chat; p95 <= 600ms acceptable for long rerank calls.
- Cost sensitivity: moderate — prefer committed discount for Dedicated but want ability to fall back to Hosted during scale events.
- Model preferences: open models where possible (quantized variants) for cost; allow fallback to smaller parameter models for non-critical traffic. Need model pinning and rollout controls.

Security / Compliance notes:
- Primary ask: Dedicated in VPC with SOC2 Type II...

- Model preferences: open models where possible (quantized variants) for cost; allow fallback to smaller parameter models for non-critical traffic. Need model pinning and rollout controls.

Security / Compliance notes:
- Primary ask: Dedicated in VPC with SOC2 Type II...

title: SignalHarbor Technica
body:
- Early signal: product and infra teams both engaged; product owns requirements, infra owns VPC/peering.
- Trial feedback: model quality good for top-3 suggestions, reranker required for noisy KB.
- Quote from PM: "Need predictable latency for agent assist — can't have long tails during business hours."
- SE notes: prefer canary traffic split controlled by API gateway + Redwood routing policies; want SDK examples for rollout/rollback.
- Security asks: SOC2 copy, pen-test summary, KMS integration guide, retention controls. InfoSec flagged network egress for VPC peering (must allow only redwood-managed egress IPs).
- Commercial: they want a 12m committed Dedicated price vs pay-as-you-go hosted comparison. Procurement open to reserved capacity with burst credit.
- POC constraints: 2-week canary window, synthetic load generator, must demo region failover using traffic shift and cold-start...

title: Copper Ridge Software
body:
Copy from call notes — Product lead (Tom Alvarez) emphasised: "Must feel instantaneous for agents; no long pauses."
Primary workload: internal IT chat for L1/L2 agents, triage + suggested responses, quick KB lookups (~6k docs).
Latency SLO: target p95 < 350ms for 1-2 sentence replies; comfortable with slightly higher for long-form summaries.
Streamed replies + token-level cost transparency are required — they want to show savings vs human-only triage.
Tool calling: need guaranteed at-least-once calls to ticketing API (create/update/search) and structured returns for UI actions.
Security asks: SAML SSO for admin console, audit log retention 1 year, option to redact PII before storage, KMS integration for keys.
EI/ops: interested in auto-batching heuristics but worried about latency spikes during bursts (support-hours peaks).
Finance asked for Hosted vs Dedicated TCO comparison (3 year)...

title: Grafton Vector Labs
body:
Summary: Grafton Vector Labs is evaluating Redwood for a RAG-driven enterprise search product used across multiple EU financial divisions. Primary concerns: strict EU/UK residency, predictable latency (<80ms target median for reranked top-5), and low cost per query (~0.4-1.2 cents targeted).

Scale & workload: planning index of ~500M small-doc vectors (250M user-facing docs + metadata), embedding dim 1536, expected sustained QPS 60-150 with spikes to 400 during market opens. Reranking: bi-stage approach (approximate ANN recall -> cross-encoder rerank). Want ability to route heavy traffic to dedicated fleets and fallback to cheaper hosted variants if capacity constrained.

Technical asks from last call: "Can you guarantee EU/UK residency for index & audit logs?" InfoSec asked for KMS/HSM integration, SOC2 + ISO27001 evidence, and 7-year audit log retention for certain pipelines. Networ...

title: Tideward Closed Network Services
body:
Lead: CISO office (Lt. Cmdr. R. Hale) - very prescriptive on supply chain and hardware provenance.
"They cannot accept outbound telemetry or cloud egress" — repeated by security during kick-off.
POC ask: on-prem private deployment with no internet egress; optional operator console via air-gapped laptop bundle.
Preferred models: audited open weights + quantized runtime; company will consider Redwood model compilation if can run fully offline.
Latency requirement: p95 < 120ms for short chat flows, tail latency allowance during batching windows; throughput burstable to 120 rps.
Cost sensitivity: moderate — willing to buy reserved capacity but needs clear unit economics vs. in-house build.
Security redlines: HSM-backed key management, signed binaries for runtime, reproducible SBOM and supply-chain attestation.
"Action item: provide sample on-prem deployment runbook and HSM integration dia...

body:

- Building an AI assist layer inside their agent console: summarize long ticket threads, suggest replies, and retrieve relevant KB snippets.
- Primary KPI for POC: reduce agent handling time without increasing escalations.
- They self-serve signed up via Hosted API; now evaluating whether to standardize on Redwood for production.

Current status (evaluation)
- POC in progress with 2 internal teams (Support Ops + KB team).

- Latency targets (stated):

title: Nimbus Logistics Optimization
body:
Summary: Enterprise logistics SaaS (fleet + TMS) evaluating Redwood Dedicated for real-time routing and customer-facing assistants. Primary driver: predictable per-hour throughput for peak loads (morning dispatch window) and tight 95th percentile latency targets for API responses used in routing decisions. Asking for committed GPU capacity plan that guarantees throughput and clear latency SLOs with fallback routing to lower-cost model variants during degradation.

POC findings / numbers:
- Baseline POC used 2xA10-equivalent nodes with continuous batching; sustained 1.2M tokens/day across routing+chat mix.
- Observed 95th P50 latency: routing model (gpt-like open model) ~220ms token latency, end-to-end (prompt+postproc) median 350ms on dedicated bench.
- Peak concurrency pattern: 700 concurrent sessions in 10:00-11:00 window; average request size 120 tokens; spiky arrival distribution....

title: Faraday Works
body:
Overview: Faraday Works builds edge telemetry & fleet management for industrial vehicles. Evaluating Redwood Dedicated to run low-latency telemetry summarization and an internal support assistant.

Key ask: guaranteed sustained throughput for two models (telemetry-summarizer-v2, support-assistant-3B-qa) with 99th pct latency SLOs and reserved GPU planning for predictable monthly spend. Team wants clear fallbacks + routing to cheaper quantized variants under load bursts.

From AE call (Jordan): 'We need predictable tail latency during morning and evening batch windows; cost controls are critical — procurement will insist on commitment ranges and rollover credits.'

Security notes: Must support SSO via SAML, customer-managed KMS, and 90-day audit log retention. Data residency requirement: NA-only for PII extracted from telemetry.

Gist of POC: 3-week Dedicated POC: week1 deploy + smoke,...

title: Northbridge Helix Analytics
body:
Account summary: large fintech analytics provider, high regulatory burden (US + EU customers). Strong preference for Private (VPC) deployment and HSM-backed keys. Key asks: 1) support AWS KMS (and CloudHSM-backed CMKs), 2) Azure Key Vault with HSM/Dedicated HSM options, 3) Google Cloud KMS + Cloud HSM, 4) BYOK import path with customer-managed root keys and clear import/rotation process.

Security notes (CRM style):
- "Open" to Redwood Private if we can demonstrate HSM-backed key usage and audit trail mapping to their SIEM.
- "Must" have KMS key usage logs forwarded to their SIEM (CloudTrail/Activity logs) and retention policy 7 years.
- Asking for explicit support of envelope encryption: store DEKs locally in Private control plane, KEK in customer KMS. Concern: extra latency for remote KEK unwrap on token cooling path.
- Customer mentioned: "We cannot accept platform-managed keys for...

title: Midnight Pegasus Infer Labs
body:
Summary: evaluating Redwood for millisecond-sensitive trading signal pipeline. Primary ask: sub-50ms p50 inference for short-context (<=64 tokens) reranking + chat ops at ~1k QPS sustained with spikes to 4k QPS. Cost sensitivity high — target unit cost < $0.0006/token equivalent for production. Interested in batching, KV/prefix caching, and aggressive quantization profiles.

Recent activities (timeline):
- 2025-01-20: Intro call (AE Jamie O., CTO A. Khatri). high-level requirements, shared architecture diagram (drive link).
- 2025-01-28: Deep-dive with infra (SE Priya) — discussed current on-prem DGX fleet, NIC topology, and firewall constraints. noted CPU+network egress concern.
- 2025-02-05: Security kickoff — provided SOC2 report; asked for SAML flow spec. (ticket SEC-2178).
- 2025-02-11: Benchmarking scoping call (Fireflies ff_20250211_7f9c). Agreed to 2-week perf POC using Redwoo...

title: Trident Throughput Solutions
body:
Summary: Trident is evaluating Redwood Dedicated for a high-throughput reranking + embedding pipeline. Primary driver: predictable token economics and strict latency SLOs for enterprise search product. Team is sensitive to warm-up behaviour and cold-start P99 spikes.

Key quotes: "We need guarantees for sustained throughput at 100k queries/day with p95 < 80ms and p99 < 250ms for short sequences" — VP of Engineering.

Requirements (call notes):
- Throughput guarantees for peak windows (burst to 5x sustained for 2 hours).
- Clear SLOs/Penalty language: p95/p99 latency bands and measurement window.
- Warm pools or pre-warmed GPUs to avoid KV cache cold starts for reranking (many small queries).
- Scale-up policy must provision capacity within 90s for traffic bursts. Scale-down should avoid frequent oscillation.
- Support for VPC deployment and KMS-managed keys, full audit logs, and SSO/S...

title: Northstar Health Systems
body:
Account summary
- Northstar Health Systems evaluating Redwood Private for on-prem/air-gapped deployment supporting: (1) clinical note summarization, (2) internal policy/document search (embeddings), (3) patient support contact center agent assist.
- High compliance posture. Treats inference outputs as part of regulated workflow; strong change-management process (CAB) and strict separation between prod/staging.

Firmographics / environment
- Large healthcare org (1k+). Primary region NA.
- Deployment: on-prem Kubernetes (air-gapped). No direct pull from public registries; requires offline artifacts (charts/images/binaries) delivered via internal artifact repository.
- Storage: mix of CSI-backed volumes; snapshot capability varies by cluster. Customer explicitly asked for a supported logical backup path when snapshots aren’t available.

Current stage: evaluation

title: Galvanic Harbor Systems
body:
— Vertical: payments platform (real-time fraud + in-chat agent).
— Primary ask: Dedicated GPU pool with throughput guarantees for peak windows (US region).
— Latency SLO targets: p95 <150ms for <256-token responses, p99 <400ms for longer pipelined flows.
— Throughput planning: target sustained 400–600 rps during business hours, 1,200 rps burst capacity during promos. Need guidance on reserved GPU sizing (estimating 6x A100-equivalents) and burst behavior.
— Autoscaling preferences: hybrid model — warm pool of pre-warmed GPUs (target 3–6 per AZ), reactive scale-up when queue length >120 and sustained for 30s, conservative scale-down with 15m cooldown. Warm pool warm-up target <= 90s (model load + kernel warming).
— Queueing behavior: want bounded queue with backpressure signal to app at 250 queued requests; prefer graceful degradation to lower-cost model variant rather than hard reject...

title: Crestline Payments, Inc.
body:
Account: Crestline Payments (global PSP) evaluating Redwood Dedicated for latency-sensitive payment workflows.
Primary contact: CTO Marcus Hall, also working with Head of Infra (Lina Ortega) and Legal (SLA review)
Quote from CTO on 2026-02-10 demo: 'We need deterministic p95 <=150ms for user-facing chat/risk calls and sustained throughput for peak banking hours.'
Business drivers: reduce per-transaction inference cost vs cloud public endpoints, predictable throughput during Black Friday-like spikes, and strict audit/KMS controls for cardholder data.
Preferred integration: VPC Private deployment with optional on-prem gateway for EU settlement nodes.
POC summary: 2-week dedicated POC ran with 4 model variants (full, quantized, truncated, reranker). Observed 28-35% token cost reduction using workload-aware batching + KV caching. See dedicated_poc_report.xlsx.
SLA ask: 99.95% uptime, clea...

POC summary: 2-week dedicated POC ran with 4 model variants (full, quantized, truncated, reranker). Observed 28-35% token cost reduction using workload-aware batching + KV caching. See dedicated_poc_report.xlsx.
SLA ask: 99.95% uptime, clea...

title: Emberlane Inference Solutions
body:
Lead: Liam Chen (AE). Enterprise FS customer evaluating Redwood Private (VPC) for chat & embeddings. Primary ask: strict BYOK + HSM-backed keys across AWS and Azure with deterministic key provenance and auditable access logs. Security emphasized: "We must control keys and prove key origin for regulators" quoted from CISO. Wants envelope encryption pattern: Data encrypted with per-tenant data keys; CMKs stored in customer AWS KMS (customer-managed CMKs) or Azure Managed HSM; option to bring key material (BYOK) and rotate externally.

Technical requirements (high level):
 - Redwood Private deployed in customer AWS account within a locked-down VPC (prefer us-east-2) with VPC endpoints for KMS and S3.
 - KMS Integration: support AWS KMS CMK (symmetric CMKs with grants), integration via VPC endpoints and IAM roles so Redwood runtime uses Envelope Encryption with ephemeral data keys; suppor...

title: Quantiva
body:
Account context
- Quantiva is an EU-based risk analytics vendor; internal security team is driving requirements. They’re treating SIEM-ready audit export as a gating control for production approval.

What they care about (quotes/paraphrases)
- “EU residency is non-negotiable. If audit events or model traffic touch US regions, it’s a no.”
- “We need to demonstrate auditability end-to-end: who changed what, when, and evidence that events can be exported to our SIEM reliably.”
- “Filtering is mandatory — we can’t ingest everything; need allowlist by category/action and ability to limit to specific workspaces/projects.”

SIEM export specifics (beta)
- Preferred destinations: S3 (for long-term retention + reprocessing) and Splunk (primary SOC workflow). Datadog is used by app teams but security uses Splunk.
- Delivery guarantees: OK with at-least-once if event_id exists and is stable; aske...

title: Lyra Oxford Systems Ltd
body:
Summary: Large EU-headquartered manufacturing automation vendor evaluating Redwood Private (VPC) for on-prem-like isolation. Primary drivers: data residency in EU, HSM-backed key control, demonstrable audit trails, and strict network isolation. They ran a 3-week POC (chat + document search) on Dedicated; now moving to procurement for private VPC.

Security intake / vendor risk: Legal & InfoSec requested full SIG/CAIQ deliverable. They want:
- Completed SIG questionnaire with evidence links (SOC2 cert, encryption diagrams).
- CAIQ mappings to our control implementations and a shared-responsibility table that their security team can drop into their vendor-risk system.
- Explicit network architecture: VPC peering vs. PrivateLink, NAT/egress controls, firewall rules, allowed IP ranges, and flow logs retention policy.

title: EmberWave Enterprise AI
body: Account evaluating Redwood Dedicated for mission-critical inference across 3 product lines (support chat, search, risk-scoring). Strong data residency constraints in NA + planned EU expansion. Decision criteria: predictable latency (p95), throughput guarantees during batch reporting windows, and clear SLA for cross-region failover. Procurement leaning toward 12–16 reserved A100-class GPUs with burst pool for traffic spikes. Legal has flagged cross-region replication in current template; asking for optional region-scoped data residency addendum. SE call 2026-02-18: EmberWave engineering asked for end-to-end measured p95 numbers using our test harness and a migration runbook. "Do not want noisy neighbors — need isolation for peak-factoring." — CTO paraphrase. Sales tasked to provide model-variant fallbacks and precise cost for fallback scenarios.

title: VectorForge Payments
body:
Summary: VectorForge is a payments-processor (authorization + dispute triage) evaluating Redwood Private (VPC) for an inference layer that performs embeddings for merchant/product search, reranking for fraud signals, and an ops chat assistant for support teams. They run in AWS but require customer-managed keys (CMK), strict audit logging forwarded to Splunk, and US-only residency for transaction PII.

Security + Compliance highlights:
- Must support SSO (SAML) and integrate with Okta.
- SOC2 and ISO27001 are pre-reqs for procurement.
- Audit logs: all inference requests + model version metadata need to be forwarded to their SIEM (Splunk) with 3 years retention; security wants an audit log retention policy and proof of log integrity.
- KMS: customer wants CMKs in their account for all persistent artifacts (model weights cache encryption at rest, KV cache snapshots). Need details on CMK...

title: MidBridge Assist
body:
Company snapshot: Mid-market SaaS that provides in-app customer support for B2B SaaS customers; strong PLG motion; product-led growth + sales for larger accounts.
Primary interest: replace slow rule-based routing + multiple knowledge connectors with a single streaming chat agent. Focus on conversational handoff, tool-calling to ticketing system.
Latency SLO: explicit — <1.5s first-token p95 for agent responses; streaming required for perceived responsiveness.
Model preference: wants tested with open/quantized variants (they mentioned Llama-2 style family) but open to Redwood-curated models if latency/cost tradeoff is good.
Cost sensitivity: moderate — willing to commit to reserved capacity if unit cost < $0.003/token effective in peak scenarios.
Security: SAML SSO mandatory for admin console; SOC2 mandatory; audit logs + KMS for encryption; US-only residency for customer data.
Ops/obs...

title: Keystone Lattice AI
body:
Summary:
- Mid-market SaaS (customer engagement platform) evaluating Redwood for productionizing chat-based support assistant and semantic search/re-ranking across product docs and support articles.
- Started on Hosted API to validate prompts and response quality; now moving to Dedicated POC for throughput predictability and data isolation.

Business/requirements highlights:
- Workloads: high-concurrency chat for live agents (multi-turn), nightly reranking/embeddings refresh job.
- Performance targets: chat p95 latency <= 250ms (token streaming acceptable), sustained 120-200 conversational requests/sec at peak across their EU+NA regions.
- Cost sensitivity: wants to compare unit token cost between hosted and dedicated; target TCO reduction ~30% vs hosted at scale.
- Model preferences: open-models first (Llama2-70B variant, quantized), option to route to smaller variants for low-priori...

title: IronHarbor Systems
body:
Summary: Enterprise logistics SaaS that wants a dedicated GPU pool for mission-critical chat & search features. Primary goals: predictable latency at 95th < 150ms for chat, steady-state throughput ~200 QPS, peak bursts up to 700-800 QPS for promotional events. Token budget: typical session ~1.2k tokens output, average request ~800 input+output tokens; expected token/sec at steady-state ~160k tokens/sec aggregated (preliminary).

Workload details / constraints:
- Primary models: finetuned 13B (low-latency chat), 70B for higher-quality summaries (nightly batch rerank).
- Prefer H100-class performance for batch rerank; okay with quantized variants for chat if latency SLO met.
- Hard requirement: VPC deployment, private control plane, SOC2 + SSO/SAML enforced. KMS + audit logging mandatory.
- Data residency: US and EU regions separately; cannot cross-replicate certain PII-containing reque...

- Hard requirement: VPC deployment, private control plane, SOC2 + SSO/SAML enforced. KMS + audit logging mandatory.
- Data residency: US and EU regions separately; cannot cross-replicate certain PII-containing reque...

title: Summit Lark Solutions
body:
Background: mid-market B2B SaaS (product analytics) with integrated in-app assistant and search. Heavy EU customer base, MTL and EU latency targets.
Why Redwood: performance predictability + unified API across hosted/dedicated; they want single control-plane for rollout and failover.
Timeline to date: started with Hosted API trial (Jan demo) -> positive latency/cost signals -> exploring Dedicated for predictable SLAs and data isolation.
POC ask: Dedicated reserved pool for 3 weeks, canary rollout to 5% traffic, automatic fallback to hosted model variant if latency or errors exceed thresholds.
Workload profile: chat assistant + embeddings for search; expected concurrency ~60-120 chats/minute; avg prompt length ~120 tokens; expected peak tokens/min ~45k.
Latency/throughput targets: p50 chat < 120ms, p95 < 350ms (EU + NA), tail latency critical for user experience; target 1k reqs/min aft...
