title: Silverpine Compliance Technologies
body:
Summary: Silverpine is a mid-stage regtech vendor building a compliance automation product for financial services customers. Primary ask: private VPC deployment with clear compliance mapping across SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA (BAA) and PCI considerations. They want to minimize PCI scope and prove audit logging and KMS-bound key control.

Key constraints / requirements (customer language):
- 'We cannot send PHI out of our VPC without a BAA and dedicated audit pipeline.'
- Data residency: primary tenancy in US (us-east) with EU region for EU customers; need residency guarantees + export controls documented.
- KMS: must integrate with customer-managed AWS KMS/HSM; Redwood must support key usage audit and key rotation policies.
- Audit logging: immutable logs to central SIEM (Splunk) + 7 year retention for compliance use cases.
- Identity: SSO/SAML + SCIM provisioning required.
- Security pos...

title: Harbor Regional AI Infrastructure
body:
Background: regional payments processor operating in EU, UK, Canada; strict data residency SLAs for payment logs and PII.
They want a Private VPC deployment with EU-only model execution for customer-facing chat and reranking. UK and Canada regional controls optional for next phase.

title: Lumenfold Intellect
body:
Chief Product Officer (Maya Patel) on call 2026-03-03: 'We need embeddings at scale but can't accept 40% cost premium for hosted at their current QPS.'
Team is mid-market SaaS (B2B product analytics) building semantic search for product docs + triage; want unified API between hosted -> dedicated.
Primary evaluation path: quick Hosted API demo -> 2-week POC using Hosted for embedding generation + search, then mirror to Dedicated if cost/throughput justify.
Their stack: Postgres + Snowflake for content, Airflow cadence for nightly batches, currently prototyping with Pinecone and Qdrant.
Security: Okta SAML SSO required, KMS integration for key custody, 12mo audit log retention requested, SOC2 compliance mandatory.
Quote from Eng lead (Ethan): 'We need predictable 95th percentile latency under 120ms for 10k simultaneous readers during peak import jobs.'
Budget: procurement earmarked in Q...

title: Lakedale Shielded Compute
body:
Overview: Lakedale Shielded Compute is a regional critical infrastructure operator evaluating a fully air-gapped on-prem Redwood Private deployment. Primary goal: enable a secure chat assistant and classified-doc summarization pipeline for operations centers with strict non-egress rules.

Recent timeline (bulleted):
- 2026-02-20: Intro call (AE Maya Chen) — high-level requirements captured; site classification = IL3; customer emphasized no internet egress.
- 2026-02-25: Technical deep-dive (SE Jonah Blake) — reviewed network segmentation, allowed OS images (RHEL8/Rocky8), signed package requirements, and HSM/KMS constraints.
- 2026-03-01: Security workshop (security + compliance triage) — Fireflies ff-20260301-09a7b3; covered FIPS, STIG-like baseline, and allowed telemetry.
- 2026-03-04: Onsite validation window requested for 2026-03-22; must include performance run with preloaded mod...

title: Osmium Rerank Labs
body:
Background: Osmium builds a federated search + rerank platform for regulated verticals (legal + compliance). They ingest ~10M docs today, expect 2-3x growth over 12 months. Currently using Pinecone for vectors + custom transformer reranker hosted on infra. Eng team is focused on reducing cost-per-query while holding or improving ranking metrics (MRR, nDCG).

Primary ask: migration plan for embeddings + reranker that preserves quality. They want evals-driven rollout: run comparative embedding+reranker evaluation harness (nDCG, MRR, recall@k, latency regression checks) before flip. Need A/B + canary capability with automatic rollback on quality regressions.

Scale & Perf targets:
- Index: 10M docs now, plan to 25M in 12mo.
- Query load: 400-600 QPS peak, batchy writes during ingest windows.
- Latency SLO: reranked end-to-end P95 <= 150ms for first-pass + reranker; P99 spike tolerance wi...

title: LumenArc SaaS Labs
body:
Summary: Mid-market product analytics SaaS evaluating Redwood Hosted API first with a 3-week POC, then likely move to Dedicated reserved capacity if unit economics and security checks pass. Key drivers: reduce per-query inference cost for large-scale product search, guarantee p95 latency for UI use, and have clear routing/fallbacks for peak events.

POC scope & success criteria: customer provided 2M doc corpus + synthetic user sessions. POC goals: embed cost per 1k queries, p95 latency under 400ms for most user queries, recall vs internal BM25 baseline.

Cost workshop takeaways (03/09):
- Batching: continuous batching with 50-100ms wait window shows ~18-27% token-cost reduction for their token distribution (short prompts + medium responses). We sketched tradeoffs at 20ms vs 100ms.
- Prefix/KV caching: high hit-rate for customer-specific product prefixes — estimated 15-30% token reduct...

title: Velaris Customer Sentience
body: High-level: Velaris runs a global CX platform used by mid+enterprise clients. Primary initiative is ticket deflection and auto-assist for agents. Current infra: Elastic + application-layer embeddings (home-grown) + a lightweight neural reranker used synchronously. Scale: ~3M unique tickets, 4M technical docs/KB articles, daily delta ~8k docs. Goals: reduce human handle time by 30%, increase automated deflection to 45% for top-tier customers, maintain agent-facing latency SLOs: end-to-end retrieval+rerank <= 180ms median, 99th < 450ms (target 150ms median for select routes). Precision targets: P@5 >= 0.80, MRR@5 improvement >= 18% vs baseline. Cost target: blended per-query (embedding + index + rerank) < $0.02 for high-volume routes; willing to trade small latency increase for cost savings. Technical preferences: vector index with HNSW/FAISS, hybrid sparse+dense scoring, incremental in...

title: Palisade Government Knowledge Systems
body:
Summary: Palisade is a homeland-focused contractor building an enterprise search product for state and federal agencies. Primary ask: high-quality RAG for classified/unclassified stores with deterministic latency and provable data isolation.

Key constraints and asks (shorthand):
- Must run in air-gapped on-prem datacenters behind agency firewall; control plane can be private (customer-managed) or hosted in gov-cloud if required.
- Indexing scale: plan to onboard ~30M documents initially, expected growth to 100M+ within 18 months. Concerned about embedding generation cost and storage for dense vectors.
- Latency SLOs: 95th percentile under 300ms for typical query (embedding + rerank), target p50 < 120ms. Most queries are short chat-like queries and doc retrieval.
- Cost sensitivity: procurement expects unit economics analysis (cost/query) for hosted vs dedicated vs private. Requested ...

title: Banyan Guardian Solutions
body: Summary: Enterprise private (VPC) deployment use-case: low-latency inference for capital-markets support platform; must satisfy strict EU data residency and auditability. Primary asks: 1) SSO/SAML integration with Okta, group-to-role RBAC mapping in Redwood Console/control plane; 2) SCIM provisioning so HR/Identity can auto-provision/deprovision users and groups; 3) KMS integration (customer-managed keys, key rotation, optional HSM) and ability to encrypt model cache and at-rest blobs; 4) Audit logging forwarded to their SIEM (Splunk) with retention controls; 5) Private control plane reachable only over peered VPC or VPN, no internet-exposed control endpoints.

title: HavenBridge Inference Systems
body:
Summary: HavenBridge is a regulated fintech (quant trading + customer-facing research assistant). Primary ask: committed Dedicated capacity with explicit throughput guarantees and clear unit economics per token. They want single-tenant VPC with KMS-managed keys and 7-year audit log retention for certain datasets.

Conversation flavor: fast-moving finance customer — ‘latency is revenue’ repeated on kickoff call. High sensitivity to tail latency and predictable per-token spend for budgeting and trading P&L attribution.

Key quotes / call snippets:
- 'We need P95 below 120ms for 32-token responses and sub-500ms for 1k-token flows if possible.'
- 'If we reserve GPUs we need transparent math: how many tokens per dollar at 70% utilization.'

Commercial posture: leaning toward 12–24 month reserved commit if perf checks out. Interested in a staged commit (small tranche now + automatic scale-u...

title: Echo Lattice Software
body:
Snapshot: mid-market product analytics SaaS. Primary goal: add conversational assistant + in-app doc search that is production-grade and privacy-safe.

Sales / technical story: started with Hosted API to iterate on prompts and embedding quality; POC validated prompt templates, embedding vectors, and latency profiles. Product team now wants Dedicated in a VPC for data isolation and compliance.

Recent timeline (bullets):
- 2026-02-10: Intro demo (Jordan, Priya) — walked through hosted flows, streaming, and embedding previews. Fireflies ff-2457.
- 2026-02-20: POC kick-off — mirror 2 product flows (support-chat, doc-search) on hosted API; Redwood provided sample batching/quant config.
- 2026-02-28: Mid-POC checkpoint call — reported p95 latency ~220ms on hosted for chat (seq len 256), embeddings quality pass for retrieval.
- 2026-03-05: Security intake opened — Echo Lattice security team...

title: InferCode Works
body:
Account: InferCode Works — early-stage commercial product: IDE plugin and cloud-based pair-programming environment.

Summary of call 2026-02-18: Aisha (CTO) + Eng lead; demo of hosted API latency vs local benchmark. They want predictable throughput for peak developer hours to avoid degraded (stuttering) completions. Emphasis on streaming token latency and continuity (no partial chunks).

POC notes: 2-week POC executed on Dedicated dev pool (m5-gpu) w/ continous batching turned on. Results attached in Drive. Observed: p50 ~95ms, p95 spiky during burst tests 420ms when cache miss + cold KV. Redwood SE suggestions: KV prewarming, prefix caching, small-quant fallback.

Security / compliance: enterprise customers require SSO + audit logs + KMS. Data residency for EU customers is a blocker (need regional routing). Legal asked about retention controls and ability to opt-out of logging for se...

title: SummitForge Applications
body:
Background: mid-market SaaS focused on customer support automation. First 6-week engagement = hosted API trial (sandbox + prod keys). Team very engineering driven; PM and infra lead active.

Trial highlights: shorthand notes from calls — 
- 'Hosted API was super easy to wire up; latency consistent for small loads' (quote from Emma).
- Embedded example used for semantic search over KB (embeddings) + chat front-end for support agents.
- Wanted a clean path to Dedicated after proving quality and cost.

Sales motion: multiple demos (product + security), POC run by SE (Diego). Pricing conversation started 2025-02-14 via email thread (see drive pricing deck). Legal asked for DPA + SOC2 evidence. Security team requested SAML SSO + KMS options.

Internal signals: high product engagement (40% of trial users created >3 sessions/day), strong interest in lower per-token cost for high-volume reran...

title: Tidal Helm Support Automation
body:
Top-line: enterprise support platform trying to replace brittle macros + manual KB lookup with RAG-driven agent assist. Primary asks: accurate citations, fresh content, multi-source retrieval (Zendesk -> Confluence -> PR changelogs), and reliable behavior during support spikes.

POC scope: ingest last 5 years of Zendesk tickets (threads + attachments), Confluence KB, release notes. Vector refresh cadence: customer prefers 4-6 hour refresh; must support partial reindexing for new articles.

Latency / throughput targets: p50 <= 200ms for retrieval+rank on agent-assist queries when served from Dedicated pool; willing to accept p95 <= 800ms for complex multi-source joins. Throughput target: sustained 80 qps for 500 agents, burst to 1200 qps during incidents. Cost sensitivity: medium — will trade some token cost for lower latency, but needs predictable unit economics for month-to-month bud...

title: CodeSight Labs
body:
Summary: CodeSight Labs building next-gen PR review assistant integrated into editors (VSCode, Web IDE). Primary goal: summarize diffs, generate review comments, and rerank candidate suggestions so senior devs see highest-signal feedback first. Wants low-latency streaming for in-editor comment previews and deterministic model/version pinning for reproducible CI checks.

Quoted from CTO: "We need review automation that reduces cycle time without creating noise — deliver concise rationale and one high-quality suggested comment, not 10 weak ones."

Technical requirements / acceptance criteria: - workload: PR-review + batch re-rerank for nightly checks - throughput: expect 1k PRs/day initially, spike to 5k/day in Q2 - latency targets: interactive inline suggestion <= 150ms median, <= 400ms P99 for streaming tokens; batch rerank can be higher (<= 5s) - model preferences: open models with a...

title: Silk IDE AI
body:
Account snapshot: Silk IDE AI — UX-first IDE copilots for enterprise dev teams. Small product org (backend + infra + editor teams) building a low-latency completions engine embedded in VSCode + JetBrains plugins. Primary ask: streaming that feels instant in editor; predictable token budgets per completion; model pinning across releases.

Recent timeline and activities:
- 2026-01-20: Intro call (Jordan) — high level needs, demo request. Interested in model/version pinning so mobile/edge builds use vetted weights.
- 2026-02-12: Deep dive with SE (Priya). Measured baseline: current provider TTFT ~180-260ms (wide jitter), median completion latency ~320ms, tail (95th) ~720ms under burst. Silk wants TTFT target <100ms for single-line completions and sustained <200ms median for multi-line suggestions.
- 2026-02-18: Sent POC plan + config suggestions (batching, tiny-prefix caching, token budg...

body:
2025-11-20 - inbound from job post; initial qualification call. Large european enterprise focused on internal knowledge search for customer support and legal. Wants EU + UK residency.
2025-12-03 - discovery w/ AE (Ava) + SE (Liam). Requirements: ~2M document index, embeddings + reranking stack, target p95 end-to-end search latency <50ms for short queries, sustainable cost per query target < $0.01. Need predictable throughput during business hours (200 qps peak).

title: AcmeAssist
body:
Account summary
- Mid-market SaaS building an LLM-powered support agent for end-user ticket deflection + agent assist.
- Primary pain: inference cost volatility and limited explainability. Quote (PM): “Our CFO is asking why inference cost went up 18% last month when usage only went up ~5%.”
- Strong interest in Redwood Optimize levers (batching/caching/quantization profiles) but wants measurement/diagnostics first.

Workload / traffic profile
- Workloads: chat (streaming) + summarization (non-streaming), some embeddings for doc retrieval.
- Concurrency: spiky during business hours (US), plus nightly backfill summarization runs.
- Typical output lengths vary widely; they suspect long-tail outputs drive cost and latency outliers.

Key requirements (from calls + emails)
- Unit economics: wants a clear definition of blended $/1M output tokens that can be reconciled against platform usage ...

title: Flashpoint LLM Economics
body:
Background: adtech personalization pipeline — per-impression scoring + rerank in <100ms target at p95.
Current infra: autoscaled k8s, GPUs in GCP us-west1, in-house model infra for batch offline. Looking to move online scoring to a managed inference layer.
Why Redwood: interested in unified runtime across hosted + dedicated so experiments can shadow prod; cost controls and batching suggestions called out in the demo.
Interest: Redwood Optimize features: batching/caching/quantization recommendations, explicit cost/perf tradeoff tooling.
Quote from call (Marcel): "We need p95 under 80ms at peak, and a quantization path that keeps NDCG delta <1.5% while cutting cost 3x."
Latency targets: p50 10-20ms, p95 <80ms, tail isolation required for flash campaigns.
Throughput: typical sustained ~150 req/s, peak bursts 600 req/s for campaign windows.
Model preference: open weights; prefer Llama-2 o...

title: PeakFlow Inference Labs
body:
POC / Performance evaluation notes:
- Use case: online personalization + search re-ranking in fast path (must not add >200-300ms tail latency).
- Representative payloads: 128 token prompts for personalization ranking; 32 token prompts for click-predictions; embeddings for offline indexing.
- Acceptance criteria (customer): p95 <= 250ms for 128-token prompts at 800 concurrent sessions (sustained), p99 < 500ms, throughput >= 1,200 reqs/sec aggregated across 3 model endpoints. Cost target: <$0.008 / 1k tokens served (projected).
- POC setup: Dedicated cluster with initial commit 4 x NVIDIA A10G equivalent; autoscale policy to burst to 8 GPUs for 15-minute windows. Kernel/quantization: run quantized 16-bit / int8 variant from catalog (eval with Redwood SE).
- Load tests planned:
 * Day 1: stepped load to baseline (0 -> 400 -> 800 -> 1,200 concurrent) to validate routing and batching behav...

title: Helios Vector Dynamics
body: Aerospace OEM evaluating dedicated GPU pool for customer support chat, manuals search, and internal developer code-assist. Wants predictable tail latency during launch prep windows. Ops lead: "Any 2s tail is unacceptable during launch ops." Prefers VPC for main region and small on-prem cluster for classified workloads. Interested in quantized 70B-class model but will accept 30B variant as fallback. Very focused on deterministic scaling and a firm burst provisioning SLA.

Wants predictable tail latency during launch prep windows. Ops lead: "Any 2s tail is unacceptable during launch ops." Prefers VPC for main region and small on-prem cluster for classified workloads. Interested in quantized 70B-class model but will accept 30B variant as fallback. Very focused on deterministic scaling and a firm burst provisioning SLA.

title: Emberlane ConverseCloud
body:
Lead from Head of Product (Samir Rao). Typical CRM-style notes below — shorthand and action items.

- POC goal: prove hosted API can meet streaming latency SLO for live SDR workflow + demonstrate reliable function/tool calls to Salesforce + Gmail + Google Calendar.
- 'If latency > 300ms at 95p we can't use hosted for agent' — direct quote from Samir on 2026-02-12 call.
- Demo highlights: streaming chunks + partial responses = good UX for reps; tool call flow worked in 82% of attempts in staging (main failures were auth/timeouts to sf sandbox).
- Performance notes: 95th pct ~280ms observed for warm sessions (single-token avg smaller responses). Cold KV cache adds ~70-120ms per first turn. Marcus recommends pre-warming cache for critical flows.
- Cost sensitivity: mid-market budget; prefers usage-first hosted for PoC but wants dedicated pricing model for predictable unit economics if we...

title: Inspector Codex
body:
Summary: Inspector Codex builds a GitHub-style PR assistant that summarizes diffs, suggests review comments, and provides ranked comment alternatives for maintainers. Primary objectives: very low end-to-end latency for in-IDE streaming suggestions, deterministic/ pin-able model versions for reproducible reviews, and reranking to present high-quality comment candidates.

Recent talk highlights: 
- CTO (R. Patel): "We need deterministic assists across releases — model pinning non-negotiable."
- Head of Eng: wants streaming token-by-token in VS Code plugin; latency budget 150ms tail for short comments, 300ms for multi-line suggestions.

Security & policies: currently must pass infosec questionnaire (SAML SSO + audit log retention), prefer KMS-backed keys and EU residency option for select customers. Retention policy requested: configurable 30/90/365 days.

Cost posture: mid-market startu...

title: Hedgerow Insight Labs
body:
Summary:
- Mid-market product analytics SaaS. Evaluating Hosted API for quick integration, then planning Dedicated VPC for production (data residency + network isolation).
- Primary contact: CTO (Marcus Reed); product lead: Amira Patel. AE owner Priya N.; SE Ethan supporting. CSM tentatively assigned post-contract (Lena C.).

POC / Demo history:
- 2026-01-20 discovery call (ff_2026-01-20_hedgerow_discussion). Customer wanted embeddings + semantic search first; low-latency generative for in-app help later.
- 2026-01-25 sandbox integration with Hosted API (2 engineers). Quick wins: embeddings indexing of docs; reranker improved click-through on support results.
- 2026-02-12 hosted API demo w/ streaming + function-calling (ff_2026-02-12_poc-demo). Customer impressed by latency but flagged cost at scale.
- 2026-02-18 internal spike: batch vs prefix cache reduced token costs ~18% on their ...

title: GuidedLaunch Labs
body:
Context: GuidedLaunch builds an in-app guided onboarding copilot for freemium->paid conversion. Primary constraint is predictable latency during step-by-step guided flows and hard token caps to control cost.

Key quotes / call notes:
- Product: "Users must not wait >300ms for short helper responses (one-sentence suggestions). p95 matters more than average."
- Head of Product: "We need strict per-session token caps so power users don't blow the budget; show us cost vs quality trade-offs."

Technical requirements and preferences:
- Start with Hosted API for speed to POC; plan to evaluate Dedicated if latency/cost goals require reserved capacity.
- Streaming responses + function/tool calling for step flows (open url, execute client-side validation, fetch user context).
- Model preferences: open model variants ok if match latency + cost (quantized weights preferred). Willing to test Redwo...

title: Pacerift InferOps
body:
- 2024-10-18 call w/ CTO (M. Sanchez): wants sub-50ms p95 for short chat prompts; target 200 req/s steady state.
- Very cost-sensitive: wants quantization-first approach, then batching only where latency windows allow. Quote: "we'd rather lose 2% quality for 3x cost savings".
- Interested in automatic KV-prefix caching for customer sessions; typical session length 5-8 turns, heavy token reuse.
- POC request: run dedicated config with int8 quantized Llama-3 variant, measure p50/p95/p99 and tokens/sec; include burst scenarios.
- Security: must support SSO + enterprise KMS; audit log retention 13 months.
- Deployment: prefer Private (VPC) but will accept Redwood Dedicated for initial POC if private control plane delayed.
- Perf ask list sent 2024-10-22 (see Drive).
- Internal stakeholders: CTO Marcos Sanchez, Head of Infra Rachel Lin, ML Lead Ben Chu.
- Commercial: procurement expects te...

title: Sunforge Interactix
body:
Background: mid-market CCaaS vendor focused on omnichannel agent assist. Primary app is an agent UI that issues actions (create ticket, add note, issue refund, escalate to fraud queue) via backend APIs.
Current ask: reliable tool-calling layer from LLM assistant -> support backend (HelixTicket API + Stripe/Adyen) with strong idempotency, audit trails, and sub-300ms median UI response for sync flows.
Constraints: PCI scope for refunds — legal insists on on-prem staging for payment-related POC; EU data residency for EU customers; SSO (SAML) and 3yr audit log retention requested.
Scale profile: typical steady-state 120 rps of agent actions, peaks up to 1.2k rps during promos (Black Friday style). Need graceful degradation under spikes (queue + async fallback).
Latency/throughput targets: p50 < 120ms, p95 < 300ms for tool-calling path when running in dedicated cluster. Throughput target f...

title: Arkadia Relevance Systems
body:
Background: Arkadia builds a blended search + answers layer for large enterprise customers (legal, pharma). Heavy emphasis on precision at top-k and auditability.
Current infra: hybrid vector index (on-cloud), bi-encoder embeddings based on older SBERT fork; ranking currently done client-side with a lightweight pairwise model.
Pain: quality regressions when trying newer embeddings; slow migration path for reranker — wants eval-driven plan with A/B and canary to avoid surfacing regressions in production.
Scale: indexing plan ~100M documents, ~10k QPS peak for search layer (burst), typical steady QPS ~800. Index size projected 6-8 TB vectors (float16/quantized options under consideration).
Latency targets: p99 < 250ms for search+rerank combined (EU region), p50 < 60ms. Cost goal: <$0.02 per complex query (embeddings + rerank) for steady-state.
Model preferences: prefer open-models with ...

title: Kaleido Content Labs
body:
Context: large media archive (news transcripts, long-form interviews, video captions) spanning ~25M documents / 6B tokens. Customer wants a production-grade RAG search for internal newsroom research + content repurposing. Key constraints: maintain relevance on long documents, control cost per query, predictable latency under load.

Current stance: evaluation stage; legal + infra teams engaged for private deployment. Prefers open/quantized model variants for reranker to control inference cost. Interested in Redwood Dedicated or Private depending on final SLA/legal terms.

Quoted from CTO (Mar 3 call): "We cannot accept a reranker that blows up latency for long transcripts — p99 needs to be predictable. Cost per user query target is $0.02-$0.05 including retrieval + rerank."

Chunking strategy discussion notes: experimenting with 2–8k token chunk sizes. Favor adaptive chunking by semant...

Chunking strategy discussion notes: experimenting with 2–8k token chunk sizes. Favor adaptive chunking by semant...

title: Orthoveritas Discovery Labs
body:
Account overview: Orthoveritas builds search/RAG for compliance and e-discovery workflows used by law firms and corporate legal. They ingest large regulatory corpora + internal policies. Primary ask: high-quality embeddings + deterministic reranking for privileged/doc-level relevance.

Sales narrative / positioning: leaning toward Redwood Private (VPC) with Optimize recommendations for quantization/batching to hit cost SLOs. Interested in Dedicated for predictable throughput during large review sprints.

Technical requirements (callouts):
- Index scale: target ~30–50M documents (mixed-size: 1k–5k tokens each) — expect ~10–25B embedding vectors after chunking.
- Latency SLO: 95th percentile <= 250ms for single-query recall+rerank flow in search UI.
- Throughput: 800–1,200 QPS during peak review periods (concurrent judge/admin workloads).
- Cost target: blended cost per query <= $0.03 (...

title: Sugarloaf BotWorks
body:
Inbound via self-serve signup, used free credits, started in eu-west-1, reporting chat latency 600-1200ms p95 — unhappy
Product lead (Celina Torres) on call 2026-02-26: 'Feels fine during demo but production traffic shows spikes at night'
Primary workload: short interactive chat on product pages + search-as-you-type for catalog (~80% short prompts <80 tokens)
Cost sensitivity: high. Wants to keep monthly bill < $2k while scaling to ~10k monthly queries
Current config: default region eu-west-1, model variant 'redwood-13b' (non-quantized), client timeout 30s, no streaming enabled
They tried switching to smaller model locally but saw hallucinations on edge cases — willing to trade small quality delta for latency improvements if fallback exists
Security: requires SSO for admin console + SOC2 report before procurement. Audit logging required for production token use
AE note: strong technic...

title: Europa Shield Dataworks
body:
Account background: mid-tier EU payments platform expanding into regulated lending. Primary concern = data residency and custodial separation. POC scope: private VPC deployment in eu-west-1, BYOK KMS integration, audit log shipping to customer SIEM, region locks for production.

Timeline (high-level):
- 2026-01-15: Intro call (Jordan + Priya) — overview of Redwood capabilities; customer asked for EU-only production.
- 2026-01-28: Sent security questionnaire (completed v1).
- 2026-02-05: Architecture review with infra team — agreed on VPC peering model and egress controls. Action: provide network diagram + CIDR ranges.
- 2026-02-22: Security deep-dive recorded (fireflies ff_2026-02-22_9a7b2c). Discussed KMS trust model, HSM attestation, and audit log schema. Quote from CISO: "production data must never leave EU borders without documented DTIA + SCCs".
- 2026-03-03: POC kickoff — test w...

title: Silverpine Developer Search
body:
Account: platform used internally by engineering org to power code search + incident runbooks. Focus: accurate reranking + low tail latency for developer queries. Typical query mix: small context code snippets (<=2KB), large repo search (cross-repo) and similarity-based code retrieval for code completion/hints.

Sales/eng summary: evaluating Redwood Dedicated and Private. POC asked for: ingestion pipeline for 120M embeddings, incremental re-index every 6 hours, end-to-end 95th p99 latency target <= 220ms for top-5 rerank, cost target <$0.002 per query (avg across mix). High priority: SSO (SAML) + audit logs + ability to bring their KMS/HSM.

Quote from eng lead on 2026-02-18 call: "We can't accept >250ms tail for developer workflow — engineers will reject it."

Data posture: code repos hosted on-prem plus mirrored GitHub enterprise; legal requires EU residency for EU customer code. Wa...

title: Aurora FinServ Analytics
body:
CRM notes (shorthand + excerpts):
- Primary contact: CTO Remy Alvarez (very latency-sensitive).
- Business case: replace legacy vector service used for trader support chat; must be 24/7 with predictable tail latency.
- POC target: p99 < 350ms for 512-token chat payloads under defined burst profile; throughput 600 RPS normal, 1200 RPS burst.
- Reserved capacity ask: Aurora wants sizing guidance -> proposal: 4 reserved A100-equivalent pods for baseline, autoscale to 6 for bursts; want capacity planning that maps to their expected QPS and token mix.
- SLA: Aurora insists on combined availability + latency SLOs (availability 99.95% + latency SLO 99th < 350ms); expects financial credits and defined measurement window. Legal issued redlines (see drive doc).
- Reliability / fallback design discussed: cross-region active-standby with automated failover for region outage; mandatory model-varia...

title: Martello Skyline Software
body:
Lead: VP Product (Claire D'Angelo). Mid-market B2B SaaS with heavy in-app contextual help and knowledge base. Started with Hosted API trial (Jan-Feb): fast to integrate, but token costs spiked for high-concurrency peak. Now evaluating Dedicated for steady throughput and predictable unit economics. Interested in Redwood because of clear batching & caching levers.

Call notes (2026-02-20 ff-20260220-6b9a2e): 'Need predictable p50/p95 in NA and EU — cannot have regional cold-start spikes during release windows.' -- Claire.

Key quotes: 'If we want to own SLAs we need dedicated capacity or a solid fallback that doesn't degrade UX.' -- Director Eng.

Security: must support SSO (SAML), SOC2 compliance proof, and KMS integration for customer secrets. Data residency: EU subset for EU customers (want VPC peering + regional routing).

Integration notes: prototype integration uses Redwood SDK + ...

title: Valence Health Lookup
body:
Topline: Mid-size clinical SaaS (201-1000 emp) building enterprise-grade RAG for clinician search across charts, consult notes, and imaging reports. Heavy PHI requirements — must run in customer VPC or on-prem; no PHI egress.

Account ownership:
- AE: Maya Patel (owner) — primary commercial contact.
- SE: Carlos Mendes — technical lead for POC and perf testing.
- CSM: Aisha Rahman — onboarding if we convert.

Background / business need:
- Goal: single search surface for clinicians to retrieve relevant passages, then run reranking to surface evidence-based answers. Replace slow internal search + manual triage.
- Scale: initial index ~20M documents (notes, imaging captions, attachments OCR). Expected growth to 60M in 12–18 months.
- Workloads: embeddings + ANN store for candidate retrieval (approx 1536-d vectors), then cross-encoder reranker for top-100 results.

Requirements & constrai...

title: OpalBridge Digital
body:
Account snapshot: OpalBridge builds a mid-market analytics SaaS with embedded conversational help, in-app summarization, and a developer-facing code/issue summarizer. Currently on OpenAI for chat + embeddings; pilot to move to Redwood Hosted with roadmap to Dedicated (VPC) in Q2. Key drivers: predictable token economics, on-prem/residency option later, stronger SLOs and per-route observability.

Discovery highlights / buyer signals: VP Eng (Katherine Moon) and Head of Product (Leo Grant) engaged; committed to reduce per-token costs and remove multi-vendor complexity. They care A LOT about no-regression on end-user summaries. Quote: "We can tolerate slight cost delta but not feature regressions — top 3 enterprise customers expect identical output."

Technical requirements (explicit):
- Workloads: conversational support (20 QPS burst, p50 < 80ms, p99 < 220ms), embeddings for semantic se...

Currently on OpenAI for chat + embeddings; pilot to move to Redwood Hosted with roadmap to Dedicated (VPC) in Q2. Key drivers: predictable token economics, on-prem/residency option later, stronger SLOs and per-route observability.

Discovery highlights / buyer signals: VP Eng (Katherine Moon) and Head of Product (Leo Grant) engaged; committed to reduce per-token costs and remove multi-vendor complexity. They care A LOT about no-regression on end-user summaries. Quote: "We can tolerate slight cost delta but not feature regressions — top 3 enterprise customers expect identical output."

- Workloads: conversational support (20 QPS burst, p50 < 80ms, p99 < 220ms), embeddings for semantic se...

title: Empiria Financial AI
body:
Account background:
- Mid-tier global consumer bank, several BUs evaluating LLM features for customer support and underwriting triage. Heavy compliance: EU+NA data residency constraints, quarterly audits.
- Decision-makers: CTO (Eva Chen), Head of AI (Marcus Delaney), VP Ops (Rohit Sinha). Procurement lead assigned; legal review underway.

Why Redwood (signal):
- Looking for predictable unit economics and dedicated GPU pool for steady inference workloads. Want unified API across hosted/dedicated/private to simplify integration. Strong interest in cost/perf tradeoffs and rollback policies.

Current technical ask (sizing workshop focus):
- Baseline traffic: 60 QPS steady-state for chat assistant (weekday business hours).
- Peak planning: 240 QPS (burst windows ~10-20 mins) tied to marketing/statement cycles.
- Average tokens per request: ~90 tokens (short multi-turn dialogs).
- Derived ...

title: Midsail Helpline Analytics
body:
Summary: midsize contact center SaaS provider focused on tier-1 chat deflection and agent augmentation. High-volume, cost-sensitive — primary goal: move 50-60% of inbound chats to self-serve + agent assist for the remainder.

Workload profile and priorities:
- Expected steady-state volume: ~120k messages/day; peak: burst windows of 10-12k messages/hour during marketing campaigns.
- Primary workloads: short chat sessions for FAQ deflection, mid-length ticket summarization (100-800 tokens), agent-assist completions, and orchestrated tool-calls for first-contact resolution.
- Latency targets: human-facing deflection responses p95 < 500ms; agent-assist p95 < 300-350ms for token streaming.
- Throughput target: sustain 200-300 concurrent real-time chat threads per region with graceful degradation under 2x spikes.
- Cost sensitivity: CFO directive to keep incremental inference spend under $4...

title: LocaleFlux Contact Systems
body:
Overview: LocaleFlux is a contact-center platform serving retail and travel verticals across NA/EU/LATAM. Primary pain: inconsistent tone+translations across locales and high escalation rates for non-English tickets. They run multi-tenant Zendesk + proprietary agent desktop.

Customer wants: agent-assist that: suggests localized replies, performs instant ticket summarization in agent language, calls internal tools (lookup order/status) securely, and provides regional routing when capacity spikes. Emphasis on deterministic fallbacks in case of degraded model capacity.

Quotes / call snippets:
- 'Must sound local — not literal translations. Tone varies by market (formal FR, friendly BR-PT).' — Head of CX
- 'Black Friday season is our make-or-break — need predictable latency and graceful degradation.' — VP Eng

Integration notes: existing systems to integrate: Zendesk (webhooks), interna...

title: Sentinel Bridge Systems
body:
SUMMARY:
- Looking at Redwood Private (VPC) as primary path for inference to keep PHI on customer-managed network and KMS.
- Primary workload: clinical triage chat + embedding-based document search (patient records, clinical guidelines).
- Performance targets: 50-100ms tail latency for short-chat flows (<=512 tokens); sustain ~50 req/s for peak clinics; throughput for nightly batch embeddings ~200k docs.

SECURITY / COMPLIANCE NEEDS:
- Must support customer-managed KMS (FIPS 140-2) and ability to rotate keys on schedule.
- Immutable audit logs forwarded to customer SIEM and archived to S3 in US regions.
- SOC2 Type II & ISO27001 evidence required; questions around penetration testing and supply-chain attestations.
- SSO via SAML + SCIM for provisioning.
- Data residency: all inference and logs must remain in US (prefer us-east-1 primary, us-west-2 DR). CTO: "cannot send PHI outside US...

title: HarborLight Support Intelligence
body:
Account background:
- Founded 2017; unified agent desktop for mid-size carriers and utilities. Heavy Twilio Flex + Zendesk deployment across N.A. operations.
- Peak live agents: ~4,200, projected peak growth to 6k seasonally.
- Current AI stack: small internal rules + third-party summarization for after-call notes; evaluating streaming suggestions for live assist.

Decision makers / roles:
- VP Product: Claire Monroe (primary economic buyer)
- Head of Engineering: Rajiv Mehta (tech signoff)
- Head of CX Ops: Laura Kim (SLOs / UX)
- Procurement: Daniel Ortiz

Recent timeline (bulleted):
- 2025-10-06: Intro discovery call (Ava + Claire) — high interest in lowering AHT and assist accuracy.
- 2025-11-12: Technical deep dive with Rajiv + Marco — demoed streaming suggestion concept; asked about p95 latency goals.
- 2025-12-03: Shared security questionnaire (HarborLight) — SSO/Okta details +...

title: TidalBank Labs
body: Inbound demo request from seed-stage fintech focused on SME banking products. Self-serve signup completed last week (trial key used for small embedding test). Early security asks: SOC2 status and SSO/SAML integration — flagged by CTO. Team is VPC-curious for next phase but wants to start on Hosted API to move fast. AE intro call 2026-02-20: CTO (Aisha Rahman) said 'we need proof SOC2 + audit log availability before we present to procurement'. Fast-moving PM wants an integration POC for customer support chat and embedding search by end of month. Cost sensitivity: small ARR expected first year, will optimize token usage aggressively. Latency targets: 50-150ms per user-facing chat request; concurrency target ~150 concurrent live users during peak. Prefer open models or quantized variants to control cost; asked about recommended models for 2-3safety/speed tradeoff. Notes stored in Drive l...

title: Ceres Pronto Inference
body:
Account background: Ceres Pronto is a payments marketplace (mid-market fintech) building a real-time offer engine and conversational payment assistant. Heavy sensitivity to tail latency; most revenue-critical flows run at <100ms p95 in production. They currently run a mix of CPU microservices and lightweight GPU endpoints in-house.

Primary asks: 
- p95 latency <= 60ms for short-chat + reranking path (prompt length ~80 tokens).
- Sustained throughput target: 100–250 QPS during peak, spiky bursts to 1k QPS expected.
- Cost constraint: need clear unit cost model; target gross monthly LLM spend <$25k for pilot.
- Strong interest in batching and KV prefix caching to reduce token cost without breaking latency SLOs.
- Will evaluate int8/4-bit quantized variants for Dedicated/Private to hit cost targets.

Timeline & recent activity: 
- 2026-02-18: Intro demo w/ Marco + Priya; demo notes in f...

title: Astraeus Realtime Labs
body:
Overview: enterprise quant trading shop building low-latency signal layer. Team: CTO (Victor Hale), Head of Infra (Rina Patel). We kicked off Dedicated evaluation 2024-09.

Key ask: deterministic tail latency <6ms p50/p95 for short completion paths; batchable work for embeddings & rerank off-path. Heavy throughput during market open windows (13:30-14:30 ET) - want reserved capacity to avoid noisy-neighbor impact.

SLA negotiation highlights: they insist on financial credits for downtime (tiered), 99.95% as baseline target proposed by them, pushing for 99.99% for core inference path. Must have <1 minute failover to fallback model variant. Require 72-hour maintenance notice, 2x per month maintenance window only outside 13:00-15:00 ET.

Security/compliance: strict KMS for keys, audit log retention 2 years, SSO via SAML + SCIM sync. Data residency: logs to remain in us-east region only. L...

Security/compliance: strict KMS for keys, audit log retention 2 years, SSO via SAML + SCIM sync. Data residency: logs to remain in us-east region only. L...

title: Terrane Pulse Labs
body:
Customer profile: mid-market product analytics SaaS, heavy user-facing chat + search. High concurrency during business hours (1000-3000 queries/min).
Primary goals: reduce per-request cost for embeddings and chat while maintaining p50 latency <200ms for chat; retain control for compliance-sensitive routes.
Quote from Maya (VP Product): 'We want predictable unit economics — if Dedicated reduces cost by 40% for our steady state, it's worth the commitment.'
Cost sensitivity: medium-high. Willing to commit to reserved capacity if ROI demonstrated in 3-6 months. Looking for clear quant tradeoffs (latency vs cost).
Technical ask: support for model pinning, canary rollouts, and automatic fallback to hosted API during Dedicated capacity spikes. Need SDK example for cache TTL and prefix caching patterns.
POC success metrics: avg token cost <= $0.0006 for chat steady state, embedding cost per v...

title: Pyxis Operational AI
body:
POC focused on Dedicated capacity evaluation: throughput guarantees, latency SLOs, reserved GPU planning, SLA negotiation.
Key ask from Pyxis: run representative performance benchmarking and deliver a sizing + SLA proposal by end of May.
Workload profile: mixed — interactive operator chat (short responses), scheduled batch embeddings for plant telemetry search, reranking for automated incident prioritization.
Targets: sustain 200 rps for chat with median latency <= 90ms and p95 <= 350ms for 16-token responses; peak burst handling up to 400 rps for short periods; throughput of 120k embeddings/hour for nightly jobs.
Reserved GPU planning: customer prefers an 8 x A100-80GB equivalent baseline, want recommendation for H100 uplift for 6-months horizon (cost delta analysis requested).

title: Halcyon Security Orchestrators
body: Summary: Halcyon is evaluating a Private (VPC) deployment to host sensitive transaction-support assistants and embeddings for compliance search. Primary procurement driver is auditable, exportable audit logs + customer-managed keys. Key asks: 1) SIEM export paths (Splunk/Datadog/Elastic) via secure forwarder; support for syslog/HTTPS with mutual-TLS; ability to push JSON and CEF formats. 2) Canonical log schema — want fields: timestamp, request_id, tenant_id, principal_id (pseudonymized), model, model_version, route, prompt_hash, response_hash, token_count_in, token_count_out, latency_ms, status_code, host/pod_id, region, client_ip (CIDR or masked), audit_action. Must be able to include redaction markers for PII removals. 3) Retention policy — SOC requires 7 years for audit trail metadata for transactional workflows; access logs (proxy/access) 1 year; debug/full payload logs kept only...

title: LintCraft Codeworks
body:
Summary: LintCraft builds a developer-facing code search + insights product used internally at customer orgs and as a hosted product. They operate ~2,200 active repos across customers (mix of mono-repos and microservices). Primary ask: scalable embeddings + reranking that keeps 95th-percentile latency < 300ms for top-k code search, cost < $0.02/query, supports daily re-indexing for active repos and weekly full re-index.

Technical constraints / requirements:
- Corpus size: ~22M files, ~150M stored embeddings currently (mixed dense + sparse metadata). Expect growth 3-5x in 12 months.
- Query profile: peak 120 qps sustained for short bursts, typical 30-50 qps. Many short queries (10-60 tokens).
- Latency SLO: 95p < 300ms (strict), p50 target 50-80ms for vector lookup + reranker.
- Throughput: want 1,000s of QPS per cluster in future; POC focused at 120 qps.
- Cost sensitivity: target av...

title: Stalwart Inference Labs
body:
Summary: Stalwart is a quant shop building a hybrid product — real-time trader assistance + nightly embedding refresh for signals. They need Dedicated capacity to guarantee throughput during market open windows and strict audit/KMS controls for IP.

Key quotes: "We can’t have tail latency spikes during market open — 95th and 99th must be narrow"; "We need predictable unit costs per token for P&L forecasting".

Workload profile: primary workload = reranking + short-form chat (50–150 tokens), secondary = embeddings batch refresh (longer sequences). Peak concurrency target: 600 concurrent qps during US market open (13:30–14:30 UTC). Steady-state daytime average: ~120 qps. Nightly embedding jobs: 200k docs processed in 3–4 hours.

Security/Deployment: VPC-only; SOC2 required; SSO (SAML) mandatory; customer-managed KMS; logs forwarded to their SIEM.

Commercial posture: Interested in 12–24...

title: Sentinel Thera Archivist
body:
Account profile: mid-size health system with integrated ambulatory network + specialty clinics.
Use-case priority: clinician-facing search (fast retrieval during consult) > patient-facing summarization later.
Data characteristics: average note 700-1,200 tokens; high duplication across templates; many small incremental updates.
Index scale: expect 25-40M documents, ~200M vectors after chunking (policy: 250-300 token chunks).
Performance ask: <200ms p95 for answers on reranked results; 50-80ms p95 for dense-only lookups (cached).
Cost sensitivity: finance wants modeled costs for 12 QPS steady-state and bursts; target <$0.03/query after committed capacity.
Security: Must keep PHI in customer-controlled network. No public internet egress for raw PHI. Full audit trail per access and per-token logging required.
Integration: Epic/CareConnect is primary EHR — Epic security team needs network ...

title: Axion Reserve Labs
body:
POC notes / rep summary:
- Customer: Axion Reserve Labs (programmatic ad personalization). Heavy sensitivity to latency p95 and edge-case tail latency for auctions.

title: Psiphon TicketLens
body:
Primary contact: Priya Natarajan (Head of ML) — focused on embedding + rerank pipeline
Key ask: production RAG for support agent + ticket deflection. Aggressive latency SLO: 120-200ms end-to-end for retrieval+rerank (p95).
Index scale: 80M current tickets, anticipating 300M+ docs (attachments + structured metadata) within 12 months.
Quality target: reranker should surface top-5 that cover 95% of correct answers in internal evals; internal benchmark uses MRR@5 and precision@3.
Cost target: below $0.003/query on avg across blended workload (search + rerank + token generation) — finance flagged higher costs as blocker.
Architecture preference: VPC private control plane + customer-managed KMS. On-prem bundle evaluated for regulated European customers.
Quote from Priya: "We need deterministic tail latency — if top-5 quality drops during peak we want automatic fallback to lighter quantized ...

title: Oakbridge Systems
body:
Account snapshot: Oakbridge (mid-tier bank, fintech arm) evaluating Redwood Dedicated for customer-facing conversational assistant + search/rerank pipeline. Heavy focus on deterministic latency and reserved GPU planning. POC running on a small dedicated pool (4xA100 equiv) through Feb-March 2026.

Recent timeline:
- 2026-01-22: Intro + requirements call (ff-2026-01-22-87421). Captured SLA expectations: p95 < 220ms for single-turn chat (<=1024 tokens), sustained throughput 1200 QPS at peak, burst up to 2k QPS for short windows.
- 2026-01-27: SE deep-dive with Mateo — reviewed model variants, quantization tradeoffs, cold-start scenarios. SE suggested warm pool + pre-warmed KV cache for top-100 prompts.

title: PlumByte AssistFlow
body:
- Self-serve signup 2026-02-18, used free credits; initial integration with web SDK completed.
- Wants to power in-app assistant embedded in product (SaaS B2B admin UI). Latency is top KPI — aiming for median RTT <200ms and progressive streaming so first tokens arrive <120ms.
- Team: CTO (Ben Ortiz), two full-time frontend engineers, 1 ML eng. Small ops footprint — prefers hosted API to avoid infra ops.

title: Eldridge Dedicated Capacity
body:
Enterprise evaluation for reserved GPU pool for customer-facing chat + search (primary) and reranking (secondary).
VP Eng quote on 2026-02-14 call: "We need predictable p95 for transactions — cannot have tail latency surprises during market opens."
Primary ask: sustained throughput 300 req/s with 5 minute bursts to 600 req/s during peak windows (market open / batch sync).
Latency targets discussed: p50/p95/p99 split for both streaming and non-streaming paths. Customer insists client-side timing must be primary SLO for product teams.
Security: must support VPC + HSM-backed KMS, SOC2 + vendor ISO attestation. Data residency for EU customers required for certain accounts.
Procurement: procurement and legal requested model of payment: 12-month reserved capacity with quarterly usage true-ups.
Quote: initial dedicated sizing sketch = 8x A100-equivalent GPUs (dedicated pool), option to burst...

title: MintGrove CustomerFlow
body:
Inbound: self-serve signup last week, used free credits and exercised embeddings + chat endpoints.
Primary product: chat-based support widget for SMB e-commerce merchants (order status, returns, product FAQ).
Main question on call: which model family to pick for hosted API — tradeoffs between Llama/Mistral/Qwen variants.
Customer quote: "If we can keep cost under $1k/mo it's a go for pilot, otherwise finance will push back."
Worried about quality vs cost: wants LLM that handles product-specific language (product SKUs, short titles) without hallucination on order data.
They prefer public cloud (no VPC/private yet) — want easy self-serve onboarding and quick benchmarks.
Asked about quantized variants (13B q8/q4o) vs smaller dense 7B instruct models — asks about latency differences and expected tokens/sec on hosted instances.
Concern: multi-tenant throughput — will have bursts during Bla...

title: Ember Graph Search
body:
Summary: Enterprise search vendor building RAG-enabled product for mid-large customers. Focus: embeddings + reranking, cost-per-query tight.

Recent activity / timeline:
- 2025-11-12: Kickoff (FF id ff_20251112_ember_kickoff) w/ Jordan, Priya, infra. High-level arch reviewed.
- 2025-11-13: Security questionnaire submitted (SCA + SOC2 scoping).
- 2025-11-14: Architecture deep-dive: hybrid retrieval (BM25 -> ANN) + cross-encoder rerank.
- 2025-11-18: Demo of Redwood hosted latency profiles; agreed to baseline SLA targets.
- Next: start 1M-vector POC ingest; schedule connector acceptance tests.

POC & scale requirements:
- Initial ingest target: 1M vectors (POC). Production target: 300M-3B vectors within 18 months.
- Ingestion throughput: steady-state 5k upserts/day, occasional bursts 50k/day during reindex/backfill.
- Latency SLO: 95th percentile at or below 200ms for end-to-end search+...

title: Maplecroft Support & Synthesis
body:
- Company: mid-stage SaaS vendor focused on conversational support for e-commerce brands. Handling ~120k tickets/mo across channels.
- Primary ask: RAG to deflect tickets + serve context to an agent UI. Need embedding index + 2-stage rerank (ann -> reranker) - want top-5 quality to be near-human.
- Scale targets: index ~30M doc fragments (KB + past tickets); steady qps 150 reqs/s peak; average query fanout 512 -> want efficient ANN / sharding.
- Latency SLOs: 95th <= 200ms end-to-end for agent prompt (embedding + ann + rerank). For customer-facing deflection flow target <= 120ms. Tight.
- Cost goals: want cost per query under $0.03 (embeddings + ann + rerank amortized). Very cost-sensitive; interested in batching, quantization and hybrid caching.
- Model preferences: prefer open embeddings (text-embedding-3-large) or Redwood-verified equivalent. Reranker: small specialized reranker ho...

title: RapidQubit Realtime
body:
Account snapshot: rapid-growth adtech company building per-request creative & ranking decisions for real-time bidding exchange. Primary pain: token-level latency spikes during peak auctions causing lost bid opportunities and increased CPM. Strong cost sensitivity — want sub-$0.0008/token unit economics at scale. Interested in Redwood for: deterministic latency, reduced per-token cost via batching/caching/quantization, and observability for per-route SLOs.

Recent timeline / activity: 
- 2026-01-15: Lead (Jordan) inbound; initial qualification call. Use-case mapped to reranking + click prediction.
- 2026-01-22: SE intro (Priya) — high-level architecture review; they run k8s on GCP us-west.
- 2026-02-03: Sent security pack + SOC2 attestation; InfoSec sent questionnaire.
- 2026-02-18: Technical deep-dive: live demo of Redwood routing + canary logic; customer asked about graceful fallback...

title: Tycho Retail Analytics
body:
- Customer: midwest retail chain, heavy personalization and in-store POS insights.
- Primary ask: Private VPC deployment in us-east-1 + optional us-west-2 for DR and latency. Must satisfy data residency (US-only) and 7-year audit log retention.
- Non-negotiable: no public inbound endpoints; control-plane connectivity preferred over private link with mutual TLS.
- Prefer customer-managed KMS; want to bring their own key and require HSM-backed wrapping; request detailed KMS key rotation policy.
- Networking: plan to use Transit Gateway / Direct Connect for stable throughput. They prefer outbound-only egress via NAT gateway and strict CIDR allowlist for model pull.
- Latency targets: 95th pct < 120ms for single-turn recommendation queries; batch embedding throughput 500-1,000 reqs/s during peak windows.
- Cost sensitivity: medium — willing to trade cost for latency in peak retail hours, ...

title: Tideway Signal Labs
body:
Short background: Tideway builds in-product search and insight cards for B2B product analytics. Heavy use of embeddings for similarity search and reranking pipelines for candidate prioritization. Initially validated hosted API for quality and streaming. Now moving to Dedicated to control tail latency and reduce per-query cost at higher volume.

POC goals: validate Dedicated pool behavior under real traffic patterns and prove safe rollback paths. Acceptance metrics: 99th latency improvement vs Hosted by at least 20%, embedding vector generation throughput >= 150 vectors/s sustained, rerank latency 95th <= 300ms.

Dedicated POC plan (agreed):
- Duration: phased 72h canary followed by 5-day scaled soak if successful.
- Traffic split: start at 3% production traffic -> monitor 24h -> 15% for next 48h -> expand to 40% only after metric gating.
- Model variants: primary = 6B quantized encode...

title: Opslyte Solutions
body:
Background: mid-market ITSM vendor (ticketing + runbooks). Goal: replace slow KB lookup + manual triage with an internal IT/helpdesk agent that can escalate to humans. Primary concerns: sub-200ms token latency for short-turn exchanges (streaming), deterministic tool-calling reliability (ticket creation, password reset flow), and full audit trail + retention controls for compliance.

Summary of conversations: • 2026-02-10 demo with Jordan (AE) + Aisha (SE): showed hosted API streaming and example tool calls (create_ticket, lookup_user). Opslyte liked streaming start-of-response speed but asked about tail latency under load.
• 2026-02-24 security deep-dive with their InfoSec (Chris M.): asked for audit log format, retention windows, KMS options, and SOC2 certification evidence.
• 2026-03-03 POC kickoff: agreed on 2-week sandbox connecting their Okta test tenant + small dataset of intern...

title: Northstar HelpGrid
body:
Account background:
- Enterprise contact-center SaaS vendor for mid-large telco customers. Global footprint, heavy EU customers with residency constraints.
- Exploring AI-first agent assist to reduce handle time and summarize tickets for CS teams.

Discovery summary (high level):
- Primary use-cases: real-time agent assist (low-latency snippets), automated ticket summarization (multi-source citations), outbound tool-calling (CRM / ticket triage), and reranking customer KB results.
- Scale: peak spike patterns (monthly billing cycles) produce 6-8x baseline concurrent load for 45-60m windows -> reliability under spikes is non-negotiable.
- Latency targets: <150ms token tail for agent assist in EU/NA, p95 under 300ms; summarization batch jobs can be seconds.
- Cost sensitivity: medium — willing to pay premium for reliability and data controls; ROI tied to FCR and handle time improvement....

body:

Recent timeline:
- 2025-11-20: Initial intro call (Jordan). basic use-case: cockpit analytics + pilot support chat. asked about private deployment options.
- 2025-12-08: Demo of hosted API. positive on latency. asked for private VPC pricing.
- 2026-01-15: Architecture deep-dive with infra (Priya). discussed VPC endpoints, peering vs PrivateLink, egress control.

- 2026-02-25: Uploaded preliminary SIG/CAIQ to procurement portal. Legal flagged gaps on key rotation & BYOK.

title: Tiltropolis Assist
body:
Inbound from website demo request (site chat widget) on 2026-02-17 — used free credits; created project 'tilt-chat-prod'.
Found via blog post about low-latency streaming examples. AE reached out 2026-02-18, intro call booked.
Primary contact: Mateo Alvarez (CTO) — early stage PLG engineering-led buy. Quotes: 'We need token streaming that doesn't block UI — 50ms chunking ideally.'
Current integration: Next.js front-end + Node express backend. They attempted direct WebSocket to Redwood streaming but got CORS + reconnect edge cases.
Mentioned wanting to fallback to smaller/cheaper model when GPU capacity constrained — open to Redwood routing policies and auto-fallbacks.
Security: requires SSO for team access, SOC2 soon; legal asked about retention controls and ability to opt-out of retaining prompts for production traffic.
Cost sensitivity: SMB budgeting. Mateo: 'We can start self-serve ...

title: Linchpin BatchGuard AI
body:
Account: Linchpin BatchGuard AI - building bidder + personalization stack for header bidding partners.
Primary pain: current infra cost blows up during day-of-campaign traffic spikes; need deterministic SLOs + cost controls.
Use-case detail: reranking candidates (top N) + short text embeddings for feature signals. Typical request: 3 candidates -> rerank -> enrichment -> respond to exchange within strict deadlines.
Latency targets: p50 8-12ms, p95 <35ms end-to-end (including network); willing to accept small additional tail for heavy bursts if can auto-fallback.
Throughput: baseline 50 QPS with bursts to 350-450 QPS (10-15s bursts common). Want autoscale behavior and predictable billing for burst capacity.
Cost sensitivity: high. Target >40% reduction in per-request GPU cost vs current managed provider. Open to quantization if quality delta within 1-2% rerank NDCG.
Optimization asks: b...

Use-case detail: reranking candidates (top N) + short text embeddings for feature signals. Typical request: 3 candidates -> rerank -> enrichment -> respond to exchange within strict deadlines.
Latency targets: p50 8-12ms, p95 <35ms end-to-end (including network); willing to accept small additional tail for heavy bursts if can auto-fallback.
Throughput: baseline 50 QPS with bursts to 350-450 QPS (10-15s bursts common). Want autoscale behavior and predictable billing for burst capacity.
Cost sensitivity: high. Target >40% reduction in per-request GPU cost vs current managed provider. Open to quantization if quality delta within 1-2% rerank NDCG.
Optimization asks: b...
normalized.nextStep: Finalize perf test plan and commercial term for Dedicated pool; schedule perf window week of 2026-01-20
blockers: Enterprise security review (data residency + KMS) not completed
Quantized model quality baseline not yet validated for reranking

title: Nightingale Assist Labs
body:
Stage: evaluating Hosted API first (fast path) with plan to benchmark Dedicated if POC meets SLOs.

Key contact(s): Liam O'Connor (Head of Product), Maya Patel (Platform Eng lead), Jordan Price (CTO).

Use-case summary:
- Primary: customer support chat agents integrated in product UI (web + mobile).
- Features required: streaming responses, tool/function calling (ticket lookup, CRM writebacks), structured outputs for UI cards, conversation history KV cache.
- Expected workload: 40-120 concurrent live agents during peak; avg 3.5 turns/session; 1.2M tokens/mo estimate for initial 50k MAU pilot.
- Latency SLO: first-token <= 1.5s across US region (p50 target 0.8-1.2s). "Showstopper" if cannot hit 1.5s - quote from Liam: "If we can't hit 1.5s first token it's a showstopper."

Model & routing preferences:
- Prefer low-latency, cost-balanced variants (13B Q4-style or optimized 7B for latenc...

title: Orion Developer Hub
body:
Summary: evaluation focused on large-scale embeddings + reranking for internal code search and knowledge RAG. Primary ask: low-latency top-k retrieval (95th pctl <150ms for single-shard queries) with frequent full/partial re-indexing (daily incremental, weekly full). Corpus scope: ~40M files (source + docs), ~1.2B tokens, estimated embedding store ~2.2 TB (float16/quantized).

Recent timeline:
- 2026-01-20: Intro call (AE: Maya) — CTO (Ravi Patel), Head of Search (Monique Liu). High-level requirements captured.
- 2026-02-03: Architecture deep-dive (SE: Liam) — discussed hybrid retrieval + cross-encoder rerank for top-50, fallback model routing, and caching strategies. Notes: need cross-encoder GPU capacity for rerank at ~200 qps burst.
- 2026-02-18: Demo — Redwood hosted demo with small corpus (50k files). Fireflies ff_2026-02-18_01 attached. Customer: "We need near-prod numbers, not ...

title: Cerium Vertex Infrastructure
body:
2026-02-12: Intro call w/ CTO (Nina Patel) + Head of Security (Marco Ruiz). High-level ask: private VPC deployment with strict controls; must pass vendor risk intake.
Key quote: 'No third-party managed keys for PII — must be customer BYOK or HSM backed.'
Security team asked for full SIG questionnaire and CAIQ mapping to our controls matrix — requested by 2026-03-12.
Primary concerns: audit log retention (7+ years), immutable append-only logs, fine-grained access to logs, separation of duties for admin ops.
Network: require VPC peering or PrivateLink, no public internet egress for inference traffic; traffic must stay in NA/EU regions.
Compliance: SOC2 Type II and ISO27001 copies required; want statements on change management + vulnerability management cadence.
POC requirements: 2-week pilot in VPC using subset of models (35M QPS target concurrency 150 rps, p95 latency <120ms for chat b...

title: Verdigris Loop AI
body:
AE notes: Very product-led team; wants low-friction dev experience. 'We need something our PMs and engs can own — not another ops burden.'
SE notes: Interested in Redwood's kv-cache and batching heuristics to reduce per-token cost. Wants kernel/quantization recommendations for Dedicated nodes.
Procurement: Budget review begins mid-March; favorable if Dedicated TCO shows 30%+ cost savings over Hosted at scale.
Security: Provided SOC2 doc stub; customer security wants retention controls and exportable audit logs. Asked about KMS/HSM support for Dedicated.
Quote request: asked for 12-month committed Dedicated price + overage burst lanes; also want per-embedding credits comparison vs. running own embedding pipeline

title: CedarBridge InsightWorks
body:
AE notes: strong product-led motion; team wants predictable latency under 250ms p95 for support chat
Customer quote (PM): "Hosted trial looks good but we need predictable infra for launch - latency spikes worry us."
Integration: uses microservices on GCP (europe and us-central regions), wants VPC peering + customer KMS
Token profile from hosted trial: avg tokens per request ~380 (prompt ~240 / response ~140); 70% of requests are chat with context, 30% are embeddings/rerank
Traffic shape: steady-state QPS ~10-15, typical concurrency 8-20, observed daily peak windows with short bursts (30-90s) up to 60-70 QPS
Cost sensitivity: mid-market procurement; wants 12–18 month committed pricing target; break-even vs hosted at ~45k monthly token volume
Security: SOC2 required, SSO/SAML for console, audit logs retention 12+ months, data residency for EU customers (RFE)
POC ask: Dedicated cost esti...

title: Hightower MedTech Analytics
body:
Account background:
- Mid-stage medtech analytics provider; strong adoption of ML in clinical ops and triage automation.
- Heavy PHI footprint; corporate mandate: all production LLM inference must be in customer VPC or on-prem with FIPS 140-2 compatible key management.
Security/governance summary:
- Primary ask: KMS/HSM integration across multi-cloud footprint (AWS KMS, Azure Key Vault, GCP KMS) + BYOK capability and HSM-backed key wraps.
- Requirements called out: envelope encryption, separate crypto key per environment, key rotation policy, audit trails forwarded to customer SIEM (Splunk), and export controls for residency.
- Infosec emphasized: ability to generate and control keys (BYOK), HSM attestation (AWS CloudHSM or Azure Dedicated HSM) for signing, and granular audit logs for token-level access.
Technical notes from recent POC run (Ravi SE-led):
- POC executed against small i...

title: OrbitStride
body:
> Quick summary: OrbitStride builds an in-app onboarding copilot for mid-market SaaS products (guided flows, stepwise tutorials, and contextual hints).

Key stakeholders:
- Riley Park (VP Product) - primary champion, cares most about consistent latency & UX. Quote: "We need the copilot to feel instant — even when tool-calls run."
- Jordan Vale (Head of Infra) - owns security/residency and Dedicated economics.
- Maya Singh (Lead PM) - owns token budget per active user target.

Use-case & asks (shorthand):
- Workload: chat/agent embedded in product UI, frequent tool-calls (lookup, exec), streaming responses to client for perceived latency.
- Targets: interactive feel; P50 responses nearly instant, P95 <= 800ms for short-turns; end-to-end including tool-call < 1.2s desired.
- Throughput: pilot simulating 500 DAU concurrent, burst to 200 req/s for peak flows.
- Cost sensitivity: strict to...

- Targets: interactive feel; P50 responses nearly instant, P95 <= 800ms for short-turns; end-to-end including tool-call < 1.2s desired.
- Throughput: pilot simulating 500 DAU concurrent, burst to 200 req/s for peak flows.

title: Silverpine Medical Search
body:
Summary: Enterprise hospital network (regional) evaluating RAG for clinician-facing search + audit trail. Primary drivers: reduce doc retrieval time, tight PHI controls, end-to-end access logging.

Key quotes: "We need sub-200ms median and p99 < 300ms for clinical search; any solution must prove auditability for every retrieval." — Head of Clinical Informatics

Requirements (high level):
- Index scale: target initial 10M documents, runway to 100M in 18 months
- Embeddings + reranking: experiment with dense retrieval + lightweight cross-encoder reranker for top-50 re-rank
- Latency: p50 < 120ms, p95 < 220ms, p99 < 300ms for in-region queries
- Cost sensitivity: target <= $0.02/query (avg across routing + rerank)
- Security: VPC-only control plane, customer-managed KMS, SOC2/ISO27001, SSO/SAML, per-request audit logs retained 7 years (gov/regional request)
- Data handling: PHI ingest vi...

title: Mosaic Retail Support
body:
Company overview: national omnichannel retailer (apparel + home), heavy seasonal peaks (Black Friday, Holiday). CS volume spikes 7-10x.
Primary problem: agents waste time toggling between systems; refunds require 3 API calls + manager approval in many cases -> high handle time.
Goal: agent assist + automated tool-calls to create/update tickets, initiate refunds, and flag escalations to L2 via API hooks.
Must-haves: idempotent tool-calls (duplicate protection), per-call audit log, end-to-end traceability tying LLM output to API ops.
Latency target: sub-500ms for assistant suggestions; <800ms end-to-end for auto-triggered refund call in interactive flows.
Throughput: baseline 2k concurrent agent sessions; peak 20k requests/min during promotions. Want reliable routing to Dedicated/Private under spikes.
Cost sensitivity: moderate; willing to pay for reserved capacity for Black Friday wind...

title: Arden Cove Logistics AI
body:
2025-11-06: inbound via marketing trial — initial interest in dedicated GPU pools for route replanning workloads.
2025-11-20: discovery call (Maya) — primary ask: predictable throughput for 1k QPS routing inference and <250ms p95 chat latency. Cost sensitivity moderate; willing to commit for reserved capacity.
2025-12-08: technical deep-dive with Liam (SE) — discussed sizing: estimate 6-8 A100-equivalent GPUs to meet 1k sustained small-token routing + burst to 3k. Discussed autoscale vs reserved mix.
2026-01-12: SLA deep-dive (ff_20260112_ac_disco) — Arden Cove legal asked for 99.95% uptime, credit schedule tied to minutes below SLO; Redwood initial proposal 99.9% with graduated credits (5%, 10% bands).
2026-02-14: follow-up (ff_20260214_sla_deepdive) — they submitted redlines to SLA: want cap removal on credits and 30-min P1 response. Product + Legal to review.

title: Foresail Security Labs
body:
Account background: Foresail Security Labs (FS Labs) is the operations and R&D arm for a regional maritime port authority (critical infrastructure). They operate several on-prem data centers across NA ports. Strong interest in air-gapped private deployment for any inference workloads.

Summary of interactions: 
- 2026-01-22: Intro call (Ava) - high level requirements, primary concern: absolute network isolation + signed model binaries.
- 2026-02-12: Technical deep-dive (Diego + SE) - Fireflies id ff_2026-02-12_foresail_screening_7a9c3b. Covered: model update mechanics, kernel signing, attestations for HSM, offline package delivery. Customer: \"We need a signed artifact + repeatable attestation; no outbound calls allowed from inference hosts.\"
- 2026-02-25: Security questionnaire submitted via Drive (FORESAIL_POC_SOW_v2.pdf). They returned an initial controls matrix; asked for FedRAMP...

title: Honeycomb Sageworks
body:
Account background:
- Founded 2018, product analytics SaaS for B2B marketplaces. Heavy emphasis on conversational product support and embedded search in product UI.
- Mid-market expansion: onboarding 30 new customers in last 12 months.
POC summary (Hosted API trial):
- Trial window: 2026-01-12 -> 2026-02-28. Ran support-bot + search rerank workloads against 200k session dataset.
- Observed traffic: avg QPS (user-facing inference): 3.2 QPS; typical daytime baseline 5–7 QPS; peak bursts up to 28 QPS sustained for ~90s during product launch tests.
- Concurrency: observed 18 concurrent streams during peaks; 24 concurrent warm KV-cached sessions in internal stress tests.
- Token mix: median prompt tokens 380, median completion tokens 120 -> average total ~500 tokens per call. Token distribution: ~76% prompt-side tokens (document context + metadata), 24% generation tokens.
- Workload split ...

title: Mariner Logix
body:
Inbound demo request via website trial signup (2026-01-09). Trial API key created, initial calls show chat+embedding workloads.
Customer rep: "we need to be 100% sure customer prompts aren't retained or used for training"
Primary contact: Alex Moreno (Head of Product) — small team, cautious about vendor data use.
Initial security questionnaire returned 2026-01-20 — flagged prompt retention, log TTL, deletion APIs, and opt-out requirements.
Conversation notes (2026-01-15 FF-20260115-832): prefers self-serve hosted API but must have clear retention/opt-out guardrails for regulated clients.
Use-case: chat-based customer support + internal doc search for KB. Wants ephemeral prompts for customer messages; embeddings for KB only (explicit consent).
Latency expectations: p95 < 400ms for short chat interactions; flexible for embeddings/async jobs.
Cost: SMB price sensitive — wants to stay on ...

title: Silkshore Platforms
body:
Summary: Mid-market CX SaaS (B2B) evaluating hosted API now moving toward Dedicated for predictable throughput and data isolation.
Hosted trial: 3-week free credits trial (Jan 28 - Feb 18) using hosted_api. Ran support chat + document search flows and an embeddings pipeline for semantic search.
Traffic snapshot during trial: avg QPS 6 (mixed: conversational + embeddings backfills), peak QPS 22 during US business hours, median concurrency observed 35 concurrent requests (spiky, 11:30-13:30 PST).
Token mix observed: ~62% generation (avg prompt 120 tokens / avg completion 240 tokens), 28% embeddings (avg 512-dim requests, mostly short inputs), 10% reranking calls (short generation with higher compute per call). Monthly extrapolation from trial: ~120M tokens/month if they convert 1:1 to production for current feature set.
Latency & SLO: product team targets p95 < 350ms for simple support-...

title: Stratagem Vaulting Systems
body:
Summary: Large European fintech evaluating Redwood Private (VPC + optional on-prem) for customer-facing support assistants and multi-tenant embeddings index. Primary drivers: strict EU data residency, vendor-managed private control plane, demonstrable SOC 2/ISO controls, and customer-managed keys.

Recent cadence:
- 2025-10-05: Initial inbound (RFP) — account created, basic requirements captured.
- 2025-11-12: Intro call (Jordan + Priya) — high-level architecture discussion; client wants dedicated VPC per region.

- 2026-01-28: Follow-up RFC deck shared (/drive/pricing/stratagem_vaulting_private_deploy_v1.pptx).

title: Crestmoor Velocity Systems
body: Summary: Mid-market product company building an in-app code assistant + support chat. Started on Hosted API trial (Jan-Feb) -> moved to short Hosted POC for product knowledge base embeddings + generation. POC results: stable quality, median latency 110-150ms for chat endpoints, peak throughput observed 40req/s during synthetic load. Business ask: predictable unit economics and ability to scale without surprise overages. Currently negotiating Redwood Dedicated committed capacity and pricing. Key negotiation items: monthly commit level, burst capacity rules (72hr burst window requested), overage unit price, ramp schedule (requested 3-month ramp; procurement pushing for 6-month minimum), ingress/egress egress cost clarifications, and SLA credits. Security: require SSO/SAML, SOC2 attestation, audit logs forwarded to their SIEM, KMS integration for customer-managed keys, and EU data reside...

title: SentryAssist Systems
body:
Account background: mid-market CCaaS provider selling agent-assist + automation to retail and telco customers.
Current stack: incumbent LLM API (vendor A) + homegrown orchestration. Pain: unpredictable throttling and degraded tool-calling under Black Friday-like spikes.
Primary ask: deterministic latency under spikes, stable tool-calling (CRM updates, internal ticket system), and lower per-ticket token cost for summarization pipelines.
Latency targets: 95th <= 450ms for suggest/autocomplete, 99th <= 900ms for full agent-assist flows (including tool-call roundtrip).
Throughput: typical steady-state 2000 reqs/min; spikes up to 18k reqs/min during peak campaigns.
Quality needs: no regression vs current model (few-shot prompts) on extractive summarization and action extraction (intent -> tool-call mapping).
Routing: want ability to route by cost tier + fallback to small quantized variant ...

title: Lithic Vector Mesh
body:
Account overview: Lithic Vector Mesh is a large commerce platform (catalog + user signals) evaluating a move from in-house FAISS clusters to a managed inference+indexing platform. Primary focus: embeddings + reranking for product search and personalization at massive scale. Target index size in first phase: 100M vectors, year-2 target 600M-1B vectors.

Requirements summary:
- Throughput: sustained 300-500 qps for product-search queries, 1200 qps batch offline reranks during catalog refresh windows.
- Latency: P50 < 40ms, P95 < 150ms for online search + rerank path (goal: ~50ms end-to-end).
- Cost sensitivity: target unit cost per online query (embedding + ANN + rerank) < $0.0005; must show predictable per-token and per-query economics.
- Model preferences: open/quantized embedding models (in-house fine-tuned on product metadata) + candidate cross-encoder for reranking (can be hosted o...

title: Verdemar SaaSworks
body:
Summary: Mid-market B2B SaaS (product analytics platform) looking to move from Hosted API trial to Dedicated for predictable latency and cost.
Trial feedback: Hosted API easy to integrate; streaming and function-calling worked. Observed tail-latency during peak synthetic load.
Traffic observed during trial (3-week):
- avg daily requests: ~18k (varies by day; peak day 46k)
- token profile: avg 160 tokens/request; 60% generation/chat, 30% embeddings, 10% reranking
- observed QPS peaks: 6-9 sustained during business hours; short spikes to 14 for 1-2 minutes
Customer targets / requirements:
- P50 latency target: <120ms for chat-turns; P95 <300ms
- Throughput: expect initial steady QPS 5-10, scale to 20 QPS over 6 months
- Concurrency planning: concurrency target 30-70 in peak windows (based on session lengths)
Model preferences: prefer open-model variants with good latency/cost tradeoff; ...

title: Lyric Legal Services
body:
Inbound SMB lead via doc-search blog post; signed up for free credits and tried basic completions.
Primary contact: Nora Patel (Head of Product). Small firm offering client intake + contract summaries for local law firms.
Key concern repeated on call: "audit logs" and retaining full request/response chains for compliance with client contracts.
Prefers US-only hosting (explicit request: US East). Does not want data leaving US for now.
Wants minimal setup: hosted API first (self-serve), may consider Dedicated later if PII footprint grows.
Quoted on call: 'We need firm-level audit trails for each document processed — timestamped, token counts, model version.'
Cost sensitivity: low QPS, bursty during peak intake windows; price matters but primary is auditability and fast time-to-value.
Initial experiments: uploaded 500 redacted intake forms, ran embedding-based search; they saw promising ...
