title: SignalMoss CX
body:
Inbound from product: building a lightweight CX automation layer for small e-commerce merchants. Primary ask = hosted API (self-serve).
- Found us via blog post on structured output + tool-calling. Signed up for free credits 2026-03-01.
- Use-case: automatic ticket summarization + structured JSON output to map to ticket fields (category, priority, order_id). Also want tool-calling to patch/update tickets in Zendesk and run triage rules.
- Perf targets: p50 summary latency < 300ms for short messages, burst throughput ~20-40 requests/sec during peak; cost sensitive (tight margin for SMB customers).
- Model prefs: prefer Redwood-verified smaller models (13B/quantized) or composite routing: low-cost base for summaries, higher-quality model for escalations. Want auto-fallback to cheaper model when capacity/cost constraints.
- Security: SOC2 required before procurement; need SSO/SAML for ad...

title: Novaterra Cognisearch
body:
01/28 call: security asked about KMS & audit logs - 'need full key control for EU data'
AE note: strong bias toward private/VPC due to regulator guidance
SE: recommend hybrid index (HNSW + IVF+PQ fallback) to balance recall and space
Client wants per-language stopword lists and phrase-matching for DE and FR
Quote from Luca: 'Search must feel native in each language — we can't have English-first fallbacks for critical queries'
Cost tradeoff discussion: willing to prioritize precision in German legal docs even if per-query cost rises 30%

title: Arcadia LegalTech
body:
Account summary (Arcadia LegalTech): mid-market legaltech vendor (case management + AI drafting assistant) exploring Redwood Optimize to lower inference cost and provide defensible cost attribution to their internal finance team.

Current posture:
- Stage: evaluation (they’ve seen demo + agreed to a scoped pilot).

title: Goldcrest SemanticBridge
body:
Snapshot from AE/SE notes and call transcripts.
- Company background: mid-market SaaS (B2B product discovery and recommendations), ~350 employees, headquartered in SF.
- Use case detail: nightly batch embedding job for ~6M docs (incremental nightly), plus real-time single-doc embeddings for product tile suggestions. Primary production surface is semantic search inside app and a 'did-you-mean' reranker for product lists.
- POC results summary: hosted API handled developer flow and small-scale ingestion smoothly. Bulk batch throughput tests (50k docs/hour) hit cost/latency inflection at ~3-4k simultaneous batch workers; observed significant CPU/GPU utilization on client-side vectorization steps. Redwood's automatic batching + prefix caching reduced per-req latency for re-rank calls from ~180ms to ~95ms median in hosted test.
- Vector DB integration: Goldcrest tested connectors to pgvect...

title: Riftline Conversify
body:
Intro: discovered via webinar on low-latency inference (Jan). AE intro 2026-01-20.
Business case: real-time trading signal enrichment + trader chat assistant. High sensitivity to p99 latency and per-token cost.
Current infra: mix of public cloud infra + small on-prem GPU pool for proprietary models. Want VPC/private control for data residency.
Requirements summary: p95 < 120ms, p99 < 250ms for 1-2k concurrent traders during market open spikes; throughput target 200-300 req/s sustained; burst 800rps.
Cost sensitivity: target avg cost/unit must be <= current self-hosted baseline; prioritize optimization levers (batching, prefix/KV caching, quantization).
Model preferences: prefer open Llama-style variants w/ 7B-13B quantized for cost; also want fallback to larger models for complex ops. Routing by latency/accuracy important.
Security: must support SSO/SAML, KMS for keys, audit logs, ret...

title: KeelPoint Systems
body:
Summary: KeelPoint is a mid-enterprise CCaaS provider looking to add a lightweight RAG layer for ticket deflection and agent assist. Primary constraint: tight latency SLOs for support flows + predictable cost per query. They want embeddings + a neural reranker in the hot path (candidate generator -> embed + ANN -> rerank top 20 -> return top 5).

Scale & targets:
- Corpus: 12M-18M canonical passages (docs split into passages ~250-400 tokens).
- QPS: baseline 200 qps, peak 1k qps during business hours (US-centric).
- Latency SLO (customer-visible): p95 < 120ms, p99 < 220ms for search+rerank combined. Prefers tail SLAs.
- Top-5 quality target: precision@5 > 0.90 on KeelPoint internal eval set; reranker must lift top-5 relevance by >=15% vs BM25.
- Cost sensitivity: target end-to-end cost per query <= $0.02 (embeddings + ANN + rerank amortized).

Model preferences & architecture notes:
-...

title: Riverbloom Product Intel
body:
Background: mid-market SaaS (product analytics) building an in-app assistant + search/rerank layer. Started with Redwood Hosted API trial in Jan 2025 to validate latency and UX. Trial ran across product and support teams (14-day trial).

POC highlights: average request profile: 1) support chat (50% of calls) average prompt 90 tokens, average completion 240 tokens; 2) product search/rerank (30%) mostly embeddings + rerank calls (average embedding size 768, avg tokens returned 40); 3) summarization / batch jobs (20%) long-context summarization (avg input 1200 tokens, output ~200 tokens).

Traffic observed during hosted trial: baseline 3 QPS, peak bursts up to 18 QPS (short spikes). Production forecast from product team (Q2 launch): steady-state 80-120 QPS, expected peak bursts 250-400 QPS during feature launches. Concurrency target: 50-120 concurrent streams depending on user session mo...

title: MintMarsh CloudQueries
body: Inbound trial signup (self-serve) — user: support@mintmarsh.io; started testing Hosted API 2026-02-26. Main pain: p95 latency spikes from EU users when calling completions. On the free tier, they report 500-800ms token times on longer prompts and occasional 504s for multi-turn chat. Key quotes: "We're seeing inconsistent response times across regions — frontend team wants <200ms median per-turn for chat UI".

title: Silverpine AI Hub
body:
Inbound via self-serve signup (marketing site). Team used free credits and hit rate limits while testing embeddings.
Found via Vercel + LangChain search query; product lead Aisha Rahman initiated contact through support chat.
Primary ask: concrete integration examples for LangChain, LlamaIndex, and Vercel AI SDK (JS + Python). Wants copy-paste quickstarts they can drop into a Next.js app.
Quote from call: 'We need a LangChain retrieval QA example with Redwood embeddings and a LlamaIndex connector that works on Vercel Edge.'
Cost sensitivity: SMB budget; optimistic ARR $1k-$5k first year. Asking for batching/quantization guidance to reduce token spend.
Security: small internal infra team. Will require SSO for admins and audit logs for usage. No on-prem requirement — public cloud only.
Model preferences: prefer open models (Llama-family/other 7B-13B) but will consider Redwood-curated va...

title: Prospero Dev Tools
body:
Summary: Prospero builds an IDE-integrated copilot aimed at enterprise engineering teams. Key concern: model/version pinning and guaranteed reproducibility for code completions across dev environments and CI. They need canary rollouts + deterministic rollback + eval gates that tie to unit tests/compile success metrics.

Recent activity (bulleted):
- 2025-11-28: Intro call w/ Ava (AE) + Diego (SE) + CTO R. Iqbal — reviewed Redwood architecture and routing. Fireflies: ff_20251128_Tc7b2x.
- 2025-11-30: Technical deep-dive with infra team — asked about per-repo model pinning, KV-cache isolation per workspace, and how to reproduce exact token streams for a given commit.
- 2025-12-01: Sent sample prompt set (50 repos) + unit-test based eval harness; awaiting Redwood eval gate template. Drive: Prospero/Redwood_POC_eval_gates_v2.md.
- 2025-12-03: Security call with Prospero security lead — SS...

title: Bellwether Dedicated Capacity
body:
Account: regional financial services firm — retail banking + fraud/risk products.
Primary ask: dedicated GPU pool for low-latency customer chat and high-volume reranking for loan decisioning.
Commercial posture: executive wants guaranteed throughput for critical weekday hours; procurement focused on uptime SLO + credits.
Security: must integrate with customer KMS (Vault/HSM) and show SOC2 + audit logging. Data residency required in us-east-1.
Quote from CTO: 'We need predictable tail latency during peak trading windows — can't have 99th spike events.'
Preference: on-prem option for regulatory workloads; VPC for general traffic. Strong preference for deterministic billing for reserved capacity.
Legal commentary: require maximum 2 scheduled maintenance events per quarter that can impact <1% of capacity; otherwise credit schedule applies.
Finance: wants ARR-band estimate + a pricing tabl...

title: Sable Trajectory Analytics
body:
STATUS: moving to procurement. Mid-market SaaS (B2B) building an AI-driven in-app support assistant + enhanced product search. Tried Hosted API first; POC OK but unit cost at projected scale pushes them toward Dedicated.

Key quotes
- "We want predictable monthly spend — spikes ok but need clear overage policy." — Head of Platform
- "Latency target: 150ms p95 for chat front-end, embeddings retrieval < 50ms for reranking."

POC findings (hosted):
- Re-ranking using 4k-context embeddings + knn performed well functionally; token costs will blow past $X at 1M queries/mo.
- Latency variability during peak hours ~200-400ms; not acceptable for live chat SLA.

Why Dedicated now:
- Need consistent p95 latency; desire for reserved GPU capacity and VPC connectivity for HIPAA-adjacent customers in their pipeline.
- Want clear ramp schedule: start with 2 reserved nodes (committed) month 1–3, add 4...

title: Mountainframe OnboardLab
body:
Account summary: Mountainframe is a mid-market product company (B2B onboarding tools) building an in-app copilot to guide new users through multi-step setup flows. Primary goal is consistent interactive latency (200-400ms perceived for message streaming chunks) and strict session token caps to control cost. They prefer starting on Redwood hosted API for speed, with plan to move to Dedicated (VPC) if throughput/predictability and residency requirements are met.

Key technical asks / constraints:
- Workload: chat/agent with tool calling (create resource, check quota, open support ticket) + guided step-by-step flows.
- Streaming is critical for UX: they stream partial responses into the modal and surface actionable buttons as tools are called.
- Latency SLO: aiming for 200-400ms per token batch (end-to-end), spike tolerance but want clear SLOs and routes to degrade gracefully.
- Cost con...

title: Silkbridge DeveloperWorks
body:
Context: Silkbridge builds an IDE-integrated developer productivity suite used internally and sold to large enterprises. Their product requires near-instant streaming completions inside editor panes and a nightly/higher-frequency embedding refresh pipeline for code search/indexing.

Key constraints and asks:
- Streaming: sub-30ms median token delivery, 95th <80ms inside IDE (PST developer locations). Must support token-by-token streaming over unreliable mobile networks.
- Determinism: ability to pin model+weights for reproducible suggestions across releases; explicit rollback and A/B canary controls.
- Background throughput: embedding refresh must handle bursts of 4-6M files during weekly monorepo syncs; target steady-state embedding throughput 80k-120k embeddings/min during refresh windows. They plan incremental delta detection and want Redwood to recommend batch sizes and retry sema...

title: SprocketAI
body:
Background: small team building a VS Code extension for distributed dev teams. Focus: fast inline suggestions + streaming completions to keep typing flow.
Prod constraints: must support ~120 concurrent active devs, 30k tokens/day initially, bursty sessions during syncs.
Latency targets: p50 <50ms for inline suggestion generation (local-ish hints), p95 <200ms for streaming completion during full-line/AI pair programming. Want sub-100ms single-token latency if possible.
Auth model: GitHub OAuth for install, but enterprise customers require SSO/SAML and SCIM provisioning. Interested in per-user API keys and short-lived tokens from Redwood.
Rate limits: customers expect per-org and per-user rate caps; want a model where IDE enforces client-side rate limiting and Redwood returns clear headers + quota endpoints. Concerned about noisy neighbors when multiple devs on same org trigger batch re...

title: Lodestar Product Analytics
body:
Lead: Lodestar (product analytics for consumer SaaS). Mid-market, heavy personalization use-case. Initially trialing Hosted API for quick integration (chat + reranking + embeddings), now evaluating Dedicated for predictable throughput and data isolation. Key pain: token costs during nightly re-ranks + real-time personalization latency spikes. AE ran preliminary cost model during demo; engineering asked for a cost workshop.

POC summary (short): integrated hosted endpoints into feature service, basic prefix caching enabled, measured end-to-end p99 latency 180-220ms on gen-1 prompts, cost runway looks tight at current call volumes. Team quote: "We need costs to be predictable before we expand to 10M monthly users."

Security: require SSO/SAML, SOC2 attestation, KMS integration and audit logging. Also data residency constraints for EU customers (want VPC + regional routing).

Commercial:...

title: Cognia Secura Technologies
body:
Account: mid-to-large fintech; data residency mandate: EU-only for customer data and logs.
Primary ask: Private VPC deployment with strict encryption posture and KMS integration.
In-transit encryption: require mTLS mutual auth between customer VPC egress and Redwood private connectors; customer will provide CA chain or use CloudHSM-backed certs.
TLS version baseline: TLS1.2 minimum, prefer TLS1.3 for control plane <-> runtime. FIPS endpoints requested for HSM operations.
At-rest: all model artefacts and KV caches must be encrypted with customer-managed keys (CMKs) in AWS KMS; EBS volumes and S3 buckets must be encrypted with CMKs and cross-account key policy reviewed.
Key management: envelope encryption; Redwood to support customer-supplied CMKs + rotation policy. Customer requires proof of key rotation window and rotation audit events.
Secrets management: prefer HashiCorp Vault integ...

title: Hyperlane InferStack
body:
Account: Hyperlane InferStack — proprietary low-latency inference for market-making and personalization. Contact: CTO Ethan Liao, Head of ML Ops Priya Nayar.

Summary of needs:
- Strict 50-100ms tail latency target for chat/rerank paths at 200 RPS burst; average payload 256 tokens.
- Very cost-sensitive per-token budget; exploring quantization + batching tradeoffs.
- Must support prefix caching for session continuity (persistent KV cache) and per-tenant model pinning.
- Strong security: SSO/SAML, KMS for key custody, audit logs, and EU data residency for EU customers.

Recent timeline (short):
- 2025-11-15: Intro call (Maya) — high level requirements; backlog: send perf questionnaire.
- 2025-12-03: Technical deep-dive (Jordan) — discussed batching windows, KV cache strategies; agreed to perf matrix.
- 2026-01-10: Sent sample workload trace + traffic histogram (drive link).
- 2026-01-1...

title: SableQuartz SaaS
body:
Topline: Mid-market product analytics vendor evaluating Redwood for customer-facing search/chat and internal reranking. Started on Hosted API (quick PoC) -> moved to short Dedicated POC to validate latency and unit economics. Team is product-led; decision driven by engineering + finance.

PoC summary: 2-week Dedicated POC in Jan 2025 using a 3-node reserved pool (NVIDIA-A100-equivalent) with burst to 5 nodes for peak. Ingested ~1.2M docs for embeddings+search; ran chat flows and reranking pipelines. Verified end-to-end integration via Redwood JS SDK and internal platform connectors.

Performance & requirements (from engineering):
- Target: p95 chat latency < 200ms for 1-2 turn prompts; sustained throughput target 400-600 req/s for batched embeddings/reranking.
- KV cache/prefix caching required for top-N user flows.
- Model preference: open variants (Mistral/llama-family) for cost; wi...

title: Orion VaultWorks
body:
Summary: Enterprise financial services customer evaluating Private VPC deployment for production inference. Strong legal/security requirements; procurement pathway coordinated by Head of Ops.

Key requirements (verbatim from calls):
- "We must keep EU customer data in EU/UK only; proof of residency controls required."
- "Need SOC2/ISO artifacts, annual pen test report, and ability to onboard our AWS KMS (HSM-backed)."
- DPA + SCCs non-negotiable, legal will redline standard indemnities and data processing clauses.
- Audit logging: immutable audit trails, 7 year retention for trade surveillance logs.
- SLOs: 99.95% uptime, p95 chat <=120ms, bulk embeddings throughput ~200 qps.

Technical notes from SE:
- Prefer pinning model variants and rollout policies; require canary with automatic fallback to smaller quantized variant.
- KMS: customer-managed keys required; prefer AWS KMS w/ dedica...

title: StoneMill Retail Intelligence
body: Mid-market->enterprise retail analytics provider evaluating Dedicated capacity for real-time personalization + chat assistant across web and POS. CTO (R. Patel) emphasized: "we need deterministic tail latency guarantees for checkout & promo flows". Primary ask: clear mapping of p50/p95/p99 definitions, separate streaming-first-byte vs non-streaming end-to-end timings, and how KD-cache/warm caches affect SLO measurement. Security wants VPC deployment + keys in customer KMS. Procurement asking for SLA credit language tied to measured tail windows. Technical team concerned about bursty Black Friday patterns and warmup behavior.

title: EchoHarbor Coachly
body:
Summary of account and requirements: EchoHarbor Coachly builds a coaching layer for mid-market contact centers — post-call QA, automated coaching highlights, and turn-to-ticket summarization. Key priorities: high fidelity transcripts, low-latency highlights for supervisor dashboards, and predictable performance during end-of-month billing spikes.

Key technical asks (from SE call 2026-02-18):
- Need sub-500ms median token latency for short agent prompts (live assist) when routing to smaller context windows.
- Batch/routing policy to separate long-billable summarization jobs from live assist traffic.
- Support for tool-calling to update ticketing system (REST webhook + idempotency) and atomic action confirmations.
- Prefix caching for repeated customer intros to reduce token cost for multi-turn flows.

Security / compliance:
- Must support SSO (SAML) + SCIM; audit logs retained 12 mont...

title: Radian Guard Capacity Trust
body:
Account snapshot: enterprise payments platform; PCI-adjacent workflows (no card PAN storage in ML stack), heavy spikes during payroll windows and end-of-day reconciliation. Primary ask: predictable, contract-backed throughput guarantees with clear concurrency and burst rules.

Recent timeline and irreversible bullets: 
- 2026-02-11: Intro call (AE Maya + SE Oliver) — high-level scope, told them Redwood dedicated fits our isolation needs. CTO asked about tokens/sec baseline.
- 2026-02-18: Security deep-dive (security PM + Redwood security SME) — KMS + audit requirements; flagged HSM integration for keys.
- 2026-02-25: Tech workshop / perf proof-of-concept kickoff (SE-led). Run plan: synthetic throughput run + replay of 48h peak telemetry.
- 2026-03-02: POC results review (Fireflies FF-20260212-8234). Redwood baseline: 9.8k sustained tokens/sec with p95 latency 160ms on quantized varian...

title: Mariner Insights Platform
body:
Account background:
- Customer since 2023 (started on hosted API for embeddings + reranking). Started POC for Dedicated in Q4-2024 based on seasonal throughput needs. Renewal for hosted contract due 2025-06 but want to fold into Dedicated reserved capacity SOW.

Primary drivers:
- Predictable throughput for peak chartering season (May-Oct).
- Strong need for regional residency (APAC) for some customers and partners.
- Cost predictability: looking to move from per-token hosted spend to committed Dedicated with Optimize to reduce unit costs.

Technical requirements / targets (from SE calls):
- Workload: real-time dispatch suggestions (chat-like), bulk document embeddings/search, post-voyage summarization (batch).
- Latency target: p95 < 300ms for short-turn dispatch queries; tail latency most important.
- Throughput: sustained ~120–250 RPS at peak across multimodel routes.
- Model prefe...

title: Picosecond AI Solutions
body:
CRM notes:
- Company: Picosecond AI — builds sub-100ms personalization and reranking for mobile ad experiences and in-app search. Extremely latency and cost sensitive.
- Primary ask: consistent p95 latency <= 60ms for chat-like requests (avg prompt 80 tokens, max 256), sustained throughput 200-350 RPS during peak windows, 10M tokens/mo budget target.
- Optimization interests: batching (micro-batching with strict latency envelope), prefix/KV caching for repeated user context, aggressive quantization (4-bit / 8-bit evals), model routing & fallbacks to balance cost vs accuracy. Wants Redwood Optimize to produce concrete batching + quant profile.
- Security: VPC deployment required for EU/NA customers, SSO + SCIM, SOC2 baseline, customer-managed KMS for keys, audit logs retention 13 months, occasional ISO requests from partners.
- POC details: agreed synthetic perf run (10k requests acros...

title: Cerulean Helm Searchware
body:
Profile: mid-market product analytics SaaS (B2B) - search-first discovery UX for product metrics + docs.
Initial ask: embeddings + semantic search to power: 1) in-app 'search insights' 2) cross-doc relevance and 3) nightly batch embedding pipeline for reindexing.
Current infra: PostgreSQL primary, S3 for assets, experiments using Pinecone managed vector DB (single region).
Traffic/scale targets: ~200k unique docs, 25k daily updates (delta ingestion), expected nightly bulk job ~100k embeddings per run.
Latency/throughput targets: 95th pct < 120ms for search rank calls at 100 qps; embedding bulk throughput target ~10k embeddings/min during batch windows (can burst).
Cost sensitivity: strong. Wants unit economics model for hosted vs dedicated. Interested in cost per 1k tokens and per-embedding unit.

title: Larkspur Trust Technologies
body:
Qualified enterprise account. Primary contact: CTO Mira Patel (security-first). Wants private VPC deployment with customer-controlled keys (HSM-backed preferred). Key points from 11/07 demo:
- CTO: "No platform-managed key material for sensitive models" — insists on BYOK and HSM wrapping.
- Security: provided preliminary key-policy checklist; asked for KMS call flow diagram and key rotation SLA.
- Infra: wants vault integration options (AWS KMS, Azure Key Vault, GCP KMS) + offline HSM for on-prem DR.
- Performance sensitivity: low-latency chat; target p95 < 120ms for 512-token replies in US east region.
- Cost: enterprise budget but wants Redwood Optimize suggestions for quantization and batching to reduce GPU hours.
- Compliance: data residency US-only, require audit logs forwarded to SIEM, 7y retention for audit trails.
- Quote from Mira on call: "We cannot accept vendor KMS unless ...

title: Nebulon InstantServe AI
body:
Summary and current status:
- Account: Nebulon builds personalization and ad-selection infra for low-latency bidding and on-site customization. Very sensitive to tail latency and per-request cost.
- Primary ask: consistent p95 latency <100ms for short-chat + reranking flows, 500-1k TPS for embeddings/rerank batch windows, aggressive cost targets (reducing token cost by 3x vs current baseline).
- Perf targets and constraints:
 * p50 target: 25-40ms; p95 target: <100ms (end-to-end from API call to model token emission).
 * typical prompt length: 16-64 tokens; rerank context size: 128 tokens.
 * batch windows: sub-10ms dynamic batching desired for chat; larger 50-200ms windows acceptable for rerank.
 * throughput bias: spiky traffic with frequent microbursts during campaign launches.
- Model preferences: open-weights family, prefer quantized variants (int8 or q4_0) for cost; fallback to ...

title: Verdant Copilot Labs
body:
Lead: Verdant Copilot Labs — large engineering org building a first-party IDE copilot and embedded code-assistant. Primary goal: ship low-latency completions inside internal IDE with predictable per-seat economics.

Key requirements / snippets from calls:
- "Need sub-50ms token completions for local developer flows when running on dedicated pools; 90th perc p95 <= 120ms for streaming." — VP Eng
- Must support model/version pinning per team (stable pin for critical services, fast-channel for experimental features).
- Cost targets: reduce per-seat inference cost by 3x vs current hosted baseline through batching + prefix/KV caching + 4-bit quantized variants. Interested in Redwood Optimize recommendations.
- Deployment: hybrid — dedicated in cloud for most teams, air-gapped on-prem for regulated finance unit. SSO/SAML required, KMS integration for key material. Audit logging + 7y retenti...

title: Cumulus UX Intelligence
body:
Product team: mid-market B2B SaaS, search + recommendations inside product UI
Primary ask: improve ordering of results for product discovery use-case; move from vector-only top-k to model-aware reranker
Current infra: Elastic + in-house embedding service; looking to replace rerank stage with Redwood reranking endpoint
POC scope agreed: take their labeled eval set (~120k query-doc pairs), run offline scoring on hosted trial, return NDCG@10, MRR, pairwise win-rate
Performance targets: p50 latency < 120ms for single rerank calls (batch size 8), p99 < 450ms acceptable for UI calls; offline scoring throughput 1-2k qps across batch nodes
Cost sensitivity: mid-market — looking for predictable cost; interested in dedicated after proof-of-concept for heavy scoring runs
Model preference: open model family ok (Llama2 / mix), prefer quantized variants for cost; want Redwood to recommend model+qua...

title: Silverpine Knowledge
body:
Summary: mid-market knowledge platform for customer success + in-app help. Looking to replace ad-hoc search with semantic layer (embeddings + FAISS/Pinecone currently).
Initial ask: embeddings quality parity with current pipeline, predictable throughput for search (sub-150ms median for 1-2K concurrent search qps), and cheaper unit token cost for nightly re-embeds.
Customer quote: 'We need consistent tail latency — spikes kill UX for live search and conversational bots.'
Current infra: nightly Spark job writes embeddings to S3, ingests to Pinecone. ~5M docs once/day re-embed, plus realtime user uploads (~10-20K/day).
Throughput targets: 200 qps embedding ingest for batch window, 800 qps read for search during business hours (parallel requests from product).
Cost sensitivity: mid-market buyer, tight unit economics; wants a clear cost comparison Hosted vs Dedicated including reserved blo...

title: Olive Avenue Health AI
body:
Lead source: inbound form + pricing page. Small chain of clinics (teletherapy + intake automation). Current stack: Firebase auth, Postgres, Intercom for messages. Primary contact is CTO (Aisha Rahman) and Product Lead (Tom Barnes). They want: 
- Start with hosted API (self-serve) to prototype triage/chat & automated intake summarization.
- Latency target: p50 100-200ms, p95 < 600ms for short session turns (avg 80 tokens). Throughput modest: ~50 concurrent users during business hours.
- Cost sensitivity noted — want per-token estimate and options for automatic caching of repeated questions.
- Security: "HIPAA-adjacent" workloads — not storing PHI permanently today but need retention control and easy purge ability. Asked explicitly: "Can Redwood hosted API be configured to avoid retaining prompts? What are options to set TTL and request-level non-persistence?"
- Compliance asks: SSO (SA...

title: Harborbridge Advisory Group
body:
Summary: evaluation stalled and ultimately closed-lost. Primary blocker = FINRA recordkeeping / compliance acceptance + Q1 budget freeze.
Discovery (2025-10-07): intro call with Head of ML Ops Lisa Romero and Head of Legal Peter Kwok. Use case: trader-facing advisory chat + reranking for client research. Heavily regulated: FINRA retention + eDiscovery requirements.
POC scope (Nov-Dec 2025): ran dedicated pool demo + sample ingestion for reranker. SE notes: p95 latency ~60ms for 512 tokens, sustained throughput 120 req/s with batching. Good perf; engineering impressed.
Security review (Dec 2025): completed Redwood security questionnaire; legal escalated — requirement for immutable long-term retention (7 years), auditable chain-of-custody, and vendor attestation specific to FINRA. Redwood proposed Private deployment + KMS + audit logs; estimated uplift to ARR + one-time ops cost ~150k.

title: Saffron Aisle Marketplaces
body:
Snapshots from calls and sales notes:
- Business: multi-vertical marketplace (home, apparel, electronics). Catalog ~25M SKUs, growing 30% YoY. Heavy seasonality (Black Friday + Holiday window = 8-12x baseline).
- Primary objective: replace brittle keyword-based search + slow business-rules reranker with semantic retrieval + learned reranker to improve conversion on long-tail SKUs.
- Technical targets (requested by VP of Search):
 * Cost target: <= $0.0015 per query (end-to-end retrieval + rerank) at baseline; keep sub-$0.005 at peak bursts via autoscaling/caching.
 * Latency SLO: p95 end-to-end < 250ms (preferred <200ms). Search path split: ANN retrieval ~<100ms, reranker ~<150ms p95.
 * Index scale: 25M 768-d vectors, expected 3-5 updates/sec (product feed), retention of embeddings for 13 months.
- Proposed architecture notes from SE: use quantized vector store with HNSW w/ sharding ...

title: Tidewell Relevance Studio
body:
Company background: Tidewell builds a headless search product for mid-market DTC brands. They operate a multi-tenant index per merchant; want to replace heuristic reranker with a learned reranker that improves cart-adds and revenue per session. Typical catalog sizes 50k-2M SKUs depending on merchant.

POC goals: prove quality lift (NDCG@10 / MRR) on a representative eval set, validate online p95 latency for top-50 rerank, and estimate cost per 1k reranks for both hosted and dedicated setups. They will run both nightly offline scoring (for ranking features & re-compute ranks) and a low-latency online rerank for storefront queries.

Performance targets: online p95 <= 120ms for top-50 reranks (soft), sustain 100-150 QPS during peak marketing events, offline batch throughput target ~40k docs/min to finish nightly refresh windows. Cost constraint: prefer <$0.12 per 1k rerank calls across t...

title: Strato Core Inference
body:
Summary: Strato Core is building a realtime personalization and ranking layer for in-app chat and content recommendations. Extremely sensitive to tail latency and token cost. Primary ask: show consistent p95 < 50ms for short-chat flows (avg 50-120 tokens), and p99 under 200ms at steady-state 8k QPS across region. Cost target: competitive with current infra (~$0.00045/token equivalent) but willing to trade up to 20% for reliability.

Workload details / requirements:
- Traffic profile: spiky daytime peak (8k qps), baseline 1.5k qps; median request ~60 tokens, 5% long tail > 800 tokens (doc snippets).
- Models: prefer LLaMA-family + 8-bit or 4-bit quantized variants for production; want Redwood to recommend quantization profiles.
- SLOs: 95th <= 50ms (chat short-turns), 99th <= 200ms; throughput target sustained 6k qps per region.
- Resilience: multi-region failover and routing; fallback...

title: Lodestar Low-Latency ML
body:
Company overview: Lodestar builds a realtime personalization and reranking layer for e-commerce/ads. Evaluating Redwood to replace an in-house LLM reranker and offload embedding/rerank throughput. Primary constraints: tight p95 latency targets, predictable cost per query, and VPC/KMS control.

Summary of requirements:
- Workloads: short-turn chat & micro-reranking for UI personalization; synchronous calls with soft SLOs.
- Latency targets: end-to-end p95 <= 70ms for 1-2 token decode steps; p95 generation latency target 80ms for short responses (<=64 tokens).
- Throughput: steady-state 200-300 QPS for rerank endpoints; spikes up to 1k QPS during promos.
- Cost sensitivity: target blended unit cost ~ $0.002/token (or equivalent per-query cost); want recommendations to trade cost vs latency.
- Model preferences: prefer open Llama-2-like variants or optimized quantized weights; fallbacks ...

title: EchoSummarize Labs
body:
Lead origin: inbound via sales enablement webinar signup (Jan webinar). Use-case: extract concise call summaries + action items for SDRs and CSMs; integrate into CRM and sequence automation.

Initial discovery (2026-02-10): inbound signup + automated email. AE (Ava) intro call 2026-02-18 (ff-20260218-echo-call-01). Attendees: CEO (Priya Shah), Head of Product (Danilo Ruiz), Head of Rev Ops (Lena Park).

Call highlights:
- Primary ask: accurate meeting summaries with speaker attribution and PII redaction (SSNs, CC numbers, emails masked).
- "We can't store raw transcripts longer than 90 days for EU customers" — explicit retention constraint from legal.
- Need simple hosted API to start (self-serve), low cost; will consider Dedicated later if latency spikes.
- Prefer public cloud deployment, but must support data residency controls for EU customers (they may route EU calls to EU region)...

First discovery call 2026-03-03 (30m) — quick demo of hosted API, token pricing, and latency expectations.

title: Mosaic Archive Systems
body:
Summary/CRM notes:
- Company: Mosaic Archive Systems, specializes in indexing decades of media: transcripts, closed captions, scripts, editorial metadata, and OCR from tape transfers. Content sizes are long: single assets 5k-250k tokens.
- Primary problem: search performance for journalist and legal teams. Need high recall + top-k precision. Currently using Elastic + nightly pipeline; want denser recall and reranking to cut manual curation.
- Scale: expect 20-60M chunks after fine-grained chunking (scene-level + sliding windows). Index growth steady 5-8% mo.
- Latency targets: 95th <= 300ms for search-retrieval+rerank in dedicated/private deployment for interactive UX. For legal workflows can accept 1-2s for deep rerank.
- Cost target: aiming for ~$0.01-0.05 per interactive query end-to-end (embedding lookup + reranker compute amortized). This drives conversation about quantized model...

title: Orion Telemetry AI
body: Summary: Edge telemetry vendor building low-latency anomaly detection + similarity search for time-series signals. Primary ask: dedicated capacity with throughput guarantees (200–400 embeddings/sec), 99th pctl latency <200ms for chat-style alerting, bulk rerank jobs overnight. POC findings: 8k context needed for some long-series aggregation prompts; KV cache cut cold-start latency by ~40ms in hosted trial. Quantization: 8-bit + mixed-4bit gave 1.9–2.4x throughput but small accuracy delta on rare classes — team worried about false positives. Preferred models: Llama-2-70B variants and a trimmed 30B for cost tiers; want ability to fall back to cheaper variant when capacity constrained. Reserved GPU ask: started with planning for 8x A100-80GB or 12x H100 depending on kernel availability. Autoscaling policy: prefer predictable reserved baseline + burst to shared pools. SLA asks: 99.9% upti...

title: SaberBit IDEPilot
body:
Account brief: Sab erBit builds a VS Code extension 'IDEPilot' used by internal developer teams and paid customers. Primary value prop is ultra-low-latency streaming completions and adaptive inline suggestions that react to cursor/context. They ship via VS Code Marketplace and have extension-level telemetry + per-install tokens.

Key asks from initial calls:
- Streaming delta tokens <50ms initial latency for inline suggestions (target: <50ms for first token, <30ms subsequent).
- Model/version pinning per-extension release channel (stable/canary) with rollout hooks.
- Per-user and per-workspace rate limits, with burst capacity during installs/updates.
- Option to run inference on Dedicated pools for enterprise customers with VPC routing.
- Audit logs for snippet usage, KMS integration for API keys, and retention controls for snippet telemetry.

title: VerityLane Product Labs
body:
Lead: VerityLane builds an embedded product assistant inside their core B2B app (mid-market customers). Evaluating Redwood: started with Hosted API trial (2 weeks), moved to a short POC using production-like traffic replay.

Key takeaways from POC:
- Traffic characteristics: weekday peak 120 QPS at 14:00, sustained daytime 28-35 QPS, overnight <5 QPS. Observed burst spikes up to 180 QPS during feature launches.
- Concurrency: UI shows ~40 concurrent sessions typical, peak concurrency observed 60 during replay.
- Token mix: avg tokens/request ~320. Breakdown: ~68% short chat exchanges (prompt+100-400 tokens), ~22% long-form generation (500-1,200 tokens), ~10% embeddings/rerank calls (avg 1,024 dims).
- Latency targets: p95 <250ms for chat; p99 <500ms preferred. Currently hitting p95 ~280ms on hosted trial for generation flows.
- Cost sensitivity: mid-market margin constraints; wants to...

title: ValencePeak
body: Summary: ValencePeak is a mid-market SaaS that provides embedded help & context-aware in-app answers for SMB customers. Primary objective: low-latency, high-QPS knowledge-base RAG pipeline that uses embeddings for retrieval and a cross-encoder reranker for final scoring. Indexing: current corpus ~1.2M docs (mixed short articles, FAQs, and transcripts); expect up to ~3M within 12 months. QPS: typical peak 500-1,200 QPS during business hours; burst up to 2.5k for product launches. Latency targets: retrieval+rerank end-to-end <=150ms (aspirational), acceptable <=250ms; 95th percentile SLA requested. Cost sensitivity: moderate-high — wants >3x cost improvement over current managed provider at parity for latency. Model preferences: open/quantized models for cost control; bi-encoder for recall, cross-encoder (smaller fine-tuned) for rerank. Routing/fallback: prefer region-aware routing (us/...

title: CodeLattice Labs
body:
Short summary: mid-stage IDE copilots vendor building embedded code assistance for enterprise dev teams. Primary ask: low-latency streaming and deterministic UX in editors (VS Code + JetBrains). TTFT focus — want p50 <= 80ms, jitter minimized, smooth token streaming (no stuttering). Token budget policy: per-session token cap + adaptive cutoff for long completions. PoC constraints: integrate with hosted API first; must show model pinning + automatic fallback (smaller quantized model) under load.

Recent call notes (fragmented CRM style):
- "When autocomplete stutters devs lose context" — product lead quote.
- Concern: websocket disconnects cause duplicate suggestions; want idempotent deltas and resume token offsets.
- Prototype uses continuous streaming (SSE) with 16-token chunking, but jitter at client seen when GC spikes on local plugin.
- Backend asks: support pinned model per-user ...

title: Crestfall RAGGuard
body:
Snapshot from calls and email threads - shorthand.
- Company: SaaS customer-service platform with embedded chat widget used by ~1,200 enterprise customers.
- Primary ask: Replace slow retrieval stack (Elastic + in-house reranker) with RAG that reduces agent handoffs and increases ticket deflection. Focus = embeddings + high-quality reranking for top-5 results.
- Scale: plan to index fast-moving KB + ticket history ~150M-300M documents (varied chunking); initial POC to validate 50M index baseline.
- Latency SLOs: VP Eng insists on <=150ms p95 end-to-end for a single RAG query (retrieve+embed+rerank). Reranker budget should add <=60ms p95. Tail latency sensitivity high (customer abandonment).
- Throughput target: sustained 200 qps per region during peak; spikes to 700 qps expected for special events. Must support autoscale and graceful throttling.
- Quality: product needs top-5 rerank p...

title: Hummingbird Privacy Integrations
body:
CRM notes (shorthand, call paraphrases):
- High priority: PHI workloads, US-only residency requirement (must keep patient data in us-east region).
- Legal: "will only proceed if vendor completes SIG/CAIQ + provides SOC2 Type II evidence and audit logging with 7yr retention".
- Infra: Wants VPC peering rather than public endpoints; egress must be blocked except to approved S3 endpoints.
- KMS: BYOK preferred — customer-managed keys in Azure/AWS KMS/HSM. Need proof of key rotation, CMEK compatibility, and zero-access patterns.
- Shared responsibility: HBPI security team expects clear matrix mapping (redwood-hosted vs customer-managed) for encryption-in-transit, encryption-at-rest, log retention, and incident response. They requested a one-page DR/incident runbook.
- POC: scope = secure-document-search on anonymized PHI, latency SLO 200ms p50, throughput 30 qps, budgeted 1mo trial.
- Quo...

title: Canyon Ridge Product Platforms
body:
Primary drivers: cost predictability, SLA, VPC/private network for customer data flow.
POC uncovered underestimation of token churn on long conversations; recommending intelligent prefix caching + short-term KV retention.
They asked for a mapped example invoice showing commit, burst, and overage across a 90-day ramp — sales ops to generate.
Legal: want Redwood DPA + custom SLA clause for 99.9% inference availability; indemnity language flagged by their GC.
Action items: Sales to send formal Dedicated term sheet (3 commit options + ramp), SE to attach latency/throughput test logs, Solutions to propose cache config and quantization profile for cost savings.
Risks: If overage caps are too loose CFO will pull budget. On-prem connector latency concerns could block go-live for some EU customers (residency requirement).
Good signal: product team already built integration with Hosted API; eng...

POC uncovered underestimation of token churn on long conversations; recommending intelligent prefix caching + short-term KV retention.
They asked for a mapped example invoice showing commit, burst, and overage across a 90-day ramp — sales ops to generate.
Legal: want Redwood DPA + custom SLA clause for 99.9% inference availability; indemnity language flagged by their GC.
Action items: Sales to send formal Dedicated term sheet (3 commit options + ramp), SE to attach latency/throughput test logs, Solutions to propose cache config and quantization profile for cost savings.
Risks: If overage caps are too loose CFO will pull budget. On-prem connector latency concerns could block go-live for some EU customers (residency requirement).
Good signal: product team already built integration with Hosted API; eng...

title: Ironwood Shelf Systems
body:
Lead: Ava Chen. Mid-market product catalog SaaS — heavy on search/recommendations. Evaluating Hosted API first (quick prototype) then want Dedicated in VPC for production. Summary of recent activity:
- 2026-02-15: Intro call (Ava) — product + infra goals, want embeddings for search + reranking layer; target p95 latency < 150ms for single-shot search flows.
- 2026-02-18: Hosted API demo (Marcus) — prototype running on test dataset, Fireflies id ff_2026-02-18_ironwood_demo. Customer liked streaming responses and embeddings quality.

title: Marketloom Analytics
body:
Account overview: midsize SaaS analytics player, product-led growth, heavy realtime support use case
Started with Hosted API to move quickly; POC validated functionality but cost/latency gap vs target
Primary requirement: 95th percentile chat latency < 70ms for agent-facing workflows (VP Product quote: 'sub-70ms is table stakes for our live product'),
Throughput target: 30k concurrent chat sessions at peak; embeddings workload ~120M tokens/mo expected after Q4 ramp
Cost sensitivity: procurement wants predictable committed spend; target committed ARR $60k-$100k with price breaks above 150M tokens/mo
Security: Okta SSO + SCIM required, customer KMS for key control, audit logging retention 1 year, US-only data residency for PII
Deployment preference: Dedicated pools in public-cloud VPC initially; may evaluate private on-prem for regulated customers in 2027
POC findings: Hosted API embedd...

title: Sienna Crest Commerce
body:
Demo completed 2026-02-18. Attendees: Head of Product (Maya Lin), Eng Manager (Tom Alvarez), Security lead (Rebecca Ho), AE Jordan Blake, SE Priya Natarajan. They were enthusiastic about latency and the reranking demo (specifically preferring model variants for cost control). Key quote from Maya: 'Sharp latency and predictable cost are table stakes — we need ironclad security sign-off before pilots.' Security asked for SOC2 evidence, KMS integration diagram, and an answer about audit log retention. We provided SOC2 summary and KMS architecture (drive links above) during the demo.

Post-demo status: two follow-up emails and one Loom sent (02/19, 02/24, 03/03). No reply from Maya or Rebecca. AE left voicemail 03/05; LinkedIn message 03/09. SE offered a 2-week pilot with a dedicated SE hours (no-charge) to unblock eval; haven’t heard back. Prod team appears to have paused new pilots due ...

title: Riverbend EchoWorks
body:
Background: mid-market product analytics SaaS. Building an in-app AI assistant for product managers and support triage. Started Hosted API trial to validate UX and cost assumptions.
Hosted trial (3 weeks): onboarding via simple SDK, ran ~70k tokens/day average during trial week, peaked at 280k tokens/day during customer demo week.
Observed traffic characteristics during trial: avg QPS ~7, observed short bursts to 28 QPS; typical session: prompt ~120 tokens, completion ~420 tokens (avg session ~540 tokens).
Token mix (trial): 30% <64 tokens, 50% 64-512 tokens, 20% >512 tokens. Long tail of >1024 tokens for doc-summarization flows (slow path).
Performance notes: Hosted 95th-pctl response latency climbed to ~430ms on heavy long-request windows (seq lengths >800). Short prompts have good tail behavior but want stricter SLOs.
Business ask: move to Dedicated for predictable cost and SLOs; m...

title: BrightQuark Marketing Engine
body:
Inbound SMB lead from content-marketing platform. Use-case: generate high volumes of short-form marketing copy (emails, headings, social) + per-campaign personalization. Primary constraints: cost sensitivity and throughput. Wants hosted_api (self-serve) — "fast time to production" and minimal ops. Quoted prospect: "We run thousands of short prompts per minute during campaigns; per-token economics matter more than raw model accuracy."

Technical requirements / asks:
- Latency/throughput: target 200-300 rps peak, p50 latency target ~200-300ms for single-pass generation; willing to accept higher tail if batching helps cost.
- Cost sensitivity: heavy interest in batching + prefix/KV caching to reduce token spend; asked about suggestions for 8-bit quantized variants or cheaper open models.
- Model preferences: open-models preferred (Llama2-like family) for cost control; okay with Redwood-c...

title: Briarcliff Response Hub
body:
- Primary product: customer-facing support agent that deflects tickets and surfaces KB answers.
- Scale: currently 50M docs of mixed-length (kb articles, transcripts). Expected 5–10M quarterly growth.
- Queries: peak 3k QPS across distributed regions; tail latency SLO: 200ms for top-1 retrieval, 350ms for full RAG (embed+rerank) 95th.
- Interested in hybrid architecture: vector DB for coarse recall + Redwood reranker to improve top-5 precision.
- Cost sensitivity: wants predictable cost per query; target <$0.02 per query at current scale.
- Model prefs: prefer open-source embedding models (1-2B) and quantized 3-6B rerankers; open to Redwood-verified variants.
- Security: must support SSO, audit logs, regional residency (EU for EEA customers), KMS integration.
- Quote from Head of Search: "Need top-5 quality parity with our manual rerank — can we achieve 90% relevance uplift vs current...

title: SprintCraft Assistants
body:
Context: SprintCraft builds an IDE-integrated copilot for enterprise dev teams (JS/TS, Python). Primary UX metric: Time-to-first-token (TTFT) inside VS Code and web IDEs. Current POC focuses on streaming latency, jitter behavior when multiple files/extensions active, and token budget strategies to balance cost vs. completion quality.

Recent calls:
- 2025-01-07 intro: Riley demoed Redwood streaming; CTO said 'devs drop the feature if TTFT > 150ms' (quote).
- 2025-01-22 integration: alpha SDK + streaming + basic fallback to batched responses when network congested. Extension captures session-level token budget but no soft preemption yet.

Key technical requirements:
- TTFT target: p95 < 100ms for single-file edits, p95 < 150ms for multi-file contexts.
- Jitter: need <20ms variance at p95 during developer typing bursts.
- Backpressure: client-side buffer + server push acknowledgements; ...

title: CallCanvas Engage
body:
Lead source: inbound trial signup (self-serve). Product: lightweight in-app meeting summarization SDK + agent for SDR coaching. Signed up 2026-03-01, used free credits to prototype. Initial contact: Jordan P. intro call 2026-03-03 (Fireflies ff_7f9b2a3c). Discovery call 2026-03-07 (demo of hosted API streaming summary). Key stakeholders: CTO (Ravi Singh), Head of Product (Marissa Cole), Legal (procurement review).

PII / retention concerns (main friction):
- They record customer calls that contain names, emails, payments mentions. Legal: "cannot hold raw transcripts >30 days"; wants redaction + configurable retention policies per workspace.
- Ask: token retention vs. message retention; need confirmation that embeddings/LM outputs are not stored by default (or can be disabled).
- Request for audit log visibility + exportable retention proofs for compliance.

Technical / product notes:
...

title: PortalFlare CopilotWorks
body:
Summary: mid-market product org building an in-app onboarding copilot (guided flows + step completion). Evaluating Hosted API first (fast experiment), then Dedicated if latency/cost profile requires it. POC: 2-week sandbox focusing on 3 high-traffic flows (signup guidance, feature discovery, billing help).

Key quotes / seller notes:
- 'Need very consistent perceived latency for short turns — streaming is required for UX.'
- 'Token spend is our biggest fear; want hard caps + graceful fallbacks.'

Performance targets / requirements:
- Latency SLO: p50 <= 100ms for small completions (50-150 tokens), p95 <= 300ms (goal). Streaming to first-token <120ms perceived.
- Throughput: bursts to 200 concurrent users during onboarding surges; typical steady-state ~30 qps.
- Cost controls: token caps per session + model routing to cheaper variants for non-critical prompts. Estimated avg token per s...

title: Driftwood AI Integration
body:
Lead source: self-serve signup (marketing site CTA).

Initial context:
- Small ecommerce platform (B2C) building conversational product finder + post-purchase support bot and personalized recommendations.
- Signed up with free credits 2026-02-18; poked around embeddings and streaming chat endpoints.
- Wants fast examples — 'give me a drop-in LangChain + Vercel example we can paste into Next.js' (quote from CTO).

Technical requirements / asks from call (2026-02-24):
- Primary integration targets: LangChain (Agents + RetrievalQA), LlamaIndex for document indexing, and Vercel AI SDK for edge serverless deploys.
- POC scope: index ~12k product docs (S3), power site search + contextual product chat.
- Latency target: ~<500ms end-user response for chat (95th pct) and <120ms per-embedding for single-vector calls (cost-sensitive).
- Throughput: bursty, ~20 req/s during promotions; mostly low...

title: ZeptoQuill Marketing Platform
body: Inbound lead from content marketing post about 'cheaper content generation'. They self-registered and ran samples on free tier. CTO (Maya Singh) and Head of Eng (Roberto Diaz) on calls. Key quotes: 'we do thousands of items per day, small token sizes but huge volume' and 'if we can't get unit cost down, margins vanish'. Very cost sensitive; primary asks: examples of cost savings from batching/prefix caching, demo of latency at 200-500 rps, and clear rate-limit policy. Interested in multi-model routing to fall back to cheaper variants for non-critical drafts. No enterprise procurement yet; small monthly budget right now. Wants to see 1M-token pricing scenarios + expected latency at 50/200/500 qps. Has basic security needs (SSO planned Q3) and wants audit logs for content provenance. Mentioned competitor tests with OpenAI (higher latency/cost) and Cohere (similar pricing). Request: shor...

title: LucidCurio Archive Discovery
body:
Background: LucidCurio operates a 20+ year media archive (video + transcripts + legacy doc formats). Primary goal: fast retrieval for editorial tooling, content discovery and legal e-discovery.

POC scope / goals:
- Index scale target: ~30M-40M chunks (expecting ingest of 8M long transcripts; avg raw doc size 4k-12k tokens).
- End-to-end p90 latency goal for editor flow: <= 400ms; interactive goal <= 250ms for short queries.
- Cost target: ~$0.01-0.03 per query (vector retrieval + optional reranker).

Technical preferences & constraints:
- Prefer private deployment (on VPC) due to contractual residency requirements. On-prem for air-gapped legal case work.
- Need SSO/SAML and KMS integration; audit logs for every retrieval.

Chunking strategy discussion (notes from 2026-02-08 call):
- Lean toward variable chunk sizes: 512 tokens for short-form captions, 800-1,024 tokens for narrative t...

title: RedRiver Forge
body:
Account summary: Mid-market CX SaaS (B2B) building an in-app assistant + enterprise search. Completed 6-week Hosted API trial; strong feature fit. Moving to evaluation for Dedicated because: 1) predictable latency at scale, 2) data isolation (VPC) and KMS, 3) better unit economics for high token volumes. Team wants to keep same API surface as hosted but pin to reserved capacity on GPUs.

Hosted trial highlights: 45M tokens total across trial; peak day 3.2M tokens. Peak QPS observed 18 during feature launch spike (late-Feb). Average concurrency during peak windows ~9. Token mix during trial: chat/conversation 70% (short prompts, avg prompt 220 tokens, avg reply 850 tokens), embeddings requests 18% (batch of docs, avg 1.2k tokens per request), reranking/rubric evals 12% (short inputs, high request rate).

Perf & cost signals: P95 response latency on hosted: 280ms for short chats (first ...

title: Saffron Cove Analytics
body:
Summary: enterprise evaluation focused on Private (VPC) with strict regional controls. HQ Toronto; product used by EU and UK business lines — must demonstrate hard separation of EU data, plus Canada-first residency and APAC sandbox.

Key asks (from CTO & CISO):
- BYOK/KMS integration (customer-managed keys; HSM-backed). Key rotation policy + cross-region key policies.
- Audit logs: immutable, 7-year retention for certain legal data; searchable exports to their SIEM.
- VPC peering + egress lock-down (no egress from EU region for EU tenants).
- Proofs: SOC2 + ISO27001 certs, pen-test report, architecture diagrams, and sample DPA language for data residency.

Recent interactions / shorthand:
- 2026-02-12 intro call (ff-2026-02-12-3081). Maya + Diego + CTO (Arjun Mehta) + CISO (Leah Wong). CISO: "we need cryptographic separation for EU customers".
- 2026-02-25 technical deep dive with inf...

title: Cascade SupportAPI
body:
POC focus: tool-calling into Zendesk + internal billing API for refunds and escalations. Wants idempotent create/update ticket flow and explicit success receipts for each tool-call (no silent failures).

- CTO Priya Rao: "We need a system that never drops transactions during Black Friday spikes."
- Concern: current webhook-based approach loses events when downstream is at capacity.
- Security: mandatory SAML SSO + org-level audit logs + KMS-managed keys. EU customers require residency; legal flagged model artifact handling for private deployments.
- Latency/Throughput targets: ticket creation < 200ms added latency end-to-end, 6k concurrent agent actions at peak (burst), 99.9% success under load.
- Model preferences: prefer trimmed open models with quantization for cost control; fallback to smaller model for non-sensitive paths.
- Billing: Prefers committed Dedicated capacity if unit e...

title: SurgeGuard CXTech
body: High-volume CX platform focused on telco + fintech verticals. Primary goal: deflect high % of inbound chats with low variable cost. Very cost-sensitive; CFO insist on unit-econ modeling before commit. POC scope narrow: web chat (live customer deflection) + agent assist for escalations. Interested in batching / prefix caching to reduce per-message tokens. Mentioned quantized 4-bit models for baseline and fallback to larger 13B when context complexity > threshold. Security: needs VPC deployment for PII redaction pipeline, SSO + audit logs. Quote from head of ops: "We need to keep human seats flat while traffic grows 2-3x this summer — if costs spike we won't buy."

body: Mid-market product team testing hosted API for 6-week trial. Started with chat + embeddings in product search; moved to stress tests last week. title: BillMinder Systems
body:
Top-line: mid-market billing SaaS (B2B) scaling support operations. Evaluating Redwood Hosted API as an initial POC to replace brittle webhook/rule stacks for subscription support agents. Plan: 2-week POC on Hosted API focusing on streaming chat latency and reliable function/tool-calling into Stripe sandbox. If POC proves latency and cost targets, move to Dedicated for predictable monthly throughput.

Key technical requirements (customer language / AE notes):
- Workload: customer support agents handling billing/subscription flows (refunds, invoice re-creation, plan changes). Heavy use of tool-calling to Stripe/Zuora sandbox; outputs must be strict JSON for automated downstream processing.
- Latency SLO: time-to-first-byte (streaming start) < 200ms for agent-facing flows; end-to-end JSON payload generation for tool call within 500ms median under 30 concurrent sessions.
- Throughput: ex...

title: Helix Financial Insights
body:
Summary: late-stage enterprise fintech account. Primary use: production reranking for trading signals + customer support embeddings. They ran a 6-week POC on Dedicated (trial GPUs). Performance: latency ~45ms median (meets target), throughput OK, but cost per 1M tokens ~30% above their target. Security: heavy emphasis on KMS/HSM integration, formal audit logs, and EU data residency for a subset of data. Security team flagged lack of FIPS-certified KMS integration in our private offering — major blocker.

Competition: Replicate won on pricing model (predictable managed infra + usage tiers) and prebuilt connectors to their trade compliance pipeline. CTO quote: "Replicate's managed infra and turnkey connectors saved our infra team 3 months — we can't justify building it now." Also evaluated Together for model governance; still preferred Replicate.

Internal dynamics: primary champion (Sr...

title: Sparrowlane Solutions
body:
CRM notes (shorthand, from AE/SE):
- Company: mid-market CX platform, 450 employees, heavy chat + search use.
- Primary ask: move quickly from hosted api to dedicated for predictability once perf locked. want a clear cost model.
- Workload: chat agents for customer support (p95 latency target ~ <200ms), batch embedding jobs for nightly search index, reranking for CSAT follow-ups.
- Throughput target: steady-state 60-100 reqs/s for chat at peak; embeddings batch ~25k docs/night.

title: Sunset Brook Labs
body:
Topline: mid-market SaaS (product analytics) building in-app assistant + semantic search. Team = product + infra + legal. Wants to start on Hosted API to iterate quickly, then move to Dedicated Reserved capacity for predictable costs & isolation. Key asks: (1) EU-only residency for customer data and logs; (2) SSO (SAML) + SOC2 evidence; (3) deterministic latency targets for EU end users (p95 < 250ms for chat), ability to failover to fallback model in other region if capacity constrained.

CRM-style notes / call paraphrases: - 'We can't send identifiable logs outside EU until legal signs off' — Head of Legal. - 'Need a clear delta on cost vs hosted; show how dedicated amortizes at 6-9 months' — Head of Product. - 'Concern about APAC customers — will routing them through EU add >100ms?' — Infra eng.

POC scope: product-search + reranking for web-app; 3-week POC running keys in eu-west r...

title: ClarityMesh
body:
Background: ClarityMesh is a mid-market SaaS vendor that powers searchable knowledge bases for B2B SaaS customers (support docs, onboarding guides, internal KB). They operate multi-tenant SaaS with a high read volume on KB search and expect peak QPS for search reranking up to ~500 qps across regions.

Primary ask: Hosted embeddings + reranking pipeline. Need low-latency top-K retrieval and a tuned reranker to push relevant KB passages to the assistant. Looking for predictable per-query economics at scale — current internal run-rate on their homegrown vector stack is getting expensive in CPU/GPU ops, want to offload to Redwood if cost/latency wins.

Indexing profile: ~12M total passages today (regularly deduped), incremental ingest cadence: hourly deltas (~10k docs/hr), retention for support docs 3+ years. Currently use 1536-d embeddings, exploring 1024 quantized models for cost. Want ...

title: AzureBloom CloudAssist
body:
Inbound SMB lead. self-serve sign-up, exploring hosted API for chat + recommendations.
Primary ask: "how to pick between Llama/Mistral/Qwen variants for our support use-case" - care about accuracy on product Q&A and hallucination rates.
Throughput/latency targets: target 10-30 qps steady, 80 qps short bursts; median p95 latency target <300ms for responses under 500 tokens.
Cost sensitivity: want to keep monthly inference spend < $3k during pilot. asking for per-1k-token cost estimates for 7B vs 13B hosted variants.
Model questions noted on call: quantized models ok? tradeoffs for int8/in4 vs fp16 on hallucination and response quality; memory and cold-start effects.
They run many multi-turn conversations with context ~3-5 turns; interested in prefix caching and KV reuse - asked if Redwood supports automatic KV cache across sessions.
Asked about fallback policies: prefer route to cheape...

Primary ask: "how to pick between Llama/Mistral/Qwen variants for our support use-case" - care about accuracy on product Q&A and hallucination rates.
Throughput/latency targets: target 10-30 qps steady, 80 qps short bursts; median p95 latency target <300ms for responses under 500 tokens.
Cost sensitivity: want to keep monthly inference spend < $3k during pilot. asking for per-1k-token cost estimates for 7B vs 13B hosted variants.
Model questions noted on call: quantized models ok? tradeoffs for int8/in4 vs fp16 on hallucination and response quality; memory and cold-start effects.
They run many multi-turn conversations with context ~3-5 turns; interested in prefix caching and KV reuse - asked if Redwood supports automatic KV cache across sessions.
Asked about fallback policies: prefer route to cheape...

title: ChatFlo Stack
body:
Inbound from product-led free trial signup — emailed signup form to team on 2026-02-25.
Founder: "Maya Rao" — CTO/early engineer. Building lightweight chat assistant embedded in their CRM product.
Primary ask: low-latency streaming for in-app assistant; want token streaming to end-user UI (websocket/HTTP chunking).
Target SLO: p95 < 300ms for single-turn responses and start-of-stream under 150ms. Concerned about cold-starts and cost for 3xx daily active users.
Prototype uses 3k token prompts with short replies ~40-120 tokens; heavy concurrent short chats during business hours.
Prefer hosted API for fast time-to-value. Low appetite for dedicated infra now; may revisit at 50k MAU.
Security: company uses Okta SSO, will require SAML configuration and basic audit logs (retention 90d).
Quote from call: "We need streaming that feels realtime — latency kills UX. If we can get sub-200ms start,...

title: BeaconFlow Labs
body:
Account summary: BeaconFlow Labs is a mid-market B2B product company building an in-app onboarding copilot (guided flows + micro-tips). Evaluating Redwood Hosted API first to prove latency/cost, then committed interest in Dedicated for predictable throughput and data isolation.

Key constraints / asks: 
- Latency SLOs: p50 target ~120-200ms, p95 target <500ms for interactive onboarding steps; streaming + function-calls must be consistent (users notice variance).
- Cost controls: token cost caps per onboarding session (target <= $0.15/session initially), need per-route token caps and throttles; interested in Optimize recommendations (batching/caching) to reduce unit cost.
- Tooling: must support function/tool calling to internal product metadata + CRM (tokenized access), streaming partial responses to UI while tool calls resolve.
- Security: SSO/SAML, SOC2 posture important, KMS for ke...

title: Cedarcrest Signalworks
body:
POC summary and ask:
- Trial: 2-week Hosted API trial (Jan 22–Feb 5) running three endpoints: chat assistant (primary), embeddings for search, short reranking calls.
- Observed production-like traffic during trial window (simulated by internal load test + beta customers): avg QPS ~3.2 across endpoints, peak observed bursts up to ~12 QPS for ~3 minutes.
- Token mix (trial sample): chat = 68% of tokens, embeddings = 22%, rerank = 10%. Average tokens per chat request ~420 (prompt+response). Embedding requests use 512-d vectors, small token payloads but high per-request rate. Total tokens/day (observed) ~220k; projected steady-state tokens/day at GA ~600k–1.2M depending on feature rollout.
- Concurrency/latency targets: product requires p95 generation latency <300ms for interactive assistant (short prompts), acceptable p95 up to 750ms for longer context. Throughput target for GA: sustaine...

title: AtlasEcho Solutions
body:
Background: mid-market product analytics SaaS; building assistant for product managers + support agents.
Priority: low-latency streaming chat for guided workflows and tool-calling to execute actions (calendar, tickets).
"We need sub-100ms token stream tail for 1k-context typical support flow" — PM quote during 10/18 demo.
Preference: start on Hosted API to iterate quickly; move to Dedicated (reserved) once steady 24/7 throughput is clear.
Security: Okta SSO required (SAML), SOC2 Type II evidence required for procurement, KMS integration desired for key control.
Data handling ask: redact PII from logs (names, emails, credit-card fragments) before stored traces; retention windows: 30d for raw traces, 365d for aggregated metrics.
POC scope: 3-week sandbox — integrate streaming chat, tool-calling hooks, measure p95 latency and cost per 1k tokens. Demo dataset: 1200 sample tickets + synthe...

title: Peninsula Aurora Labs
body:
Stage: procurement. Mid-market product analytics SaaS evaluating Hosted API first (POC in Dec) then Dedicated for predictable unit costs. Primary contact: CTO Marco Tan; Procurement lead: Elaine Park. Use-case: user-facing chat assistant (low-latency, 100-300 RPS spikes), embeddings for semantic search across product docs, reranking on search results.

Key technical/contract asks (from calls):
- Dedicated commit options: want two proposals: 1) committed GPU pool sized for 50k-100k tokens/day baseline OR 2) QPS-based commit (40 sustained r/s) with 3-month ramp.
- Burst: must support 2x baseline for 2 hours/day (expected during feature releases) — ask: is burst free within commit or capped?
- Overage: prefer predictable overage (not pay-as-you-go variable). Proposed Redwood draft: 20% overage cap at 1.5x unit price for first 6 months, then review.
- Ramp schedule: 3-month ramp to full c...

title: SparrowCrest InstantFlow
body:
- Lead came from self-serve trial; started with hosted API (api-key)
- Primary complaint: "random latency spikes, user-facing timeouts on busy hours" — customer sees 200-800ms typical but 1.8-2.5s spikes, some requests hitting client 10s timeout set in front-end
- Use-case: support chat widget + relevancy search; short prompts + chat history appended (avg 400 tokens/session)
- They send full chat history on each request; SE suspects large context + network RTT to eu-west causing spikes
- Customer quote: "Feels fast most times but when it slows customers drop off — need predictable response times"
- Current model: default hosted model (medium size); open to smaller model for latency-sensitive endpoints
- Cost note: high token churn due to repeated system prompt + unused verbose context; wants guidance to reduce token usage without losing quality
- Security: SOC2 required, audit logs fo...

title: Marigold Murmur Apps
body:
Inbound from signup form — small indie studio building a social chat layer inside a mobile casual game.
Primary ask: low-latency chat generation for NPC assistants + player-to-player chat moderation pipeline.
Quoted from call: "We need <200ms median per-turn latency in NA or the experience feels laggy."
Currently using in-house rule-based moderation; wants to test Redwood's content filters + streaming messages.
Cost sensitivity high — monthly budget ~ $500-$2k initially; wants to validate self-serve before commit.
Traffic pattern: unpredictable peaks during evening hours; bursts up to ~3k concurrent chat clients.
Interested in token batching recommendations and prefix caching to control costs.
Technical: mobile clients on iOS/Android; backend on GCP us-central1; preference for hosted API in us-central region.
Security: basic audit logs and retention controls, no enterprise SSO require...

title: Frugalist CX
body:
Summary: Frugalist CX is a mid-market e-commerce support platform focused on maximizing chat deflection and minimizing per-ticket spend. High-volume, low-margin support environment: ~25k messages/hour currently across NA and EU, expected 3x during holiday spikes. Primary asks: sub-$0.03 cost-per-resolved-ticket, median suggestion latency <300ms for inline agent assist, deterministic behavior during burst windows.

Recent conversations / quotes: 
- "We need to hold costs or this never passes procurement." (Sr. Head of Support)
- "If suggestions take >300ms agents will ignore them — adoption drops off." (Lead Support Ops)
- "Show us a POC that proves 40% chat deflection with < $0.03 ticket cost and comparable NPS"

Technical priorities: batching/caching, aggressive KV prefix cache for common flows, quantized model options (8-bit/4-bit) for both hosted and dedicated, top-k model routing ...

title: NeuroQuill Assist
body:
Summary: NeuroQuill is building an IDE copilot (VS Code + web) focused on extremely snappy inline completions and multi-line streaming snippets. Primary goal is best-in-class developer feel: TTFT target <= 80ms for single-line completions, tail jitter < 25ms at 95th percentile, graceful backpressure for large diff outputs. Interested in Redwood Dedicated for predictable low-latency routing and Optimize for token budget recommendations.

Key requirements / ask: 
- Low-latency streaming (time-to-first-token) instrumentation to correlate client rendering time with server TTFT. Need per-route TTFT and per-model metrics.
- Jitter analysis: want to see distribution (p50/p95/p99) for TTFT and inter-token gaps; must be stable during editor idle vs. burst typing.
- Backpressure handling: ability to signal the client to pause stream when user buffer or local compute is saturated. Prefer built-i...

title: Lodestar Assistive Systems
body:
VP Eng (Hector Alvarez): 'We cannot afford a drop in quality when we move - need prompt parity or we roll back.'
Primary migration plan: start on Redwood Hosted (compat shim translating OpenAI calls), validate prompt parity & metrics, then migrate to Dedicated reserved pool for cost predictability.
POC scope (2 weeks): canonical prompt set (200 prompts) for chat & rerank; embedding similarity tests (50 queries); automated diffing; baseline against OpenAI production outputs.
Success metrics: prompt-parity >= 98% manual acceptance; p95 latency < 300ms for chat (short context); embedding recall within 2% of baseline; token cost reduction target 20-30% post-Dedicated optimizations.
Technical concerns called out: function-calling schema parity, response ordering differences, KV cache warmup on Dedicated, quantized-model regressions for long-tail queries.
Operational asks: rollout plan with...

title: VeloxCraft Labs
body:
Summary: Mid-market SaaS product team. Completed 3-week Hosted API trial; team satisfied with latency and quality. Now evaluating Dedicated for predictable throughput and data isolation. POC goal: validate steady-state QPS, peak concurrency behavior, and token-unit economics tied to UI flows.

Trial findings (hosted API):
- Avg request profile: 0.65 QPS sustained across telemetry during business hours; baseline 12 QPS simulated on demo day.
- Token mix: avg prompt 85 tokens, avg completion 220 tokens; 70% short intents (<=200 tokens), 20% long context retrievals (>=1,500 tokens), 10% embeddings/rerank calls.
- 95th pct latency: 360ms for simple chat paths; 900–1,600ms for long-context generation (acceptable but want <1s most of the time).
- Cost sensitivity: product-led growth; cost per MAU important — customer expects ~30% improvement vs current hosted over scale.

Quotes/paraphrases...

title: Northbridge FinTech Analytics
body:
Summary: Large payments analytics platform; high-risk production workloads (fraud, regulation). Primary interest in Redwood Dedicated with VPC + optional on-prem node for sensitive flows. Evaluation focused on throughput guarantees and per-model tuning.

Workload & targets (from SE discovery):
- Workload types: conversational support agents (chat with history), embeddings for nearline search/reranking, batch reranking for transaction scoring.
- Latency SLO targets: chat p50 < 60ms (short context), p95 < 200ms (8k); for long-context (32k) p95 target relaxed to < 400ms. Reranking/embeddings throughput target: sustained 150-250 QPS, bursts to 400 QPS for 1–3 minutes.
- Availability target: 99.95% platform uptime for dedicated pool; credits model required for missed SLOs.

Model-specific tuning asks:
- Context length: primary models evaluated at 8k and 32k. Need advice on dynamic context ...

title: MezzoQuery Labs
body:
Account snapshot: mid-market e-commerce search SaaS. Core product = storefront product discovery; currently BM25 + light learning-to-rank. Exploring LLM-based reranking for top-10 results.

Recent activity:
- 2026-02-10 discovery call (Samir) — product team + CTO on call. They described current infra: search index on Elastic, candidate generation at 250 qps, currently rerank offline with light GBDT. Interested in replacing offline heavy feature pipeline with LLM reranker for better long-tail relevance. (fireflies ff_20260210_mezzoquery_intro)
- 2026-03-02 POC kickoff with Redwood hosted API. Agreed scope: implement rerank endpoint that consumes topK candidates and returns score + prefer: structured reason tags. Validate accuracy uplift on holdout eval set (MRR and NDCG). (fireflies ff_20260302_poc_kickoff)
- 2026-03-04 exchanged pricing worksheet; asked for Dedicated pricing scenarios...

title: BryteBay Solutions
body:
Founder-led call: Samir Patel (CEO) + Head of Eng (Maya) — 35m
Inbound: signed up for free credits, hit ~200k tokens in trial, asked to talk pricing
Primary ask: clear cost per 1M tokens & predictable overages; wants apples-to-apples vs OpenAI/Anthropic
Workload: multichannel support assistant (chat) + vector search for KB (embeddings) + reranking on retrieval results
Latency: target p95 < 350ms for short-chat flows; can tolerate 600-800ms for long retrieval answer composition
Throughput: bursty, avg 20 reqs/s, peak 120 reqs/s during morning support window
Cost sensitivity: high — startup runway conscious, prefers usage-based with caps and alerts
Model preferences: wants ability to switch between cost/tier models (cheaper open LLMs for bulk inference, higher-quality model for escalation paths), asks about Redwood model routing/fallback
Security: public cloud ok; requests SSO for team ...

title: Meadowridge VertexSearch
body:
CRM notes:
- Company snapshot: mid-market B2B SaaS (product search & recommendations).
- Primary contact: Sarah Lin (Head of Product), Eng lead: Martin Cho.
- Use-case details: embeddings-driven semantic search across product catalog + user behavioral signals; nightly ETL creates/upserts ~150k embeddings, real-time ingestion for new items (100-500/day).
- Requirements:
 * Throughput: 5k QPS target for peak search traffic; p95 latency target <100ms for search queries.
 * Batch jobs: nightly window must complete within 2 hours; currently using Airflow + Postgres (pgvector) -> considering switching heavy-indexing to vector DB (Pinecone) for scale.
 * Integration: need connectors for Pinecone and an option to keep warm caches in Redis; also must support pgvector fallback.
 * Model preferences: prefer open/Redwood-verified embeddings models (256-1024 dims) to avoid vendor lock; evaluating ...

 * Model preferences: prefer open/Redwood-verified embeddings models (256-1024 dims) to avoid vendor lock; evaluating ...

title: Greystone Docsprint
body:
Lead profile: small legal-tech startup building a lawyer-facing doc search assistant. Inbound → read Redwood blog on RAG. Wants hosted API (self-serve) — prefers not to manage infra.

Highlights from discovery call:
- Dataset: ~50k internal docs (contracts, briefs, templates). Mostly text, some scanned PDFs (OCR done).
- Primary workload: semantic search + short generative follow-ups (answer synthesis), heavy reliance on embeddings + reranking.
- Latency target: 150-300ms for retrieval path; can accept higher latency for final long-form synthesis.
- Throughput: low (5-15 qps), cost sensitivity high — target <$0.02/query expressed repeatedly.
- Preferred vector stores: experimenting with pgvector (Supabase) and Pinecone for managed option. Wants examples for both.
- Routing/fallback: wants simple policy — prefer original high-quality model when available, fallback to smaller cheaper mo...

title: Boreal Cortex Archives
body:
Primary contact: Maya Srinivasan (Head of Search Engineering) — very cost conscious, wants unit economics by day 10 of POC.
"We need sub-ms lookup for hot assets during prime-time events" — paraphrase from Maya on 2026-01 call.
Wants deterministic fallbacks: prefer local private inference cluster for PII-sensitive queries
Interested in Redwood Optimize suggestions: batching defaults, per-model quant profiles, cache TTL heuristics
Asked: can Dedicated autoscale to handle 2x traffic spikes w/o hitting higher egress? (SEC/Arch to confirm)
Long docs (video transcripts) cause vector bloat; proposal to pre-chunk + canonicalize to reduce cardinality
Procurement: prefers annual commit with step-down SLA credits; legal requested sample DPA and KMS binding language
Metrics they will track: cost/query, p99 latency (search + rerank), vector storage $/GB, recall@10 on canonical evaluation set
Stub...
