title: Plumridge Inbound AI
body:
Inbound SMB lead — e-commerce CX team. Primary ask: how to get Hosted API into production with strict cost guardrails.

Context / pain:
- Monthly chat volume projected ~150k sessions (avg. 120 tokens/session) but cost modelling unclear.
- Heavy cost sensitivity: CFO flagged potential cloud bill spike; wants per-session below ~$0.02 (net token cost target).
- Engineering prefers self-serve Hosted API (fast integration) — not ready for Dedicated or Private.

Technical requirements (as told by Eng lead):
- Workloads: chat-first support bot + product snippet summarization + light personalization embeddings for recommendations.
- Latency target: 150-300ms tail for single-turn chat (consumer-facing). Throughput: 8-12 qps peak during campaigns.
- Model preference: open/quantized family if possible (lower cost), but must match quality bar for CS responses.
- Routing/fallback: want cheap fallb...

title: Veridian Support Labs
body:
Lead: Veridian operates an omnichannel retail ops platform. Primary ask: reduce AHT and shrink ticket backlog via automated first-touch + agent assist. Key constraints: EU data residency for EU customers, strict retention policy for PII, SSO/SAML, and ability to run POC inside VPC.

POC goals (customer language): "Show me accurate citations back to KB, keep answers fresh within 24h, and handle 2x to 3x traffic spikes without tail latency blowups." They emphasized multi-source retrieval — Zendesk tickets, Confluence docs, product changelogs, and a SQL metadata store.

Technical targets (customer): 95th-pctl latency <250ms for agent assist responses, end-to-end ticket summary in <700ms, throughput ~400 req/s baseline with autoscaling headroom to 1k/s for peaks. Cost sensitivity: willing to commit to reserved capacity if we can show 30-40% unit-cost savings vs current managed endpoints.
...

title: Helio Health
body:
Account context
- Helio Health evaluating Redwood Dedicated for regulated workloads (PHI-adjacent workflows, but they state prompts are de-identified). Strong security posture; wants SOC2 evidence, SSO, and audit trails.
- Buyer personas: Security Ops + Platform Engineering. They are pragmatic about beta limitations but want crisp expectations around delivery guarantees and backfill.

SIEM / audit export beta requirements (Helio-specific)
- Destinations:
 - Datadog Logs intake (primary): HTTPS intake, API key managed by Helio. Wants consistent tags/facets: env, workspace/tenant, category, action, actor_type.
 - S3 (secondary/archive): cross-account bucket, SSE-KMS required, partitioning by date/hour preferred for lifecycle policies.
- Filtering:
 - Initial allowlist: auth/admin/config changes + key management + export config changes + model/endpoint changes. Explicitly wants to exclud...

title: Montclair AI Systems
body:
Key account summary:
- Customer since 2023 (started on hosted API).
- Primary product owner: VP of ML Ops (Elena Markovic).
- Use: conversational travel assistant (chat), fast embeddings for search, reranking on booking results.
- Pain: token cost variability, intermittent tail latency during EU holiday peaks, no APAC region today -> poor local latency.
- Asked for: Dedicated pool for steady throughput (SLO 100ms p50 chat), autoscaling for seasonal peaks, Optimize recommendations to reduce per-token spend via batching + cache.
- Security: must support SSO (SAML), KMS for keys, audit logs retained 1 year, and APAC data residency for certain PII.
- Quote from Elena on 2026-02-12 call: "We need predictable cost and a path to deploy closer to APAC customers — otherwise conversion drops."
- Wants a 6-week Optimize pilot to demonstrate 20-30% unit cost reduction before committing to Dedicat...

title: PopShift Support Labs
body:
Summary: PopShift builds conversational automation for mid-size ecommerce platforms. Primary goal: reduce live agent load by maximizing automated deflection while keeping agent-assist quality high. They are extremely cost-sensitive — focused on unit economics for high-volume chat (12M msgs/mo).

Key requirements (shorthand):
- workload: high-volume short-chat + occasional long context threads; mix of templated replies + dynamic slot-filling calls to internal tools
- throughput: sustain 800-1,200 rps in peaks; 50ms-200ms target for p50, p95 < 700ms, p99 goal < 1.5s
- reliability: graceful degradation; fallbacks to smaller/cheaper model variants during capacity pressure
- cost: aggressive targets, want pricing scenarios for dedicated reserved capacity + burst to hosted API
- quality: maintain >90% automated resolution for top 25 intents; ticket summarization accuracy for NPS tracking
- ...

title: SunRidge Inferkit Solutions
body:
- Mid-market SaaS (product analytics + in-app help), product-led growth.
- Evaluated Hosted API first: 3-week PoC (Jan 28 - Feb 18). Quick start successful, integrated with front-end via SDK + streaming.
- PoC findings: median latency ~85ms per token (small prompt), p50 tail for multi-turn chat ~120-150ms; under load 200-350ms. Throughput requirement: sustained 25-35 QPS during peak; burst spikes to ~80 QPS for launches.
- Cost sensitivity: high. Want unit economics visibility. Explicit ask: show cost vs token mix, and optimizations (batching, prefix cache) that reduce cost by >=25%.
- Primary use: hosted real-time chat assistant for customers + nightly reranking/embeddings for search.
- Model preferences: open models ok if quality matches; prefers Redwood to manage model variants and fallbacks.
- Security: must have SSO (SAML), SOC2 compliance evidence, audit logs for tokens, KMS int...

title: CompileMate
body:
Inbound from CTO (Ethan Park) — building a VS Code copilot extension used by their internal dev teams and beta customers.
Primary ask: streaming completions with character-level low-latency (dev experience: inline suggestions as you type) — p99 target < 120ms for local devs in NA.
Concern: current hosted vendors throttle per-token and per-connection; they need predictable per-developer rate limits and team quotas to avoid noisy neighbors when 200+ devs sync heavy repos.
Model/version pinning is a hard requirement: QA wants deterministic suggestions with a pinned model for productized features. Also need automated fallback to a quantized model when latency spikes or cost thresholds hit.
Auth & SSO: SAML required, SCIM for user provisioning desirable. IT asked about session lifetime, token refresh flow for long-running editor sessions (VS Code keeps sockets open).
Security: must keep au...

title: Ashmont Security Insights
body:
Current state: stalled after detailed discovery. Went dark 2026-02-20 after procurement question.

Key context:
- Use case: internal threat-detection reranking + summarization of large incident logs; also plan to surface short contextual summaries for support agents.
- Latency/throughput: need 10ms median token latency for live alerts and sustained 120 tps for batch reranking windows. Cost sensitivity moderate — prefer predictable committed pricing.
- Model prefs: open Llama-family variants acceptable if quantized; want fallback to smaller distilled models in low-cost tier.

Security/Procurement path:
- Heavy emphasis on data residency and KMS. Security team requested audit log examples and HSM/KMS integration patterns.
- ISO requirement is a checkbox for their vendor shortlist. SOC2 also expected but ISO was flagged as critical.
- Asked for full network topology for Private (VPC) dep...

title: CinderPath Searchify
body:
Inbound: signed up via blog post on RAG best practices, created org in self-serve portal 2026-02-26.
Primary product: customer-facing doc search for SMB SaaS customers (help center + knowledge base).
Initial ask: embeddings + reranking pipeline — want to use Redwood Hosted API for embeddings and a small instruction-tuned reranker model for score refinement.
Technical constraints: target p95 retrieval+rerank latency <= 250ms; expected steady QPS 3-6; cold-start ingestion ~150k short docs (KB articles).
Cost sensitivity: small monthly budget, looking to validate <$0.02/query (including vector DB egress and rerank cost).
Model preferences: open models preferred (smaller embed dims ok), but will evaluate Redwood-verified embeddings first. Interested in 1536 or 1024-dim options and batching recommendations.
Security: SOC2 compliance required by procurement; audit logs for API calls helpful...

title: Larkspur Payments
body:
Inbound via docs: self-serve sign-up + free credits used — signed up 2026-02-15.
CEO (Ari Park) on intro call: 'Need a low-friction hosted path first; want SOC2 proof before contract.'
Primary use: AI chat for merchants (support triage) + embeddings for transaction/receipt search + reranking for fraud signals.
Security: Okta SAML planned, SOC2 Type II in progress (auditor scheduled for late Mar). Wants audit logs and KMS options listed in pack.
Cost sensitivity: SMB budget. Pilot target = $1k-$2k/month (approx 10k tokens/month), scale to $5k-$15k as volume grows.
VPC curiosity: product team asked about Private/VPC but says 'not ready to self-host; want hosted with VPC option later'.
Technical note from CTO (J. Lin): 'P90 latency for chat should be under ~300ms. Embedding calls should be <100ms ideally.'

title: Flickermoss Chatly
body:
Inbound via self-serve signup (trial credits used). Found via blog post on in-app assistants. Lightweight startup building a B2B SaaS in-app chat widget for SMBs (help center + contextual product tips).

- First contact: 2026-02-25 (signup + trial). 120k tokens used first week; most activity from web widgets.
- 2026-03-05 discovery call (FF transcript id ff_20260305_8421). AE demo'd hosted API + streaming; SE joined to discuss latency tuning. Quote from founder: "users abandon replies >300ms — need near-instant streaming".
- Primary technical ask: streaming responses with partial tokens delivered ASAP; p95 latency target 150-250ms for short exchanges (<= 100 tokens). Also needs graceful fallback to smaller models during burst.
- Cost sensitivity: startup with seed funding, wants to keep per-conversation token cost low; interested in batching/caching recommendations.
- Security: basic ...

title: WardenBridge Archive Solutions
body: High-security gov customer. Procurement-heavy, long procurement windows. Wants on-prem air-gapped deployment or private VPC with HSM and strict retention controls. POC scoped to 3 months. AE has relationship via prior org. Quote from CISO: "cannot accept exfil risk; need full offline audit trail and FIPS modules". SE feedback: heavy emphasis on reranker reproducibility and deterministic embeddings across quantized variants. Likes Redwood runtime's kernel selection idea but needs IL5 proof points. Drive links above: security FAQ + HW spec. Short-form: POC -> security review -> SOW -> procurement (GFE servers).

title: ThoughtMesh IDE Labs
body:
Product: JetBrains plugin (IntelliJ/PyCharm) that ships a local indexer + remote inference fallback.
Primary ask: low-latency streaming completions (sub-50ms for local cached completions, <200ms for remote) and model/version pinning so QA can reproduce completions.
Indexing: indexer runs locally (user machine) and optionally uploads hashed embeddings to private VPC for cross-user search — customer must control retention and residency.
Quote from CTO: "We need deterministic completions for codegen CI checks — pin model and tokenizer versions, and lock quantization profile."
Security: will need SSO/SAML, audit logs for inference requests, KMS integration for model keys, and ability to run Redwood control plane in their VPC.
Cost sensitivity: moderate — willing to pay for predictable latency and good developer UX; wants recommendations from Redwood Optimize (batching vs caching tradeoffs...

title: MarketWeave Relevancy Hub
body:
Use-case summary: replace brittle keyword search w/ RAG-style semantic layer + reranker on product page and search results. Focus: lower cost-per-query during holiday spikes while preserving top-of-funnel relevance.
Traffic profile: baseline QPS ~400, seasonal peak ~8-10k QPS (holiday windows). Bursts often 4-6x baseline for 2-6 hour windows.
Indexing scale: planning 30-60M active SKUs (variants included). Retain 90 days of embedding deltas for freshness; full reindex monthly.
Latency targets: p50 < 80ms for embeddings lookup + rerank; p95 <= 250ms end-to-end for search queries under normal load.
Cost sensitivity: CFO requires modeled $/query at 1k/5k/10k QPS scenarios; target blended $0.0008-$0.002 per query for search+rerank.
Model preferences: open checkpoints (Llama2-family / Falcon variants) or Redwood-curated 70B opt for reranker; willing to accept quantized variants if accuracy...

title: Tilted Anchor Platforms
body:
Summary: Tilted Anchor is a mid-market product analytics SaaS building an in-app assistant (chat) + semantic search layer. Started with Redwood Hosted API to validate quality and integration. Post-trial they want a Dedicated plan for steady traffic and predictable unit costs. POC takeaways and ask:
- Workload mix: primary chat (user-facing assistant) ~65% of calls, embeddings for search ~25%, reranking/other endpoints ~10%.
- Observed traffic during 2-week hosted trial: baseline sustained QPS ~12, typical working-hour spikes to ~75-95 QPS during customer release windows.
- Token mix observed (hosted trial telemetry): ~60% short responses (<64 tokens), ~30% medium responses (64-512 tokens), ~10% long responses (>512 tokens). Avg tokens per chat response ~130 tokens. Embeddings input avg ~480 tokens (chunked docs).
- Daily token volume (trial extrapolated): ~9M tokens/day typical, peak ...

title: Ironclad CloudWorks
body:
Customer priority: full Private (VPC) deploy inside AWS account w/ strict egress controls.
Encryption posture asks (detailed):
- In-transit: mutual TLS enforced for all inter-service traffic; client certs stored in customer CA; Redwood must support mTLS termination in customer subnet or present client cert to internal services.
- At-rest: must be AES-256 encrypted, all model KV caches and user-provided artifacts stored on EBS with encryption-by-default; DB snapshots encrypted.
- Key management: BYOK required. Customer uses AWS KMS with CloudHSM-backed CMKs and requires Redwood to integrate via Grant/Encrypt APIs without exporting key material.
- Key rotation: policy = automatic rotation every 90 days; requirement to demonstrate zero-downtime key rollover for ongoing KV cache and token decrypts; rotation flow must be documented and validated in POC.
- Secrets management: customer uses ...

title: HarborLynx Platforms
body:
Inbound from product org. Pilot on Hosted API completed 2/05 — optimistic on latency, mixed on cost. Product lead (CTO Marco Silva) said: "Hosted latency is fine for user flows but cost/unit is high at 1M tokens/mo scale." Wants move-to-Dedicated analysis. Short bullets: 
- P0: in-app chat + document search in single service
- P1: reranking search results for commerce site
- Security: must support company KMS + SAML SSO
- Data residency: US only; some EU later
- Fallback policy desired: hosted -> dedicated -> smaller-quantized on heavy load

title: Prismark Realtime IO
body: High-performance game studio building live NPC personalization + in-game chat moderation. CTO (Rana) is very latency-focused: "sub-50ms 95th tail for short chat calls". Cost is a big factor - 24/7 inference for global player base. Interested in batching/caching to reduce cost but cannot increase tail latency. Wants deterministic routing: EU players -> EU, fallback to smaller quantized model when capacity saturated. Security: must support SSO/SAML, customer-managed KMS, and per-request audit logs. Quoted: 'We will tolerate small accuracy hit for 4x cost reduction but not >8% drop in conversational quality.'

title: GoldenMarsh SaaSWorks
body:
Context: mid-market product analytics SaaS; building in-app assistant + search. Running Hosted API trial to validate UX and rough cost before committing to Dedicated. Team: Product Eng (5), ML infra (2), Security (1), Finance/Procurement involved for pricing.

Hosted API trial findings (observed):
- Avg QPS during demo traffic: 9.2 QPS (baseline test flows)
- 95th percentile burst QPS observed: 68 QPS (concurrent users during Friday peak tests)
- Measured concurrency on service: typical 12 concurrent streams, stress to 42 in replay tests
- Token mix per request (trial sample): ~65 prompt tokens / ~145 generated tokens => ~210 tokens total (prompt:gen ≈ 30:70)
- Embeddings traffic: intermittent batched jobs, ~2k embedding calls/day in trial; small token footprint but high QPS in short windows (bursty).
- Latency targets: target p95 <= 350ms for chat small responses; current hosted p95 ...

title: Velora Surfacing
body:
- Inbound via trial. Product team: Elena V (Sr PM), 2 search engs, 1 data scientist.
- Primary pain: current hybrid BM25+vector pipeline returns noisy top results; want reranker to reorder by business signals (clicks, recency, price).
- Preferred flow: start Hosted API for iteration, move to Dedicated for predictable latency/cost after POC.
- Reranking asks: accept candidate list + structured features; offline scoring for nightly re-ranks; support feature hashes + small dense vectors per candidate.
- Latency targets: 50ms p95 for front-door rerank; 200ms for batch/offline scoring SLA not strict but cost-sensitive. Quote: "predictable tail latency > peak throughput" (Elena).
- Security: SSO (SAML), audit logs, KMS for key wrapping, US data residency required.
- POC plan: Velora to deliver eval set (~50k queries with labels), Redwood to run hosted rerank baseline + 2 variants (transform...

title: Windward Ledgerly
body:
Lead profile: early-stage fintech (series A, stealth payments/reconciliation product). Using hosted API to prototype chat assistant and embeddings-based transaction search. Security is a gating factor — SOC2 docs and SSO/IdP are early asks. VPC/on-prem curiosity flagged for long-term (9-12mo), but starting hosted.

Use-case detail: - Chat: customer support assistant for merchant support reps (context window ~800 tokens typical). - Embeddings: fast vector search across transaction metadata for reconciliation and dispute triage. - Reranking: lightweight model to rerank transaction anomaly candidates from heuristics.

Tech targets: p50 latency <150ms for short chat replies, p95 <350ms; embedding vector response <200ms; concurrency 15-25; cost sensitivity moderate (startup budget), expects clear per-token billing and quota alerts. Prefers smaller open models if quality comparable, but wil...

title: Iron Harbor Critical Ops
body:
Summary:
- DoD-facing contact center operator supporting critical comms (emergency dispatch, infra ops).
- Must deploy in completely air-gapped on-prem enclave maintained by customer SOC. No egress to public internet allowed; outbound updates require packaged media and manual approval.
- Primary features wanted: agent assist (real-time suggestions + canned responses), ticket summarization (post-call TL;DR), tool-calling integration (RA/TA to call internal CRM/dispatch tools), offline reranking for search across document stores, and automated playbook triggers for high-severity incidents.

Key constraints/requirements:
- Security: FIPS 140-2 HSM required for key management, SOC2+ISO27001 posture, detailed audit logs retained 7+ years, SIEM integration (Splunk), SSO via SAML and RBAC.
- Compliance: anticipating IL4/DoD STIG checks; vendor to provide SBOM, signed supply chain attestation...

title: Thunderbloom SaaSworks
body:
Summary: mid-market product analytics SaaS. Started with Redwood hosted API trial (Q4/2025) to validate integration and latency. Positive functional results; now moving to Dedicated sizing + pricing.

Hosted trial notes:
- Trial window: 2025-12-01 -> 2026-02-28. Integrated via SDK (server-side) and one client-side embed for demo.
- Workloads run: primary = real-time support chat assistant; secondary = document search (embeddings) for knowledge base; occasional reranking for recommendations.
- Integration comment from PM: "responses were better than previous endpoint, latency OK for MVP but need firm p99 SLOs".
- Security: asked for SSO/SAML + audit logs, KMS for key management, paper trail for data residency in US and EU. Legal did initial questionnaire 2026-02-20.

Traffic & token observations (hosted trial):
- Monthly active end-users (prod-like pilot): ~11k.
- Average daily active:...

title: LanternLoop Diagnostics
body:
Founder-led account. Maya Brooks (CEO) is the primary technical decision-maker; engineering lead Tom Hsieh joins on calls.
- Use case: customer support automation for mid-tail e-commerce merchants (automated replies, triage, short summaries).
- Current setup: prototyping with OpenAI (gpt-4o-lite) via prompt templates; costs spiked on high-concurrency weekends.
- Wants: clear per-1k token pricing, predictable monthly bill, and simple way to self-serve.
- Quote from call: "If Redwood can be within ~15% of our OpenAI unit cost and give better latency guarantees, we're willing to switch for production."
- Concern: founder expects granular SKU + conversion examples (e.g., 10k tickets/month => tokens burned).
- Notes: prefers public cloud (AWS us-east) for simplicity; not asking for Private/Dedicated yet.
- Elastic burst and latency: wants 95th percentile token latency for 32/128 token flow...

title: ShipmentWise Assist
body: Mid-market e‑commerce platform that handles post-purchase flows for ~350 merchants. Primary ask: conversational returns & shipping assistant that can handle high concurrency during promotions. Priority is strict p95 latency (target <= 300ms for agent responses in streaming mode) during peak season; cost-sensitive about per-token spend at scale. Interested in Hosted API first for fast POC, then Dedicated for predictable latency and data isolation. SE notes: want to test tool-calling connecting to WMS and Order DB; need KV cache warm-up strategy for prefills. Sales notes: VP Product said 'can't have >300ms p95 during holiday peaks' and Procurement flagged data residency requirements for EU merchants. Security doing SOC2 mapping; legal flagged retention and DPA clauses. Next: run 72-hr holiday traffic replay POC on Hosted with streaming enabled, gather p95 telemetry, then sizing call for...

title: Emberfield KnowledgeFlux
body:
Quick CRM notes / call snippets:
- Business: Emberfield builds a knowledge-layer SaaS used by ~300 mid-market customers for in-app help & support automation.
- Current stack: Elasticsearch + FAISS for embeddings, custom cross-encoder on CPU for rerank (high cost, unpredictable tail latency).
- Problem: want to reduce per-query cost and tighten p95 latency at scale (peak traffic: ~600 QPS, avg 200 QPS). Index size currently ~350k canonical docs (mixed product docs + transcripts) and growing ~30%/yr.
- Desired architecture: hosted vector search (ANN) + Redwood-hosted reranker (cross-encoder) with configurable fallback to lightweight scoring. Goal: p95 end-to-end rerank+retrieve <200ms, target median <120ms.
- Cost targets: business wants cost-per-query (embeddings + rerank) <= $0.005 on average; willing to trade small latency increase for 2x cost reduction vs current infra.
- Routing/fa...

title: Azurefen EmbedWorks
body:
Snapshot notes and call fragments:
- Customer profile: mid-market SaaS focused on B2B product analytics (search across docs, changelogs, support notes).
- Primary drivers: improve search relevance and surface related product docs via semantic embeddings; reduce cost per query at scale.
- Quote from CTO: 'We need sub-150ms median for question -> top-K; batch jobs can be slower but cost must be predictable.'
- Product lead emphasized: 'Nightly reindex is fine for bulk, but hot updates need <15m freshness for new release notes.'
- They began with Hosted API to iterate quickly; now assessing Dedicated because of predictable throughput and VPC controls.
- Security: SOC2 yes, SSO/SAML mandatory, KMS for key control, audit logs for query provenance, potential EU residency for a subset of customer data.
- Risk: index growth (expected to reach 12M vectors in 9 months) could blow embedding & st...

title: Larkspur Systems
body:
Status: went dark after requirements handed off to compliance/procurement.
Short notes from last calls:
- Evan Liu (Head of Platform): 'need bank-grade audit logs and KMS key separation'
- Security: concerns about cross-tenant telemetry; asked for data residency proof and audit log retention controls.
- Procurement: verbal freeze due to budget re-org (Q1); procurement rep Rosalind P. asked for a consolidated TCO with Dedicated vs Private options.
- Internal champion (lead PM) moved teams mid-Jan; replaced contact is TBD.
AE attempted 3 follow-ups (1/20, 1/28, 2/12) — only auto-replies or silence.
SE recommended a focused one-pager: security checklist + minimal POC scope.
Suggested pilot: 4-week POC, dedicated GPU pool, redacted test data.
Risks: high security bar + no clear sponsor = likely closed-lost if no re-engagement by Mar.
Links: drive deck + security Q/A attached.
Tone: fragme...

title: Saltmarsh CX Labs
body:
2026-02-20 – Inbound sign-up from Saltmarsh CX Labs; trial credits used to run first test prompts. Seen via marketing demo form.
Initial discovery call 2026-02-25 w/ Ava Chen (owner) + Marco (SE). Attendees: CTO Maya Reddy, Head of Ops Jon Park.
Use-case: automate triage and initial resolutions for SMB customers; summarize incoming support threads into short action items and suggested replies. Primary channels: email + Zendesk + Intercom.
Workload shape: high volume short tickets (avg 120 words), expect bursty daytime traffic; target 8-12 qps during peak, median latency budget ~200-300ms per token streaming preferred.
Model preferences: prefer open models for cost control; want Redwood to list recommended quantized variants. Willing to try smaller models for classification/rerank and larger for summary generation.
Feature asks: structured output (JSON schema for ticket fields), tool c...

title: Ironwood Sentry AI
body:
Snapshot from AE/SE interactions and security review. POC never started — fell apart during security and resourcing alignment. Key points:
- 2025-11-04 inbound lead via trade show; initial discovery call (Ava Chen + Diego). Company running camera analytics across 12 facilities, wants near-real-time event classification and operator summarization.
- They insist on private deployment inside their VPC or fully on-prem for 3 'critical' sites. KMS control mandatory; FIPS and export considerations flagged.
- Security sent initial questionnaire (see linked doc). They flagged: no third-party KMS allowed unless HSM integration; 3-year retention + audit export requirements.
- SE calls 2025-12-03 demo + architecture walkthrough. Ironwood liked runtime performance claims but asked for proof of KMS/HSM integration and proof of audit log retention.
- Procurement then announced a company-wide cap on...

title: Marigold LessonLoop
body:
Inbound self-signup from classroom teacher founder. Tried free credits; small proof-of-concept using Hosted API.

Summary / context:
- Product: in-product classroom copilot for K-12 tutoring + teacher assist (lesson summarization, answer grading suggestions, paraphrase for parents).
- Primary technical need: cheap, reliable text generation + realtime moderation of student inputs.
- Current stack: Node/Next.js frontend, Firebase auth; exploring SAML for school district SSO later.
- Latency/throughput: low concurrency (~5-20 reqs/min); interactive responses should feel snappy — target ~300-600ms for short replies (single-turn).
- Cost sensitivity: founder says "we're scrappy — can't absorb high per-token bills." Wants guidance on batching, caching, and sampling smaller models.

Recent activity (timeline):
- 2026-03-01: Self-registered, used free credits to call text-generation endpoint;...

normalized.notes:

Summary / context:
- Product: in-product classroom copilot for K-12 tutoring + teacher assist (lesson summarization, answer grading suggestions, paraphrase for parents).

- Latency/throughput: low concurrency (~5-20 reqs/min); interactive responses should feel snappy — target ~300-600ms for short replies (single-turn).

title: VertoFlux Platforms
body:
Account summary:
- Mid-market product analytics SaaS. Product org (20 engs on ML/platform teams) evaluating Redwood for chat & knowledge features; migrating from a mix of in-house LLM proxies + OpenAI for spikes.
- Started Hosted API trial 2024-11-10 (self-serve onboarding). AE Jordan intro call 11/12, demo with SE Priya 11/20 (Fireflies: ff_8b2c9a34-vertoflux-demo-2024-11-20).
Hosted API trial observations (prod-shadowing + staged traffic):
- 3-week trial window; baseline traffic reproducer attached in Drive.
- Observed avg QPS (chat endpoints): 6.2 QPS during business hours, peak 12 QPS (short burst 17 QPS).
- Concurrency (in-flight tokens): avg 4.5, peak sustained concurrency 9 for 10m windows.
- Token mix: 68% short chat exchanges (~60-120 tokens / request), 22% medium summarization (300-600 tokens), 10% long-report generation (700-1500 tokens).
- Weighted avg tokens/request ~210;...

title: Sunberry Support Co.
body:
SMB ecom (subscription boxes) - support volume: 40-80 msgs/day, peak seasonal spikes
Primary ask: hosted_api (self-serve). want fast onboarding, low ops overhead.
Workload: chat-first (support agent), lightweight summarization of long customer messages, auto-triage to ticketing system.
Latency target: ~300-500ms acceptable for hosted; throughput low, cost-sensitive.
Model preferences: open to smaller open models or Redwood-curated small models; cost > bleeding-edge capability. prefer deterministic output for templated replies.
Key privacy questions raised repeatedly by Head of Ops (Maya):
 - How long are request/response logs retained by default?
 - Is there an opt-out so end-users' prompts are not logged?
 - Are prompts/metadata ever used to train models?
 - Do you provide a delete/eraser API that fully removes historical prompts?
 - Can we configure retention per-tenant (7/30/90 day...

title: Hazelstrand Product Hub
body:
Account summary: mid-market product analytics company building an in-app assistant and semantic search for customers. Primary goal: reduce support load and improve discoverability of product docs. Evaluation path: Hosted API for quick integration, then move to Dedicated if latency/costs justify.

Key requirements / quotes: " need sub-200ms turn for conversational UI at p95, and predictable monthly cost " — product lead. High token volume for embeddings refresh (daily ingestion of release notes + docs).

POC & cost-workshop specifics: will run a 2-week Dedicated POC to validate tail latency and throughput. Requested Redwood to run a cost-optimization workshop covering:
- batching strategies (session grouping vs. dynamic batching)
- prefix/KV caching policies (how to cache repeated system prompts & static docs)
- quantization profiles (want recommendation: FP16 vs 8-bit vs 4-bit for the...

title: Elmcrest Catalyst Works
body:
Summary:
- Mid-market product analytics SaaS. Building guided in-app assistant + enterprise doc-search. Current provider: ProviderX (custom compat layer).
- Goal: migrate to Redwood Hosted for fast time-to-value, then move to Dedicated for predictable throughput & data isolation.
Key asks from Elmcrest:
- Prompt parity with ProviderX (exact or within tolerable delta). CTO: 'We need output parity for core workflows — small wording shifts are OK but benchmarks must pass).'
- Regression detection: automated evals on 12k canonical prompts, token-level diffs, highlight hallucination/capability regressions.
- Compat layer: mapping old API calls -> Redwood tool-calling and stop-sequence semantics; want a short shim so frontend changes are minimal.
- Latency/Throughput: p95 generation < 250ms for short prompts in prod Dedicated; batch reranking throughput 600 rps target for embeddings+rerank ...

title: ApertureCompass Search
body:
CTO Marco: 'Big constraint is predictable unit cost under sustained QPS — can't have 3x cost spike during peak'.
AE: demo feedback — liked Optimize suggestions for batching & caching; asked for concrete quant savings examples.
SE Priya: recommends hybrid reranking (lightweight vector re-rank on GPU, heavier model fallback only on low-confidence items).
Security: wants SSO/SAML integration + proof of SOC2 + KMS/HSM details. retention policy discussion pending.
POC success criteria (agreed): stable p95 rerank latency <150ms at 500 QPS for 1 hour; embedding recall @k=50 > 0.82; cost per effective query <= $0.005.
Notes from pricing sync: APC prefers committed monthly throughput pricing vs purely per-token for predictability. exploring reserved capacity if numbers justify.
Jira RWD-5421: tracking KMS integration sample and encryption-at-rest questions.
Quick wins suggested: enable prefix/...

title: Labwell QueryLane
body:
Inbound from docs search demo on website. Small engineering org (Backend: 2, Fullstack: 3, ML: 1) building an internal knowledge assistant for corporate docs and contracts. Interested primarily in self-serve Hosted API to get started; wants quick POC before committing. Key asks: hosted embeddings + a simple reranking flow (embed -> vector DB -> candidate fetch -> rerank with cross-encoder). Considering Pinecone/Weaviate for vector store but asked if Redwood hosted embeddings and reranking can replace some of that stack.

Call notes (2026-03-01):
- Customer: "Need something that works in days not weeks."
- They have ~250k documents (mix: policies, contracts, support articles). Average doc length long; plan to chunk ~800-1,000 tokens.
- Embedding requirements: prefer 1536-dim if available; want ability to lower precision/quantize for cost.
- Reranking: they expect a top-50 candidate fet...

title: Sparrowbridge Maritime AI
body:
POC started 2024-08-01 (4-week plan) — now stalled. CRM notes: 
- POC scope: embeddings + reranking for manifest/search, chat assist for dispatch ops. Milestones: infra provisioning, model onboarding, integration tests, accuracy/latency eval.
- Initial successes: infra provisioned in Dedicated pod, basic embedding pipeline validated on sample manifests.
- Stalled items: private VPC peering failed; security asked for ISO27001 evidence + HSM/KMS architecture diagrams. Security review opened 2024-09-05, queued with vendor risk team.
- Quote from Head of Infra: "No engineering cycles until end of quarter — Q4 hiring freeze." made clear on 2024-10-03 call.
- Business context: recent procurement freeze after senior leadership reorg. ROI case still positive but finance paused approvals.
- Competitive pressure: selected competitor (hosted-only) offered faster go-live and an easier compliance ...

title: Cedar Sprout Fintech
body:
Inbound from Y Combinator alum founders (pmt rails + dispute automation).
Primary objective: build an embedded doc search + assistant for merchants (customer support first), second-phase: real-time transaction reranking for fraud signals.
Current plan: start on hosted API to iterate quickly; 'VPC-curious' — likely to revisit at Series A or if regulated customers require dedicated network.
Security: asked for SOC2 Type II report and SSO/SAML integration instructions during first demo. Prioritized audit logs and KMS; data residency discussion for EU customers later.
Performance targets: conversational support agents should respond <200ms token-stream start, end-to-end latency for templated replies <400ms under typical load; embeddings throughput target ~10-20 QPS during indexing windows.
Cost sensitivity: seed-stage budget; estimating $5k-20k ARR depending on usage growth. Interested in...

title: Windfall Compute Solutions
body:
Account summary: existing hosted API customer since 2023; currently negotiating renewal + cross-sell to Dedicated and Redwood Optimize.
Primary ask: predictable throughput for reranking and forecast jobs, lower unit cost for heavy embedding/retail catalog workloads.
Workloads: embeddings for product catalog search (~300k reqs/day), reranking for real-time quote engine (target 200 qps), internal ops chat (p95 < 200ms desired).
Performance targets: rerank p95 < 250ms at sustained 160-200 qps during peak; embeddings < 50ms for search requests.
Optimize ask: automated batching/quant suggestions + cache config to target 25-35% cost reduction vs current hosted spend.
Regions: must add eu-west-1 for EU customers (GDPR/data residency). Long-term ask: APAC region for SEA office (SIN/JPN).
Security/legal: SAML SSO + SCIM, CMK/KMS integration, audit log retention policy (7 years for compliance),...

title: Flareline ChatCraft
body:
Inbound SMB lead — approached via signup form; interested in fast self-serve onboarding.
Primary contact: Matt Rivera, CTO (matt@flareline.io) — small engineering org, heavy on product teams.
Use-case: chat support widget + automated ticket triage; want embeddings for KB search + short-form summarization for closed tickets.
Key pain: traffic spikes during marketing pushes and product launches cause 429s from current vendor; customers complain about dropped messages.
Quote: "We can tolerate a few hundred ms extra latency but cannot drop messages — need graceful backoff and visible Retry-After".
Preference: hosted API (self-serve) — wants simple per-team API keys and per-key quotas; low ops overhead.
Cost profile: cost-sensitive — expecting 1M tokens/month if POC scales; wants unit-cost guidance and tips to reduce spend (batching, streaming, cache).
Security: wants SSO for admin portal ...

title: Cypress Grove Support
body:
Early-stage SMB — PLG-led sign-ups historically; this account is self-serve but legal-heavy.
Primary contact: Naomi Park (Head of Product). Secondary: Jordan Lee (Legal).
Naomi: 'We want to avoid model hallucinations in replies; rerank + retrieval is critical.'
Legal: wants list of sub-processors and confirmation of SOC2 Type II coverage for our hosted API environment.
Ravi: recommended starting POC with embeddings + semantic search, then add low-latency chat for escalation flows.
Cost sensitivity flagged: prefer batching/Optimize suggestions to keep per-customer cost predictable.
Data residency: serves some EU SMBs; asked whether hosted API offers EU-region processing and how that maps to DPA clauses.
Asked about sub-processors for logging/analytics and whether Redwood uses cloud KMS or has HSM options for keys.
Customer requested: 1) SOC2 Type II PDF, 2) complete sub-processor CSV, ...

title: Northbridge Silkworks
body:
Lead source: self-serve signup (trial credits used).
Contact: Priya Kapoor (CTO, founding engineer) — dev-first, prefers code examples.
Initial discovery call 2026-02-20 (record: ff_20260220_nb_silk_discussion).

Current situation:
- Building product recommendation + chat help widget for Shopify headless storefront (Next.js/Vercel).
- POC uses LlamaIndex for document search and LangChain for orchestration; currently running small fine-tuned Llama 2 locally for dev.
- Signed up for Redwood hosted API to replace homegrown inference due to GPU ops overhead.

Requirements and constraints (verbatim/notes):
- "Need a drop-in LangChain + Vercel adapter example in TypeScript — Edge-compatible."
- Latency target: 200-400ms p95 for chat responses; willing to sacrifice small token-level latency for cost.
- Embeddings: 20-50 req/s peak during search spikes; want batching recommendations.
- Routin...

title: Emberfield Support Co.
body:
Lead source: inbound via blog post (ticket-summarization + tool-calling). Signed up self-serve trial last week — used Hosted API free credits.

Initial discovery call (2026-02-12) with CEO (Lina Park) + Eng Lead (Omar Ruiz). Quick notes:
- Use-case: automated ticket summarization + structured extraction (priority, product, root_cause) to pre-fill ticket fields and drive triage rules. Also want tool-calling: ability to trigger ticket actions (assign, escalate, add tag) from model output.
- Workload: mostly short tickets (50-300 tokens), 3k–8k msgs/day now, expect 20–30k in 6mo. Latency target: median < 150ms for single-turn summary, 95th < 400ms acceptable. Throughput: bursty (morning spikes). Cost sensitivity: high — SMB budget, wants guidance on batching and prefix caching.
- Model preferences: prefers community/open models; ok with quantized variants. Wants Redwood-hosted variants o...

title: Sparrowbridge Insights
body:
Account background: mid-market analytics SaaS (customer success + insights product). Product team building an agentic assistant that chains search -> plan -> action (open tickets, push metadata). Evaluating Hosted API first for speed of shipping, then Dedicated if we need predictable throughput and lower tail latency.

POC details / technical asks:
- Workload: multi-turn agentic chat (avg session 4-7 tool calls), high concurrency during business hours (~200-400 concurrent agents in prod target).
- Latency SLO: interactive streaming goal = p50 <80ms, p95 <400ms token latency; overall turn time <2s for simple queries.
- Tool-calls: heavy reliance on internal search + ticketing API. Need reliable function-calls with clear retry/backoff semantics and idempotency. "Agent must not hang on tool call" — quote from Head of Product.
- Streaming: want chunked streaming + partial tool outputs, ab...

title: Hummingbird Wellness Studio
body:
Inbound via self-serve signup (trial); founder Clara Patel reached out after playing with API keys. Team = 12 (8 admin/clinicians + 4 eng/ops). Primary goal: deploy a front-end triage chat and summarize intake forms into EHR fields.

Conversation highlights:
- "Founder: 'Need quick triage to reduce admin hours, must avoid storing PHI longer than necessary.'"
- They are NOT asking for Private/VPC yet — want hosted API on public cloud but with strong retention controls and audit logs.
- Concerned about HIPAA-adjacent wording: they will not store PHI intentionally but intake sometimes includes identifiers; ask for redaction options and opt-out of persistent logging.
- Latency needs: acceptable to 200-500ms per response; throughput low (peak ~20 concurrent users). Cost-sensitive — early stage.
- Model preferences: prefer open-base models or small Llama2-70B quantized variants; also open t...

title: Breezefold Labs
body:
Inbound SMB lead — developer-first SaaS (B2B helpdesk). Came in through self-serve signup, using hosted API sandbox. Primary ask: choose an economical model family for chat+embeddings for a support assistant.

Key customer constraints / quotes:
- "We want tight latency for chat (target ~200-300ms p95) but can't pay top-tier pricing — cost sensitivity high."
- "Embeddings must be fast and affordable for nightly reindexing (thousands docs) — prefer ~100ms per request on hosted."
- Multi-model routing interest: cheaper base model for standard queries + higher-quality model for escalations.

Technical details discussed:
- Workload: primarily chat assistant (conversational + retrieval augmented), embeddings for semantic search, small amount of reranking for search results. Expected qps: 3-10 sustained, spikes to ~50 during peak support hours.
- Latency targets: chat p95 <= 300ms; embedding...

title: PineQuill HostedWorks
body:
Lead source: self-serve signup (trial credits redeemed).

Initial context:
- SMB SaaS (helpdesk widgets) serving SMB merchants. Lightweight embedding index (~150k tokens).
- Primary app: in-product support chat + article search + short summarization of support threads.
- Concurrent active users peak ~20, expected steady 8-10.
- Cost sensitivity: target monthly API spend <= $2k for first 6 months. Quote from PO: \"budget is tight; need to keep host costs predictable.\"

Model selection questions surfaced on discovery call (2026-03-03, ff-20260303-4421):
- Trying Llama-2-13B (hosted) vs Mistral-7B vs Qwen-7B-small — which is best tradeoff for our use case?
- Curious about quantized/opt variants to reduce token cost.
- Concern: hallucination on product spec answers (we must not invent policy or pricing).
- Preference: lower latency for live chat (p50 target <300ms), p95 <700ms.
- Through...

title: BerryTrail AI
body:
Lead source: inbound (community meetup & blog).
Summary: small ecommerce platform (~120 employees) building conversational product discovery + support assistant. Goal is to replace brittle keyword search with embeddings + reranker, then add a chat layer for product guidance. Very cost conscious but prioritizes non-hallucinating answers for SKU/spec info.
What we heard on 3/9 call:
- "Customer quote: 'We need reliable answers about SKUs and specs — hallucinations cost us returns.'"
- Current infra: frontend + search on AWS; wants hosted_api (public cloud) to prototype quickly, then evaluate Dedicated later if traffic grows.
- POC scope: embeddings for 1M product text tokens (catalog + specs + reviews), reranker for top-10, chat for post-search follow-up. Expected POC volume: ~50k queries/month.
- Latency targets: embeddings batch job OK to be minutes; chat interactions need p95 <= 250m...

title: Suncrest KnowledgeForge
body:
Lead inbound from blog post on "Doc search patterns for small firms" — came in via contact form.
Primary use case: searchable knowledge base for small regional law firms + quick Q/A for intake teams.
They built a lightweight RAG flow: index PDFs -> embeddings -> store in external vector DB -> retrieve -> rerank -> prompt generation.
Current prototype: embeddings extracted with open-source encoder locally, using a self-hosted Weaviate instance (dev), re-ranking done client-side with a small LLM.
Questions raised on call: can Redwood Hosted API return embeddings only (yes) and do you provide direct connectors to Pinecone/Weaviate? — customer wants a recommended pattern for small infra teams.
Cost sensitivity: estimating 100k docs initial index, ~500k embeddings; worried about per-embed costs and storage egress if they use external vector DB.
Latency target: interactive search for parale...

title: Helios Forge Analytics
body: Account summary: Helios Forge is a 3-year Redwood hosted customer using embeddings and chat for internal MLOps tools and plant support chatbots. They want to transition critical inference into a Dedicated/reserved model to meet 24/7 SLAs and reduce noisy tail-cost. Key asks: 1) Dedicated sizing for three reserved pools (low-latency and high-throughput profiles), 2) Optimize pilot for nightly rerank and batch embedding jobs (target >=25% cost reduction), 3) Private deployment option with control plane in Redwood but runtime on-customer-premises for Japanese factories to satisfy residency. Technical: current models are quantized LLaMA variants and a custom reranker; want compatibility with KV cache and efficient batching. Business: procurement prefers a 12-month commitment with staged ramp; requests 10-18% committed discount and clear SLA for burst scaling. Risks: procurement QBR in lat...

title: Sproutwell Labs
body:
Inbound SMB lead via self-serve signup (founder-led). Founder/CEO: Maya Patel; Eng lead: Aaron Li. Early discovery call 2026-03-08 (Fireflies ff_20260308_sproutwell_discussion). Short, founder-heavy — main ask: clear, apples-to-apples pricing vs OpenAI and Anthropic. Quoted: "We need a predictable monthly spend cap — can't have surprises on token bills."

Key fragments from call / follow-ups:
- Signup source: product Hunt / organic. Started trial 2026-03-01, first API call 2026-03-03.
- Usage profile: expect 1.5-3M tokens / month in first 3 months; mostly chat (customer support) + embeddings for search. Low concurrency (10-30 rps peak), single region (us-east).
- Latency/throughput: target p95 < 300ms for chat responses; ok with stream/partial results. Throughput not huge.
- Model preference: wants quality closer to gpt-4 family but cost matters — asked about Redwood-verified model ca...

title: Montclair KnowledgeRun
body:
Account snapshot + recent 활동:
- Background: mid-market SaaS providing an in-app customer help center + chat. Primary goal: replace brittle keyword search with RAG + reranking to reduce deflection SLA and agent handle time.
- Scale & traffic: customer estimates ~420k canonical KB docs (moderate index size), refresh cadence daily (delta updates). Peak QPS requirement: sustained 300-450 QPS for search endpoints (support chat and widget). Burst tolerance required up to ~1k QPS for incident windows.
- Technical goals: embeddings-based retrieval (dense ANN) + lightweight cross-encoder reranker for top-5 candidates. Vector dim: 1536 (current plan), target recall at 10 ~ 0.92. Want Redwood to manage index hosting (ANN) and rerank execution path.
- Latency / cost targets: p95 end-to-end (query -> top5 rerank -> final answer) < 150ms for US region; cost sensitivity: aiming for <= $0.002 per end...

title: SparkKnock Play
body:
Early-stage consumer chat platform for mobile/social games. Focus on low-latency live chat and ephemeral messages.
Primary pain: current hosted provider (open-source proxy) introduces 200-300ms tail latency spikes during spikes — players notice.
Use-case: communal chat + 1:1 messaging + system-generated narrative prompts for live events.
Quote from CTO on 2026-03-02: 'We need sub-100ms p95 for chat typing indicator and moderation checks under 200ms, otherwise gameplay feels laggy.'
Moderation: wants lightweight on-request safety check with immediate fallback — prefer to run hosted moderation API, not send full transcripts to vendor without redaction.
Cost sensitivity: SMB; expects to pay per-token but wants predictable monthly cap for new launches. Looking at $1k-4k/mo initially.
Technical: mobile clients in NA, use GCP us-central and AWS us-east for live events; prefer routing by reg...

title: Umbraview ProductOps
body:
Mid-market SaaS (product analytics) — heavy product search + support chat; embeddings for svcs
Started on hosted API to validate latency/quality; POC showed strong relevance on reranker vs current TF-IDF baseline
Primary ask: predictable cost for high-volume reranking and embeddings queries; want fixed commit with burst and defined overage
Quote from Head of Product (Clara): 'We need predictability — spikes kill our unit economics. If we do Dedicated we must know exact $/M tokens month-to-month.'
SE feedback: typical request pattern = many short queries (avg tokens/request ~ 150) but high QPS during daytime — p95 latency and small tail are critical
Procurement focus: commit level, month-by-month ramp schedule, burst allowances, cap on overage multiplier (target <= 1.5x), early-termination and true-up clauses
Legal wants: sample SOW with explicit SLA for latency percentiles, list of ac...

Started on hosted API to validate latency/quality; POC showed strong relevance on reranker vs current TF-IDF baseline
Primary ask: predictable cost for high-volume reranking and embeddings queries; want fixed commit with burst and defined overage
Quote from Head of Product (Clara): 'We need predictability — spikes kill our unit economics. If we do Dedicated we must know exact $/M tokens month-to-month.'
SE feedback: typical request pattern = many short queries (avg tokens/request ~ 150) but high QPS during daytime — p95 latency and small tail are critical
Procurement focus: commit level, month-by-month ramp schedule, burst allowances, cap on overage multiplier (target <= 1.5x), early-termination and true-up clauses
Legal wants: sample SOW with explicit SLA for latency percentiles, list of ac...

title: LyricFlow Interactive
body:
Summary: Mid-market SaaS building an embedded conversational assistant for customer success + ops automation.
Workload: Agentic chat that does multi-step flows: user prompt -> KB search -> action (ticket create, run query) -> confirmation to user.
Primary goals: 1) hit median latency <150ms for streaming first token/partial responses; 2) high reliability for tool calls (idempotency, retries, timeouts); 3) predictable cost for bursty nightly workflows.
POC scope: hosted API only initially. Measure: streaming ttf (time-to-first-token), end-to-end tool-call success rate, KV cache hit improvement with prefix caching.
Tech notes from SE (Diego): currently prototyping with streaming + function-calls. Observed a couple of 5-10s tool-call timeouts in internal sim; wants guidance on backoff and fallback model variants.
Quote: Product lead said, 'We need the assistant to take actions reliably —...

title: Meridian Pulse Works
body:
CRM notes:
- Account: Meridian Pulse Works (mid-market SaaS, product analytics).
- Primary contact: Riley Park (VP Product), engages with infra lead Samir J. and finance mgr Nora L.
- Use-case: customer-facing chat assistant for product docs + vector search for support workflows. Also embeddings for similarity and summarization for feature notes.
- Performance targets: target p95 latency ~100-150ms for chat; throughput ~40 rps avg, peak test saw 120 rps. Cost sensitive — finance wants predictable unit economics.
- POC summary: hosted API POC deemed functionally successful. Observed p95 tail when concurrent burst simulated; SE suspects cache warming + KV eviction under load.
- Model preference: open-family models OK (Redwood-verified profiles preferred). Must be able to pin model version and fallback to smaller variant under capacity pressure.
- Security / deployment: require VPC peeri...

title: Sunridge Pulse
body:
2026-03-02 — Intro discovery call. Founder-led: Ethan Park (founder, ex-Slack).
Founder: 'We need predictable dev pricing; currently prototyping on OpenAI but costs spiking with embeddings.'
Primary angle: self-serve hosted API; likely start as pay-as-you-go, convert to committed if math works.
Wants clear comparison: per-token rates, avg latency for 1k token chat, and any free tier/credits for trials.
Security: startup needs SOC2 posture acceptable for early customers; SSO not a blocker today but audit logs desired.
Technical notes: expects 50-200 QPS at peak once product-market fit; initial launch 2k DAU target, embeddings for relevance search (small vector DB), chat for support bot.
Model prefs: open to open weights but wants API parity (streaming + function-calls); concerned about hallucination/regression risk.
Routing/fallback: founder asked about automatic fallbacks to smaller-m...

title: Pinecrest Analytics
body: Summary: strong product fit for personalization + reranking; technical validation done, team liked runtime stability. Primary barrier is unit economics. Finance says per-conversation token cost + projected GPU run-rate yields negative ROI compared to keeping in-house Distil-ensemble (current infra).

title: NextWave EduWorks
body:
Inbound via hosted API signup — used free credits to test basic Q&A and summarization (2026-02-25 sign-up).
Founder/CTO: Priya Nadar — technical lead on call. Key ask: 'FERPA-safe options; can student submissions be excluded from training?'
Primary use cases: classroom copilot (inline essay feedback + rubric scoring), automated moderation of forum posts (age gating, profanity/harassment filters), teacher-facing weekly reading summaries.
Budget: constrained. Pilot target spend $200–600/month; favourable outcome could expand to $3–25k ARR for district pilots. Wants concrete token-cost examples for common flows.
Security: Google Workspace SSO in use — asked about SAML + SCIM provisioning. Needs audit logging, simple retention controls, and US-only residency for student data.
Tech stack: serverless backend on GCP, Node.js frontend. Prefers public_cloud hosted API (no private infra for now...

title: CedarForge Retrieval Systems
body:
Company background: developer platform that powers over 450 product teams; heavy codebase indexing needs.
Primary ask: embeddings at scale (100M+ vectors), fast reranking to support IDE feedback and search UIs.
Key constraints: frequent re-index (nightly + nearline deltas), commits produce many small updates, want incremental indexing APIs.
Perf target: 95th pctile query latency < 120ms for top-k (k=10) across global users; tail latency important.
Cost target: target < $0.01/query on average; finance will push back if > $0.03.
Model preference: open 2k-8k token encoder models (1536–4096 dim), test both dense and hybrid rerank approaches.
Routing/fallback: want automatic fallback to lower-cost region or quantized index during bursts; regional data residency for EU customers.
Security: SSO/SAML for developer portal, KMS integration mandatory, retention controls for PII removed from repo...

title: Quiet Lark Labs
body:
Inbound PLG lead from indie micro-startup. Founder/engineer (Ava Chen) building a lightweight chat/code-assistant for niche vertical (indie game dev tools). Wants fast time-to-market, low overhead. Using public cloud only for now.

Summary: lightweight prototype -> go-live for small set of customers (50-200 MAU). Key constraints: low token cost, predictable latency for east coast NA + EU testers, simple integration (JS/Go SDK).

What Ava told us: "I just need something I can drop into the product and forget — no infra to manage." Wants hosted API; will consider Dedicated only if cost gets worse as scale grows.

Security: asked about SOC2 report and audit logs; retention controls later. Not asking for VPC or on-prem.

Technical notes: will start with gpt-like open models from Redwood model catalog; unsure on quantized variants — wants recommendation for low-cost, decent-latency profile...

title: Silverpine WealthTech
body:
Context: pilot for advisor-facing assistant + internal doc search. Leaning toward Dedicated with VPC for PII and residency.
Q4 POC ran well on latency; engineering liked KV cache and autoscaling demo. Cost model looked acceptable at committed capacity.
Sales convo: CFO paused all platform procurements and new headcount for next 6 months — 'we cannot onboard any new long-term commitments until FY review.', stated Head of Finance.
Security: security team asked for SOC2 report, KMS integration details, and a short architecture diagram for air-gapped scenarios. We provided doc set; review was positive but deprioritized due to freeze.
Champion (Dir. of Product) is still supportive but cannot push procurement while hiring freeze active. No alternate sponsor identified.
Competitive pressure: they ran pricing with OpenAI + in-house host; procurement said managed vendor may be easier but cost-...

title: Sterling Citadel Systems
body:
Topline: large defense-tier contact center integrator. Onboarding for mission-critical 24/7 ops. Must be fully air-gapped for classification boundary; cannot allow outbound tokens.

Summary of requirements:
- Workloads: live agent assist (real-time cues), post-call ticket summarization (structured), tool-calling to case mgmt and escalation workflows, and automated surge handling during incidents.
- Perf targets: median agent assist latency <120ms for token stream (per-agent), sustained throughput 10k tokens/sec during peak events, burst to 50k tokens/sec for incident windows.
- Reliability: automatic failover to local fallback models when primary unavailable; prefer deterministic degrade to text-only (no remote fallthrough).
- Models: open-model preference for on-prem licensing; need support for quantized LLMs and ability to pin to specific model versions.
- Security: FIPS-validated c...

- Models: open-model preference for on-prem licensing; need support for quantized LLMs and ability to pin to specific model versions.

body:

title: BrambleBridge SaaSWorks
body:
Account summary: BrambleBridge builds an in-app help center and smart search for mid-market SaaS apps. Evaluating Redwood to replace a mix of self-hosted search + open API cost spike. Started with Hosted API to validate quality; moving toward Dedicated if unit economics and residency checks out.

Key requirements / constraints:
- Workloads: real-time support chat (avg session ~9 turns), document search and reranking for KB (3M documents), nightly embedding refreshes.
- Latency SLOs: p95 < 120ms for reranking calls, p99 < 300ms for chat turn (interactive path).
- Throughput: steady 40-60 qps during peak; batch offline embedding jobs 2M docs/day window.
- Cost sensitivity: target < $200 / 1M tokens equivalent for inference-heavy flows; CTO wants 30-40% unit cost reduction over current setup.
- Data residency: primary in us-west; legal requires contractual language around residency for E...

title: CartBloom Commerce
body:
- Lead came in via blog post about 'retail AI best practices' and tried quickstart.
- Quote from CEO (call 2/22): "We need answers that sound product-aware, not generic. Budget is tight."
- SE notes (2/24): CartBloom leans toward smaller, high-quality open models; asked specific perf numbers for Llama-13B vs Mistral-7B-instruct on 512 token sequences.
- Concern: token cost for long chat sessions + retrieval context growth. Suggested approaches: prefix caching, selective retrieval, answer-length caps.
- They asked: "Which variant gives best embed quality per $ spent?" — recommended run small embedding A/B (Mistral/Qwen) in hosted API and measure recall on their 10k-doc corpus.
- Trial config: start with Mistral-7B-instruct quantized, fallback Llama-2-13B-q4 for longer-form answers only. (Proposal: switch model by route at server side).
- "Need SOC2 attestation" ticked on security doc; ...

body:
Inbound: signed up via self-serve sandbox on 2026-02-18, used free credits to run small toy flows.
Found via blog post on classroom copilots; left feedback form requesting moderation + low-cost options.
Call 2026-03-02 (FF id ff-20260302-6543) — 30m discovery with co-founder (Aisha Rahman) + product lead. AE (Elliot) intro + quick runthrough of hosted API.
Primary product interest: hosted API (self-serve). Wants minimal infra ops; public cloud only for now.
Workloads: student-facing in-class assistant (short-chat), teacher assistant for grading suggestions, automated content moderation on uploads/comments.
Latency sensitivity: interactive chat target ~200-400ms median token latency; can tolerate higher for grading batch jobs.
Throughput: small concurrent users now (peak 10-20 students), room to grow next school year. Budget constrained — focus on cost-per-token and burst limits.

title: LatticeWave Support
body: Inbound SMB lead. Self-serve signups already created trial org (3 developers). Primary use: chat-based customer support + internal doc search and answer summarization. POC ask: 4-week trial, ~5k messages/month, expected concurrency ~15-25 QPS during peak. Latency target: 95th percentile < 300ms for short chat responses (prompt + 256 tokens). Cost sensitivity: high — finance asked for per-conversation pricing estimate and recommendations for batching/caching. Security: legal asked for SOC2 Type II, full sub-processors list, and a DPA template to review. Also asked about data residency (prefer US only), audit logs retention (90 days), and whether Redwood uses any third-party NLP enrichment vendors. Quote from Head of Eng: "We can move fast if SOC2 and DPA look clean — small ARR but rapid scale potential if cost/perf works." AE notes: prospect is open to hosted API only (no VPC/private) ...

body:
Inbound SMB lead via self-serve sign-up on 2026-03-01. Small startup (12 engs), building an embeddable developer tool that surfaces code examples + contextual docs in VS Code and on their web dashboard. Primary ask: clear quickstarts and working adapters for LangChain and LlamaIndex plus a Vercel/Next.js serverless example. They want to move fast — POC in 2 weeks.

Key signals / quotes:
- "We signed up and want to wire Redwood in this week — can you give us a copy-paste LangChain adapter?"
- Cost-sensitive: expects ~200k tokens/mo during POC, long-term growth but wants predictable per-token forecasting.
- Latency target: 200-400ms median for chat completions in UI; background embedding jobs can be batch/higher latency.
- No VPC/on-prem requirement today — public cloud only; they prefer hosted API to avoid infra.

title: DeckTrail Analytics
body:
Inbound from sales enablement app maker. CTO (Maya Rivers) reached out via sales enablement Slack community — interested in Redwood hosted API for call summarization and search overlay.
- Prefers self-serve trial first; wants to validate accuracy and cost vs AssemblyAI + OpenAI.
- Main concerns: PII in transcripts (names, emails, credit-card like strings). Asked: 'Can we redact PII before storing? How long do transcripts live in your system?'
- Wants control over retention (30/90/never persist) and ability to disable logs for specific routes.
- Use-case: Chrome/Zoom connector collects transcript -> stream to Redwood for real-time summary + finalize summary stored in customer's DB. Also embeddings for search (vector store in customer infra later).
- Latency: soft requirement 300-800ms for streaming sentence-level summaries; cost-sensitive — target <$0.01 per processed minute.
- Securit...

body:

Key ask: local indexing + encrypted KV cache so repo code never leaves customer infra. Quoted: 'we cannot send private repos to third-party models' (Priya).

title: CodeRipple Labs
body:
Lead: CodeRipple Labs — small devtools startup building an in-editor AI pair programmer (VS Code + web) focused on whole-file refactors and long context code search.

Stage: discovery — early inbound from founder (CTO ex-small-OSS biz). Self-serve sign-up completed, played with REST endpoint.

Primary asks / pain: 
- Long context windows: need 50k+ token window for whole-repo retrieval + code-aware summarization. Quote: "We need entire file + call graph preface for accurate suggestions."
- Rate limits: unpredictable bursts from IDE telemetry + background indexing; want clear per-key burst policy and ability to request higher short-term bursts.
- SDKs: wants first-class JS/TS SDK with streaming chunk events, reconnect and auth refresh hooks; also Python SDK for backend batch reranking.
- Latency: interactive suggestions target ~150-300ms token latency for short completions; tolerate hi...

title: Cedarwood Telecom AI
body:
Summary: Strategic telco account evaluating Redwood Private for low-latency call summarization + reranking in agent flows.
Initial interest prompted by 2024 pilot with open models; want predictable costs for 24/7 contact center workloads.
01/10/2025 - Intro call (Ava/Diego) w/ Head of AI (Marcel K.) and Platform Procurement. High-level requirements captured.
01/22/2025 - Technical deep-dive + Fireflies meeting ff_20250122_cedar-01. Discussed KV cache needs, real-time latency targets (p95 < 150ms) and batch embedding jobs at night.

title: LatchPoint Labs
body:
Lead source: inbound via blog post about low-latency chat widgets. Founder (Maya Rao) did initial sign-up and created API key but hasn't started high-volume calls. Small engineering team (2 engs) building an AI-first in-product assistant for their B2B dashboard.

Founder call summary (2026-03-05):
- Maya (founder/CTO) on call with AE + SE; very price sensitive — wants to understand real per-1M-token cost including caching/batching benefits.
- "We compare prices with OpenAI/Anthropic — but we also care about predictable latency and regional availability."
- Use-case: interactive chat + code snippet generation in app; average prompt length ~150 tokens, responses ~250 tokens. QPS expected low (0.5-2 rps) but spiky when customers onboard.
- Prefers hosted API for now (no infra ops), may consider Dedicated when ARR grows.
- Wants a simple calc: monthly dev/dev+prod token burn examples and ...

title: Lighthouse MedTransit
body: Stage: evaluation. Short: large enterprise healthcare payments operator; processes claims + card refunds (PCI + PHI). Primary ask: agent-assist for contact center + accurate ticket summarization, plus secure tool-calls to payment vault (no PAN in prompts). Reliability under spikes is critical (monthly billing cycles + claim surges). Security: Okta SSO + SCIM, require audited KMS with HSM, audit logging retained 7yrs, strict egress controls from VPC, SOC2/ISO artifacts before procurement. POC details: 4-week pilot; sanitized production chat logs (scrubbed PANs) to validate summarization + action flow. Success metrics: reduce avg handle time (AHT) by 30%, >95% summary accuracy on top-2 intents, 0 incidents in security review. Quotes: "Can't have raw PANs leave our VPC" (Head of Ops), "Need cryptographic proof of key separation and HSM-backed KMS" (Security). Integrations: Okta, internal...

title: CourtWise LegalTech
body:
Lead: small legaltech startup building a secure search/summarization layer for plaintiff/defendant docs. Inbound via product signup -> support form. Wants hosted API (self-serve) if we can meet audit/logging needs and US-only residency.

Key pain: current search infra expensive, needs redaction + provenance for summaries when used in client deliverables.

Security reqs (customer quotes/highlights):
- "We need provable audit logs tied to user IDs, not just request ids."
- Must keep content in US region; SOC2 is nice but not a blocker today; retention policy controls required.

Cost sensitivity: SMB budget, low to medium ARR initially. Interested in usage tiering and developer-friendly onboarding.

Technical details discussed:
- Primary workload: embeddings + semantic search for confidential docs, and batch summarization for case briefs.
- Latency requirements: interactive search <200ms...

title: Staccato Spark Labs
body:
Lead origin: inbound self-serve signup (trial credits used). Product: B2B SaaS (workflow automation for sales ops) embedding an in-app assistant in their web app. Lightweight company, 20 engs, product-led. Primary contact: CTO (Maya Singh) and Head of Product (Leo Navarro).

Synopsis / requirements:
- Use-case: live in-app chat assistant for end-users (agents + end-customers) and contextual product help. Also quick KB search and triage flows.
- Latency: p50 token latency target <= 80ms, p95 for initial response <= 200ms. Streaming required (server->client token stream), incremental UI updates.
- Throughput: expect 100-300 concurrent active users in early months; ramp plan to 1k concurrent next 6mo. Typical session length 40-120 turns.
- Cost sensitivity: moderate — favor cost-effective model variants but willing to trade some cost for sub-200ms streaming SLA.
- Models: prefer open / s...

- Throughput: expect 100-300 concurrent active users in early months; ramp plan to 1k concurrent next 6mo. Typical session length 40-120 turns.
- Cost sensitivity: moderate — favor cost-effective model variants but willing to trade some cost for sub-200ms streaming SLA.

title: BryteBridge Marketing Platform
body:
Discovery notes (concise):
- Inbound from content marketing campaign (signup form + demo request).
- Company: BryteBridge — SMB marketing platform that auto-generates campaign content (multi-tenant SaaS for small brands).
- Primary ask: high-volume, low-cost text generation for daily newsletter + social + landing page variants.
- Volume: estimated 8M tokens/month in steady state; initial POC target = 1.2M tokens/month.
- Latency/throughput: p50 target <250ms for single prompt flows used by editor UI; bulk batch jobs run asynchronously (throughput 200-400 reqs/sec during batch windows).
- Cost sensitivity: high. Current vendor spend ~ $2.5k/month; want better unit economics at scale.
- Technical preference: open-models/quantized variants acceptable; interested in Redwood's model catalog + quantization guidance.
- Critical features called out: prefix/KV caching for template-heavy conten...

title: Call Cartography LLC
body:
Prospect inbound from self-serve trial (signed up via quickstart). Early discovery call 2026-03-02. Primary product interest: hosted API for call-summary + downstream lead scoring. Key ask: PII handling and retention. Wants option to redact/scrub PII at ingest and configurable retention windows (30/90/365 days).

Call snippets (rep paraphrase): "We need to be sure transcripts with customer emails/cc numbers do not leak into embeddings or long-term storage." Said they will test with 1000 weekly calls. Cost sensitivity moderate — SMB budget. Latency target: median generation <800ms for 1.2k token conversations; willing to accept up to 1.5s for batched summaries.

Security: requested SOC2 report, KMS support, audit logs; asks about retention controls exposed in Console (UI + API). Legal sent initial questionnaire (attached on Drive).

Notes on integration: prototyping with webhooks + Red...

title: Saffron Basin Technologies
body: Short, messy CRM notes from AE/SE: Trial felt productive. Customer integrated chat widget + search; product team ran two playbooks (support + product help). Quote from PM: "Latency makes or breaks UX for quick responses". They saw embedding costs spike during nightly re-index job -> want suggestions for batching/caching. SE recommended prefix caching and routing low-priority traffic to cheaper quantized variants. Legal flagged DPA + data residency for EU customers (they have EU customers; want EU residency option). Procurement expects final numbers for committed capacity (QPS/concurrency) before procurement approval. Next: internal infra & security call with Priya + Marcus, send detailed trafﬁc histogram (per-minute qps for 6 weeks) and token distribution CSV. Drive contains the integration guide and security FAQ links. Fireflies transcript has PM quote and a sketch of staging plan.

title: ElmSpire KnowledgeWorks
body:
Inbound via doc-search blog post; signed up for free tier and created first embedding index. Quick notes from discovery call: 
- CTO (Maya Singh) is primary technical contact; wants hosted API to avoid ops overhead.
- Primary workload: legal document search + contract Q&A (RAG). Low QPS (10-30 reqs/min) but need fast reranking for top-k (p95 latency target 150-250ms).
- Currently evaluating vector DBs: Pinecone + Weaviate; asking about recommended approach for reranking pipeline (embeddings -> ANN -> rerank via Hosted API LM).
- Cost sensitivity: SMB budget, expects usage to start small; asked about token pricing + embedding unit definitions.
- Model preference: open models preferred (Llama2 style), but open to Redwood-curated models if latency/cost are better.
- Security: requires SSO for dashboard, audit logs for queries, and ability to set retention to 90 days for PII. Not on-prem;...

title: SynerFlow Cognify
body:
Customer profile: mid-market SaaS providing product analytics and in-app recommendations. Product teams own ML infra; ops team maintains nightly embedding job for 8M records.
Primary objective: replace brittle FAISS cron pipeline with managed embeddings + semantic search to reduce ops time and improve query latency for top-k relevance.
POC scope details: incremental embedding pipeline (API -> batch job), vector DB choice TBD (they lean to managed but want exportability). Will test with dims=1536 / cosine.
Performance targets: nightly batch to finish <4 hours for 2M new/updated rows; real-time search p95 <100ms for top-10; throughput target 200 qps sustained for customer-facing endpoints.
Security: must pass infosec checklist: SOC2, SSO/SAML integration, audit logs retention 1 year, KMS for key management, optional data residency in us-east-1.
Model preference: open quantized embedding...

title: Ink & Gear Studios
body:
Founder: single dev + 1 contractor, building narrative co-op game w/ chat NPCs
Primary ask: cheap, low-latency chat for many short conversations per user session
Cost sensitivity: very high - will likely run under 5k ARR initially, could spike on playtests
Model preference: open models or small footprint variants (quantized), prefers predictable per-token pricing
Latency target: 150-300ms p50 for chat completions; can tolerate p95 up to 800ms during playtests
Throughput: low concurrency (10s of concurrent users), but many short requests per minute
Routing/fallback: wants simple automatic fallback to smaller model if quota exceeded
Security: no enterprise SSO yet; wants audit logs and ability to rotate API keys (KMS later)
Quote from founder: 'We need something that just works out of the box and doesn't bankrupt us during a launch test.'
Tried: community open-source runtimes locally bu...

title: Hearthline Postsale
body:
Quick CRM notes, rough and chatty.
- Company: mid-market ecommerce platform focused on post-purchase experience (returns + shipping).
- Primary pain: high touch manual returns chats, CS costs spike during seasonal events (Nov-Dec). Want to automate ~60% of inbound returns flows w/ safe tool-calling to order service.
- P95 latency is a gating metric: they want reliable p95 for user-facing assistant. Target we discussed: first-token streaming <= 200ms, end-to-end p95 for short-turn responses (<=120 tokens) <= 600ms under normal load; tolerate higher during brief peaks if fallback works.
- Throughput: typical steady-state ~25-40 RPS, expected seasonal peaks up to 350-500 RPS per region (NA). Concurrency: ~120 concurrent live chats typical.
- Model preferences: prefer open/LLM variants they can run on Dedicated later (Llama-2-Chat style), but open to Redwood hosted for POC for speed. Want...

title: Orion Scanwell
body:
Prospect: building a customer-facing KB for 8 enterprise customers. Key priorities: very low tail latency for reranking, predictable cost per query, and simple hosted integration.
- 2026-01-14: Intro call (Priya) — high-level product fit; mentioned "can't tolerate >300ms p95 end-to-end".
- 2026-01-21: Tech deep-dive (SE: Marco) — went through indexing architecture, current Pinecone + in-house reranker prototype; POC ask: compare Redwood hosted API vs self-hosted cost + perf.
- 2026-02-10: Demo (Marco, Priya) — showed streaming + rerank flows; Fireflies ff_20260210_orion_demo_77.
- POC scope agreed: 2-week hosted API POC, ingest 420k docs, simulate 220 QPS sustained with burst loads, run rerank A/B vs current prototype.
- Quote from Head of Search: "If you can hit 120ms p95 rerank at 220 QPS and show 30% lower TCO we'll move to annual committed capacity."
- Security: wants SOC2 report ...

title: Gilded Pelican Tech
body:
Lead background: small D2C e-comm platform (headless storefront + chat support). Came in via webinar + community post. Trialled hosted API for: 1) customer support assistant (summaries + resolutions), 2) recommendations / related-products endpoint. Initial onboarding: created API key, ran smoke tests.
Stalled behaviour: low usage after initial key exchange — only a couple of evaluation calls. Customer quote from 1/14: "Looks promising but need a clearer cost story vs what we're already running." Switched to parallel PoC with Anthropic embeddings on 1/30 "to test vector quality and pricing".
Technical notes (from SE call 1/18): prefix caching would help their chat workflow, batching not yet implemented in their client. Observed latency spikes around 200ms for short prompts; larger >1k token tests had cost concerns. They asked whether Redwood has auto-fallback model routing if regional ...
