title: Trident Travel Works
body:
Stalled -> closed-lost to incumbent. High level: Trident wanted to replace an in-house + Anthropic stack for customer support / booking flows. They required strict, deterministic structured output (JSON schema) for downstream processing and reliable tool-calling to their booking microservices.
POC summary:
- Performance: Redwood hit latency targets under configured batching on Dedicated nodes.
- Cost: Quantized runtime gave ~10% lower per-token cost in internal runs.
- Quality/regression: In POC edge cases, Redwood-generated structured outputs occasionally omitted required keys (e.g., 'flight_options.price' or produced nested arrays when a flat list expected). Incumbent stack produced consistent schema across sessions; Trident flagged this as unacceptable for automated pipeline.
Quote from Liam O'Connor (Head of AI): "We can't build orchestration around outputs that drift — a single m...

title: SpringForge KBloom
body:
Summary: SaaS helpdesk vendor building KB RAG to power triage + automated replies. Focus: embeddings + light reranking pipeline (dense retriever -> small LM reranker).

Key metrics/requirements:
- Indexing: ~1.0-1.5M unique passages (mix of short FAQs, long articles, chat transcripts).
- QPS: peak 500-800 QPS for query endpoints, sustained 300-500 QPS during business hours.
- Latency targets: p50 <60ms, p95 <250ms for end-to-end (embedding lookup + rerank + response assembly).
- Cost sensitivity: strong; target <$0.004 per query (inc. embeddings + rerank tokens).
- Model preferences: open models preferred; want ability to pin model versions; interested in 8-bit quantized options for reranker.
- Routing/fallback: want simple downgrade path (cheaper embedding-only fallbacks) during spikes.

What we did: POC run on hosted API using ada-like embedding model + rerank with smaller Llama var...

title: BriefPulse
body:
Inbound MQL from gated benchmarking blog post; signed up for free credits on 2026-02-20.
Marketing lead (Ava Chen) is primary champion — wants to replace current multi-vendor copy pipeline with one API to simplify ops.
Workload profile: bursty high-volume content generation (50-200 articles/month), plus daily short-form social posts and segmented newsletters.
Cost sensitivity high — finance asked for a target unit cost per article and a clear scaling plan. They repeatedly asked: 'How do we get to predictable per-article cost?'
Technical ask: built-in batching + prefix/KV caching to amortize prompt overhead across bursts; interested in per-route caching suggestions from Redwood Optimize.
Latency: prefers ~ <800ms for single-article generation end-to-end; streaming acceptable if it reduces perceived latency.
Model preferences: open models acceptable, will evaluate Redwood model catalog ...

title: Shearwater Support Labs
body:
Lead background: small support SaaS (ticketing + chat widget) targeting ecom merchants. Signed up via self-serve, using hosted API. Primary pain: perceived latency from EU customers after they integrated the chat widget (avg p95 reported ~700-900ms, goal p95 <300-400ms for core UX). Strong cost sensitivity; want to avoid moving to Dedicated for now — prefer tuning in hosted tier.

Customer comments / paraphrases: "Users abandon chat if reply takes >1s"; "We like the simple API but need guidance on timeouts and region choice".

Technical notes & observed patterns: - Most traffic originates in NA (60%) and EU (35%), EU traffic hits NA region endpoint currently (they left default). - Typical prompts: short chat context + 3-5 KB of recent messages; sometimes include long product descriptions (payloads up to 2k tokens). - Concurrency: front-end opens 20-30 concurrent requests during peak; ...

title: Frostline Knowledgebox
body:
Lead summary:
- SMB (50-150 eng + product/content team), building internal doc search + customer support RAG feature called "Knowledgebox".
- Inbound from product docs: reading 'embeddings + reranking' guide and API quickstart.
- Wants hosted API self-serve: low friction sign-up, easy SDKs, clear pricing.

Technical requirements / asks (from call):
- Workload: indexing ~200-2,000 docs (mostly HTML/PDF), queries ~10-50 qps initially, want 95th pctl latency <200ms per query (embedding + retrieval + rerank).
- Prefer embeddings + kNN retrieval in their own Postgres/Supabase or small Weaviate instance; asking whether they should store embeddings in DB vs calling Redwood for embeddings on-demand.
- Reranking: want to run a cheap (instruction-tuned) reranker for top-k results; questions about combining Redwood hosted generation for rerank step and vector DB for recall.
- Model preferences: ...

title: Ironsail Logistics Collective
body: Account is a long-term hosted API customer (3 years). Exploring Dedicated to stabilize peak throughput for last-mile routing and telemetry summarization workloads. POC completed (two-node Dedicated) in Jan-Feb 2026; saw ~18–22% reduction in average latency for chat flows and 12% token-cost improvement when enabling prefix caching. Customer very interested in Redwood Optimize recommendations — SE reviewed batching + quantization playbook and customer asked for a 2-week pilot. EU data residency is gating renewal: ops/legal said 'no EU processing without residency guarantee'. Procurement timeline: target May sign. AE notes: need final SOW + amendment covering v2 residency clause and updated SLA for P99. Short-term ask: price alignment for burst capacity and explicit fallback routing to hosted during EU maintenance windows. PSR open for priority networking help (INF-1189).

title: Elm Harbor Assistants
body:
Inbound SMB lead via self-serve (free credits). Small engineering team (2 backend, 1 frontend). Primary product: chat-based support assistant + doc search across help center. Key pain: perceived slowness on user-facing chat — several customer complaints this week.

Observed behavior (customer reports / internal testing):
- Users in US experiencing ~600-900ms extra RTT vs EU users; account originally created with default region eu-west-1.
- Frontend shows request timeouts at ~12s (client-side) when prompts include long context + history (~1.5-3k tokens).
- Team tried increasing client timeout but worried about cost and UX.

What they've asked/said: 
- "We signed up fast, but users are saying the bot feels slow — is this something we can fix without moving infrastructure?" 
- Very cost-sensitive; prefer hosted API but may upgrade if clear perf wins.

Redwood quick wins suggested on call...

title: Brookline Pulse
body:
Founder-led inbound. Founder: Elliot Park (CTO/Founder) on the call. Lead source: self-serve signup + inbound demo request.

Summary bullets:
- Elliot signed up for free API credits 2026-02-25, ran basic chat + embeddings tests against their support content ingestion (~25k tokens).
- Tried Redwood hosted endpoint; reports \"responses seem OK but need cost numbers vs OpenAI/Anthropic to justify switch\". Quote: \"We need hard $/1k-token examples and perf tradeoffs — founders won't commit otherwise.\"
- Primary workload: customer support chat assistant + internal doc search. Peak expected volume: 50-200 daily chats, bursty during release windows. Latency target: sub-300ms tail for chat responses to keep UX tight. Cost sensitivity: moderate — want predictable pay-as-you-go now, may consider Dedicated only if traffic > sustained 100k tokens/day.
- Model preference: open to Redwood catalog...

Primary tech stack: Node backend, Postgres, client chat widget; exploring Pinecone and Weaviate for vector store.
Requirements discussed on 2026-02-18 call (ff_2026-02-18_01):
- Need embeddings + reranking combo: embed all docs, then candidate retrieval + on-the-fly rerank using model scoring.
- Latency target: P95 < 250ms for chat responses (we expect small corpus per tenant, avg 5-10k docs).
- Throughput: low-medium (tens reqs/sec), cost sensitive: want to keep monthly infra under ~$3k initially.
- Model preferences: open models preferred; willing to t...

title: Mariner Spark
body:
Inbound SMB lead from productized in-app assistant startup. Primary product: embeddable chat for customer success + in-app help; multi-tenant SaaS with 20k MAU forecast.

Key technical constraints: streaming responses w/ sub-200ms token emit preferred, p99 < 500ms end-to-end for common short-turn interactions, < 200ms cold-start desirable. Heavy emphasis on smooth UX (progressive token streaming, partial responses). Will integrate via browser SDK and server-side proxy for rate limiting.

Model preferences: open to small/medium open models with quantized variants; wants ability to route lower-cost requests to smaller models and fall back to higher-capacity models for long-running sessions. Cost sensitivity high — tight margin for token budget.

Security/compliance: SOC2 in roadmap, needs SSO (SAML) now for internal admin console, audit logging for admin events. Data residency not a blo...

title: Lilypad Socket
body:
Summary: inbound SMB trial from organic signup (marketing blog link). Signed up for Hosted API; created first API key but only ~5 calls total over 10 days. Appears to be evaluating multiple vendors — CTO indicated they want 'lowest latency for chat widgets' and are attracted to competitor promo credits. Pricing confused team; misc. console errors they saw may be due to using wrong region. Wants SOC2 + SSO before committing but initial interest is self-serve Hosted API (public cloud).

Recent timeline (short):
- 2025-12-09: created account + free credits auto-provisioned.
- 2025-12-10: first API key created; 2 test completions recorded.
- 2025-12-15: automated onboarding email opened, no follow-up.
- 2026-01-20: AE outreach (intro) — got brief reply from CTO: 'evaluating latency & cost; using competitor credits for PoC'.
- 2026-02-18: 30m demo scheduled; Fireflies ff-2026-02-18-9912. D...

title: Fortress Life Sciences
body:
Account summary: large pharma R&D + regulatory submissions group. Evaluating Redwood Private (on-prem VPC + air-gapped variant) for downstream document search, trial report summarization, and assisted manuscript drafting. Primary driver: eliminate data egress risk and meet EU/US residency for certain study datasets. Infosec: high bar — full SOC2+ISO27001 evidence requested. Legal asking for model packaging assurances (no outbound calls in air-gapped mode).

Key quotes: "We cannot allow any telemetry leaving the datacenter without explicit approved channels" — Head of Infosec.

Commercial: leaning toward 3-year committed Dedicated / Private bundle if PoC proves offline packaging + KMS integration. Price sensitivity moderate; value tied to auditability and demonstrable controls.

Technical ask list (short): offline model tarball delivery, signed checksums, HSM/KMS integration guide, aud...

title: InkHaven Marketing AI
body:
Lead profile: small SaaS marketing platform that generates outbound and on-site content for SMB customers. Primary pain: unpredictable token costs during campaign bursts. Interested in hosted_api for fast time-to-value and self-serve flow.

Discovery call highlights:
- "We need to keep per-campaign cost predictable — spikes kill margins" said CEO.
- Use-case: generate thousands of personalized subject lines + short bodies, scale for JIT campaign runs.
- Tested self-serve with free credits; engineering saw immediate gains from batching (batch=8) but concerned about p95 spikes during bursts.
- Wants concrete guidance on caching/prefix reuse for template-heavy workloads.
- Security: small company but requires SSO for their agency customers and audit logs for billing. SOC2 not required immediately.

What we sent/need to send:
- Sent quickstart + example batching code snippets (JS/Python) ...

title: VoxMemo Labs
body: Inbound SMB lead from call-summary chrome extension. Signed up for free credits via self-serve and ran quick PoC with hosted API. Main ask: how do we handle PII in transcripts and embeddings? Wants clear retention rules and ability to purge customer data on demand. -- From call: "We can’t risk storing customer phone numbers or SSNs in embeddings long-term. Is there a way to redact at ingestion or automatically expire vectors after X days?". They prefer public cloud (no VPC yet) and want predictable per-token pricing. Mentioned they currently use Google Speech for STT and then call-summary pipeline that posts transcripts to our ingestion endpoint. Questions: default retention window, deletion api, KMS for keys, audit logs for deletion events. Legal asked for SOC2 notes; engineering asked about rate limits and streaming latency (targets: 200-400ms per token for chat/summary path). Cost ...

title: Coralrill AI
body: Inbound SMB lead. Team=Coralrill AI (SaaS for customer support automation). Very early discovery but high intent to buy if POC shows cost/latency OK. Primary asks: 1) Hosted API — want self-serve path, Node + Python SDKs tested. 2) Billing: prefer credit card (fast), but procurement asked if invoice option available for subscriptions >$5k — may escalate if volume grows. 3) Discounts: early-stage startup, asked about startup credits or longer free-credit window to complete a 2-week POC. Asked explicitly: "Can Redwood extend credits to 60 days?" 4) Security: SOC2 required, audit logs and residency for EU customers (some EU-based customers later). Sent SOC2 summary but procurement may want more artifacts. 5) Workloads: chat for agent assist (latency sensitive), embeddings for retrieval (cost-sensitive). Target latency p50 <200ms, p95 <600ms for chat; throughput moderate (15-30 rps peak) ...

title: LittleBay Helpers
body:
SMB inbound. Small product team (Customer Success automation startup). Primary asks: low-latency chat for support agents, embeddings for knowledge base search, and cheap reranking for triage. Wants public cloud hosted API (self-serve) — will consider Dedicated later if volume grows.

Model selection questions are the most active thread: they repeatedly asked for guidance on Llama vs Mistral vs Qwen and tradeoffs between 7B/13B/14B families. Key quotes: "Can we get chat-like quality at ~1/3 the cost?" and "We need p50 latency <300ms for short responses, p95 <800ms when longer."

Current thinking from customer: try Mistral-Instruct-7B for low cost, Llama-2-13B for highest helpfulness on instruction prompts, Qwen-14 if bilingual support needed (EN/CN). Concerned about token cost and tail latency on larger models. Asking about quantized variants and whether Redwood's hosted kernels give b...

title: Silverpine Concierge AI
body:
Lead: Silverpine Concierge AI (small ecommerce CX tooling startup).
Context: landed via self-serve signup; immediate pain = monthly inference spend creeping up as trial customers increase.
What they asked for (verbatim/paraphrase): "Can you show us low-friction ways to cut token spend by at least 30% without hurting reply quality?"
Current behaviour: chat assistant calls include full product descriptions + long customer context on every turn. Doc-search calls send whole doc sections (no retrieval compression). No prefix/KV caching in client.
Key metrics shared: avg tokens per chat req ~1200, median response length 220 tokens, 8k requests/month during trial spike.
Primary asks: token usage audit (per-endpoint), batching guidance for web chat (client-side + server-side batching patterns), prompt compression templates (system + user prompt reductions), quick wins for prefix/KV caching an...

title: MorningBeam AI Co
body:
Inbound lead: self-serve signup (trial) on 2026-03-01. Primary contact: Priya Natarajan (CTO) - small remote eng team, two full-time SWEs + contractor.

Quick summary:
- Product: support chat assistant that auto-summarizes conversations into CRM notes and suggests ticket tags.
- Workload: chat + post-chat summarization + nightly batch embeddings for search/agent recall.
- Current model usage: exploring gpt-style LLMs via hosted API; high churn on prompts.
- Cost sensitivity: high. CFO flagged token spend after month 1 of trial.

Requirements and asks (from call 2026-03-03):
- "Can we get a token audit?" -> want breakdown by endpoint, route, top prompts.
- Batching/caching: interested in prefix caching for common greetings and customer metadata caching.
- Prompt compression: need examples of templating + suggested variable placement to reduce tokens.
- Target latency: 150-400ms p95 for...

title: Pinegrove Peddlers
body:
Inbound from Shopify app contact form — 'want to add product Q&A to site search'
Maya (Head of Product): 'need low lift integration, high accuracy on specs (dimensions, materials) — customers ask these a lot',
Traffic profile: very bursty — weekends + Friday nights, Black Friday window bigger but one-off. Typical 2k qps spikes -> actually ~200 qps sustained, spikes up to ~1.2k for short bursts according to logs
Primary ask: hosted API (self-serve) to avoid infra ops; open to Dedicated later if predictable traffic grows
Cost sensitivity flagged multiple times — wants per-token cost estimate and examples of batching savings
SE notes: recommend small-model routing for cheap fallback + a higher-quality model for canonical responses; enable prefix caching for product descriptions
Security: SOC2 is a checkbox for procurement; asked about audit logs + ability to set 30d retention on logs
Int...

title: PollenStack Labs
body:
Inbound via self-serve signup (March 5). Small B2B SaaS focused on in-app onboarding + support chat for HR tools. Wants hosted API only for now — lower ops overhead. Key asks:
- Streaming responses (websocket/SSE) with immediate partial tokens (UX must feel instantaneous).
- Latency goal: ~150ms L95 in US region, occasional spikes acceptable but want guidance on batching/caching tradeoffs.
- Throughput: estimated 30-60 reqs/s during peak, ~50 concurrent active chats in POC.
- Cost sensitive: startup budget, asks about token-level cost controls and burst limits.
- Model preferences: open to small open models for cost, but needs Redwood-verified profiles + fallback to larger model for complex queries.
- Security: SSO/SAML is required for internal admin UI, audit logs for message events, 30-day retention initially. SOC2 is requested in roadmap but not blocking.
- Current stack: prototype...

title: Runway Inferwise
body:
Origin: inbound from pricing page -> signed up for hosted API free trial.
Primary contact: Priya Desai (CTO) - internal platform owner for support chat.
Workload: user-facing chat + vector-based doc search. Expect steady background embedding traffic + chat bursts during marketing emails.
Traffic profile and targets: steady 10-15 QPS baseline across regions; expected normal peak 60-80 QPS; planned seasonal/campaign spikes up to 350-450 QPS for ~20-30 minutes.
Latency SLO: 95th <= 200ms for short replies (client-side acceptable up to 300ms if burst queued).
Cost sensitivity: moderate. Want to avoid overprovisioning; prefer smart batching and short KV cache TTLs to reduce token usage.
Rate-limiting concerns (from call): CTO quote - "We can tolerate a few throttled admin calls, but customer-facing chat hitting 429s during flash sales is a showstopper."
Requested features/examples: per-rou...

title: Sundial Classroom AI
body:
Lead came in via organic search + blog (teachers using chat assistants). Founder (Elena Park) is primary decision maker; CTO (Ravi Patel) technical lead. Key context: small K-12 focused startup building a teacher-facing copilot to summarize classroom transcripts, generate differentiated lesson prompts, and flag student safety issues. Budget: very constrained — wants to keep hosted costs under ~$500/month initially.

Requirements / asks observed during calls:
- Use-case: classroom copilot (session-based chat), automated moderation on student messages, lightweight summarization for teacher notes. Mostly text workloads, occasional short audio transcripts (transcribed upstream).
- Latency target: interactive UX — acceptable p95 ~500-800ms for student-facing quick replies; teacher workflows can tolerate higher latency.
- Throughput: low-medium (hundreds of requests/day), but bursty during ...

title: Emerald Bridge Integrations
body:
Summary: midsize SaaS platform that resells and integrates disparate helpdesk tools for retail clients. Primary ask: robust tool-calling to create/update tickets, trigger refunds via internal REST APIs, and escalate to L3 via Slack/Jira. POC scope narrow: Zendesk create/update + refund endpoint + automated escalation flow.

Requirements & constraints:
- Tool-calls must be idempotent and show audit trail (who/what/when).
- Refund flow goes through internal refunds API that touches PCI-adjacent services — legal demands scoping and tokenization; cannot store raw PAN.
- Must provide per-request trace IDs and tie AI-decisions to logs for 7+ months (audit_logging).
- EU customers require residency; prefer control plane in public_cloud but data plane in VPC/EU region.
- Burst reliability: peak 5x normal traffic for 20-40 minutes (holiday sale spikes). Need graceful degradation (fallback to q...

title: Sunbolt DocSearch
body:
Lead: Aditya Rao (CTO). SMB, product-led startup building a contextual doc search + RAG assistant for small legal firms. Wants fast POC on hosted API first. Key asks:
- Embedding size: asked about recommendations for 1536 vs 1024 dims and effect on vector DB cost.
- Reranking: prefers two-stage (embeddings recall -> lightweight reranker) — asked whether Redwood hosted API supports rerank endpoints or recommended workflow.
- Vector DB integration: wants sample connectors for Pinecone and Redis Vector Similarity; asked about best practices for upserts and batch sizes.
- Latency / throughput: target median query latency <= 220ms for single-shot search; batch indexing throughput target ~30k docs/hour.
- Cost sensitivity: tight budget first 6 months, expects <$1k/mo initially.
- Security: SSO required (SAML), audit logs needed; SOC2 proof requested but data residency not required.
Conversa...

title: Lucent Arrow AI
body:
Lead source: inbound, self-serve signup (trial started 2026-02-12).
Initial contact: quick email from AE pointing to quickstart; customer tried default region (us-east-1) and later eu-west-1; reported "noticeable lag for EU customers" on chat UI.
Key quote (founder): "Responses feel OK but spinner time kills engagement — we lose 30% conversion on chat flows."
Technical summary:
- Workload: mostly short-form support chat & article summarization + some embeddings for search.
- Traffic pattern: spiky around feature launches, 5-15 qps baseline, bursts to 80 qps for 30-120s.
- Latency targets: p95 < 400ms token generation latency, end-to-end chat response < 1.2s preferred.
- Cost sensitivity: medium, willing to trade small incremental cost for latency improvements.
What they tried so far:
- Default SDK settings with no explicit timeout; front-end uses 15s fetch timeout but spinner UI shows...

title: Spectra Foundry Search
body:
Snapshot: Mid-market B2B search SaaS (developer docs + support KB). Evaluating Redwood Hosted API first for prototypes then moving to Dedicated if perf/cost aligns. POC started 2026-02-20 focused on embeddings throughput + semantic search relevance.

Goals/targets: QPS 120 steady, peak 250; vector search p95 <50ms, p99 <80ms; overnight batch indexing ~1.2M docs within 6-8 hours. Cost sensitivity: wants predictable per-query economics, concerned about egress and cross-region charges.

Technical notes: currently testing with Pinecone + Weaviate (client-managed). Using a mix of chunked doc embeddings + hierarchical reranker. Early Hosted results: embedding throughput ~12k/sec (hosted), query latencies 60-140ms depending on vector store. Bulk ingest hitting client-side limits — need server-side streaming/batching. Redwood SE suggested Optimize tuning (batch sizes, KV caching) and Dedicate...

normalized.notes:
Snapshot: Mid-market B2B search SaaS (developer docs + support KB). Evaluating Redwood Hosted API first for prototypes then moving to Dedicated if perf/cost aligns. POC started 2026-02-20 focused on embeddings throughput + semantic search relevance.

Goals/targets: QPS 120 steady, peak 250; vector search p95 <50ms, p99 <80ms; overnight batch indexing ~1.2M docs within 6-8 hours. Cost sensitivity: wants predictable per-query economics, concerned about egress and cross-region charges.

Technical notes: currently testing with Pinecone + Weaviate (client-managed). Using a mix of chunked doc embeddings + hierarchical reranker. Early Hosted results: embedding throughput ~12k/sec (hosted), query latencies 60-140ms depending on vector store. Bulk ingest hitting client-side limits — need server-side streaming/batching. Redwood SE suggested Optimize tuning (batch sizes, KV caching) and Dedicate...
normalized.nextStep: Review POC perf matrix; finalize Dedicated pricing; security & legal SOW

title: GnomeForge Systems
body: Inbound PLG lead from indie dev + micro-startup. Founder/CTO (Elliot Park) building a tiny multi-tenant coding assistant for indie SaaS customers. Signed up via docs and quickstart; no AE outreach yet. Key points: prefers hosted API (fast time-to-market), will likely stick with public cloud. Current sandbox usage: experimenting with chat + embeddings for code search. Quote from call: "We need something that 'just works' — latency under ~250ms p50 for short chats, and simple token billing we can pass to customers." Budget: very cost-sensitive; target < $1k/mo initially, with willingness to ramp if product-market fit. Wants to test smaller open models first (Llama-family, distilled) but may use Redwood-verified models if cost/perf is right. Security: expects basic audit logs and 30d retention; not asking for VPC or private deployment now. Operational concerns raised: rate limits per sub...

title: Sunleaf AI Ventures
body:
Founder-led account. Founder: Ava Chen (ex-mobile dev). Product: personal productivity assistant for freelancers — in-app chat + contextual summarization + search over user docs. Currently prototyping on OpenAI/Anthropic; wants to migrate to Redwood hosted API if unit economics are better. Key asks:
- Clear per-token math (generation vs prompt vs embeddings). 'I need a spreadsheet I can paste our usage into and see monthly costs' — direct quote.
- Comparison table vs OpenAI & Anthropic for both cost and latency at 97th pct.
- Free credits to run a 2-week A/B between models.
- SDK ergonomics: quick Node/Python examples and streaming support.
- Concerned about retention and audit logs for future compliance; SOC2 mention is comforting but not blocker today.
Sales notes / tactics: keep close to founder; decision is fast if pricing works. Recommend 2 scenarios: low-cost quantized models fo...

title: PerchWave LLM Helpers
body:
Inbound via self-serve signup (referral from Vercel AI docs) — signed up 2026-02-24, exploring hosted API for production MVP.
Primary contact: Priya Natarajan (CTO) — small team building a Next.js + Vercel app that surfaces internal docs and code examples.
Use-case details: embeddings for semantic search across product docs; chat/code assistant in the web app; low-latency top-level chat (target < 300ms for p95).
Integration ask: concrete examples + adapters for LangChain (python) and LlamaIndex (node/python), and Vercel AI SDK example that uses Redwood hosted_api streaming responses.
Tech stack: Next.js app on Vercel (edge functions), backend analytics in Vercel serverless, vector DB = Pinecone (trial), some local python tooling for prompt engineering.
Quote from call: "We need something we can ship this week — minimal infra, good defaults, and cost guidance."
Cost sensitivity: high —...

title: Humble Harbor AIWorks
body: Inbound SMB lead from small CX SaaS. CEO/CTO duo (Lena Ortiz, Carlos Mejia) are focused on rapid cost controls after 2 months of trial. Primary ask: 'Can you show us quick wins to cut token spend without hurting quality?' Preference for hosted_api, want self-serve POC. Key items: batching suggestions, prefix caching for KB lookups, prompt compression + stop token trimming. Wants a lightweight token audit for last 50k requests. Cost sensitivity: high — target to reduce prod token spend by 30% within first month. Latency: p95 target ~200-300ms; throughput spikes up to 10 reqs/sec during peak. Quote from CTO: "If we can get a 25-30% cost improvement with minimal infra changes, we can commit to paid tier."

title: Solaris Merchant AI
body: Inbound from marketing trial signup. CTO (Aisha Rahman) and Head of Security (Tom Li) on intro call. Primary ask: Host API for product-search embeddings + personalization for storefronts. Emphasis on low-cost embeddings + <50ms query latency target for cached results. They signed up self-serve but Legal flagged data handling; sent early security questionnaire. Key asks: SOC2 report or bridge letter, full sub-processor list, DPA with EU data residency clause, KMS options for secrets. They mentioned a prior vendor took 3 weeks for DPA — want faster. Technical: traffic bursty (holiday cadence), 2–4 RPS baseline now, expect 50–100 RPS in peak during holiday POC. Cost sensitivity high — prefer quantized model suggestions and caching. From call: "We want something that scales but won't surprise our billing." Follow-ups: sent SOC2 summary (redacted) 2026-03-03; Legal requested our sub-proces...

title: Sunrise Arc VectorSearch
body:
Product lead: Marcus Hale (CTO) - wants semantic search to replace current ES + heuristic reranker
Use-case: in-app product docs + user history search; high recall + contextual rerank is critical
"We need embeddings that are cheap enough to run nightly for 2M docs and fast enough at query time for 60+ qps" - Marcus
Current infra: AWS (us-east-1), some EU customers require EU residency - evaluating multi-region VPC
POC specifics: ingest pipeline uses Spark -> parquet shards -> batch embedding jobs (PySpark). Target: complete nightly job within 3 hours
Vector DB: Proofing with Pinecone + self-hosted Qdrant as baseline. Asking about Redwood-managed vector store integrations and throughput guarantees
Performance constraints: tail p95 latency <= 120ms for semantic search at 60 concurrent QPS (embedding lookup + rerank)
Cost sensitivity: wants per-embedding unit cost and optimistic Dedicate...

title: Rivetwise Payments
body: Inbound SMB fintech. Found via conference booth and blog post. Product: chat-based support agent + merchant-facing transaction classifier. Priorities: low latency for user-facing chat (<200ms token tail under typical qps), cost-conscious (startup budget), easy SDK and quickstart. Security first asks: "SOC2 or equivalent", SSO/SAML required soon (want Okta integration), audit logs, KMS support. Team currently on AWS us-east-1. VPC curiosity: "may want dedicated VPC or private link" in Q3 but will start on hosted API. Engineering quote: 'we need SOC2 docs to move fast; can't expose PCI data until we align scope' — wants guidance on tokenization/redaction. Current POC approach: use hosted embeddings for doc search (merchant docs) and hosted chat for customer support. Key questions from SE call: model choices (small/medium for cost), streaming vs non-streaming tradeoffs, KV cache and pref...

title: HummingJar Solutions
body:
Inbound from demo form + self-serve signup. CTO (Neha Patel) created API key and ran embeddings on 3k doc subset. Likes quick results, wants simple hosted flow.

Call notes (2026-02-25, 30m):
- "HummingJar just needs a reliable doc search layer for client support and internal research."
- They tried embeddings with open-source encoder, looking at Pinecone for vector store.
- Concern: reranking stage and how to integrate BM25 + embed rerank efficiently.
- Latency sensitivity: interactive UI, target 150-300ms for embed nearest-neighbor + rerank (for single-user queries).
- Throughput: low to start (5-20 qps), expect peak bursts during client onboarding.
- Cost sensitivity: tight. Asked for example pricing for ~2M tokens/month and embedding storage estimate for ~200k vectors.
- Security: require SSO/SAML for admin console, audit logs for admin actions, KMS for keys later. No on-prem requ...

- Latency sensitivity: interactive UI, target 150-300ms for embed nearest-neighbor + rerank (for single-user queries).
- Throughput: low to start (5-20 qps), expect peak bursts during client onboarding.
- Cost sensitivity: tight. Asked for example pricing for ~2M tokens/month and embedding storage estimate for ~200k vectors.
- Security: require SSO/SAML for admin console, audit logs for admin actions, KMS for keys later. No on-prem requ...

title: Rapid Syntax Assist
body:
Summary: Rapid Syntax Assist builds an IDE copilot integrated into VSCode and JetBrains. Primary ask: low-latency streaming for line/statement completions + token budget tuning to avoid expensive long continuations during rapid typing. Team wants deterministic TTFT and predictable partial token delivery so UX doesn't stutter.

Recent timeline:
- 2026-02-12: Intro call (AE + SE). High level product fit: embedding search + streaming completions.
- 2026-02-20: Deep dive w/ CTO (Priya joined). CTO quote: "Users abandon completions if first token takes >120ms, and jitter kills flow."
- 2026-03-05: Demo of hosted API (live stream). Fireflies ff-20260305-32. Observed: first-byte 80–220ms depending on region, periodic spikes when backend batching triggered.
- 2026-03-10: Security scoping call. Need SOC2 + KMS + SSO. Fireflies ff-20260310-11.
- 2026-03-12: SE proposed POC plan: Dedicated reser...

title: SignalFoundry
body:
Summary: Mid-market product company (search + discovery in e‑commerce vertical) evaluating Redwood for relevance/rerank.

Customer goals & context:
- Replace brittle heuristic re-ranker; want ML-based rerank endpoint to rescore top-K and run offline batch scoring for weekly models.
- Primary product is an on-site search + personalized recommendations layer; rerank will be integrated in the final scoring pipeline (server-side).
- Will start on Hosted API to speed iteration, then move to Dedicated once QPS and security boxes cleared.

POC / eval criteria (explicit):
- Support for a dedicated rerank endpoint (rescore top-K lists), low per-request latency for interactive flows (target p95 <= 200ms at K=20), and cheap bulk/offline scoring for nightly model runs.
- Ability to run offline batches: need an API or job runner to score ~5M candidate pairs overnight (cost guidance requested).
- T...

title: Pelican Stone Interactive
body:
Inbound SMB trial — customer runs a mid-sized ecommerce brand. Self-serve signup, started with hosted API.

Problem summary:
- Primary concern: chat latency for EU shoppers (they're NA HQ but ~40% traffic from EU).
- Reported behavior: intermittent spikes where response round-trip hits 900ms-1.2s (per-response), causing UI drop/abandon on support popups. CTO: "users churn when reply >700ms".

What we saw / debugging so far:
- They tested from eu-west-1 endpoints and routed some requests through a CDN/proxy — adds variability.
- Default SDK timeout left at 30s; front-end gives up earlier (~5s) but perceived latency is on first byte.
- Prompts include long conversation history + 2k tokens of system/context on many requests. No prefix caching configured.
- Model in use: mid-sized open checkpoint (latency-profiled) — good throughput but not pinned to region.

SE recommendations given on c...

title: Borealis SecureAI
body:
- Initial interest: build IR assistant and internal doc search across incident logs.
- 2024-06-10 intro call: strong exec buy-in (CTO + Head of Product). AO: Jordan Hayes.
- 2024-07-21 demo: showed private deployment flow + latency/cost estimates. Customer asked for on-prem options and KMS integration.
- Security team responded 2024-08: requires full architecture whitepaper, penetration testing plan, and table-top audit before any POC.
- Key quote from Head of Security: "We cannot start a proof-of-concept until we validate offline execution and HSM key control."
- SE (Priya) supplied architecture doc and quantization notes 2024-08-05; security requested longer retention controls and proof of SOC2 evidence.
- Internal champion (Principal ML Engineer, Diego Alvarez) left the company 2024-09; project lost momentum.
- 2024-09-12 finance flagged hiring/budget freeze; procurement deprioriti...

title: Tilled Ink
body:
Inbound from blog post 'Docs search at scale' — signed up for free credits and submitted 'questions' form. Short call 2026-03-01 (ff_20260301_3456).
- Company: Tilled Ink (SaaS, knowledge search for boutique legal firms). CTO Kyle Moran; ML lead Anna Liu.
- Primary ask: self-serve Hosted API to run embeddings + lightweight reranker for site search. Volume: ~100k queries/day initially; expect growth.
- Perf targets: similarity search + rerank end-to-end <= 300ms for UI; p95 embedding latency < 100ms ideally.
- Architecture: metadata in Postgres, preference to keep vectors in Pinecone but evaluating Weaviate. Asking about recommended pipeline and SDK examples (Node + Python).
- Cost sensitivity: tight budget; wants rough monthly cost estimate for 100k q/day + 500k monthly embedding ops. AE provided ballpark but asked for detailed cost model.
- Security: SOC2 mandatory, admin SSO require...

title: Pinegate Helpbotics
body:
Inbound via self-serve signup (trial) -> AE outreach. Primary contact: Zoe Patel (Head of Platform). Small engineering team building a hosted chat widget for SMBs. Security is first gate before usage expansion.

Key ask: quick SOC2 snapshot (scope, cert date, auditor), full list of subprocessors (analytics, storage, monitoring), and Redwood DPA that they can review with their counsel.

From call 2026-03-03: "We like Redwood latency but need to show SOC2 and who sees logs — our compliance officer will pull the trigger only after DPA approved." Zoe also asked about token retention and ability to opt out of logs for PII.

Technical requirements: average session length ~120 tokens, bursty traffic 50 req/s during peak, latency target 150-250ms p50 for chat. Cost-sensitive — wants hosted per-token pricing and guidance on batching benefits.

Notes shorthand: quick win candidate if SOC2 + DPA...

title: Copperlark Software Solutions
body:
Summary: mid-market SaaS (product analytics) building a contextual support + doc search assistant. Narrowing path: start Hosted API to validate feature, then shift to Dedicated for predictable throughput and cost. Team is cost-sensitive and cares about p95 latency and unit economics.

Recent convo highlights:
- CTO (R. Meyer): "We need consistent 50ms p95 for UI snappiness; can tolerate quantization if quality stays within threshold."
- PM: concerned about monthly token bill spikes, asked for batching/caching levers and concrete cost model.
- SE asked: prefer models with KV cache friendly profiles; want recommendations for quantization profiles that preserve entity extraction accuracy.

POC details:
- Workload: user-facing chat + background reranker (embeddings) for doc search. Peak: 220 RPS during internal load tests; expected steady 40-70 RPS.
- Targets: p95 < 60ms (interactive), 95...

title: Goldenport AI Inference
body: Status: CLOSED-LOST to Replicate (3/15/24). Summary: Goldenport evaluated Redwood for real-time bidding and reranking across ad campaigns. Primary drivers: cost-per-request economics and ability to self-host within their existing infra. Key quote from Head of ML: "Replicate's self-hosted workflow and predictable infra cost made the decision easy." Security: had SOC2 & SSO requirements; Redwood provided artifacts but timeline for compliance review was longer than Goldenport's procurement window. Pricing: Redwood hosted pricing looked competitive for short POC, but projected ARR and long-term op-ex favored Replicate. Security: had SOC2 & SSO requirements; Redwood provided artifacts but timeline for compliance review was longer than Goldenport's procurement window. Pricing: Redwood hosted pricing looked competitive for short POC, but projected ARR and long-term op-ex favored Replicate. Internal dynamics: no senior eng champion to drive cross-team procurement (data infra team stretched). Blockers encountered: procurement budget freeze until Q3, security review raised additional encryption-at-rest questions (asked for HSM/KMS diagram), and competing pilot w...

title: Stonebridge Merchant AI
body:
Summary: Opportunity has high technical fit (real-time reranking + chat assistant for merchant support) but is stalled in procurement/legal. Key issues: 1) Stonebridge legal returned aggressive DPA/MSA redlines asking for unlimited indemnity carveouts, data deletion guarantees beyond our standard, and on-premise audit access clauses. 2) Procurement vendor questionnaire backlog — procurement requires an up-to-date vendor security assessment; they flagged need for ISO evidence and completed penetration test report. 3) Internal champion (Head of Data, Mateo Alvarez) accepted competitor role 2/24; sponsorship weakened. 4) Finance mentioned Q3 budget freeze; cost is solvable if procurement clears, but timing looks risky.

What we've provided: SOC2 report (2024), architecture diagram with KMS flow, POC cost runbook, sandbox logs. Pending items requested by Stonebridge: signed DPA with their...

title: Skyward Botworks
body:
Inbound SMB lead via self-serve signup (used free credits). Concerned about logs & prompt storage — CTO (Ellen) asked "how long do you retain prompts and can users opt-out of retention?". Key excerpts from call:
- wants explicit opt-out flag so end-users can choose not to have their prompts retained for 30/90/365 day windows.
- asks whether prompts are used for model training or shared with other tenants (answer: hosted API: no training on customer data; need to provide citation).
- needs retention SLA example they can paste into vendor security questionnaire.
- cost sensitivity: low spend initially, scaling to $1k+/mo if latency good.
- latency target: p95 under 600ms for small chat sessions, throughput modest (peak 20rps).
- prefers public-cloud region us-east, but may ask for eu residency for EU customers.
- SE demo scheduled but wants data-retention excerpt before demo to share wi...

title: Saffron Bench Labs
body:
Inbound self-serve signup from marketing email — created a trial org on 2026-02-18.
AE intro call 2026-02-23 (FF-20260223-2201): CTO + Head of Ops attended. Primary ask: fast integration for chat-based order support.
Early discovery: they want hosted API for speed-to-market, but legal is concerned about data persistence and prompt storage.
Key quote from CTO: 'If you store prompts tied to customer PII we can't move forward — need opt-out and deletion guarantees.'
Questions raised by ops/security: retention timeline for logs, whether prompts are stored in cleartext, how long embeddings are kept, and if there's an API to purge a user's data on request.
They asked whether Redwood supports per-customer retention windows and whether we provide a 'no-logs' option for specific routes.
SE showed hosted retention defaults (30 days) and policy variations during call; customer requested written ...

title: Copper Vector SMB, LLC
body:
Background: Copper Vector runs a lightweight D2C storefront + help center. Primary features planned: chat-based customer support, answer-finding over docs (doc-search/embeddings), and short-order-summary emails (summarization). They signed up self-serve to test hosted API. Engineering team uses Node.js SDK and a mix of cron jobs for nightly vector indexing.

Pain / ask: CTO (Jon Park) is worried about variable token spend — "we have a few power users and the token bill spiked during promotions" — wants concrete levers to keep costs predictable while retaining latency for live chat. Asking for: batching/caching patterns, prompt compression tips, and a token-usage audit template they can run against logs.

Technical notes: current flow is per-message chat calls from the browser proxied through their backend (no KV cache yet). Nightly embedding job processes ~2M small items/month — looki...

title: Orchid Gleam Solutions
body:
Inbound self-serve signup (organic from blog). Trial created 2026-02-18, API key issued same day.

Summary: SMB martech startup building an in-app help & template assistant for ecommerce merchants. Interested in lightweight hosted API — quick time-to-value, cost predictability. Primary POC: customer support snippets + contextual KB search.

Usage pattern: generated keys, ran a few sandbox calls (first 24h spike ~150 tokens), then near-zero activity for two weeks. Reported no error but low traffic; says they are testing 'LumenAI' due to promo credits.

Quotes: "Love the latency numbers but need to see unit costs at 100k tokens/month — currently a little higher than their promo offer." — CTO (Ethan R.)

Security/ops: asked about data retention and audit logs; willing to use public cloud. No immediate SSO requirement for trial.

Recent activity / timeline:
- 2026-02-18: self-serve signup...

title: DevProbe Assist
body:
Inbound from developer tools startup building an in-editor code assistant. Self-serve signup last week, ingested ~120k tokens during trial (backend + browser streaming). Team: CTO (ex-platform eng), 2x ML engineers, 3x frontend. Primary asks: stable hosted API with predictable burst limits, websocket/streaming SDK for low-latency token-by-token updates, long context (100k tokens) support or efficient chunking/embedding fallback, cost caps on per-session token usage.

CRM-style snippets:
- 2026-02-22: signup via website, trial credits applied, spike in token usage observed (trial session with 60k-context run).

title: Fusewell Labs
body: Founder-led discovery. Founder Maya Patel (ex-SupportOps) reached out after self-serve signup. Primary ask: pricing clarity and head-to-head cost/latency comparison with OpenAI/Anthropic. Use case = in-app support assistant + KB search. Prefers hosted API for fast iteration. Volume estimate: 1k active users, peak concurrency ~30, expected 50-150k tokens/day. Latency target: <250ms token latency (p50) for chat flows; throughput moderate. Cost sensitivity high — founder said "if we can't beat OpenAI cost per interaction we can't justify switching". Security: basic SSO + audit logs acceptable for now; legal later may require SOC2. Founder flexible on model choice but wants Redwood to show model variants and fallback routing. Requested pricing doc, a short latency benchmark, and code sample for embeddings + search. Founder's timeline: wants decision by mid-April if numbers look good. CRM ...

title: BrightWeld Systems
body:
Founder: Jonas Park (ex-dev tools startup) - building developer onboarding assistant + docs search for customers.
Primary ask: clear hosted pricing (per-token + monthly baseline) and how caching affects billing.
Quote: 'We need to know our worst-case bill when usage spikes — can't have surprises when we go live.'
Comparing: currently evaluating OpenAI and Anthropic for cost/latency. Biggest friction w/ competitors = opaque enterprise minimums and cold-start latency variability.
Latency target: chat <= 200ms p95 for short queries (seq len ~128).
Throughput: modest - dozens of concurrent users initially; want autoscaling bursts up to ~300rps for brief campaigns.
Model preference: open-models if cost-effective (gpt-like quality), but will consider Redwood catalog if pricing beats Anthropic on mid-sized payloads.
Security: SMB but requires SSO for internal admin, audit logs for compliance...

title: Syntactic Sparrow
body:
Company: early-stage code-assistant startup, seed-funded, 3 devs + 2 ML engineers
Primary pain: model latency for interactive completion and maintain very long context across sessions
Quote from CTO: "We need consistent 80-120ms median latency for 512-token completions and ability to store 50k-100k token buffers per user."
Asks: explicit SDK examples for streaming + reconnect, token-window support, client-side caching strategies
Cost sensitivity: high — founder asks about predictable spend, prefers per-request pricing with clear per-1000-token costing
Traffic profile: bursty dev/test at night, daytime interactive sessions ~50 RPS peak across users (projected)
Security: wants audit logs and retention controls now; SAML may be requested for enterprise customers later
Technical constraints: public-cloud only for now (no VPC/on-prem), prefer us-west region, no air-gapped needs
Integration...

title: Poppy Legal AI
body:
Inbound via docs site trial (self-serve signup) — CTO created account 2026-01-18.
Tried hosted API (generate + embeddings) with small contract corpus (1.2k docs) — initial latencies acceptable for SMB workload.
Primary ask: early security questionnaire before procurement — SOC2 report + list of sub-processors + DPA with EU data residency clause.
Quote from CTO: 'We need a DPA we can hand to our legal counsel. SOC2 report will clear 80% of concerns.'
They are NA-based but have EU clients; data residency clause optional but helpful.
Cost sensitivity: SMB budget, looking for usage-based hosted API; likely $5k-$15k ARR first year if terms acceptable.
Technical: wants token-level audit logs for admin console, ability to rotate API keys (KMS integration optional), retention controls discussion.
Security team requested: sub-processor CSV (names, regions, purpose), SOC2 type II summary, conta...

title: Starry Harbor Bots
body:
Inbound signup via marketing site 2026-02-09 — created free account and tried quickstart chat demo.
Discovery call 2026-02-12 (Ava): product manager Maya (founder) + eng lead Tom joined. Primary pain: reduce average handle time for live chat and consolidate FAQ embeddings.
POC ask: 2-week trial to validate hosted API latency and cost with 10 concurrent agents; target p95 latency < 200ms for reply generation (short contexts).
Cost sensitivity: small seed-stage startup, want to stay on credit card but finance would accept invoice if > $5k/yr. Quote requested for both CC and invoice flows.
Security: asked for SOC2 summary, SSO options — currently use Okta; want SAML SSO for admin console later.
Procurement quote: "Can we get a startup discount + extra credits?" — asked on call; mentioned YC alumni but not confirmed.

title: Seafold Insights
body:
Summary: SMB SaaS search startup building a lightweight RAG feature for legal and policy docs. Inbound from blog post about embeddings + reranking. Interested primarily in Hosted API for speed to market; may consider Dedicated later if usage grows.

Technical priorities: - End-to-end PoC: ingest ~200k docs (PDFs + HTML), produce embeddings, store in Supabase (currently) or Pinecone. Wants examples for Supabase->Redwood pipeline. - Reranking flow: coarse retrieval via vector DB (top-50), then dense re-rank with a reranker model or hybrid BM25 + embedding score. Asks about best practices and cost tradeoffs. - Latency targets: 90th pctile for user queries < 400ms for retrieval+rerank ideally; OK to accept 500-700ms for complex queries during PoC. - Throughput: expected 1k-5k queries/day at launch, scaling to ~100k/mo in 6-9 months.

Model preferences / constraints: - Prefer open/hosted s...

title: VelocityScribe Marketing AI
body:
Inbound MQL from blog post + demo CTA ("Lowering LLM costs for marketing teams"), signed up for self-serve trial 2025-01-20.
Product lead (CTO) Vikram Patel — cares deeply about unit economics. Quote: "We generate ~2M tokens/day during peak campaigns — cost is everything."
Primary workload: high-volume generation of ad copy, landing page variants, and email subject lines. Also nightly batch runs to produce 100k+ short variants for A/B tests.
Wants easy SDK + examples for batching and prefix caching; asked about automatic KV caching or persistent cache between tokens.
Prefer hosted/self-serve initially; possible shift to Dedicated later if predictable throughput is needed.
Security: needs SOC2 proof and SSO (SAML) for enterprise customers they onboard. Also wants audit logs for generated content history.
Interest in streaming responses not critical; more concerned about throughput and ...

title: Verdant Foxplay
body:
Lead source: inbound self-serve signup (playstore promo). Small indie studio focused on synchronous mobile social games. Wants hosted API to avoid infra ops.

Key asks / quotes:
- 'We need chat that feels instant — target p95 <100ms from mobile clients, ideally ~50ms in region NA.' — CTO (Maya).
- Concerned about spam/abuse in open lobbies; wants moderation + built-in profanity filtering and rate limiting.
- Willing to start with hosted, open to Dedicated later if cost/latency justify.

Technical preferences / constraints:
- Primary clients are Unity (mobile) + Node backend for relay. Need example Unity SDK + websocket/streaming guidance.
- Low budget first year; cost sensitivity high (peak concurrent players small but bursts matter).
- Wants prefix caching for NPC/bot flows and per-player embedding cache for personalization.

Sales/ops notes:
- Gave AE demo account and 10k free-credi...

title: ContentFlux AI Studios
body:
2026-02-25: Inbound sign-up from marketing campaign; trial credits used; demo requested.
2026-03-01: Intro call (30m) w/ Ava (AE) + Diego (SE) — product fit looks promising for high-volume email + landing page copy.
Customer quote: 'we need 20M tokens/month at lowest possible cost; latency < 300ms for short prompts' — concerned about per-token pricing.
They rely on template-based generation; high repetition — interested in prefix caching + batching to save cost.
Security: wants SSO + SOC2 baseline; needs audit logs for later procurement but not blocking onboarding.
Technical: uses AWS us-east-1; will start with hosted API, may move to Dedicated if scale justified.
POC plan: 2-week smoke test (10k requests/day), measure cost per output, validate caching config.
Action items: SE to share example batching config and expected cost delta; AE to send pricing deck + T&Cs.

title: LichenLoop Technologies
body:
Founder-led inbound (Maya Lin, CEO/CTO) — early discovery. Interested in Redwood hosted API for chat + embeddings; lean, cost-sensitive startup. Key points from 2026-03-03 call (Fireflies ff_20260303_lichenloop_discussion):
- Company background: launched beta of dev docs search and onboarding assistant in Jan. Team 7 engineers, Node.js backend. Expecting 5k monthly active users first two quarters.
- Primary workload: chat support widget (low concurrency, frequent short-turn messages) + backend embedding index for product docs (batch upload + incremental updates).
- Targets: average response latency <250ms for chat on p95; embedding indexing throughput not critical (>100 reqs/hr ok). Cost sensitive — founder quoted: 'If tokens cost us 3x OpenAI for embeddings we'll struggle.'
- Wants straightforward per-token pricing, clear quotas/rate limits, and predictable monthly bill (prefers usag...

title: PromptSmith Devs
body:
Lead source: inbound signup + community forum question. Small devtool startup building an in-editor code assistant (VS Code & Web IDE). Wants hosted API self-serve path but needs quick answers on long-context support and rate limits.

Key asks / requirements:
- Long context: want to pass file/repo-level context up to ~500k tokens total (expect to chunk/stream). Interested in Redwood's prefix/KV cache — "would prefer to cache repo prefixes and only stream deltas" (quote from CTO).
- Rate limits: expecting spikes from early adopters; typical steady traffic 200-500 reqs/min, burst to 2k reqs/min during product launches. Needs guidance on throttling and per-route rate caps.
- SDKs: heavy preference for ready-made Node + TypeScript SDK; also Python. Wants streaming SDK support for chunked responses and ability to cancel tokens.
- Cost sensitivity: early-stage, price-conscious. Looking for ...

- Long context: want to pass file/repo-level context up to ~500k tokens total (expect to chunk/stream). Interested in Redwood's prefix/KV cache — "would prefer to cache repo prefixes and only stream deltas" (quote from CTO).
- Rate limits: expecting spikes from early adopters; typical steady traffic 200-500 reqs/min, burst to 2k reqs/min during product launches. Needs guidance on throttling and per-route rate caps.
- SDKs: heavy preference for ready-made Node + TypeScript SDK; also Python. Wants streaming SDK support for chunked responses and ability to cancel tokens.
- Cost sensitivity: early-stage, price-conscious. Looking for ...

title: Papyrus Nook AI
body:
SMB dev-first startup building an in-app conversational assistant for small retailers.
Inbound self-serve -> qualified for discovery call. Mostly public-cloud usage; do not need VPC today.
Primary pain: perceived latency from EU users.
Customer quote on call: 'Feels snappy for US, but EU customers hit timeouts in chat, losing sessions.'
They use our JS SDK in a single-page app with default client-side timeout (10s). Asked if they should increase or move region.
SE note: obvious win is region routing (serve EU users from eu-west or eu-central), adjust client timeout to 30s and enable streaming for perceived latency.
Perf recommendations provided: shorten prompt preamble (send only last N turns), use system prompt compression, enable streaming, set retry with exponential backoff, consider token limit enforcement on client to avoid very long prompts.
Cost sensitivity: small budget, will ...

title: Dandelion Deskline
body:
2026-03-05 - Inbound signup via hosted-api free tier; form field: 'privacy, retention, opt-out'
2026-03-08 - Kickoff call (FF id ff_20260308_1830). Attendees: COO (Lina Park), Head of Product (Ethan R.), AE (Ava), SE (Marcus).
Customer voice: 'We need to know what you store and for how long — cannot retain customer prompts beyond a short window; some customers require 7-day max.'
Primary workload: conversational support assistant + ticket summarization. Expected volume: 50-200 API calls/day initially, spike ~1k/day if successful.
Key ask: explicit opt-out for storing raw prompts; ability to purge specific convo history on-demand via API or support within 24 hours.
Security asks: SOC2 report, audit logs for all API calls, KMS integration for keys, and a customer-facing retention policy link.

title: Copperlark Sparkworks
body:
Small ecommerce brand (DTC) with ~30 employees. Heavy customer support volume during product drops.
Primary pain: token bills from current OpenAI usage spike; looking for straightforward levers to cut cost without doubling latency.
Wants self-serve path — will prefer Hosted API & Optimize suggestions over Dedicated for now.
Use-case details: chat-based customer support + product search embeddings for storefront. Typical convo length 10-35 messages, average user session ~220 tokens input + 450 tokens model response (current).
Customer quote from call: 'We love the accuracy but can't sustain these bills — need <50% of what we're spending now.'
Asks: batching for high-throughput QPS during flash sales, prefix caching for repeated order-status queries, prompt compression/rewrites to trim token counts, quick token-usage audit of their current logs.
Technical constraints: single-tenant stor...

title: HelpWeave Cloud
body: Inbound from product hunt post + demo request via signup form. CTO (Maya Patel) reached out asking for a self-serve path: minimal ops, hosted API, quick credit card onboarding. Primary ask: ship a ticket-summarization feature that integrates with their existing ticketing microservices. Wants structured JSON output (summary, severity, recommended_actions) and function/tool-calling so the model can emit a payload that our backend will POST to /tickets/update. Cost sensitive — prefer smaller hosted models or aggressive token caching. Quote from call: "We need something cheap and dependable — can you give us a sandbox key we can hit from our staging environment?". Okta SSO is company standard; SOC2 Type II in procurement checklist. No hard data residency requirement; everything can stay in us-east. Interested in Redwood hosted API for fast time-to-value; will evaluate Dedicated later if v...

title: Sidecar Labs
body:
Lead type: PLG self-signup (indie dev). Founder: Maya Gonzalez (former product designer). Signed up 2026-03-01, redeemed free credits.

Summary / ask: building a tiny collaborative assistant inside a Next.js SaaS. Wants hosted API (no infra). Prefers open model family but needs predictable latency and unit cost. Prototype scope: chat + embeddings for ~10k short docs, search fallback, server-side and client-side short prompt calls.

Technical constraints / targets: p95 latency target ~300-400ms for chat (short prompts), expected steady traffic 2-8 rps initially, burst to ~20 rps at peak; embeddings workload ~200 requests/day. Cost sensitivity high — budget ~$100/month early. Wants suggestions for batching + caching.

What we tried so far: redeemed credits, integrated JS SDK, ran into CORS error when calling from browser (likely missing allowed origins). Tested a couple of open models f...

title: OliveQuay Support
body:
Lead source: self-serve signup (trial credits) 2026-02-26.

Summary:
- SMB support platform for e-commerce merchants; ~120 employees, 50 support agents planned for pilot.
- Primary interest: low-friction hosted API for chat + embeddings (agent assistant + internal doc search).
- Target latency: 150-300ms median per response for short-turn chat; throughput estimate 5-10 RPS during peak.
- Cost sensitivity: moderate — looking for predictable per-token pricing and burst/bandwidth behavior.

Security/Compliance ask (early):
- Asked explicitly for SOC2 Type II report, DPA template, and full sub-processor list. "We need to show our legal team where data goes and who touches it." — Mark Chen (Head of IT).
- Questions on: encryption at rest (KMS), encryption in transit, log retention and access controls, ability to redact PII, retention/deletion APIs.
- Wants confirmation: we run hosted infer...

title: StrandNote Creative Bot
body:
Lead source: inbound from content-marketing webinar + CTA on blog (template scaling). Quick summary:
- Company: SaaS platform that generates marketing templates at scale for SMB customers (newsletters, ad variants, landing page copy).
- Volume profile: spikes during campaign runs; expected steady-state ~5k-15k generation requests/day, some days spiking to 50k+ short bursts. Typical prompt lengths: 50-200 tokens; output 60-300 tokens.
- Primary ask: low-friction hosted API; want predictable per-request cost and latency (p95 < 500ms for single-turn generation).
- Cost sensitivity high: "we can't afford $0.02/1k tokens for steady 10s of millions tokens/month" — asked for batching/quantization recommendations and examples.

title: Silver Octet Studios
body:
- Indie dev shop: 2 founders (product + frontend). Building a lightweight writing + code-assist web app shipped on Vercel. PLG self-serve signup; no AE outreach yet.
- Primary intent: host a low-latency chat assistant for short completions (<=200 tokens) and embeddings for instant doc search.
- Founder quote: "We care about predictable p99 latency and keeping per-token cost low during viral spikes". Wants a sample cost calc for 10k MAU.
- Tech stack: Next.js + Prisma, stores embeddings in Postgres; calling Redwood hosted API from serverless functions.
- Security: minimal for now (SOC2 interest, audit logs nice-to-have). Public cloud only, no VPC/on-prem required.
- Current metrics: small dev cohort, test load produced ~1M test tokens/day, avg latency ~110-130ms for short prompts. Embedding recall looks promising; sent sample eval.
- Ask: short onboarding playbook, example prompt libra...

title: Wickham CloudPrompter
body:
2/20 inbound: signed up for free tier, connected API key, ran basic chat test — latency looks good
2/25 discovery call (ff_2026-02-25_01): CTO Mark Wickham + Head of Ops Sara. They run a small helpdesk app for SMB retailers.
They are very cost-sensitive — expect low volume initially (100k tokens/month) but plan to scale seasonally.
Main ask: hosted API (self-serve) but legal/security team will run questionnaire before upgrade.
Quote from Mark: 'We like the fast onboarding — but legal will need the SOC2 and a DPA before we push to prod.'
Security asks enumerated on call: SOC2 Type II report, list of sub-processors (CDN, KMS, logging), DPA template, PCI/PII handling notes.
SE note (Ravi): recommend Redwood hosted_api with token-level audit logging + prefix caching off during POC to simplify logs.

title: Hyperbridge Consumer Insights
body:
Customer: large retail analytics firm, heavy emphasis on sub-100ms query latency for live personalization
AE notes: strong product fit but procurement & infrastructure teams prefer minimizing external managed vendors
SE notes: POC performance looked good but integration with org KMS policies was major friction
Customer quote (infra lead): 'We're consolidating on Vertex/SageMaker for inference; operational simplicity > small perf gains',
Observed: decision driver moved from cost/latency to vendor consolidation + security policy during vendor rationalization
AE attempted remedy: proposed Dedicated with customer-managed KMS and private VPC deployment; CIO did not greenlight additional vendor onboarding
POC ask trimmed twice to reduce scope and cost; final trimmed ARR estimate ~ $120k, but still blocked by cloud-spend freeze
Competitive pressure: procurement RFP explicitly included manage...

title: Sunberry Labs
body:
Initial inbound PLG lead from community article. Small indie team (2 devs). Primary product: lightweight code-assistant + help chat for their micro-SaaS. Wants low integration friction and predictable per-request cost.

Callouts from last support exchange:
- 'We're prototyping a live code assistant for ~200 active users, 2k queries/day target' (Ava)
- Concern: 'If usage spikes during launch week, token bills could blow past our budget'
- Technical: currently running on Vercel, calling Redwood hosted API from serverless functions; prefers public cloud and simple API key flow.

Eng asks: recommended batching and caching patterns for prompt templates, how prefix/KV caching works for chat.

Marketing/PLG motion: good candidate to nurture with cost-optimization doc + small usage credits for first paid month. Low chance for Dedicated or Private. Offer SE office hours if they escalate to SSO...

title: Indigo Spark Ops
body:
Lead profile: early-stage ecommerce analytics + customer support tool. Wants to add conversational support widgets and semantic product search. Signed up self-serve, already integrated SDK on staging.

Quotes / call highlights:
- 'We want something that just works, low lift to integrate' (CTO).
- 'Billing is the main hangup — our finance team prefers invoices for commitments > $1k/mo' (Founder).

Procurement / pricing notes:
- Interested in hosted API (self-serve) but asking about invoice option vs CC.
- Asked about startup discount and whether credits can be extended beyond initial 90 days for the POC.
- Cost sensitivity: high; target < $0.006/token effective for generative chat during POC.

Technical preferences / constraints:
- Workloads: chat for support-agent, embeddings for semantic-search, occasional summarization of long tickets.
- Latency target: P95 < 400ms for short chat re...

title: Bronze Pennant Tech
body:
Founder: Samir Patel (ex-CTO at small ecomm SaaS). Lean team (3 devs).
Primary goal: replace rule-based chat response system with lightweight LLM-based support assistant to cut triage time.
Wants hosted API only initially — self-serve with predictable, low friction billing.
Key ask: clear per-token math including embedding costs, any bandwidth or concurrency surcharges, and discounts on committed usage.
Founder quote: 'I need to understand the delta vs OpenAI — both cost and output quality — in plain numbers.'
Latency target: <200ms median for short chat turns; acceptable tail to 400ms for longer context.
Throughput: ~50 concurrent users at peak, average 2–3 tokens/sec per session.
Model preference: open to mid-sized open models (GPT-like) if cost/latency tradeoffs are favorable; also curious about Redwood-verified profiles.
Fallback routing: simple fallback to smaller model for cost ...

title: Onyx Orbit Solutions
body:
Summary: Mid-size logistics SaaS (TMS + marketplace). Interested in embeddings for catalog and product-search, personalization and a chat support assistant. Strong preference for private networking (VPC/PrivateLink) and strict egress controls.

Key quotes/points: 
- CTO (R. Patel): "No internet egress for inference — must be PrivateLink or provable VPC peering; otherwise can't proceed."
- Head of Infra: Roadmap for PrivateLink support from Redwood flagged as 'Q4 or later' — this is a hard blocker.
- Procurement: Budget hire freeze announced for Q3; POC spend needs PO pre-approval now.

Engagement summary (shorthand):
- Apr 02: Intro call (Ava) — high-level architecture fit, asked for PrivateLink timeline.
- Apr 12: Technical deep-dive (Diego + infra) — shared POC plan, ran sample embeddings on hosted; infra requested VPC/PrivateLink option. (FF-20240412-8273)
- May 08: POC results (Dr...

title: Cranberry Drift, Inc.
body:
Inbound SMB lead — small product company building a customer-facing chat and intelligent search. Currently trialing hosted API free tier. Key points:
- Workload: chat for customer support + embeddings for product search. Early PoC: 10k monthly active users simulated; bursts to ~30 rps. Latency target: p50 <200ms, p95 <500ms.
- Cost sensitivity: high. Wants to understand per-token cost and batching/caching tips. Asked for startup discount and credits extension.
- Procurement: prefers credit card but may want invoice if commit >$5k/mo. Finance needs invoice threshold and net-30 terms.
- Security: SOC2 baseline, SSO via SAML, audit logs required for support team access. No on-prem requirement; public cloud acceptable.
- Model preferences: open to Llama- or Mistral-family variants; wants Redwood recommendations for quantized variants to save cost.
- Quote from PM: \"We can prototype in tw...

- Procurement: prefers credit card but may want invoice if commit >$5k/mo. Finance needs invoice threshold and net-30 terms.
- Security: SOC2 baseline, SSO via SAML, audit logs required for support team access. No on-prem requirement; public cloud acceptable.
- Model preferences: open to Llama- or Mistral-family variants; wants Redwood recommendations for quantized variants to save cost.

title: Pinecrest Inference Co
body:
Short summary: SMB (support SaaS) evaluating hosted API for customer-facing chat assistant + internal summarization. Primary concerns are legal & security — asking for SOC2 Type II report, full list of sub-processors, and a countersigned DPA before spinning up a paid pilot.

From call: 'We love the latency on the demo, but legal won't sign off until we see SOC2 and a sub-processor roster.' — Head of Ops.

Technical: expected p95 latency target ~400-700ms for short-chat (<512 tokens) flows; peak throughput estimate ~15-25 req/s during morning windows. Cost sensitivity: trying to keep monthly inference spend under $3k during pilot.

Current status: AE sent standard DPA and link to SOC2 artifact (internal) + exported sub-processors list. Security asked for KMS/encryption-at-rest details and data retention options. SE (Ravi) to confirm token retention window and whether logs are redacted ...

title: Sparkquilt Labs
body:
- Founder-led discovery (CEO: Lena Park) — technical founder, building chat-first product for creators.
- Inbound via self-serve signup; tried free tier then hit rate limits during small load test.
- Primary ask: transparent pricing and economics vs OpenAI/Anthropic. "If cost per 1M tokens is similar, we can move fast."
- Workload: multi-turn chat, 8k token context windows periodically, embeddings for content search.
- Latency target: p95 < 400ms for short responses (<=128 tokens); okay with higher latencies for long generation.
- Throughput: steady 10–50 rps peak, bursty usage afternoons; cost-sensitive (pre-seed runway).
- Preference: hosted API on public cloud; not ready for Dedicated/Private.
- Security: SOC2 preferred, SSO optional — not a hard blocker.
- Evaluation: comparing quality and unit economics vs OpenAI (gpt-style) and Anthropic (Claude-style).
- Technical call notes: v...

title: Saffron Edge Intelligence
body:
Summary: Went from demo -> procurement quickly once RTB POC showed latency improvements, but ultimately lost to customer's decision to consolidate on hyperscaler-managed endpoints (AWS Bedrock + ECS).

Key context and quotes:
- 2024-11-12 demo with Eng/Prod ops: real-time scoring POC hit target (p95 < 45ms) on Redwood Dedicated. Team impressed by batching and KV-cache results. (ff_2024-11-12_874)
- Procurement/update call 2025-01-20: procurement pushes for single-vendor consolidation. CTO: "We have a multi-year AWS commit and want minimal vendor sprawl." (ff_2025-01-20_1120)
- Security review: Redwood passed SOC2 checklist but legal flagged additional wording for KMS/HSM and enterprise billing controls; procurement said AWS already has templates in place so lower friction.
- Champion churn: initial champion (Head of ML Ops) promoted out in Dec; no strong replacement to drive cross-org...

title: Merry Metrics LLC
body:
Inbound self-serve signup Mar 2 — CTO (Lina Park) signed up with free credits and started pilot.
Initial discovery call 2026-03-03 w/ Lina + Sr. Eng (Jordan) — want quick wins on cost.
Primary ask: reduce per-conversation token spend. Mentioned "batching/caching", "prompt compression", token audits several times.
Current setup: embeddings per message for rerank + chat context ~3k tokens avg -> expensive.
Target traffic: ~8-12 chats/minute during peak; monthly token volume estimate 4-6M tokens (current pilot already ~1.2M in 2 weeks).
Latency SLO: p95 chat < 400ms (they are fine with 2-3x latency for background rerank jobs).
Model preference: prefer smaller open models (for cost) with ability to fallback to larger model for complex queries. Open to Redwood model catalog recommendations.
Security/compliance: small SMB — SOC2 not required yet, but need audit logs and US-only residency fo...

title: RapidLeaf Supportworks
body:
CRM notes (shorthand):
- SMB inbound from product hunt promo, signed up self-serve.
- Use case: automating first-line support + triage; heavy short bursts when weekly newsletter lands.
- Traffic pattern: steady 50-200 req/min normal, spikes to 5k-8k req/min for 2-10 min windows.
- Primary ask: how do we avoid 429s during short marketing spikes? prefer graceful degradation over hard failures.
- Quota concerns: want predictable spend — need rate-limit ceilings per key + an easy way to request temporary burst increases.
- Concurrency: worried about per-connection concurrency limits affecting streaming replies; asked if streaming requests count against concurrency pool.
- Suggested solution discussion: prefix/KV caching, adaptive throttling, retry-after headers, dynamic fallback to smaller model variant for overflow, token-based budget alerts.
- "We can’t have customer-facing 429s during ...

title: KettleByte Retail
body: SMB inbound via self-serve signup. Interested in hosted API only — low-friction integration into existing Rails backend. Primary use: chat-based customer support + product recommendation snippets. Latency target ~<200ms tail for short prompts, 50–200 reqs/min expected initially. Cost sensitive; will start small and expand. Key friction: privacy/retention questions from customer success and legal — they asked specifically: "Do you store user prompts?", "Can users opt out of logging?", "How long are logs retained and where are they stored?". They flagged PCI/PHI risk threshold — said they will scrub PII but want opt-out and retention controls. Would like sample privacy appendix and 30/90/365 day retention options. Mentioned competitors: "we tried OpenAI — worried about prompt retention; need clear opt-out." AE noted: keep messaging simple: Hosted API + short retention TTL + configurable...

body: Inbound from blog post (scaling content series). Self-serve sign-up last week; used free credits for small PoC. Wants hosted API — emphasis on low operational overhead. Primary ask: get per-campaign cost estimates and caching/batching knobs. Team is very cost sensitive: daily batch runs that create 50-200K tokens per day in peak. Quoted: 'We need predictable spend; can't have surprise spikes.' Interested in prefix caching (repeated intros/templates) and KV cache reuse for newsletters. No enterprise procurement yet — founder + 2 engs + content ops evaluating. Security: acceptable with audit logs and retention controls; SSO not required now. Wants a 48-hour stress test with burst >10 rps and examples of batching configurations. Engineering note: prefers REST + simple SDK, expects to pipe outputs into existing template engine. Requested pricing deck and example rate-limit settings. AE de...

title: Buttercup Botlane
body:
Inbound self-serve signup from product Hunt link. Created org and requested first API key on 2026-01-11. Issued key, onboarding email sequence sent. Very low usage after key issuance — ~5 requests/day, mainly simple hello world calls.

AE note 2026-01-20: customer says initial integration worked but they hit token budgeting confusion and swapped some traffic to competitor (OpenAI) because of promotional credits. Quote from founder: "We liked the latency but were nudged by credits and an out-of-the-box connector from our analytics tool."

SE did light debug 2026-02-03: found they were using synchronous retry wrappers that trip over rate-limits, recommended client-side backoff and smaller batch windows. SE proposed sample batching config; customer acknowledged but low priority.

Security: small team, wants SSO later. Asked for SOC2 summary and audit logging sample — docs shared (drive l...

title: Cerulean Markets Ltd
body:
Short: Cerulean is a retail-tech platform focused on personalized product experiences. Good technical alignment: need VPC, KMS, SOC2 evidence, and committed capacity pricing options. POC designed to validate end-to-end latency and embed refresh throughput. 

Customer comments/snippets: 
- "This is a priority when we can re-hire the ML infra engineer" (Head of Product). 
- "Loved the latency numbers in demo, but we aren't able to add new contracts this FY." (Finance) 

Security: security team reviewed our SOC2 summary and KMS options; no technical blocker identified. Procurement: paused for company-wide vendor freeze. Internal champion moved teams in Feb which removed sponsorship. 

Sales actions taken: responded to security doc requests, provided three committed-capacity pricing scenarios, offered a short-term trial option to avoid long commitments. Trial idea declined due to procurem...

title: Sunnyvale Chatica
body:
Inbound via website signup — CTO (Priya Rao) demo request
Primary product: in-app AI assistant for B2B SaaS dashboards (customer success + quick answers)
Latency is critical: quoted goal <200ms p99 for streaming messages; 50ms median desired
Using GCP (us-west1) for app hosting today; prefer public cloud hosted API — want minimal infra ops
Cost sensitivity: small startup budget, wants predictable per-token costs; interested in batching suggestions
Model preference: open models with streaming support; will consider Redwood model catalog for verified perf
Security: SOC2 required eventually, currently self-hosted auth via Firebase; audit logs desired
Quote from CTO on 2026-03-02: 'We need streaming that's actually real-time — users notice 200ms vs 1s.'
Founder asked about SDK examples and client-side streaming sample; also asked about cold-start times and warm pools
PoC plan: 1) try host...
