title: RamenByte Solutions
body:
Lead source: inbound signup (self-serve) + trial credit used. Small engineering team building a docs + KB search and an in-product assistant for their developer customers. Interested in fast integration examples for LangChain and LlamaIndex, plus a Vercel AI SDK adapter so they can deploy prototype on Vercel Edge Runtime.

Highlights:
- POC ask: replace current OpenAI embeddings -> Redwood embeddings for site search; expect ~100k queries/month.
- Latency target: <200ms tail for single-doc embedding/semantic search; important for UX.
- Cost sensitivity: high — wants guidance on batching, caching, and model/quantization tradeoffs.
- Model preference: open models (Llama-family) and Redwood-curated small models for cost; ready to try quantized variants.
- Security: SMB but asked about audit logs and simple residency controls; no enterprise SSO yet.

Quote from call: "We want a palate-swap...

title: WillowStride Analytics
body:
Lead source: inbound signup (product trial). CTO (Maya Chen) initial call 2026-03-02; wants low-friction POC on Hosted API. 
- POC scope: chat-based customer support assistant + semantic search for KB (50k doc corpus).
- Metrics wanted: <250ms p50 generation for chat; 40-60 reqs/sec sustained during business hours; token cost target ~$0.0015-0.003/token (very cost-sensitive).
- Model preference: open-source medium-sized models (7B-13B) if performance viable; will consider 70B only if latency and cost constraints met.
- Routing: prefer single-region (us-east) public cloud for simplicity; no current need for VPC/on-prem.
- Security: require SSO (SAML) and basic audit logs; not a heavy compliance customer yet.
- Procurement: default plan is credit card on file for self-serve; team asks if invoice is possible for early-stage startups (they prefer card but want invoice option if ARR grows)...

title: Pipeline Poet Technologies
body:
Inbound from founder (Elena Morales) after trial signup. Key points from 11 Mar call:
- Company: small CI/CD + release notes automation startup. Want to add conversational assistant for changelogs and a searchable index of release docs.
- Workload: mix of short chat completions (in-product assistant) + embeddings for doc-search. Low QPS initially (10-50 reqs/min).
- Latency/throughput: soft target <300ms 95p for chat; not latency-critical for embeddings. Cost-sensitive — planning to optimize batch size and caching.
- Models: prefer open-model variants for cost control but ok to test Redwood catalog. Interested in recommendation on quantized endpoints.
- Procurement: "We signed up with CC but Finance may want invoice/PO if annual >$5k" — founder asks for clear CC vs invoice path. Also asked about startup discount and extension of free credits (current trial ends in 2 weeks).
- Security...

title: PixelPint.io
body:
Inbound PLG signup via hackathon listing (March Indie Hackathon) — used free credits immediately.
Founder: Maya Lin (ex-frontend at tiny fintech). Building a tiny SaaS: in-app marketing copy + tiny code-assistant for product templates.
Tech stack: Next.js on Vercel, Node SDK, client-side streaming for UX. Deployed to public cloud only.
Usage so far: ~200k tokens over 3 days (mostly generation + embeddings for small search). Card on file (self-serve).
Latency target: <300ms p50 for interactive chat flows; willing to accept 500ms p95 during cold starts.
Cost sensitivity: high — unit economics tight. Wants recommendations on batching/kv-cache to reduce token spend.
Model preference: open-ish models (smaller, fast), but will try Redwood curated models if perf/cost tradeoffs are clear.
Security: lightweight. Wants audit logs and ability to purge data; no SOC2 reqs yet. Interested in retent...

title: StitchNote Marketing Engine
body:
Lead source: webinar — 'Scaling content gen on a budget'
Founder (Marin): 'We run 50M+ captions/month seasonally; cost is primary blocker'
Shorthand: likes hosted API for fast time-to-value; not ready for dedicated until ARR > $50k
AE notes: emphasize batching, KV/prefix caching, and optimistic token estimates
SE notes: prepared small sample script for continuous batching using Node SDK; to send before POC
Legal: initial Qs on data retention + residency — answered, wants short note for contract
Quote from call: 'If you can show 2x cost reduction vs current provider we'll move forward'
Next communications: send quickstart link + caching guide + pricing vignette by 2026-03-11

Founder (Marin): 'We run 50M+ captions/month seasonally; cost is primary blocker'
Shorthand: likes hosted API for fast time-to-value; not ready for dedicated until ARR > $50k
AE notes: emphasize batching, KV/prefix caching, and optimistic token estimates
SE notes: prepared small sample script for continuous batching using Node SDK; to send before POC
Legal: initial Qs on data retention + residency — answered, wants short note for contract
Quote from call: 'If you can show 2x cost reduction vs current provider we'll move forward'
Next communications: send quickstart link + caching guide + pricing vignette by 2026-03-11

title: Nimbus Slate SearchCo
body:
Inbound lead from blog post on 'cheap reranking for customer support' + contact form on product docs.
Primary workload: customer support knowledge base (PDFs, KB articles) + internal research search. Expecting ~250k docs initially, growth to ~1M.
Volume/latency targets: p95 < 300ms for short queries; baseline throughput ~50-100 qps, spiky during business hours.
Cost sensitivity: SMB budget, want to compare token pricing + embedding unit costs. Interested in Redwood Optimize suggestions (batching + cache).
Model preferences: open to Mistral/Redwood-verified small models for first pass; want reranking with a larger (but cheaper) model fallback. Asked about automatic fallback routing.
Vector DB: currently on managed Milvus; evaluating move to Pinecone. Asked: how tightly coupled is Redwood to specific vector DBs? Want examples for Pinecone + Milvus connectors.
Security: require SSO/SAML ...

title: Windward Nook Search
body:
Lead came from product docs 'search + rerank' page — filled brief form referencing 'small legal KB'
Customer quote on demo: 'We need good precision on short answers; hallucination is a big blocker for legal content.'
AE notes: user is comfortable with public cloud only (no VPC/on-prem), wants SOC2 copy before POC
SE notes: sample data size ~250k docs; prefers embeddings refresh cadence daily; concerned about embedding dimensionality and index cost
Pricing sensitivity: asking for a 3-month POC budget estimate; likely $10k-$25k ARR if POC succeeds
Requested artifacts: SOC2 report, SSO integration guide, example Pinecone + Supabase adapter snippets, sample prompts for reranker

title: CopperWind Systems
body:
Inbound lead via docs example -> small engineering team building B2B support chat
Primary goal: replace rule-based routing with model-backed intent+function calling for order lookup and KB search
Using hosted API, prefer public cloud; cost-conscious — want guidance on token usage / batching
POC uses streaming mode to render tokens live in UI; they enable 'json_mode' to force structured output
Problem description (quote from dev): "When we stream, the client tries to parse partial JSON and fails: 'Unexpected end of JSON input' — works fine when not streaming."
SE notes: schema defined for functions: order_lookup(params:{order_id:string}) and inventory_check(params:{sku:string, include_backorder:bool})
Observed: model sometimes emits trailing commas, or emits newline + partial object ('{"order_id": "123"') then finishes later -> client parse fails mid-stream
Also observed function-calli...

title: BirchLoop Helpline
body: Initial inbound via self-serve signup + product email drip. Small ecommerce CX startup building in-chat support and knowledge base search. User quotes: "we saw lots of 429s with our previous vendor during promo spikes — customers get dropped and CS load doubles". Wants clear guidance: per-minute concurrency, per-second token rate, retries/backoff recommended. Interested in automatic fallback to cheaper model on spikes and webhook alerts when nearing quota. Wants simple admin UI to raise burst limits for weekend campaigns. Priorities: low latency for chat, predictable cost, minimal engineering ops. Risk: if expected traffic causes 429s they will pause rollout. Action items agreed: send rate-limit best-practices doc, sample concurrency configs, short POA for POC (2 weeks), and offer temporary increased burst for campaign day.

title: Cobalt Pegasus Search
body:
Inbound from product page (doc search). Small legaltech startup building RAG-powered contract search + clause discovery.

Lead: CTO (Aisha Rahman) trialed free credits; uploaded 120k docs to staging. Interested in hosted self-serve — wants to avoid infra ops.

Pain points:
- current vector DB (hosted Milvus) works but rerank quality inconsistent; want retrieve-then-rerank pipeline.
- cost sensitivity: want to understand per-embedding + per-token pricing at projected 50k queries/month.
- latency SLO: target 150-300ms 95p for single-doc retrieval+rerank; burst to 50 qps.
- security: SOC2 + SSO required for upcoming pilots; some EU customers ask about residency.

Technical asks from call on 2026-03-03 (ffly_20260303_1893):
- example flow: embed -> vector DB -> topK -> cross-encoder rerank on hosted API (cheaper hybrid?)
- supported embedding dimensions and model mapping for compatibility...

title: Sparkline Devtools
body:
Lead: Sparkline Devtools — small devtool startup building an in-editor code assistant for teams. Primary pain: high-context windows (40k-65k tokens) to handle multi-file repositories and cross-file references. Came in via self-serve signup; good product fit for Hosted API if we can demonstrate cost controls and rate-limit behavior.

Shorthand from calls:
- "We need the model to see entire repo slices — not just single file" (CTO).
- Wants TypeScript-first SDK examples, streaming examples for editor integration (partial tokens & cancel handling).
- Concerned about per-token cost for CI/e2e tests and internal micro-benchmark runs. Needs guidance on batching + cache strategies.
- Security: SMB but customer wants SSO later; immediate ask is audit logs + retention controls and assurance on public-cloud residency.

POC goals: demonstrate stable latencies <250ms p50 for small completions; sh...

title: HollowGrove Assistant Co
body:
Inbound SMB; B2B SaaS building in-app chat assistant for customer success and onboarding flows.
Primary priority: streaming experience that feels instantaneous; front-end shows tokens as they arrive.
Quote from founder: "Users drop off if assistant waits more than 200ms to start streaming."
Current stack: React web app + Node backend; experimenting with Redwood SDK (hosted) via quickstart.
They prefer open models for cost but will consider Redwood-curated variants if latency improves.
Data residency not a blocker now; planning SOC2 + SSO before any contract > $10k ARR.
Cost sensitivity: will monitor token spend closely during growth; interested in Optimize recommendations for batching and caching.
POC milestone: replace staging inference calls with Redwood streaming endpoint and measure TTFB and p95 latency under 200 concurrent users.
Suggested approach from SE: reduce prompt size, ac...

title: Lodestar APIWorks
body:
- Inbound from webinar signup (self-serve). Onboarding team building a lightweight chat widget.
- Primary ask: how Hosted API handles bursts/429s — want predictable UX on onboarding spikes.
- Ben (CTO): "We can't have new signups bring the site down with 429 storms. Need short grace for initial onboarding and clear headers for client backoff."
- Concern: free-tier + onboarding email campaign will create short 2-5min traffic surges (5x normal). Need burst allowance w/ throttling not hard-block.
- Wants: per-key concurrency limits, per-route quotas, Retry-After semantics, webhook alert for quota threshold (90%), console visibility for token consumption.
- Security: SOC2 expected by audit team; SSO required for admin; audit logs mandatory.
- Demo 2026-03-02: walked through quota UI; SE showed per-route quotas + soft-burst prototype. CTO liked sample Retry-After header and incremental bac...

title: Needlepoint Interact
body:
• Lead came via self-serve sign-up (trial credits used) — completed quickstart with JS SDK.
• Early discovery call 2026-02-18 (FF-2026-02-18-03A7). AE Jordan + SE Priya.
• Primary use-case: in-chat product recommendations + backend search augmentation for merchant storefronts.
• Workload: chat + embeddings (real-time suggestions, reranking). Expecting bursts at 200–400 RPS during sales peaks.
• Latency target: p95 < 250ms for generation endpoints; embeddings async acceptable up to 1s.
• Cost sensitivity: SMB budget-conscious; looking to estimate per-month tokens ~10–30M.
• Security ask (early): SOC2 report access, DPA signed, full sub-processor list, KMS/HSM support not required now.
• Residency: prefer US region but may require EU for enterprise merchants — want clarification on EU-only processing for certain customers.
• Current blocker: legal sent DPA request and short questionnair...

title: Maple Gleam
body:
CRM notes:
- Early-stage B2B: builds in-app assistants for SMBs (SaaS helpdesk + product guidance).
- CTO (Maya R.) on call — sounded pragmatic: "need streaming that feels instant; users bail at >400ms".
- Product lead wants native streaming UI (token-by-token) and ability to interrupt generation.
- AE (Ava) suggested quick POC: 1) sample keys + script, 2) 1-week trial with 50k tokens, 3) run against their retrieval set.
- Cost sensitivity: freemium model; if unit cost too high, will shard heavy ops to smaller models.
- Security: SOC2 required downstream for customers; currently enough to start on public cloud but legal asked for DPA.
- Quote from CTO: "We want sub-250ms first byte for prototype, otherwise UX suffers."
- Good candidate for Hosted API self-serve -> likely upgrade to reserved capacity only if growth justifies.
- Suggested materials shared: streaming best-practices, toke...

title: Sienna Hollow Assist
body:
Context: SMB, PLG motion. Using hosted_api in public cloud, wants predictable per-token billing. Low tolerance for engineering time on infra.
Primary workload: conversational support bot that calls internal tools (order_lookup, ticket_create) via function-calling. Also uses structured JSON mode for downstream parsers.
Reported failure pattern: Node stream emits chunks where JSON object is split across events -> customer's parser attempts JSON.parse(chunk) and fails with 'Unexpected end of JSON input'.
Customer quote: 'We need deterministic JSON frames — our runtime expects each message to be a complete JSON object or a stable framing.'
Function schema issues: some function responses omit optional fields, sometimes return snake_case vs schema camelCase, and we saw mismatches where function.parameters.required includes fields not present in model output.
They use TypeScript on backend, ...

title: Verdant Lane Boutique
body:
Inbound lead: small direct-to-consumer apparel + home goods shop. Signed up via hosted API free tier; testing product Q&A widget for product pages and batch enrichment for missing descriptions. Primary constraints: cost + bursty traffic (weekend promos), limited engineering time (1 FE dev and 1 backend).

Use-case details:
- Product Q&A: expected traffic is bursty — 100–400 qps during promos, baseline ~10 qps. Wants responses fast for UX snippets: target P95 <200ms for search/autocomplete and <300ms for long-form Q&A.
- Catalog enrichment: run nightly job to generate short descriptions and normalize attributes. ~6k SKUs, update pipeline must be idempotent.

Model preferences / routing:
- Prefers smaller open models (quantized) for cost; willing to use larger model for fallback on complex queries. Interested in automatic fallback + prefix caching.

Security / compliance:
- SMB but asks...

title: SparkGlen AppWorks
body:
Inbound self-serve signup 2025-11-05 via quickstart; auto-provisioned key + free credits
Initial activity: ~150k tokens first week (chat + embeddings), then usage fell to <2k tokens/week
AE outreach 2025-11-12: discovered they're building in-app assistant for mobile app — latency important, but micro-call cost is a blocker
Security ask: SOC2 evidence, SSO (SAML) support and retention controls — security team flagged before moving forward
Competing trials: primary eval against OpenAI (promo credits) and Anthropic; dev noted OpenAI felt cheaper for small requests
Technical feedback: devs confused about recommended batching/prompt caching patterns; want sample code for continuous batching and KV-cache hints
Customer quote: 'Great onboarding — but we can't justify the unit cost until we see batching savings or a short credit window', paraphrased
Current disposition: low engagement, likely...

body: SMB dev tools startup. Building in-doc assistant + API-driven search for product docs and code snippets. Primary stack: Next.js on Vercel (serverless edge), Python backend for indexing. Wants hosted API (self-serve) but needs fast integration examples. Asks specifically: 1) LangChain chains + Retriever example using Redwood hosted embeddings, 2) LlamaIndex loader/reader snippet for their markdown repo, 3) Vercel AI SDK example for server actions and streaming in docs UI. Quote from CTO: "We need a 20-min starter that our engineers can drop into a PR." Cost sensitivity high for embeddings-heavy workload — asked for batching/kv-cache guidance. Security: must support SAML SSO and audit logs; exploring KMS for customer keys later. title: Lodestar Vertex Solutions
body: CRM notes (shorthand) - inbound SMB lead; primary contact: Maya Reed (Head of Product). Wants a fast POC: searchable knowledge base + conversational support widget embedded in Vercel site. Tech stack: Next.js on Vercel, backend serverless functions, some Python scripts for offline embedding jobs. Key asks: 1) quickstart showing LangChain + LlamaIndex connecting to Redwood hosted API (examples for both Node and Python), 2) Vercel AI SDK adapter snippet that uses streaming responses from Redwood, 3) guidance around batching/embedding cost vs latency, recommended model variants and quantization suggestions from Redwood Optimize, 4) note on rate limits and best practices for token caching. Maya quote from 3/7: 'Need something we can ship in two weeks — show me code, not slides.'

title: Sagehaven HostedFlow
body:
Inbound SMB lead via self-serve signup. Team: 6 engineers + 2 product. Primary product: small-business-facing helpdesk (ticketing + AI first-responder) — plan to add answer-summarization + reranking for KB search. Signed up, used free credits, ran initial stress tests and hit 429s.

Call highlights (03/08 tech screen):
- "We need predictable behavior during morning spikes — mornings and release windows create 10-20x bursts for 2-5 minutes." — Head of Eng.
- Typical steady traffic: 5-15 requests/minute. Burst profile: 50-250 requests in short windows (30-180s) during product updates and email sends.
- Latency targets: p95 generation for short prompts < 500ms; okay to route larger jobs to async queue.
- Cost sensitivity: high — want fallbacks to cheaper models when load spikes and automatic routing to cheaper variants.

Technical concerns raised:
- Received intermittent 429s during expl...

title: Verdure Loop
body: Inbound SMB signup via web form. Trial API key issued 2025-01-18. Initial goal: reduce CS response time and add product-based recommendations in checkout. Low adoption after key — integration stalled at basic intent routing. Team is cost-sensitive; mentioned "can't justify new spend until conversion impact is clear". They tested competitor free tier for NLU; liked latency but found hallucination rates problematic. SE call 2025-01-22: demo'd quick embedding-based search with node SDK; dev said 'looks promising but we need sample metrics on tokens/cost per session'. AE follow-up 2025-02-04: offered 2k credits, low uptake. No SAML/VPC needs; public cloud okay. Quote from founder: "We moved fast to test — but devs are swamped, need a turnkey example to show value to ops."

title: DevSherpa Labs
body:
Inbound from lead form: devtool startup building a context-aware code assistant. Wants hosted API + SDKs. Key asks: support for very long conversation/context windows (project-level context spanning multiple files), predictable per-user rate limits, easy TypeScript + Python SDK, streaming completion plus chunked embeddings. Interested in prefix/KV caching to reduce token costs. Team is cost-conscious but values dev DX. 

Call 2026-02-18 w/ Founder (Sami Patel) + CTO (Leah Park) — demo run-through. Quote from Sami: "We need the assistant to keep ~200k tokens of project context and surface relevant code snippets across repos. If the model forgets state, we lose trust."

Technical notes: primary cloud = AWS (us-east-1). Want simple API key flow for early beta, SSO later. Concerned about rate limiting for CI-style batch usages (hundreds of requests/min during indexing) vs interactive edit...

title: Indexly Labs
body:
(Chopped notes from calls/emails)
- Lead: Mateo Alvarez, CTO. Early-stage SaaS (help docs + onboarding). Primary ask: replace noisy FAISS prototype with hosted embeddings + a simple reranking layer.
- Quote: 'We want something plug-and-play — embeddings + a cheap reranker — without managing GPUs.'
- Technical constraints: currently indexing md docs in Postgres; prototype uses sentence-transformers local; looking at Pinecone/Weaviate but cost unclear.
- Questions asked: preferred embedding dims (512 vs 1536) tradeoffs; batching recommendations for 2–4k docs initial import; how to run reranking cheaply (two-stage: vector recall then small LLM rerank vs hybrid TF-IDF+rerank); sample latencies for hosted_api embedding endpoint + text-generation for 512-d vectors under p95.
- Cost sensitivity: target < $2k/mo for prototype (embedding + reranking traffic), willing to scale after product-mar...

title: SparrowBurst Systems
body:
Inbound SMB lead via docs widget. Self-serve trial started 2026-02-20. Key ask: how does Redwood handle traffic spikes, 429s, and temporary concurrency increases for flash sales. They run a merchant chat widget that sees sharp traffic during promotions (10x baseline). Concerned about conversion loss if users hit 429s. Cost-sensitive: wants predictable burst billing and a simple fallback story.

Call 2026-02-25 w/ CTO + Eng lead: discussed default quotas, per-key concurrency, and soft vs hard 429s. We demoed exponential backoff advice and suggested route-level rate limiting. CTO: "If our chat starts returning 429s during promo peaks we lose trust — need safe fallback to degraded flow, not outage."

Technical asks: transient quota lift API, real-time quota monitoring, per-route SLOs, token-based cost forecast for 48-72 hour spike. Prefer public cloud hosted API for speed to market. Will...

title: Copperleaf CX Automation
body: Status: STALLED — POC not started. Summary: After positive product demo (chat + reranking for triage) we could not lock on POC success criteria nor secure engineering/resourcing from Copperleaf. Security escalation required on-prem or VPC + KMS + residency in us-east; they flagged FedRAMP/ISO as must-have for certain enterprise customers. Business side said ROI looks good, but finance put a hiring/budget freeze in place for Q1 FY26. Internal champion (Head of AI Ops, Mateo Ruiz) left company in Jan '26 — replacement TBD. Competitor (Anthropic Enterprise) provided a short-term pilot commitment + heavy discount; Copperleaf is leaning toward that for quick go-live. Repeated outreach: 3x emails, 2x calls, one calendar decline. AE note: "They liked our performance numbers but couldn't guarantee SRE support for a private POC until next headcount cycle." POC requirements unresolved: measurab...

title: Clearshelf Retrievals
body:
Lead created after self-serve signup and initial free credits run. Customer uploaded a ~2GB doc corpus (PDFs) to S3 and ran embedding job locally. Wants hosted API for speed-to-prod; evaluating integration with existing vector DB (currently running Pinecone) and considering Weaviate for cheaper storage.

Key fragments from calls/emails:
- 'Search results need to feel authoritative — hallucinations are a deal-breaker.'
- 'Primary flow: user asks, we return passages + reranked list + confidence score.'
- SE asked about embedding model: they prefer mid-sized embedding (cost vs perf) — asked about Redwood recommended model (text-embedding-3-small vs text-embedding-3-large).
- Latency/throughput targets: 150-300ms p50 for single-document search, target 30-50 qps at peak; cost sensitive for SMB.
- Data residency: not strict, US-only preferred but not mandatory.
- Security: SOC2 required, SS...

title: PetalWave Interactive
body:
Lead source: inbound self-serve sign-up (trial credit). Product contact: CTO/Co-founder Aisha Rahman. Use-case: embed product docs for in-app support + lightweight conversational assistant for onboarding flows. Early priorities: latency under 300ms p50 for short chat turns, embeddings for semantic search across ~50k docs, cost-conscious — will likely start on lower tier and optimize.

Key CRM notes / shorthand: 
- 2026-03-01: Self-serve signup with CC, claimed trial credits, ran initial tests against 'open-debug' model. Hit token limits and observed 500ms p50 on dev instance.
- 2026-03-02: Discovery call (Maya + Samir) 30m. Walked through hosted API, model catalog, burst capacity. CTO asked about invoice option: "Our finance team prefers invoice for monthly charges > $1k — can we do NET30?"
- 2026-03-04: Demo of embeddings workflow. Shared sample index + recall numbers: 0.78 at top-5....

title: Saltcove KnowledgeOps
body:
Lead source: doc-search quickstart template (public cloud) — organic inbound
Primary workload: embeddings for short legal docs (~1-2 pages), reranking candidate set 10-50
Latency target: p95 < 250ms for embedding+search lookup (they know SMB constraints)
Throughput: light — ~200 queries/day now, potential to spike to 2k/day during trials
Model prefs: open/reproducible embeddings (smaller footprint). Asked about Redwood verified models and whether they can bring their own embeddings model
Vector DB: currently evaluating Pinecone (POC started), asked about first-class connectors and sample code for Pinecone + Redwood hosted embeddings
Reranking: prefer a 2-step retrieval — vector search + lightweight reranker model. Asked if Redwood hosted API supports returning candidate context + reranker call pattern
Data sensitivity: customer contracts may include PII; wants audit logging + SSO. No ...

title: Lintree Sparkworks
body: Inbound SMB lead, marketing origin (blog + webinar). Trial created; first API key used once in week 1 then almost no traffic. CTO Evan Morales: "we like the latency and simple SDKs but need clearer per-route cost and tokenization examples". AE Jordan Kim did 2 short demos showing hosted API endpoints — interested in self-serve but finance flagged billing language. SE Aisha Rahman ran a quick perf test; observed 120ms p50 for single-turn chat (positive). Main friction: customer confused by free-credit application and how billing maps to routes (chat vs embeddings). title: Timberline SupplyChain Analytics
body:
Account snapshot: pilot originally scoped Nov 2025 for reranking + embeddings across inventory signals. Strong technical fit: prefers Dedicated for predictable throughput and VPC for data residency. Key stakeholders: CTO (Marta Li), Head of ML (Daniel Osei), Dir. Procurement (R. Campos).

Recent timeline:
- 2025-11-20: Intro call (Jordan/SE). High-level product fit; agreed to tech deep-dive.
- 2025-12-03: Technical demo (Priya). Discussed batching, KV cache, quantization. Follow-up asked for perf numbers at 50 req/s.
- 2026-01-11: POC kickoff call (Fireflies ff_2026-01-11_8h4g2k). Agreed to 4-week POC: reranking on order-matching workload.
- 2026-01-25: POC progress sync. Initial throughput OK, cost estimate higher than expected.
- 2026-02-15: Procurement/legal requested SOC2 + KMS attestation; provided security FAQ (drive link).
- 2026-02-20: Execs communicate hiring freeze across ML...

title: Meadowlark Interfaces
body: SMB inbound from marketing widget; lightweight engineering org (frontend-heavy). Primary ask: 'We want a drop-in example to get knowledge base Q&A running on Vercel + Next.js using LangChain or LlamaIndex.' They prefer hosted_api for now (no Private). Key constraints: run from Vercel Edge / Serverless, low mem budget per function, latency target ~200-300ms for first token, embeddings throughput ~20-50 qps cost-conscious. Requests: JS/TS quickstart repo, Vercel AI adapter recommendation, sample prompt engineering for product-recs, migration notes for when they scale to Dedicated. Security: need SOC2 attestation and SSO plan (Okta). Quote: 'If you give us a one-click copyable example that works with Next.js + Vercel we can roll it into our codebase this week.' Timeline: POC by end of month. Next: send repo, confirm time for walkthrough. Contacts: CTO Maya Lin, Frontend lead Aaron Cho (s...

title: Glowthread Works
body: Inbound self-serve signup from solo CTO Elliot Ramos building a lightweight code-assistant for indie devs. Signed up 2026-02-20 and created API key. Wants hosted API only for now - 'don't want infra burdens, just reliable latency'. Primary workload: dev assistant (short-context code completions) + occasional semantic search via embeddings. Traffic: low concurrency, bursty. Latency target: median <250ms for short-turn completions. Cost sensitivity: high (hobby budget). Model preference: small Llama-family / Redwood curated small models for cost; wants fallback to larger model on edge cases. Requires streaming + function-calling for editor plugin prototype. No VPC/on-prem. Security: basic audit logs acceptable; mentions SOC2 if company hires. Quotes: 'If the API is predictable and cheap, I'll build the plugin and tell my indie circle.' Prototype: v0.2 VS Code extension, 3 alpha testers....

title: Sienna Ports Solutions
body: Inbound SMB. Team builds a consumer-first chat feature (iOS + web). Signed up self-serve, granted credits. Key observations: first API key issued but usage stalled after initial smoke tests. Dev feedback: Redwood JS SDK bundle pulls in polyfills - inflates apk size; asked for tiny snippet for REST calls. Business: price-sensitive, wants predictable per-month spend ~5k-15k ARR. Security: SOC2 acceptable; no SSO yet. Barriers: billing card decline + perceived latency vs competitor. Sales posture: early, low-touch; signal is warm but slipping to competitor. Suggested tactical actions: 1) send trimmed SDK example + minimal fetch snippet for iOS/web; 2) offer 1-week credit top-up and waived first month for SMB commit; 3) provide single endpoint latency report for NW US region and a small benchmark comparing token p95. CRM-style entries below.

title: PebbleGrain SearchLab
body:
Inbound SMB lead from blog post on embeddings (organic). Contact: Maya Patel, CTO <maya@pebblegrain.com>. Use-case: contract repository search + client-facing summary generation. POC goals and constraints:
- Data: ~100k documents, avg 1.5-2k tokens/doc, mix of PDFs and text extracted from contracts.
- Perf targets: p90 retrieval+rerank latency <200ms; peak throughput ~50 qps during business hours.
- Cost: high sensitivity, want cost estimates for embeddings + token generation; asked about capping spend for POC.
- Stack: Postgres metadata + S3 storage; planning vector DB (currently evaluating Pinecone / Milvus / Weaviate).
- Specific asks from technical call (03/09): sample bulk ingest notebook, recommended embedding dim and model, recommended reranker model, guidance on prefix/KV caching with hosted API, SDK example for streaming rerank.

title: Glasswell ChatWorks
body:
Company background: mid-stage SMB (SaaS helpdesk) building a chat-first support assistant — 50-70 agents, customer base 10k+
Primary pain: monthly Hosted API spend has crept up; they're seeing spikes when training/refreshing embeddings and during ticket surges.
Quoted from CTO Markus: "We're okay with public cloud, but token cost is the only thing stopping scale — show us concrete levers."
Environment: all public cloud, self-serve signup done, using API keys; client uses short-lived sessions and sends full conversation history on every call (inefficient).
Observed behaviour (from sample logs): avg conversation length 450 tokens; they resubmit entire KB context for reranking + embed on every query. Estimates: ~3.2M tokens/month currently (rough estimate from partial logs).
Customer asks: batching for small messages, prefix KV caching for chat sessions, prompt compression (templates + s...

title: Sundial Burgeon Systems
body:
Lead source: inbound via docs + quickstart; technical contact: lead dev (Rina Patel)
Primary ask: make streaming chat + function-calling robust in their single-page app frontend
Symptoms: UI JSON.parse errors when streaming JSON mode -> partial chunks arrive ("Unexpected end of JSON input").
They use function schema with required properties; sometimes model returns 'null' for optional fields and their parser chokes.
Customer quote: "We want streaming for snappy UX but not if it means parsing chaos on intermittent network drops."
Current implementation: client collects 'delta' tokens and feeds to JSON.parse once it sees closing brace; this fails when model emits additional metadata chunks or uses trailing commas in streaming fragments.
SE notes: suggest using Redwood streaming event types (event: 'message' / 'done') or application-level envelope "{"chunk":...,"final":false}" to avoid r...

title: Kythera MedSearch
body:
Post-demo silence. Demo attended and product fit was positive (search + rerank + chat for clinicians). Key asks: HIPAA controls, KMS, on-prem option or VPC, data residency in us-east, tokenization/PII redaction. They explicitly asked for quantized model recommendations and cost estimate for 1000 qps inference (typical clinician load).

Current state: Legal/InfoSec placed hold — heavy backlog + demands for SOC2 report copies and encryption-at-rest details. Internal champion (Head of Data Science) changed teams last month -> no clear owner to push pilot. Budget conversation paused due to org-wide hiring/budget freeze through Q2.

Competitive pressure: they have an existing relationship with Azure and are evaluating Anthropic for safety features. Prospect comment on demo: "liked the observability and rollout features" but: "we need security sign-off before procurement."

Suggested approa...

title: InkBloom Innovations
body: Self-serve inbound. Indie dev duo (founder + eng) building a paid coding assistant plugin for VS Code. Signed up 2026-03-01, tried hosted API with toy prompts. Quote from founder: "latency under 250ms for completions is a hard constraint for UX". Prefers public cloud — not ready for Dedicated or Private. Cost-sensitive; wants guidance on batching + token estimates. Hit default quota limit after ~300 reqs/day (trial). Saw occasional 429s and one 503 during spike; wants SLA info for production. Interested in embeddings later for repo search. No AE touch yet, just automated welcome flow. Wants simple example for streaming to UI and a snippet showing cost estimate per completion. Will consider upgrade to paid monthly once initial plugin has 100 DAU.

title: Peachgrove Retail Tech
body:
Inbound via website trial credit redemption. Dev lead (Marco, Sr. Eng) spun up key routes and ran small traffic (30k tokens/day) — wants to understand how to get token cost down before converting. Primary asks: batching for chat endpoints, prefix/KV caching for session-based support, prompt compression + template tokenization, monthly token audit and recommendations. 

Call 2026-03-02 w/ Liam, Marco, Ops:
- Marco: 'we can move fast but need clear ROI on switching routing/quant profiles'
- Wants example token savings on their average session (4 prompts + convo history ~1.2k tokens)
- Concerned about latency impact of batching during peak (holiday spikes)

Current config: using hosted API, default model, no batching, no caching. Typical SLA target 300-500ms p50 for live chat; cost target: reduce per-user inference cost by ~50% to be sustainable for freemium to paid conversion.

Suggeste...

title: Sparkling Elm Studios
body:
Lead from product team building an embeddable knowledge assistant for client docs and support. Small dev team (2 engineers + 1 PM) — shipping on Vercel (Edge Functions / Next.js). Signed up via self-serve; wants hosted_api for simplicity.

What they asked for / priorities:
- Concrete example repos: LangChain + LlamaIndex adapters that target Redwood Hosted API, specifically examples that run in Vercel Edge and Vercel Serverless functions.
- Streaming + SSE in Edge runtime (they report 'no chunks / single payload' when testing).
- Recommendations for prefix caching and batching for doc-search workload; cost sensitivity.
- Quickstart that shows embeddings pipeline -> index -> query with low token usage.

Quotable from call: "We don't want to manage infra — we need a tiny example we can drop into our Next.js app and ship this week. Also, streaming needs to be snappy for UX."

Tech notes ...

title: SparkDock Labs
body:
Background: small YC-ish startup building a VS Code + web companion dev assistant. Heavy emphasis on multi-file context (codebase context + PR diffs). Typical session context: 50–150k tokens (they chunk + fetch from embeddings). Currently prototyping with a mix of open models and OpenAI; pain points: rate limits, cost on long context, streaming integration for editor, and per-user rate limiting.

What they asked for: short turnaround, self-serve Hosted API that scales with spikes; example JS/TS SDK, websocket streaming example for editor, guidance on prefix caching and batching to keep latency <350ms for small prompts and <2s for code completions that touch cached context. Also wants developer-friendly quota primitives (per-user, per-route).

title: Riverbend SurgeAssist
body:
Inbound SMB lead from Product Hunt; self-serve signup converted to trial (credits used).
- Product: lightweight chat widget for ecommerce support (React widget). Goal: replace canned KB answers + outbound agent triage.
- Trial behavior: bursts of traffic in mornings (08:30-09:30 PST) + peak after campaigns -> observed intermittent 429s and queued requests. Frontend team reported ~200 concurrent websocket channels, many short-lived.
- Key ask: guidance on Hosted API rate limiting, burst handling and quota tuning. Wants sample client-side throttling + exponential backoff patterns and recommended concurrency targets for their plan size.
- Constraints: public cloud only (no VPC yet), cost-sensitive (target < $0.0006 per token average), minimal infra ops.
- Security: SOC2 is nice-to-have; they need audit logs for support escalations. No strict data residency requirement.
- Model preference...

title: Sprocket Hush Analytics
body:
Inbound SMB lead. Signed up self-serve last week using free credits. Primary product interest: hosted_api (early PoC). Main workload: a support assistant that streams partial answers and calls internal tools for account lookups (tool-calling + structured JSON output). Customer reports intermittent failures when using JSON-mode + function-calls in streaming: sometimes receives partial stream with invalid JSON (trailing commas / missing closing brace), sometimes the function call payload arrives as a string instead of parsed object. They are on a Node.js front-end -> server relay model; experimenting with browser streaming which hit CORS/connection-reset several times.

Customer quote: "Streaming works 85% of the time but when it fails it breaks our downstream parsers — we need deterministic JSON from function calls."

Initial contact (2026-02-22): quick discovery call with Omar (AE) an...

They are on a Node.js front-end -> server relay model; experimenting with browser streaming which hit CORS/connection-reset several times.

Customer quote: "Streaming works 85% of the time but when it fails it breaks our downstream parsers — we need deterministic JSON from function calls."

Initial contact (2026-02-22): quick discovery call with Omar (AE) an...

Usage pattern: 2 quick sanity calls, then no substantial traffic. 90% of calls are 1-2 token completions (warmup).

title: Marigold Promptworks
body:
Inbound self-serve signup (trial credits used). Early discovery call with Ava on 2026-02-20. Product: small dev team building an in-app code assistant + support bot. Wants low-latency hosted API to avoid infra ops. Procurement: prefers credit card but asks whether invoice is possible once ARR > $5k/mo. "Startup" — asked about startup discount and potential free credit extension past trial. Team is cost sensitive; will test with 3k-5k tokens/day.

Key quotes: "We want something plug-and-play — no infra headaches. If billing is simple we can scale quickly."

Risk profile: SMB, high product-market-fit for prompt tooling, limited GRC resources. SOC2 is important for customers; they require SSO for eval environments. No on-prem need now.

Commercial hints: recommend 3 month extension of free credits to cover current POC, offer startup promo code (if eligible). Provide clear CC vs invoice t...

title: Citrus Dock Analytics
body:
Inbound SMB lead via partner referral. Self-serve signup 2026-02-10, used free credits, quickly hit higher-than-expected token spend. CTO (Miguel) worried about monthly cost. Primary ask: concrete levers to reduce per-token cost for chat/embeddings workload. Wants simple checklist: batching, prefix/KV caching, prompt compression, token audit. POC preference: hosted API (US-EAST), 2-week pilot, target ~50k queries/day, latency target p50 200-400ms, strict monthly spend target ~$1-2k. Quote from CTO: "We need the economics to make this sustainable or we pull back UX features."
SE deep-dive 2026-03-05 (ff-20260305-8421) — walked through sample traffic, payload shapes. Identified high-variance prompt templates and repeated system prompts that could be cached. Discussed using shorter context windows and prompt compression (client-side). Agreed to run token usage audit across last 30k queri...

title: Rosebay Sentinel Tech
body:
Inbound SMB trial; signed up self-serve, got free credits. Primary contact: Dev Lead - Mateo Alvarez (mateo@rosebaysentinel.com).

Problem summary (devs): streaming responses when using function/tool-calling deliver JSON objects incrementally. Their client-side parser expects whole JSON per message — currently fails midway (SyntaxError). Also reported that when the runtime emits a function call, the shape sometimes omits optional fields, and their strict validator rejects it.

What we've observed / customer quotes:
- 'We can get coherent text, but our tool invocation layer drops events when an array opens across stream chunks.'
- 'Function arg schema mismatch: model returns named args but types differ (string vs array) — our code errors out.'

Technical details (from call & Fireflies):
- Using Hosted API (trial), streaming=true, tool-calling enabled.
- Client lib: in-house NodeJS stre...

title: Lattice Sparrow Solutions
body:
Inbound SMB — self-serve signup, evaluating hosted API for support chat widget.
Primary contact: Maya Patel (CTO) - maya@latticesparrow.com. Wants quick hosted setup; cost-sensitive.
POC constraints: small budget, 6-week evaluation window; decision tied to parsing reliability for streaming tool calls.
Reported behaviour: with json_mode enabled + streaming=true, client gets tokenized chunks that split JSON objects across events -> JSON.parse throws on partial text.
Function calling specific: model emits function call payload where 'arguments' arrives as an escaped string (e.g. "{\"user_id\":123}") — their parser expects an actual object. Happens intermittently in stream mode.
Repro environment: React front-end using fetch + ReadableStream; Node SDK v2.1.0. Tried upgrade to 2.2.0 locally — no fix. SE asked for raw event stream and SDK debug logs.
Customer included minimal repro in Drive...

title: Cobalt Nimbus LLC
body:
Inbound SMB lead. Self-serve signup on hosted API. Primary contact: Maria Lopez (CTO). Use case: conversational agent that calls internal tools (ticket search, run diagnostics) via function/tool-calls; need structured JSON output to map to internal RPC.

Summary of problem: In streaming + JSON_MODE the model sometimes emits function_call events in multiple chunks. The Node SDK handler receives first chunk containing 'function_call' and partial 'arguments' string; later chunks complete it. Customer's parser tries to JSON.parse on first visible chunk and fails with 'Unexpected token' errors. Also observed cases where model returns single quotes instead of double quotes inside arguments ("TypeError: Unexpected token '\' in JSON at position 0"). They attempted a server-side workaround to buffer until end_of_stream but that increases latency and complexity.

Customer also reports that when...

title: Sunlit Marigold Studios
body:
Inbound SMB lead — engineering-first team building a support summarization assistant integrated with Zendesk
Primary pain: when using streaming + json_mode to synthesize structured output the client sees JSON.parse errors intermittently
Customer quote: "When we stream function calls the payload sometimes arrives split and parse throws; then our handler craps out."
They are using tool-calling (function schema) to trigger an internal reranker and an external ticket updater tool (POST to internal webhook)
Current function schema: {id: integer, action: string, payload: object} — but model often emits id as string and occasionally omits nested keys when streaming chunks are truncated
Observed failure modes: partial JSON across SSE chunks, extra trailing commas from some templates, and occasionally the model emits a top-level comment field not in the schema
SE notes: likely combination of (...

body:
Account summary
- Prospect: Lattice AI (mid-market). Building enterprise search + assistant features (heavy embeddings + reranking, plus chat for end-user Q&A).
- Current pain: incidents during model/runtime upgrades with existing provider; they now require phased rollouts with measurable gates for ANY change impacting inference (model version, runtime build, routing policy, batching/caching config).
- Buying motion: engineering-led evaluation with security + change-management sign-off required. Timeline driven by their Q2 product launch.

Workload / technical requirements (from discovery + 02/06 deep dive)
- Mix: embeddings (high QPS), reranking (latency-sensitive), chat (streaming).
- Latency targets (stated informally): rerank p95 < ~150ms, embeddings p95 < ~250ms, chat p95 TTFT < ~1.2s and steady token latency; they care most about tail spikes and saturation behavior.
- Throughput...

title: Helio Health Systems
body:
Account summary
- Large provider network + analytics org. Primary concern is PHI handling + operational safety; they’re pushing hard for Private (on-prem) or at minimum a tightly controlled VPC with explicit PHI boundaries.
- Their security team is treating “optimization config changes” (batching/caching/quant profiles) as production changes that must be auditable and reversible, not “tuning knobs.”

Workload / use-case
- Internal clinician note summarization + discharge instruction drafting (high sensitivity).
- Patient portal search / retrieval over clinical content (embeddings + reranking).
- Contact center agent assist (summarize prior encounters, suggest next steps). They called out streaming as “nice to have,” but predictable p95 is mandatory.

Target requirements captured (from POC check-ins + security review)
- Latency: p95 <= 900ms for short prompts; p99 <= 2.5s. They have pe...

title: Archivalytics Legal RAG Systems
body:
Account profile: mid-sized e-discovery provider focused on government and regulated enterprise customers. Primary ask: scalable embeddings + reranking pipeline inside customer VPC with strict retention and audit logging.

Key technical constraints / requirements: 
- Index scale: currently ~25M unique documents, expecting 3-4x growth over 12 months (target architecture must handle up to 100M vectors).
- Embedding cadence: nightly bulk re-embed + incremental near-real-time updates for litigation uploads (minutes).
- Reranking strategy: bi-encoder candidate retrieval (ANN) + cross-encoder reranker on top-K=50-200; want to offload cross-encoder to dedicated GPU pool in Private deployment.
- Latency SLOs: baseline retrieval latency <80ms, end-to-end (retrieve+rerank) p95 <500ms for standard queries; for urgent e-discovery queries allow higher p95 up to 1.2s.
- Cost sensitivity: target cost...

title: Aurora Health
body:
Account summary
- Regulated healthcare AI vendor building LLM workflows on PHI (clinical notes + payer docs). Strong interest driven by public benchmark report (latency/$ + p95 stability) and wants to validate we can hit SLOs inside their own VPC.
- Primary ask: Redwood Private (customer VPC deployment) + Dedicated-like predictability (reserved capacity, predictable throughput). They explicitly do NOT want PHI leaving their AWS account.

Key contacts (Aurora)
- Head of Platform Eng (technical buyer): cares about deployment model, operability, rollback/fallback, observability.
- Security/Vendor Risk lead: running standard healthcare vendor intake (SIG-lite + pen test summary + DPA/BAA).
- Product lead for clinical summarization: cares about quality stability + “no regression” guardrails.

- Saw Redwood benchmark landing page and shared internally as evidence that “ser...

title: Helio Health
body:
Account summary
- Regulated healthcare customer evaluating Redwood Private (VPC) + Runtime LTS channel as a stability baseline. They are very sensitive to change-control, reproducibility, and “prove what’s running” requirements.
- Primary driver for LTS: they’ve been burned by inference runtime upgrades changing latency / batching behavior without clear rollback paths. Quote from security review: “We can’t have surprise kernel/scheduler changes mid-quarter.”

Workload / technical fit
- Workloads in scope for pilot:
 1) Patient message summarization + routing (non-emergency triage). Input sizes: 1–6k tokens, output 200–600 tokens. Peak: ~120 req/s during clinic hours.
 2) Internal clinician knowledge search (embeddings + rerank). Index size ~40M docs. Rerank p95 requirement is strict.
- Latency/throughput targets (stated, not yet validated):
 - Chat/summarization: p95 < 900ms for first...

title: Aurora RailWorks Systems
body:
Summary: Large EU-based rail operator evaluating Redwood Private VPC + on-prem air-gapped variant for safety-critical command-and-control assistants and maintenance diagnostics.
Primary ask: fully offline deployment that can run in Aurora's air-gapped datacenters (no persistent egress). Full KMS/HSM integration, signed model artifacts, and tamper-evident audit logs.
Stakeholders: CTO (evan.muller@aurorarailworks.com), Head of OT Security (marie.leconte@aurorarailworks.com), Procurement lead (tom.haswell@aurorarailworks.com).
Quote from OT Security: 'We cannot accept any solution that requires outbound internet for model telemetry. Auditability and key custody are non-negotiable.'
Model preferences: prefer to use Redwood-verified open model family; require model provenance + signed weights; ability to pin model versions per environment; quantized options suggested for lower-latency on ...

title: Cascade Briefing Analytics
body:
Account background: mid-stage legal compliance startup (platform used by corporate legal teams and e-discovery vendors). Primary problem: build high-precision enterprise RAG for compliance/legal discovery across 100M+ docs while meeting strict audit and retention rules.

Recent timeline (bulleted):
- 2026-01-20: Intro call (Maya) — discussed high-level architecture, use cases. Customer emphasised 'auditability and immutable logs + full-text traceability' as non-negotiable.
- 2026-02-03: Deep-dive w/SE (Liam) — reviewed sample corpus (40M docs exported), initial embedding baseline using open models. Discussed hybrid retrieval (BM25 + dense embeddings) + learned reranker. POC scope agreed: 10M doc sample, 4-week run.

title: Atlas Payments
body:
Account summary:
- Atlas Payments is evaluating Redwood for tier-1 internal tools (support + risk ops) and potentially customer-facing merchant support in the future. They are unusually focused on reliability automation and auditability due to payments incident processes.

Primary requirements (Atlas “must haves”):
1) High availability / multi-region behavior
- They want multi-region routing with clear semantics: what constitutes a region-level event vs partial degradation, and how traffic shifts.
- Target RTO: “minutes, not tens of minutes.” Quote from platform lead: “If a region is impaired, we need deterministic automation; we can’t rely on a human to approve each flip at 3am.”
- Preference is active/active with graceful degradation. They accept temporary shed/Retry-After under overload, but want bounded impact.
- Dedicated: wants predictable throughput and explicit headroom strate...

title: Northstar Health
body:
Account summary
- Regulated healthcare provider network + patient engagement apps. Evaluating Redwood for Private deployment (VPC) due to PHI handling and strict data residency requirements.
- They want multi-region availability but with hard boundaries: failover must not violate residency (no cross-border) and must be auditable.

Current stage
- Evaluation. They are comparing options (Azure OpenAI in locked-down tenant, Bedrock, and "self-host open models"). Redwood is in for Private because they want routing/observability + cost levers without building a full serving stack.

Workloads / use cases (stated)
- Patient portal assistant (chat): appointment/benefits Q&A with retrieval; must be stable under traffic spikes.
- Clinical note summarization (batch + near-real-time): throughput heavy, cost sensitive; needs quality monitoring.
- Internal doc search for care teams (embeddings + re...

title: Northstar Support AI
body:
Account summary
- Product: AI support agent embedded in helpdesk workflows (chat + agent-assist). Heavy on streaming responses + tool calling; PII present (customer tickets).
- Current state: running a mix of open models + a commercial API; wants to consolidate inference for cost predictability + operational controls.
- Primary decision drivers (their words): "safe rollouts without waking up the on-call" + "we need a paper trail" + "latency is the product".

Workload / technical requirements
- Workload type: support-agent chat (streaming) + summarization of long tickets; occasional embeddings for ticket retrieval.
- Latency: strong sensitivity on interactive turns. Target discussed on call: p95 end-to-end response start < 900ms for typical prompts; streaming first token perceived latency is key.
- Throughput: spikes during business hours; needs predictable behavior under burst. Intere...

title: HarborBank
body:
Account summary (regulated enterprise):
- HarborBank is evaluating Redwood Dedicated (reserved pools) with a strong pull toward Redwood Private in a VPC depending on change-management/audit posture.
- Primary theme across stakeholders: “we can’t take an outage for a model upgrade” + “every change must be attributable, reviewable, and exportable to our SIEM.”

Current workload / drivers:
- Internal knowledge assistant for policies/procedures + customer support workflows. Mix of chat + embeddings (RAG).
- Seasonal/bursty workloads (end-of-quarter compliance reviews; incident response). Wants predictable performance; prefers reserved capacity.
- Latency targets: p95 < 900ms for short queries; p99 < 2.5s. Throughput target discussed as 250–400 RPS sustained during peak windows (final sizing TBD).

Deployment + architecture preferences:
- Strong preference for VPC deployment (Redwood Priva...

title: Northbridge Healthcare
body:
Account summary
- Regulated healthcare system (mix of hospitals + outpatient). Evaluating Redwood primarily for Private deployment due to PHI handling + network controls.
- Current pain: internal platform team is stuck between (a) too-broad admin roles in existing tooling, and (b) brittle per-service IAM policies. They want consistent least-privilege, with strong audit trails for “who changed what” and “why access was denied.”

Workload / technical context
- Use cases under consideration:
 - Clinician-facing note summarization (high sensitivity; strict data residency + retention)
 - Internal knowledge base search over policies/procedures (embeddings + reranking)
 - Support agent assist for scheduling + billing (chat + tool calling)
- Performance: “latency matters but predictability matters more.” Target p95 for chat tokens < ~250ms/token streaming on internal network; embeddings throu...

title: Northstar Search
body:
High-level:
- Northstar is building enterprise search (KBs) with heavy embeddings + reranking load. They care about tail latency, cost, and change control.

What they want from Redwood:
- Hosted API quickstart for embeddings now; likely Dedicated for reranker + answer generation once traffic patterns confirmed.
- Strong routing controls: per-route model pinning, fallback model definition, and explicit rollouts.

Quantization Profile Library interest:
- Very positive on the concept, but wants strict opt-in and predictability.
- Explicit ask: "No silent changes." They don’t want an alias like "recommended" to change behavior without a user action + visible changelog.
- Wants route-level toggle + ability to test in staging first, then canary in prod.

Throughput/latency targets (customer-provided, needs validation):
- Index bursts: embeddings QPS spikes 1.2k–2k during reindex; steady-sta...

normalized.nextStep: Run a 2-week POC on a Dedicated pool (A10/L4) with route-level quantization profile opt-in for reranker routes; share compatibility matrix + pricing and confirm rollback guardrails.
blockers: Need clear compatibility matrix for quantization profiles by GPU SKU + model variant (asked for a one-pager they can share internally).
Worried about silent behavior changes if "recommended" profile alias moves without explicit action (request: pin versions + visible changelog).
Need perf evidence that p95 latency won’t regress under peak traffic with KV cache + batching enabled.
Procurement wants written SLA language for Dedicated and operational rollback guarantees for any cost-optimization features.

title: FinBank
body: Schedule follow-up to confirm target RPO/RTO per component and review Redwood Private Day-2 backup/restore runbooks + staging DR drill plan; FinBank to share current VPC architecture and KMS constraints.

normalized.notes: Schedule follow-up to confirm target RPO/RTO per component and review Redwood Private Day-2 backup/restore runbooks + staging DR drill plan; FinBank to share current VPC architecture and KMS constraints.

blockers: Needs written, customer-consumable DR plan for VPC single-region and guidance to expand to multi-AZ; wants clear RPO/RTO commitments (not just best-effort).

title: Northstar HR
body:
Account summary
- Northstar HR is evaluating Redwood for production LLM inference powering recruiter-facing workflows (candidate summary + doc Q&A over resumes/ATS notes) and internal support-agent for ops.
- Core requirement: strict latency SLA + predictable tail latency under load. They have had regressions during model rollouts and want automated safety rails.

Firmographics / environment
- Currently running mixed workloads: real-time chat-style generation + embeddings for search.
- Deployment preference: VPC/private networking (no public egress for inference traffic). Us-east primary; they require EU data residency for a subset of customers.
- Compliance asks: SOC2 alignment, SSO/SAML, audit logging + export, retention controls. KMS integration requested.

Key requirements (captured from calls)
- Latency SLO targets (their words):
 - "We cannot exceed p95 ~350ms for short response...

title: Briefcase Compliance Tech
body:
Account overview: enterprise legal/compliance vendor focused on enterprise counsel + external law firms. Heavy emphasis on immutable audit trails and retention policy enforcement. Customer has ~25M documents (mixed PDFs, TIFFs, email PSTs) across on-prem repositories and AWS S3; expects index growth to ~40M documents by year-end.

Discovery notes (shorthand):
- Prioritized: accurate recall for e-discovery + provable chain-of-custody.
- Wants hybrid retrieval: embeddings index + BM25 prefilter then rerank with cross-encoder.
- "If we can't prove retention and immutable audit logs we can't go forward" — Head of Privacy on call.
- Budget sensitivity on per-query cost; target <$0.02 per user query at 95th percentile.

Technical constraints:
- Must deploy inside VPC or on-prem for regulated clients; no traffic egress to public internet for indexed material.
- Retention policy: 7 years for ...

title: Verdant Sentry Technologies
body:
Top-lines: 1) Enterprise healthtech, strict PHI-like rules, cannot send unredacted patient data outside their VPC. 2) Customer asking for Redwood Private (VPC) POC — full deploy inside their AWS account via Terraform modules + Helm charts.

Discovery call notes (2026-01-10):
- Intro with CTO (L. Martinez) + Head of Data (S. Osei). They run analytics on de-identified EHR and want low-latency summarization and embeddings for research UI.
- Primary concern: data residency & cryptographic key control. "We need KMS we control; no token or secret should leave our account."
- Compliance: SOC2 required, ISO27001 preferred, audit trails mandatory for model inputs/outputs for 7 years (per legal).

POC ask (agreed):
- Deploy Redwood Private control plane (customer-managed) + inference nodes in customer VPC (us-east-1) using provided Terraform modules.
- Helm charts to install inference runtime i...

title: Lighthouse Keel Solutions
body:
Summary: mid-market product analytics vendor building AI-powered support and in-app assistants. Started with Hosted API eval in Dec '25; strong interest in Dedicated for predictable latency and data isolation. Wants clear path from Hosted -> Dedicated with same API surface and minimal app changes.

Key contacts:
- Rajiv Menon (CTO) — technical sponsor, cares about rollback safety and audit logs. Quote: "We cannot have a POC that causes more ops churn than value. Canary with automatic rollback is a must."
- Erin Cole (Head of Product) — owns metrics and user experience, requested p95 latency targets and controlled quality checks during canary.

Technical requirements and targets:
- Workloads: chat (agent), semantic search/embeddings, doc summarization.
- Latency: chat p95 target <= 220ms in-region (US-West), conversational tail acceptable only during warm KV cache.
- Throughput: target...

title: Northstar Payments Co.
body:
CRM shorthand:
- Champion is platform team; they are tired of “black box” endpoints.
- Quote: “We don’t want to become an inference ops company.”
- Quote: “Noisy neighbor is a non-starter for production.”
- Wants: predictable throughput + clear levers on cost/latency.
- Must-have: SSO/SAML, SOC2, audit logs, KMS option, retention controls.
- Residency: US only now; EU later.
- Buying process: security review + legal redlines; procurement wants firm pricing model for pilot + path to annual commit.
- Demo: avoid empty dashboards; run synthetic traffic generator ahead of time; verify quotas/keys; don’t click into flaky Console views.

title: Aurora Financial
body:
Account context (Stephanie Nguyen / Markus Klein):
- Aurora Financial is treating inference as a regulated production dependency (customer support + internal knowledge assistant). Technical fit is solid; procurement/security is the gating function.

Key security questions (from Feb 20 call):
- SSO/SAML: wants Okta SAML SSO for Console + admin access. Also asked if API keys can be governed/rotated with RBAC and how break-glass is handled.
- SCIM: wants automated user lifecycle (joiner/mover/leaver). Specifically asked whether SCIM covers group-to-role mapping and whether deprovisioning immediately removes access to Console and API.
- RBAC: asked for role granularity (viewer vs admin vs billing vs security/audit). Wants clarity on whether RBAC applies to both Console actions and API operations.
- Audit logs + SIEM: wants event taxonomy and fields (who/what/when, IP, action, object), ret...

Needs clarity on SCIM provisioning scope and RBAC coverage (console vs API)
Encryption/KMS boundary questions (customer-managed keys expectations) and how it differs between Dedicated vs Private
Data retention/deletion controls need to match internal policy (30-day default not acceptable; wants configurable retention + log retention options)

title: LexGuard Discovery Services
body:
Summary: LexGuard provides enterprise e-discovery and legal document review for top-100 law firms. Exploring Redwood for a next-gen RAG pipeline: dense embeddings + reranking to improve precision on precedent retrieval. Key constraints: must run in customer VPC/private control plane, 7-year retention for audit trails, and tamper-evident audit logs.

Why Redwood: team emphasized unified runtime + private deployment path. Interested in dedicated pools for predictable throughput during large review jobs.

Scale & indexing: customer expects initial corpus of ~120M documents (emails, PDFs, discovery bundles). Estimated embedding vectors ~120M - plan to shard index by matter/client. Warm-up ingestion test requested: 10M docs over 48 hours.

Latency & throughput targets: interactive lawyer-facing search: p95 < 300ms (end-to-end, including reranker). Batch review queries (legal analytics): su...

title: Northbridge Capital Markets
body:
Regulated enterprise prospect (capital markets). Interest is Private deployment in customer VPC with Dedicated capacity add-on; data isolation and change-management controls are gating items.

Primary use cases (per Platform Eng + Compliance):
- Internal research assistant over market commentary + internal policy docs (RAG/doc-search)
- Summarize trader chat/email threads for supervision workflows (summarization)

- Deployment: Private in VPC (AWS preferred). No public hosted endpoints for production traffic. Explicit controls for any outbound calls.

title: CumulusBridge Analytics
body:
Account snapshot: mid-market analytics SaaS focused on customer insights and in-app help. Started with Hosted API to validate UX; now wants Dedicated for predictable latency and data isolation. Key asks: 1) tight 95th-p latency SLO for live chat (target 50-100ms RPC for short convos), 2) predictable token cost with reserve/burst pricing, 3) ability to pin model versions + canary rollouts, 4) strict audit logging and KMS-managed keys, 5) data residency in NA and option for on-prem connector for sensitive clients.

POC scope (what Redwood will deliver):
- Dedicated small pool (2 GPU equiv) deployed in customer's VPC for initial canary.
- Canary routing config: start at 5% traffic -> 20% -> 50% over 48h windows if health gates pass.
- Model variants: primary = open-quantized-8b (latency optimized), fallback = open-quantized-4b (lower cost, compatible API), cold fallback = hosted-lower-ti...

title: Clearmantle Compliance Discovery
body:
Account snapshot: Clearmantle is a legal compliance SaaS provider serving mid-market to enterprise customers (primarily financial services and healthcare clients). They ingest client contracts, regulatory filings, and investigation documents for e-discovery and compliance review.

Key priorities / asks:
- Need private VPC deployment (will host in customer-managed AWS VPC or air-gapped colo for a subset of clients).
- Indexing scale: initial target 50M documents, long-term 200M+ documents (mix of OCR PDFs, email corpora, Office docs).
- Embeddings + reranking focus — want vector search + learned reranker integrated into query pipeline to reduce false positives on discovery queries.
- Latency SLO: 95th percentile end-to-end query <250ms for typical reranked top-5 queries; exploratory (long-tail) queries can be slower but must not exceed 2s.
- Cost constraints: procurement target ~$0.01-...

title: Aurora CX
body:
Account summary:
- Aurora CX builds customer support automation (agent assist + auto-drafts + post-call/ticket summarization). They want to standardize LLM inference behind a single API with predictable latency/cost and better ops tooling than they have today.

Use case details (from discovery + POC scoping):
- Primary workflows:
 - Agent assist (chat-style, streaming required): suggest responses with citations from KB + recent ticket context.
 - Auto-draft / summarization: summarize long conversations + generate next-step actions and CRM fields.
 - Ticket triage: classify, route to queues, detect priority/escalation.
 - Optional: embeddings for KB retrieval + reranking.
- Volume / performance (directional, not final):
 - Peak hours: high concurrency during business hours; wants predictable p95 latency.
 - Target p95 for agent assist: "sub-second first token" and stable streaming (the...

title: Mistral Gateways Inc
body:
Customer is an infra vendor building gateway appliances for telco customers — sensitive latency requirements for chat and live routing
Primary ask: private VPC deployment with strict egress controls, IP allowlists, and KMS integration for model logs and user data
Prefers dedicated control plane in Redwood Private but must ensure data never leaves us-east-1 region for EU customers' tenants (data residency requirement)
Infosec quote: 'We need cryptographic separation of keys and ability to rotate without downtime'
SE note: exploring PrivateLink because customer cannot accept VPC peering across accounts due to routing constraints in their environment
Customer wants per-request audit entries exported to their SIEM (Splunk) and S3 with 7-year retention for compliance
Cost sensitivity: medium — willing to pay premium for audited, private deployment but wants Optimize recommendations to redu...

title: VerdictSearch Compliance Labs
body:
Lead: CTO (Anika Rao) — heavy emphasis on retention + audit trails for e-discovery chain of custody.
Primary workload: ingestion of historical litigation and regulatory archives (~18M docs, mixed PDFs/MSG/TIFF), nightly delta loads.
Architecture ask: vector index in private VPC with on-prem options for cold storage; live reranking using cross-encoder on dedicated GPUs for sub-200ms re-rank of top-50 candidates.
Cost sensitivity: willing to pay premium for auditability but wants per-query < $0.15 target for average rerank flow (embed+approx search+rerank).
Model preference: open quantized encoders for embedding (1280-1536 dim) + smaller cross-encoder for rerank. Explicitly asked about support for int8/q4_0 profiles and offline kernel tuning.
Security: SSO (SAML + Okta), enterprise KMS, immutable audit logs with 7+ year retention, separation of metadata and content per compliance contro...

title: Elmshore DeltaWorks
body: Mid-market product analytics SaaS. Primary team: product + ML infra building in-product insights and assistant features. Started with Hosted API exploratory integration Sept 2025; impressed with latency and dev ergonomics. Now evaluating Dedicated for predictable throughput and control. Wants a POC that demonstrates canary rollout, fallback to hosted, and region failover. Strong security posture: SOC2 in place, SSO mandatory, KMS for keys. Cost sensitivity moderate — willing to pay for predictable latency and isolation but expects Optimize recommendations to reduce token spend. Key ask: canary with rollback triggers and a repeatable playbook for production cutover.

title: Winterbourne Regional Search
body:
Account background: mid-tier UK financial services firm, multiple regulated products, heavy search usage across customer portal and internal knowledge base.
Primary ask: low-latency regional search (UK first, EU second) for RAG workflows; strict in-country data residency for user data and audit logs.
Scale: indexing target ~120M-250M docs initially; expect growth to 500M within 18 months. Vector index cardinality high (many short docs + metadata).
Latency SLOs: 95th percentile end-to-end query <120ms for user-facing search; reranker path (embedding + ANN + cross-encoder rerank) target 80-150ms depending on config.
Cost target: procurement looking for <$0.01 per query and ideally ~ $0.002-0.005 for common queries after batching/caching.
Model preferences: prefer open models for cost control; willing to evaluate quantized variants. Need support for hybrid pipelines: sparse retrieval + d...

title: Delta Sentry Capital
body:
Account overview:
- Delta Sentry Capital: mid-size national credit institution with BNPL and digital lending products. Heavy PCI scope concerns due to stored/processed card tokens. Looking to centralize ML inference in a private VPC to reduce scope and get predictable latency for underwriting and fraud models.

Recent timeline (bulleted):
- 2026-01-28: Intro call (Liam) — high-level product fit: low-latency inference + key management.
- 2026-02-12: Kickoff / Security intro (fireflies ff-20260212-DSC-kickoff) — DS sec + infra joined. Asked for SOC 2 + ISO mapping and PCI considerations.

title: Sentinel Arc Compliance AI
body:
Customer background: incumbent search is Elastic + custom rerank microservice. Pain: maintenance cost, drift of embeddings infra, and unpredictable tail latency.
They care most about: predictable cost for discovery workflows, audited retention for evidentiary trails, and easy key ownership (customer-managed KMS).
Quote from CISO: 'We need defensible logs and a playback of what model returned what snippet — legal-grade traceability.'
POC ask (high level): 2-week private VPC POC. Ingest 2.5M docs (sample set) -> run 10k queries over mixed sets comparing current stack vs Redwood. Produce latency, cost-per-query, and precision@5 metrics.
Technical constraints: cannot route EU case files through public multi-tenant control plane; require regional routing or dedicated tenancy for those sets.
Integration ask: SCIM for identities, SSO for all consoles, and event export to Splunk for audit ing...

title: PolarGrid Financial Systems
body:
Account overview: PolarGrid is a regulated payments platform (EU + NA operations). Heavy controls required for finance vertical. They are evaluating Redwood Private (VPC) for production inference across fraud-detection embeddings and customer support chat.

Key technical requirements / likes:
- Must run in customer VPC with customer-managed KMS (BYOK + HSM support).
- SSO via SAML + SCIM provisioning (Okta). Need full user/group sync and group-to-role mapping in console.
- RBAC in control plane: want roles that mirror internal AZ/Team hierarchy (admin, infra, app-owner, auditor, read-only) and audit trails per token/route.
- Audit logging: immutable export of request metadata (no plaintext PII), long retention for audit (7 yrs proposed for EU financial records).
- Data residency: EU residency required for EU customers; routing policy must ensure inference for EU accounts remains insid...

title: Silverfjord Interactives
body:
Context: mid-market customer success platform. Primary goal is lower TCO for chat and embeddings vs current multi-cloud model.

title: Mercurial Code Steward
body:
Topline: fintech company; heavy regulatory constraints. Primary ask = enterprise-grade IDE copilot that can run in a private VPC or on-prem for regulated teams.

Key quotes: "We cannot send proprietary models or code to a public multi-tenant endpoint." — Head of InfoSec.

Requirements summary (handy): low-latency streaming for in-editor completions (target: median <50ms, p95 <150ms), model/version pinning per team (ability to freeze on a narrowed model + rollback), detailed audit logs per request + per-user mapping, retention controls (configurable retention windows; legal wants 12 months for dev artifacts), deterministic routing to private capacity, KMS/HSM integration for keys, SAML SSO via Okta, SCIM user sync.

POC notes: want private control plane or VPC private deployment for pilot. Will start with 50 devs in a single US region (data residency required in us-east-1). Performance...

title: Northbridge Health Lattice
body:
Account summary: enterprise health system (50 hospitals + integrated clinics). Primary initiative: replace slow legacy clinical search with RAG-backed clinical search + reranking to surface most relevant notes and structured data snippets. Key constraints: cannot route PHI to multi-tenant hosted inference; full chain-of-custody and per-request audit trail required. CTO quote: 'We will not allow PHI to leave our environment; any third-party service must be deployable into our VPC or on-prem with HSM-backed keys.'

Requirements (high level):
- Index scale: target 30–60M clinical documents (notes, consults, imaging reports).
- Query profile: 20k QPD peak across 8am-6pm; burstable to 60k with priority routing.
- Latency: 95th pct. end-to-end (embedding + ANN + reranker) target < 200ms for triage UI, < 400ms for deep reviewer flows.
- Cost sensitivity: want <$0.10/query (embeddings + reran...

title: Lodestar Bridgeworks
body:
Mid-market analytics platform (B2B) — chat assistant for product teams; in-app summarization for meeting notes; embeddings for search
Started with Hosted API to validate accuracy and prompt flows; now pushing to Dedicated for predictable latency and data isolation
CTO (Maya Patel) quote: 'We can't accept >300ms P95 for user-facing chat — need consistent sub-200 if possible',
Primary infra: GCP europe-west1 for EU customers; US presence in us-east-1 for global users
Security: Okta SAML SSO already in place; vendor must support customer-managed KMS and audit logs; retention policy 30 days for PII-related logs
Cost sensitivity: team prefers monthly reserved commit if unit economics beat Hosted PAYG by >20%
Operational concern: KV cache warm-up and cold-start behaviour — wants plan for cache pre-warming during cutover
Asks from SE: clear canary % control, automatic rollback trigger based ...

title: VerityLock BriefVault
body:
Snapshot: mid-stage enterprise legal discovery vendor building a multi-tenant RAG product for regulated customers. Key ask = private VPC deployment, full audit logging + 7yr retention / legal-hold integration. Scale: production target ~250M canonical docs (contracts, filings, transcripts) -> estimated ~350M embeddings; wants cost-per-query economics for 100-200 qps steady state. POC scope = ingest 10k sample docs, run embeddings + reranking pipeline, validate recall@10 and cost model.

Technical desiderata:
- Two-stage retrieval: ANN (HNSW/FAISS) at vector store -> candidate topK 200 -> cross-encoder rerank (small distil-cross) for final top10.
- Latency SLOs: median retrieval <120ms, end-to-end rerank <=350ms for single-query (p95 <700ms acceptable under load).
- Throughput target: 150 qps baseline; burst to 400 qps during e-discovery runs.
- Cost target: ~$0.003-0.006 per end-user q...
