title: Acme AI, LLC
body:
Account summary
- Large enterprise prospect building an AI layer across multiple product lines; wants a single inference provider with clear enterprise tier semantics.
- Primary concern is reliability under shared-fleet pressure. Repeatedly referenced "noisy neighbor" incidents with prior vendor.

Workloads / routes
- Hot routes they care about:
 - /v1/chat/completions (customer-facing agent + internal copilots)
 - embeddings (doc search + personalization)
 - rerank (search relevance)
- Workload shape (as described):
 - Chat: spiky bursts tied to launches/marketing events; long-context prompts for enterprise customers; expects streaming.
 - Embeddings: nightly batch + some online; cost sensitive but less latency sensitive.
 - Rerank: latency sensitive in search flow.

SLA / SLO asks (their words)
- "If the fleet is on fire, we need to know we still get served." 
- Wants explicit enter...

title: Sunward Vault AI
body:
Quick CRM summary / fragments from calls:
- Prospect: Sunward Vault AI — underwriting platform for commercial insurance. Heavy PII, retention rules, strict data residency for EU and UK customers.
- Primary ask: Private VPC deployment (customer-managed networking) w/ KMS integration (HSM-backed CMK), CloudTrail/CloudWatch export to customer's logging account, and per-request audit logs.
- Latency target: p95 generation latency <= 120ms for short-chat flows (context ~512 tokens). Throughput: peak 400 concurrent chat sessions, burst to 1200 for renewals season.
- Cost sensitivity: mid-high — will trade some latency for cost; interested in Optimize recommendations (quantization, batching).
- Model preferences: want Redwood-curated open models first; will validate against fine-tuned BERT/encoder-decoder models for reranking/embeddings. Needs deterministic routing + fallback to smaller quan...

title: Emerald Cove Digital Ops
body:
Short note: high-security fintech -- cannot send PII outside tenant VPC
Customer priority: SSO via Okta or AzureAD; SCIM group provisioning maps to RBAC roles in control plane
Wanted: fine-grained RBAC (team-level model access, deploy/run vs. admin vs. billing), ability to pin model versions per team
Control plane separation required: control plane (Redwood console) in customer VPC or customer-managed control plane; data plane must never leave VPC
KMS: require AWS KMS / customer-managed key (CMK) for token/key material; HSM-backed keys preferred for production; verify key rotation and customer access audit
Audit logging: 7-year retention requested by compliance; logs must include admin actions, model rollouts, key usage events; syslog/CloudWatch export acceptable
SCIM specifics: group -> role mapping, support for nested groups, delta syncs, provisioning disabled for service accounts b...

title: Corroborate Compliance Discovery
body:
Overview: Corroborate builds a compliance-first e-discovery product used by large law firms and corporate legal teams. They ingest email, contracts, litigation bundles (~200M docs peak for enterprise customers).
Primary ask: RAG-enabled search with high-quality embeddings + reranking pipeline to surface short, defensible answers and original doc provenance. Focused on: indexing scale, cost-per-query, end-to-end latency and auditability.
Technical constraints: must run in customer VPC or on-prem private deployment. No persisted PII in Redwood-hosted logs. KMS-managed keys, HSM available for on-prem. Retention: 7+ years for activity logs; audit logs must be immutable for 90 days minimum.
Performance targets: 95th percentile RL retrieval + rerank < 250ms for primary workflows (goal: 150ms). Throughput target for enterprise plan: 300 qps sustained for batch discovery runs. Cost target: ~$...

title: Northbridge Banking
body:
Account context (Northbridge Banking):
- Champion: Director, GenAI Platform (eng) + Security Architect (IAM).
- Buyer signals: security-first, but platform team cares a lot about predictable latency + cost per 1k tokens. They are currently benchmarking multiple vendors and an internal vLLM path.

Discovery highlights:
- They’re building a shared internal "GenAI gateway" for multiple app teams.
- Primary driver is compliance + controllability (auditability, residency, retention), not access to proprietary models.
- They asked early about: SAML/SCIM, audit log contents, retention defaults, and whether any data leaves the VPC.

Pilot outcomes (as captured in closeout):
- Latency/throughput: met pilot SLOs for initial load; some spikes tied to bursty traffic patterns (SE recommended batching/rate shaping).
- Ops/observability: Console dashboards were positively received; they want SIEM-fr...

title: Viridian Support Shield
body:
Summary: Large fintech payments platform with embedded payments + support for health-biller partners (PCI + PHI overlap). Looking to replace a brittle internal LLM proxy and move to a vendor with a private VPC path and strict audit controls. Priority: agent-assist (contextual suggestions + tool calling to payment platform), ticket summarization for compliance, reranking for fraud triage, and reliable latency under high spikes (billing cycles, promotions).

Key requirements / constraints:
- Must support VPC-private deployment; customer-managed VPC peering preferred.
- Encrypt-at-rest with customer KMS/HSM (FIPS/HSM attestations required).
- PCI DSS and HIPAA controls; 7-year audit log retention for dispute investigations.
- SSO/SAML + SCIM for automatic user provisioning.
- Data residency: primary US regions, some EU tenant isolation for partner markets.
- Redaction/PII/PHI detection o...

title: Ardent Horizon Insurance
body:
- Enterprise evaluation for Private VPC deployment (primary ask: run POC inside customer VPC).
- Key asks: full KMS integration, EU data residency (eu-west-1), immutable audit logs for all inference requests, SSO (SAML) + SCIM.
- From CTO: "Can you guarantee audit trail for every token-level inference call? Need retention control for 7 years for compliance audits."
- AE/SE summary: prefer Terraform + Helm deployment path; customer uses EKS 1.26 on AWS with strict outbound policies. POC scope: deploy minimal Redwood control plane in our managed account, but all inference nodes run in customer VPC subnets; provide TF module + Helm chart.
- Cost sensitivity: medium-high — interested in optimize recommendations (quantization, batching) to reduce infra spend.
- Model preference: open model family (Llama-family or Mistral) with quantized 4-bit variants for cost. Must support pinning to mode...

title: JuriVault Compliance Discovery
body:
High level: enterprise legal discovery + compliance monitoring platform. Heavy emphasis on immutable audit trails and retention for regulatory e-discovery (7 years requested).
Workload profile: nightly bulk ingestion of ~250M doc fragments (OCR + text), incremental daily delta ~1.5M docs. Typical query pattern: interactive lawyer search (low latency, P99 target 200ms across reranking stack) + scheduled batch rerank jobs for custodian lists.
Embeddings strategy: client prefers hybrid approach — sparse indexing for recall + dense embeddings for rerank. Initial PoC to validate faiss/HNSW vs managed vector DB latency under VPC constraints.
Reranking: cross-encoder supervised reranker in front of dense retrieval; want deterministic fallback to smaller model for cold starts. P99 SLA target for end-to-end query (retrieve+rerank) = 200ms; acceptable average = 80-120ms. Cost sensitivity: targe...

title: Nyx VPC Integrations
body:
Quick summary: Nyx is a mid-to-large fintech building a customer support + fraud triage stack. Primary interest is Redwood Private (VPC) deployment with strict EU/NA data residency controls and KMS BYOK. They are in parallel PoC talks with Anthropic and a managed on-prem vendor; security is the gating factor.

Qualification notes (high level):
- Decision makers: CTO (L. Moreno), Head of Infra (G. Chen), Legal (procurement lead: R. Patel). AE intro call 2026-01-15.
- Current infra: multi-account AWS, primary regions us-east-1 and eu-west-1; strict cross-region replication rules.
- Data classification: PII + transaction traces -> cannot leave region for storage or audit logs.
- Compliance must-haves: SOC2 Type II + ISO27001 evidence, SAML SSO, enterprise KMS (AWS KMS with HSM/BYOK), 7+ year audit log retention or export to customer SIEM.

Architecture fit notes (from 2026-03-02 technica...

title: Summit Insurance
body:
Account: Summit Insurance — enterprise insurance carrier (NA). Buying team includes Security, Procurement, and Claims Ops. Deal is in procurement with a formal RFP (security-heavy).

What they care about (verbatim-ish):
- “Retention is our biggest gating item.” They want explicit language for: prompt/response content, system logs, and audit logs. They’re okay with some operational logging, but need it bounded + documented. They also asked whether any content is used to train models (answer must follow approved ‘no training on customer data’ definition and disclaimers).
- Audit logs: they want to export audit logs to existing SIEM (Splunk). Asked for examples of event records and whether API token creation/rotation and permission changes are logged. Also asked if exports can go to customer-owned S3 bucket.
- RFP due date: currently tracking internal deadline of 2026-02-21 to submit a c...

title: Helix Telemetry Networks
body:
Account snapshot: Helix is an enterprise IoT analytics vendor (telemetry ingestion from edge devices, real-time anomaly scores, customer-facing chat ops + internal assistant). Evaluating Redwood Dedicated for predictable high-throughput model serving across NA + EU regions.

Primary ask (summary):
- Reserved GPU pool planning: need a design that supports primary NA region (us-east) + failover to eu-west with <500ms extra failover latency for chat sessions.
- Throughput guarantees: sustained 1.2k concurrent chat sessions with 16-token/sec per session average token emission => planning baseline 4x concurrency headroom.
- Latency SLOs: p95 <= 120ms for short-turn chat (<=256 tokens), p95 <= 250ms for long-turns (<=1024 tokens).
- Failover headroom: plan for 25% spare capacity in each region to absorb zone failures and sudden traffic spikes; prefer cross-region burst policy with automated...

title: Spectra Keystone AI
body:
Background: mid-size trading + risk analytics platform. Interested in Redwood Private (VPC) for regulated EU/US customers. Primary IdP: Okta. Key asks: SSO (SAML 2.0), SCIM provisioning of groups/roles, and fine-grained RBAC surfaced in Redwood Console/control plane. Also strict data residency for EU customers and audit logging for inference events.

POC ask: 2-week private VPC POC via our AWS account: - synthetic chat load for trader-assistant (p95 token latency target <200ms) - embeddings pipeline for KYC doc search (batch throughput 200-400/s) - reranking integration with existing search stack.

Security: they require encrypted SAML assertions, SCIM v2 SCIM push for new users + group sync, and RBAC to map Okta groups to Redwood roles (admin, infra, app-readonly). Must show audit trail for model/version used per request (token-level metadata preferred). KMS: require HSM-backed keys ...

title: Pyxis Throughput Labs
body:
Shorthand: 'need guaranteed throughput during flash sales; cannot rely on noisy public pool'.
CTO (S. Malik) quote: 'If we lose 2 minutes during checkout we lose conversions — latency is revenue here.'
AE notes: focus on quantized fallback economics; show token/cost delta clearly in deck.
Security: legal flagged data residency; possible requirement for Private deployment in EU regions for 20% of traffic.
Open questions: exact retention window for audit logs (7 vs 10 yrs), license for LLM-XL commercial use, procurement lead time for reserved GPUs.
Action items: SE to deliver Sizing Workbook + GPU cost model by 2026-03-17; Marcus to engage procurement for headcount approvals.

body:

- HQ: US (NA). Team describes themselves as "mid-size care ops platform"; engineering team ~25.

title: Suncrest Sentry Works
body:
Top-line: Suncrest is a B2B SaaS (support automation) with ~450 engineers and an aggressive roadmap to ship AI-first features. They evaluated Redwood Hosted API first (fast onboarding) and are now moving to a Dedicated POC to validate production economics and reliability. Key PV: low p95 latency for chat (target <= 180ms per token on short-turn interactions), steady throughput for peak support hours (~120 concurrent chat sessions), and clear rollback/fallback behavior.

POC ask (high level):
- Dedicated pool with 4 gpus (NVIDIA A100 equivalent), ability to scale to 8 during peaks.
- Canary routing: route 5% of prod traffic to Redwood Dedicated model variant (quantized Q4-8) for 48 hours, monitor latency, token cost, and response quality (BLEU-like heuristics + human eval).
- Fallback plan: automatic region failover to hosted API in the event of Dedicated capacity degradation; model va...

title: Palisade VPC Security
body:
Summary:
- Large fintech platform evaluating Redwood Private (VPC) for customer-facing conversational workflows and document reranking across regulated data sets.
- Primary ask: compliance mapping for SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA/BAA, and PCI considerations for cardholder data in caches.

Key contacts:
- CISO: Anika Sorensen (wants BYOK/HSM + attestable audit trail)
- Head of Platform: Ravi Patel (latency SLA, autoscale concerns)
- Data Privacy Lead: Marta Gomez (EU residency + retention rules)

Requirements / constraints (on call 2026-02-11):
- Must deploy in customer VPC with private control plane (no egress of raw payload to public Redwood-hosted shared infra).
- KMS: BYOK required; HSM-backed keys preferred. Customer uses AWS KMS + CloudHSM in eu-west-1 for EU data.
- Audit logging: token-level and request/response metadata for 365 days; retain exportable logs to S3/GCS with immutable ...

title: Northwind Analytics
body:
Account summary:
- Northwind Analytics is evaluating Redwood for a performance-sensitive, customer-facing chat experience embedded in their BI product. Primary pain is burst-driven tail latency (p95/p99) when traffic ramps quickly and prompt lengths vary (short chat turns mixed with occasional long context windows). They have strict SLOs and are sensitive to latency spikes more than median latency.

Workload details (from discovery + deep dive):
- Type: chat generation (streaming), some summarization + retrieval-augmented flows.
- Traffic pattern: predictable daily peaks + sharp bursts during “reporting hour”; lots of request cancellations (user interrupts / regenerate) => churn.
- Concurrency target (stated): 250-400 concurrent decode sessions during peak; bursts can exceed this briefly.
- Prompt length mix: most turns small, but intermittent long contexts ("2k-8k tokens") + some lon...

title: Elysian Healthcare Analytics
body:
Summary: enterprise healthcare analytics vendor evaluating Redwood Private (VPC) for PHI-adjacent workloads. Strong mandate: all telemetry and audit logs must remain in EU region, encrypted at rest with customer-managed KMS, and exported to Elysian SIEM (Splunk in eu-west-1).

Recent activity (bulleted):
- 2025-01-14: Intro call (Alyssa/Marco) — high level requirements, Elysian wants VPC pattern + in-VPC SIEM forwarding.
- 2025-01-28: Security deep-dive (ff_20250128_elysian_sec_review) — CTO (M. Renault): "logs cannot leave eu-west-1, we need retention 7y for audit logs". Discussed stubbed Drive doc (log-schema-draft.xlsx).
- 2025-01-30: Mail thread with infra (thread-CAJk3y...) asking for syslog/TLS and API push options.
- 2025-02-02: PoC kickoff: provisioning test VPC, sample traffic, log export test planned week of 2025-02-09.

Audit logging requirements (detailed):
- Destination: ...

title: Tradehaven Retail Tech
body:
Summary: Enterprise procurement for Dedicated capacity to support live shopping chat + catalog reranking. Primary ask: predictable throughput for peak events (Black Friday-like spikes), strict latency SLOs for chat, and contractual guarantees that map to reserved GPU planning. Procurement/legal pushing back on MSA/DPA terms which is delaying PO.

Recent timeline / activity: 
- 2026-01-20: Intro call (AE Maya + SE Ethan). High-level requirements captured: 1k sustained concurrent chat sessions, 3k QPS for lightweight reranking at peak, target p95 generation latency <200ms for chat, <120ms for short-form reranks.
- 2026-01-27: POC kickoff — small dedicated pool (4xA100 equiv) for feature validation. Fireflies: ff_2026-02-18_Tradehaven-POC-v2.
- 2026-02-18: Throughput test results delivered: single H100-equivalent gives ~180 rps for 8k token LLM path; observed batching & KV cache reduced ...

title: Photon Health
body:
Account summary (Photon Health): Enterprise evaluation for Redwood Private. Primary drivers: compliance posture, US-only data residency, and retention controls. They are not shopping for 'model access'—they want predictable ops + evidence for audit.

Contacts (per Lauren/Markus notes):
- VP Eng (sponsor) + Security lead (auditor-facing). Names redacted in CRM until NDA signed.

What they asked for explicitly:
- 'Retention policies by data type' (request/response logs vs audit logs vs traces/metrics) with TTLs, purge semantics, and evidence.
- Legal hold / break-glass path for incident response (needs audit trail).
- Audit log export: time-windowed exports, actor filters, event type filters; ability to attach retention config export to same evidence package.
- A baseline compliance report pack (even if not a full SOC2 report) that can be generated on-demand during security review.

Wha...

title: Aperture Loom Analytics
body:
Prospect profile: mid-market B2B SaaS (product analytics) with ~300 engs. Started on Redwood Hosted API to validate dev experience. Now moving to Dedicated evaluation for predictable SLOs and data isolation. Short, choppy CRM notes below:
- PM quote: 'we need predictable latency during peak import windows; we can't have tail spikes break sessions.'
- Primary drivers: latency stability, cost predictability, security controls (VPC/KMS/retention), and routing fallback to avoid outages.
- Use-case specifics: in-app chat assistant (95% of traffic), embeddings-driven doc-search for product help, reranking for UI recommendations.
- Traffic profile: steady ~50 req/s for chat, bursts to 200 req/s during release days. Req size mostly short contexts (avg 250 tokens), some long-history queries for power users.
- Model preference: prefer open/quantized variants; want Redwood to recommend quantizat...

title: SageForge Technologies
body:
POC candidate: Enterprise code assistant for internal dev platform (SageForge Labs).

Primary ask:
- IDE copilots (VSCode + JetBrains) with streaming completions under 100ms perceived latency for single-line completions; target 30-50ms for short completions in region.
- Model/version pinning per-repo and per-team (must pin to vetted LTS model in prod).
- Low-latency streaming API to support live typing + token-level cost/latency telemetry.
- Autoswitch/fallback: must fallback to a smaller quantized model when dedicated capacity constrained.
- Auditability: immutable audit logs (user, repo, prompt hash, model version) retained 3+ years and exportable to Splunk.
- Identity: Okta SSO (SAML) + SCIM provisioning + RBAC sync.
- Keys: KMS/HSM with FIPS-140-2 support and customer-managed key option.
- Deployment: prefer VPC private deployment behind customer's transit gateway; on-prem option ...

title: Regal Wealth Management
body:
Account context (ABM regulated pilot):
- Wealth management platform + broker-dealer arm; governance is heavy (InfoSec + IAM + Risk + Data Governance all have sign-off).
- They are exploring an internal GenAI platform for advisor copilots and client comms; key constraint is auditability + access controls, not just model quality.

What we heard / priorities:
- “We need tamper-evident audit logs we can export to our SIEM. If we can’t show who accessed what, it’s dead.”
- SSO is mandatory. SAML for workforce + SCIM provisioning preferred. They have strict IdP metadata validation and want to see supported patterns.
- Data boundaries: they’re sensitive to client PII; prefer a Private (VPC) deployment. Dedicated was mentioned as a fallback if Private timeline slips, but their first preference is VPC/private control plane.
- Retention/residency: asked about configurable retention and whether ...

title: Fenris VPC Integrations
body:
Summary: Fenris is a mid-enterprise healthcare-payments platform evaluating Redwood Private (VPC) for claims NLP and payer chat.

Key constraints and ask: wants firm compliance mapping across SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA/BAA, and PCI scoping guidance. Must run in their VPC with KMS integration (customer-managed CMKs), detailed audit logging pushed to their log collector, and ability to set per-tenant data residency and log retention. Legal will require a BAA before any ePHI flows. Network team: require mutual TLS + IP allowlist + explicit diagram of egress flows.

Compliance mapping (high level):
- SOC 2: map Redwood controls to AICPA categories (Security, Availability, Confidentiality). Provide Type II report and evidence: penetration test summary, change management logs, patching SLAs. For Private VPC: recommend providing configuration checklist showing VPC isolation, subnets, security g...

title: Hightower Analytics
body:
Account qualified by AE after technical discovery call 2026-02-18.
Primary ask: dedicated GPU pool sizing + throughput guarantees for low-latency reranking pipeline (financial transactions).
AE: Elena Park - emphasis on predictable per-token cost and fallback behavior under capacity constraints.
SE: Marcus leading bench design; recommended starting with 8xA100-equivalent pool for baseline tests.
Customer stressed: "must not add >50ms tail latency on live trading flows", wants p95 guarantees; latency SLOs drive contractual terms.
Security: CISO requires SOC2 + KMS integration + 7-year audit log retention for transaction metadata (redacted inputs).
They run mixed workload: synchronous chat (SLAs), reranking on streams (high throughput), nightly embedding jobs (batch).
Customer asked for explicit fallback policy: model-variant routing + graceful degrade to CPU-safe model if GPU capacity ...

title: Verity Edge Discovery
body: Initial inbound from Director of eDiscovery (Julia Park). Very specific ask: private deployment (VPC/on-prem) + strict retention + full audit trails. Wants two-stage RAG: vector index for recall + cross-encoder reranker for legal precision. Quote from call: "We need deterministic provenance and auditability — no opaque filters." Team is skeptical of cloud-only vendors. Cost sensitivity at scale (250M documents) is primary negotiation lever. Candidate models: quantized open LLMs for embedding + specialized legal reranker. Security sent Redwood questionnaire 2025-02-01, asked for HSM/KMS flow and retention deletion SLA. Good fit for Redwood Private + Optimize for batching/quant recommendations. Internal champion: Head of Search Eng (Michael Tran).

title: Helmstone DigitalCare
body:
AE notes: strong bias to private deployment - said 'no PHI in vendor-managed multi-tenant environment'.
Infosec: wants architecture diagram showing HSM key flow + proof of key never exported; request for shared responsibility matrix.
SE notes: working on SCIM attribute map - AD uses custom attribute 'deptRole' for role assignment; mapping required to 'redwood_role' in SCIM payload.
Legal: redlined DPA attached (drive:/legal/helmstone-dpa-draft.pdf) - key asks: breach notification 24h, data residency clause, and right to audit subprocessor list.
POC results: SAML assertions OK for authentication; console role binding fails for nested AD groups (ticket INF-3941 opened).
Quote from CISO: 'We need RBAC that mirrors our AD groups — developers shouldn't be able to elevate production model access without an audit trail.'
Operational ask: audit logs must be exportable in NDJSON and pushed to ...

title: Celestis Reserve CloudWorks
body:
Summary: Celestis runs a payments risk engine + customer support chat. Primary concern is predictable tail latency during peak settlement windows.
"They will not accept black-box availability guarantees" — procurement/legal insisted on explicit credit schedule and max outage cap.
AE notes: company strongly leaning to Dedicated with Private control plane (VPC). Will consider Hosted only if SLA mirrors Dedicated economics.
SE notes: target workload = chat model (mix of 1-shot and multi-turn) + nightly re-ranking job for fraud signals. Want both low-latency interactive and batch throughput.
Security: must have KMS integration, per-request audit logging to customer's Splunk, 7-year retention option for specific event logs.
Quote from VP Ops: 'If we lose 10 minutes during peak cutover we lose millions — SLA credits must be meaningful, and scheduled maintenance windows must be planned aroun...

title: Willowmere Inference Systems
body:
Primary ask: full VPC private deployment for EU-only insurance workloads — no PII leaving EU regions
Willowmere: latency requirement for chat agents <= 120ms p95 for 512-token generation; throughput 150 concurrent sessions typical
Cost sensitivity: mid — willing to pay for reserved dedicated capacity if predictable unit economics demonstrated
Model preference: open model family compatible with quantized weights (Llama-family variants or Mistral-like), Redwood to propose verified variants
Routing/fallback: want automatic fallback to smaller quantized variant on Dedicated if contention; prefer same API surface for hosted vs private
Security: require SSO/SAML integration for console access, SOC2 attestation copy, audit logs to customer S3, CloudTrail forwarding to Splunk
KMS: strict requirement for customer-managed CMKs in AWS KMS; HSM discussion flagged for later phases
Data residency: ...

Routing/fallback: want automatic fallback to smaller quantized variant on Dedicated if contention; prefer same API surface for hosted vs private
Security: require SSO/SAML integration for console access, SOC2 attestation copy, audit logs to customer S3, CloudTrail forwarding to Splunk
KMS: strict requirement for customer-managed CMKs in AWS KMS; HSM discussion flagged for later phases
Data residency: ...

blockers: final legal review on data residency clause

title: Oxbow Enterprise Assistant
body:
Account background: mid-size payments processor with heavy compliance (PCI + regional finance regulators). Wants an enterprise code assistant integrated into dev IDEs (VS Code, JetBrains) with streaming completions. Requirements fragments:
- Low-latency target: 30-50ms per token for IDE completions; 400 concurrent coding sessions baseline.
- Model/version needs: pin to approved model builds, explicit rollout window, canary + rollback. Must support quantized fallback (e.g., 7B q4) when load spikes.
- Security: SSO/SAML via Okta + SCIM provisioning; audit logs retained 7 years; per-request trace IDs; WORM-style retention for specific artifacts.
- Data residency: US-only for PII-related code; desire for private control plane with optional on-prem inference nodes.
- Cryptography: KMS/HSM with FIPS attestations; TLS peering to VPC; end-to-end encryption for cached KV.
- Observability: per-...

title: Denali LogicWorks
body:
(short) enterprise dev platform for market-data & trading tools. Must run inside VPC; cannot exfil any PII or market data. Primary ask: IDE copilot integrated with internal VSCode fleet + low-latency streaming for token-level completions (95th pct < 100ms target on local infra).

Key stakeholders: CTO (Maya met), Head of Platform (Elliot Ramos), Security (Anika Patel), Procurement (Samir Shah).

Security/Compliance: SSO/SAML required, full audit logs per user/session, KMS-backed key management, 7-year retention for audit trails but ability to TTL code content per policy. \"Do not allow any model weights leave premise\" — quoted from security call.

POC scope: private deployment (VPC peered to Denali), 2-week perf run with 50 devs, version pinning by repo/team, streaming to VSCode LSP, fallback policy to smaller quantized model under load.

Risk notes: Network team will not sign off wi...

title: SilverLark Systems
body:
Background: Platform engineering org (backend services + payments). Strong SRE culture.
Need: Enterprise-grade IDE copilot for internal developers building payment flows; must run in VPC or on-prem; no PII leaves cluster.
Primary ask: sub-100ms streaming completions in VS Code via low-latency socket; deterministic model pinning per-service; fallbacks to smaller quantized models to preserve SLAs.
Security: mandatory SSO with SAML, audit logs for every suggestion/action, retention controls (90/365 days), KMS-managed keys for model cache encryption.
Compliance: preparing for SOC2 + upcoming PCI audit; interested in proof points for private deployment and remote attestation for hosts.
Cost sensitivity: willing to pay for reserved capacity for hot services (payment-processing team) but want Optimize to reduce token cost for batch jobs.

title: Mosaic Response Labs
body:
Primary business: outsourced contact center technology for healthcare payments + billing disputes (handles PHI and card data).
Key ask: agent assist that surfaces relevant claim details, suggested replies, plus one-click tool-calls into their agent console (SaaS UI) – must be auditable.
Quote on call: "We cannot have model outputs written to any public bucket; everything must live in our VPC and be logged" (CISO).
POC ask: 6-week POC in customer VPC, parallel run with existing agent-assist stack during business hours only; success metrics: 20% reduction in handle time, 95% suggestion accept rate >= 70%.
Spike behavior: seasonal payments windows causing 3-4x request spikes; require autoscale + graceful degradation (fallback to smaller quantized model or sync reranking).
Retention: legal requires transcripts retained for up to 7 years for certain cases; need configurable retention polic...

title: Cygnus Data Guardian
body:

Evaluating Redwood Private (VPC) to replace a fragile in-house inference stack — primary drivers: strong audit trail, KMS integration, model failover, and predictable RL throughput.

title: NovaRisk Aegis Dataworks
body:
Opportunity summary: NovaRisk Aegis Dataworks is evaluating Redwood Private (VPC) to host an internal risk-modeling assistant and sensitive-document embedding index. Primary drivers: strict data residency, audit trail for regulators, KMS/HSM key ownership, and ability to run behind the customer's VPC with private endpoints. They have enterprise risk and infosec teams; procurement will require SOC 2 + ISO 27001 evidence and a HIPAA BAA for a subset of records. PCI not in-scope for current pilots but security team asked for notes on tokenization strategy if later card data processing is needed.

Compliance mapping notes (high level):
- SOC 2: They want scope mapping against Redwood control families (CC1-CC7 equivalent). Main ask: logging/alerting evidence, change management, and penetration test reports. They requested sample SOC 2 report + description of our Private deployment responsi...

title: Elmbridge RankWorks
body:
Summary: Elmbridge RankWorks is an EU-headquartered knowledge platform for regulated professional services (legal + compliance). Primary ask: low-latency regional search across UK + EU zones with strong data residency controls and a reranking layer for precision. Targeting a multi-region architecture: primary EU (Frankfurt) + failover UK (London) with automatic routing and fast cross-region failover.

Scale & performance requirements:
- Index size: initial 80M vectors, expected growth to 180M within 12 months.
- Embedding dimension: 1536 (current transformer embeddings) but open to switching to 1024/768 for cost optimization.
- Query volume: baseline 200 qps with peak bursts to ~500 qps; steady-state 150k queries/day.
- Latency SLO: end-to-end search (ANN + top-K rerank) 60ms p95 EU region, reranker tail <= 45ms p95.
- Cost target: <$0.003 per user query (emb + ANN + rerank) or simila...

title: SlateBridge ITOps
body:
Account background: mid-market ITSM vendor building internal helpdesk chatbot for support engineers and NOC. Product teams (PM + Eng) driving vendor selection.
Primary ask: replace heuristic macros + KB search with a streaming chat agent that can call tools (ticket open/assign, run diagnostics), provide sub-200ms first-byte for short responses, and keep full audit trail for compliance.
Quote from CTO on demo: "We need replies that feel instant for our admins — long tails kill adoption. Also need a complete audit log tied to ticket IDs."
Initial interest: Hosted API for quick POC then Dedicated for predictable latency & isolation if POC meets SLOs. Open to Private only if data residency required later.
Integrations: ServiceNow + internal agent tooling (Ops CLI), Okta SSO. Want function/tool-calling to open/annotate tickets and run triage scripts via a secure runtime.
Cost sensitivity: ...

title: Arkadia Finance
body:
Account summary
- Enterprise finance logo. Primary pain: inference spend trending up due to increased support automation usage + compliance summarization. They’re cost-sensitive but latency-sensitive on customer-facing routes.
- Strong preference for Redwood Private (VPC) with dedicated capacity for predictable throughput + org isolation.

Key requirements (from calls + email)
- Workloads:
 - Chat: customer support assistant for agents (internal), plus limited customer-facing chat for FAQ.
 - Summarization: call notes + case summarization (batch + near-real-time).
 - Embeddings/doc search: policy + procedure retrieval; wants consistent embedding versions and rollback.
- Performance targets:
 - Customer-facing chat: p95 < ~900ms first token (goal), streaming required.
 - Agent-assist: p95 < ~1.5s first token; more tolerant of batching if risk is clear.
- Cost controls:
 - Wants token-l...

title: QuantGrid
body:
Account summary
- QuantGrid is an enterprise SaaS platform team building an LLM layer used by multiple product surfaces (customer-facing assistant + internal analytics copilot). They are trying to consolidate inference across business units and reduce operational churn from frequent runtime updates.
- Deal motion: Dedicated for steady-state + Private (VPC) for regulated tenants. They also asked about Optimize-style guidance for batching/caching to control unit costs.

Current stack (as described)
- Self-hosted vLLM on EKS (multi-tenant clusters) with a mix of A100 80GB and H100 (region-dependent). Some workloads also run on on-demand GPU nodes for burst.
- Routing/orchestration: internal gateway + basic load balancing; they mentioned experimenting with Ray Serve previously but pulled back due to operational overhead.
- Observability: Grafana + Prometheus, token metrics are partial; p9...

title: Ironwood Capacity Planning
body:
Account background: internal ML infra team (InferOps) building latency-sensitive chat + reranking pipelines for trading desk and client support. Heavy emphasis on 99th percentile latency and predictable token economics.

Initial ask: dedicated GPU pool with throughput guarantees and clear fallbacks (cheaper quantized models) during peaks. Team wants an SLA that covers latency SLOs (50ms-token targets at steady-state, 95ms at peak for short queries) and reserved GPU planning (monthly commit + burst).

title: Railgate Systems
body:
Summary: evaluating Redwood Hosted API initially; strong preference to migrate to Dedicated for production (data isolation & predictable latency).
Buying group: VP Product (Maya Holt) champion, Head of Infra (Kenji Sato) technical lead, CFO involved for committed pricing.

title: Northstar Support Platform
body:
Account summary:
- Mid-market support platform building LLM-powered agent assist (draft replies + case summarization) embedded in their product.
- Primary pain is not model quality, it’s operational friction: "getting to first successful call is too easy to mess up" and "we need a clear go-live path for our team and customers".

What they asked for (verbatim / paraphrase):
- "We need a production readiness checklist we can follow and share internally".
- "We don’t want surprises with quotas/rate limits once we turn on traffic".
- "Streaming disconnects are confusing — our engineers aren’t sure what’s expected vs broken".
- "Give us the steps for go-live: keys, env vars, first request, streaming, retries, monitoring, budgets." (their words)

Current state / context:
- They run a multi-tenant SaaS; will start with internal dogfood then phased rollout to a subset of customers.
- Traffic ...

title: Rivermark Clarity Platforms
body:
Background: Rivermark builds a customer analytics UI + automated insights (SaaS). Heavily interactive product recommendations and in-app help -- latency sensitive.
Initial onboarding: started on Redwood Hosted API (Q3 trial). Good dev DX, quick proof-of-concept for embeddings + doc-search. Now evaluating Dedicated for predictable throughput and data separation.
Key stakeholders: VP Eng (Tom Keane), Head of Platform (Priya Malhotra), SecOps (Jared Lee), Product Manager (Renee Ortiz). AE: Maya Rodriguez, SE: Ethan Park, CSM: Lina Gomez.
Quote from Tom on 2025-10-07 call: 'If Dedicated doesn't beat our hosted latency tail and unit cost by end of POC, we can't justify new commit.'
Commercial sensitivity: target ARR band 50k-150k; procurement will push on pricing floors and burst capacity terms.
Security ask: must support SSO/SAML, KMS-backed key management, audit logs exported to their SI...

title: Covenant Discovery Systems
body:
Summary: covenant builds an on-prem / VPC-hosted RAG product for large law firms and regulators. Primary ask: scale embeddings index to tens of millions of documents (12M core docs for POC), sub-200ms p95 end-to-end for 1-stage search with reranking, target cost per interactive query < $0.02.

Key constraints: must run in customer-managed VPC or on-prem private deployment; immutable audit logs for every query and assistant turn; retention policy configurable up to 7 years and support for legal-hold; KMS/HSM integration required for document keys.

Behavioral needs: hybrid ANN + cross-encoder reranker pipeline; deterministic reranker versioning for reproducible results in audits; redaction and PII masking for transcripts; role-based access control tied to SSO/SAML groups.

Sales notes (fragments):
- met legal/compliance team 2026-01-25 — heavy emphasis on auditability and retention enf...

title: CivicGavel Discovery Systems
body:
Summary: enterprise legal discovery vendor evaluating Redwood for embeddings + 2-stage rerank (ANN recall -> cross-encoder reranker). Heavy emphasis on auditability, retention, and private deployment.
Scale: currently ~12M parsed docs (contracts, briefs, correspondence) + expected ingest of 30M historical records over 6 months. Target index size ~40-60M passages.
Performance targets: P99 query latency with reranker <= 350ms, average latency (user-facing) ~120-200ms. Throughput target 200 qps for peak support searches; nightly batch reranking runs for reindexing.
Cost sensitivity: aiming for <$0.015/query (embedding recall + rerank amortized). Interested in Redwood Optimize recommendations (batching + quantization) to reduce token spend on reranker cross-encoder steps.
Model preferences: open to community LLMs for embedding (SBERT-family or fine-tuned LegalBERT embeddings) and a smalle...

title: Voyant PrivateNet Solutions
body:
Initial intro 2025-11-10 (Maya) — strong interest in private VPC with EU-only residency.
Customer quote: 'We cannot have any egress to non-EU datacenters; need JSON audit trails for every inference call.'
Security review kickoff 2026-01-12 — security requested flow diagrams, IP ranges, PrivateLink pricing impact.
Networking prefers AWS PrivateLink over VPC peering due to centralized egress/firewall model.
Active evaluation of KMS: want BYOK with customer-managed keys + HSM-backed attestations.
Compliance: SOC2 and ISO27001 required; legal asked for retention policy configurable per tenant.
POC target: 2-week integration inside dev VPC, then perf validation with 50 rps sustained for chat workload.
Cost sensitivity: willing to pay premium for private isolation but expects batching/Optimize recommendations to reduce token unit costs.
SE runbooks requested: firewall ACLs, allowed IP range...

title: Railstreet SupportCore
body:
Summary: Mid-market SaaS (IT/helpdesk) product team evaluating Redwood for internal support chatbot (ticketing + runbooks). Start with Hosted API POC -> likely move to Dedicated if latency / cost profile meets SLOs.

Key asks and constraints:
- Primary workload: internal IT chat agent (employees only) + escalation routing to human agents. Emphasis on streaming responses and tool calling (create ticket, query CMDB).
- Latency target: p95 end-to-end token streaming latency < 250ms for preferred flows; soft requirement for 95% of queries to begin streaming within 150-200ms.
- Audit & retention: must retain chat transcripts for 1 year with searchable audit logs; ability to shorten retention per team and export logs on demand.
- Security: SSO/SAML, enterprise KMS, SOC2 type 2/as part of procurement checklist; infosec wants network topology and data residency options.
- Cost sensitivity: mi...

title: SentinelCare Systems, Inc.
body:
CRM notes: Private VPC / compliance mapping POC.

Summary: SentinelCare has a HIPAA-bound PHI ingestion workflow for clinical notes and support chat. They require a Private VPC deployment (preferred) with customer-managed KMS, strict audit logging, and data residency in US-East. Primary decision criteria: legal/BAA approval, Infosec sign-off on audit trails, predictable latency for near-real-time summarization, and demonstrable token-level cost breakdown.

Compliance mapping (high-level) - discussed on 2025-01-29 and 2025-02-06: 
- SOC 2: customer requested Redwood's SOC 2 Type II report and a short control mapping document. They care about CC6 (access controls), CC7 (change management) and CC8 (system operations). Action: provide SOC2 summary + table mapping Redwood Private controls to their auditor checklist. (linked doc in drive).
- ISO 27001: need scope statement showing Private V...

title: Lyra Payments Network
body:
2026-02-15: Intro call w/ Maya. High-level ask: move fraud scoring inference off batch to always-on low-latency Dedicated GPU pool.
2026-02-18: POC kickoff. Want 99.95% availability for inference endpoints; critical for payment auth paths.
POC scope: stress tests to 400 RPS sustained, p95 latency target < 120ms for single-turn prompts (32-64 tokens).
Interested in automatic model-variant fallback: prefer quantized 8-bit model when primary saturated; acceptable quality drop for non-critical scoring only.
Customer explicitly: "we cannot tolerate >200ms p95 on auth path" — ops lead quote.
Security: must pass SOC2 + have SSO + audit logs. Data residency: US only for payments data; need KMS integration with vendor HSM.
Cost sensitivity: will commit to 12-month reserved capacity if unit costs predictable and can supply burst options for peak Black Friday traffic.
Legal requested sample SLA ...

title: Latency Guardian Technologies
body:
CRM notes (concise, call fragments and timeline):

- 2025-11-20: Initial inbound from infra procurement (R. Malik). Interested in dedicated GPUs for low-latency trading signals. Wants baseline throughput guarantees.
- 2026-01-15: Demo with Maya + Carlos. Ran synthetic latency demo (p95 token generation ~18ms on small context). Customer asked about reserved GPU planning: target steady-state 40 concurrent gen streams, + 2x burst for market open.
- 2026-02-02: Security questionnaire submitted. Key asks: on-prem deployment package, KMS integration, audit log retention (7 years), SOC2 report.
- 2026-02-28: Call (FF id ff_2026-02-28_lgt_onboarding_call). Legal introduced procurement checklist. Primary headaches: insurance limits, indemnity caps, export controls.
- 2026-03-04: Deep dive w/ security (FF id ff_2026-03-04_security_review). They flagged clause that ties SLA credits to uptime onl...

title: Velarum Aerospace Systems
body:
Summary:
- Velarum Aerospace Systems evaluating Redwood Private (VPC) for inference workloads that must meet ITAR/data residency constraints. Primary use: streaming telemetry embeddings + real-time ops chat for mission control, plus document search/reranking across maintenance manuals.

Qualification highlights (from calls):
- Security/Compliance: must be ITAR-compatible, SOC2 required, ISO27001 preferred. Primary legal ask: ensure no egress of raw telemetry outside U.S. region.
- KMS/BYOK: customer wants customer-managed keys (AWS KMS + HSM option). Integration needs to support envelope encryption and HSM-backed attestation.
- Audit Logging: central logging into their Splunk instance; logs must include token-level request identifiers, model version, route, and correlation id. Retention policy: 7 years for specific telemetry events.
- Network/topology: VPC-only deployment, no internet...

title: WritWise Discovery LLC
body:
Summary: evaluating Redwood Private VPC deployment for enterprise RAG search across 8-10TB of legal docs (contract corp repositories, discovery sets). Interested in embeddings + reranking pipeline to reduce false positives in e-discovery.

Call notes 2026-02-18 with GC + Head of Legal Ops:
- Customer insists on audit trail for all queries (per user) and immutable retention for 7 years; retention controls and WORM-like policies required.
- Quote from GC: 'We need an auditable chain — who queried what document and when; redaction/meta must survive export.'
- Performance ask: 95th percentile latency <250ms for vector similarity + top-k reranking hybrid (k=50 -> rerank top10) on typical query.
- Scale: 8-10TB index, expecting 200-600 QPS peak for batch review seasons; average ~120 QPS. Cost sensitivity: high for sustained queries; will evaluate cost per query vs in-house Elastic + custom ...

title: Northwind Analytics (LLM)
body:
Account summary
- Northwind evaluating Redwood for long-context summarization of customer-owned datasets (reports, tickets, PDFs). Primary pain today is tail latency variability and cost predictability when requests swing between short prompts and very long-context summarization.
- They are currently prototyping on a mix of managed endpoints + internal GPU nodes; want a consistent runtime + clearer operational knobs.

Use case / workload
- Workload: long-context summarization ("quarterly narrative" + "case timeline") and some doc-search/embedding for retrieval.
- Context window requirements: routinely 16k–32k tokens; target to support 64k for a subset (exec summaries / consolidated case bundles).
- Traffic pattern: bursty during end-of-month reporting; mixed workloads (short interactive + long offline jobs) hitting same service unless we split pools.
- Latency: p50 is acceptable; main...

title: Solstice Sentinel Platforms
body:
POC summary and customer signals:
- Company: mid-market SaaS (support/knowledge) — needs predictable latency + control over data residency.
- Current state: positive on hosted API (fast onboarding) but want Dedicated for SLA, VPC peering, and long-term unit economics.
- Primary workloads: agent chat (low-latency), doc search (embeddings), reranking pipeline for search results.
- Targets: p95 <150ms chat, 200 qps sustained for peak flows, cost sensitivity medium-high (looking to reduce token unit cost after migration).
- Security: require SSO/SAML, SOC2 attestation, audit logs, KMS integration, and EU data residency for EU customers. Legal flagged retention policy agreement.

POC technical plan (agreed in architecture call):
- Duration: 30 days Dedicated POC (staged).
- Canary plan: start with 5% of production traffic -> 20% after 72h -> 50% after 7 days -> full traffic on success. Can...

title: Cobalt Concise Coaching
body:
Key contact: VP Product — Arjun Menon. Wants: short coaching highlights (< 30s), per-agent QA scorecards, and automated ticket drafts.
Primary deployment ask: customer wants private control plane deployed in their GCP VPC (in region us-central1) + optional on-prem connector for secure transcript storage.
Quote from call: 'We cannot have more than 300ms perceived delay when agent requests a summary during wrap-up.'
Current stack: Twilio to ingest calls -> in-house ASR -> chaining to LLM for summary. Pain points: KV cache hurts concurrency, expensive token costs during peak hours, flaky autoscaling.
Security: legal flagged retention duration and export controls; require per-customer KMS and audit logs forwarded to their SIEM.
POC dataset: 2 months of anonymized transcripts + 5 labelled QA rubrics (attached in Drive).
Pricing sensitivity: willing to commit to reserved capacity if unit ec...

title: LitMesh Privacy & Discovery
body:
Account snapshot: LitMesh builds compliance search for large law firms and internal legal ops. They ingest client contracts, court filings, regulatory records. Primary ask: high-recall RAG with deterministic audit trails and per-tenant retention policies.

Discovery/requirements (from 2026-02 calls):
- Index scale: pilot 5M documents (~1.2TB raw), prod target 40-60M docs across 3 legal practice groups. Expect frequent updates (delta pushes nightly, some near-real-time ingestion for filings).
- Embeddings: want 1536-d embeddings (open model OK) but open to Redwood-verified vector models if quality/cost better. Concerned about embedding compute window on full reindex.
- Reranking: central to UX. Prefer lightweight cross-encoder reranker for top-50 candidates, latency SLO 150-250ms p95 for rerank step.
- Latency/throughput: search latency end-to-end target <= 300ms p95 for single query (...

title: Silica Savant Search
body:
Top-line: Silica Savant is a legal search product used by enterprise law firms. They ingest contracts, briefs, and precedent sets. Current stack: Elastic for index, custom embedding pipeline (OpenAI embeddings), cross-encoder reranker (self-hosted on variable GPU infra). Main drivers: cost at scale and unpredictable latency during peak review windows.

Customer quotes / call excerpts:
- CTO (A. Morales): "We spend >$60k/mo on embeddings and the reranker infra is brittle during month-end reviews. If we can cut cost 3x while maintaining or improving relevance, this is a go."
- Head of Search (L. Chen): "We're terrified of regressions — a bad migration is a product disaster. Need evals + canary routes + clear rollback."

Evals plan (requested):
- Baseline harness (existing reranker + OpenAI emb) -> store metrics (MRR, NDCG, latency distribution).
- Experiment A: local open quantized embe...

title: Deep Anchor Maritime Analytics
body:
Summary: Enterprise maritime analytics provider evaluating Redwood Dedicated for real-time on-bridge assistance, log embeddings for search, and reranking for route suggestions. Primary driver is deterministic throughput during port-traffic spikes and strict latency SLOs for captain-facing tooling.

Workload profile (customer-supplied estimates / traces):
- Baseline steady-state QPS: 20–35 requests/sec (typical daytime operations).
- Average per-request tokens: 120 tokens input + 280 tokens output (templated explanations + route contexts) => ~400 tokens/req.
- Calculated steady-state token/sec: 20 * 400 = 8,000 tps (lower bound) to 35 * 400 = 14,000 tps (upper bound).
- Peak / burst scenario (harbor approach & emergency mode): short bursts up to 110–130 QPS observed in traces. Peak token/sec ~ 130 * 400 = 52,000 tps.

title: Aurelia Regional AI Labs
body:
Summary: Global mid-sized banking division evaluating Redwood Private (VPC) for regulated models. Primary ask: strict data residency and regional controls (EU-only for core PII/modeling, separate UK tenancy, Canada for Canadian customer segments, APAC sandbox in Singapore). High compliance bar — SOC2/ISO proof required + audit logging into Splunk/Microsoft Sentinel. CISO wants BYOK with HSM-backed KMS; legal wants explicit processing boundary language (no telemetry leaving region).

Highlights / quotes:
- CISO (Maria Dupont): 'We need cryptographic control — keys never leave our KMS. If model telemetry leaves region we cannot sign off.'
- CTO (Oliver Reed): 'Performance targets: 50ms p50 token latency for support-agent flows, bursts to 200 QPS for embeddings. Cost must be predictable — interested in Dedicated reserved capacity for core workloads.'

- 2026-0...

title: Cerise Orbit Analytics
body:
Company: B2B product analytics platform; customers are product managers + data teams.
Primary objective: replace 3rd-party vector search + chat with integrated Redwood-backed assistant to reduce latency and TCO.
Started with Hosted API to validate model outputs and prompt templates; impressed by quality but cost and burst predictability are concerns.
POC ask: Dedicated pool that can guarantee 25 RPS with tail-latency SLOs and region failover between us-west and eu-west.
Quote from VP Eng on 2026-01-15: 'We need predictable per-tenant budgets and a plan to degrade gracefully if capacity is constrained.'
Model preferences: prefer open LLM variants (Llama2 / Falcon family), want verified quantized profiles. Want 70B variant for core assistant, fallback 13B/7B for heavy loads.
Routing wishes: smart routing by latency & cost tier; prefer canary rollout with traffic shifting and immediate r...

title: Meridian SecureAI Systems
body:
Account background: large mid-tier trading platform (regulated, EU customers). Evaluating Private VPC deployment for production inference. Primary ask: strong controls for PII and transaction metadata. Team: Head of ML infra (Anika Rao) + SecOps (Gareth Flynn).

Summary of requirements:
- Deployment: Private VPC with option to run on-prem for air-gapped pockets. Data residency: EU-first (primary region eu-west-1) with ability to store audit logs in customer's S3/GCS.
- Identity: SAML IdP (Okta) + SCIM provisioning for groups. Must map IdP groups to console roles (admin, ops, viewer). Auto-provisioning on user create; deprovision on suspend.
- RBAC: Need granular RBAC in control plane: model deploy, rollout, billing/view-only, key admin. Enforce least-privilege; support custom roles.
- KMS: BYOK support via customer-managed KMS/HSM. Need proof-of-concept for envelope encryption and rot...

title: Lodestar VPC AuditWorks
body:
- Account: large payments processor; strict audit & residency rules.
- Primary ask: private (VPC) deployment with SIEM-first audit pipeline and customer-controlled KMS.
- Security team: wants full event stream for all inference transactions (request metadata, model id/version, input hash, token counts) forwarded to their Splunk cluster via an internal forwarder inside VPC.
- Legal: retention constraints, cannot retain PII longer than 90 days; prefer ability to set per-log-type retention.
- Ops: requests syslog/JSON over TLS endpoint + S3-compatible archive for long-term immutable storage.
- Quote from SecOps: "We need field-level mapping for every user-facing request — tieback to merchant id and redaction policy must be demonstrable."
- Priorities: (1) SIEM export + schema, (2) KMS key hierarchy & rotation plan, (3) retention SLAs, (4) admin access logs and RBAC auditability.

title: Copperlark Collective
body:
CRM notes: mid-market PLG company building an in-product assistant and support augmentation. Primary ask: predictable latency and cost for heavy chat workloads plus embeddings for search. Initially tried hosted_api for quick iteration — liked the SDK and streaming. Now pushing to Dedicated for production: want reserved GPU pools, VPC peering, and strict audit logs.

POC scope (agreed): 14-day Dedicated POC in us-east-1, small canary (5% traffic from beta users) -> ramp to 30% if p95 latency < 300ms and error rate <0.5%. Canary routing plan: route by user cohort via routing policy tied to user_id hash; telemetry surfaced in Redwood Console.

Fallback / resilience plan: primary Dedicated cluster (us-east-1) -> fallback to hosted_api (lower-cost model variant) if queue depth or pod failure detected; secondary failover to eu-west-1 region for read-only features (embeddings) only. Model va...

title: Meridian Vectorworks
body:
Account snapshot: mid-sized enterprise search vendor; builds verticalized RAG for finance and legal customers. Heavy emphasis on embedding quality + reranker performance. Currently running cross-encoder reranker on top of a dense-vector candidate set (bi-encoder) — expensive at scale. Looking to migrate to lighter reranker + evaluation-driven rollout to cut cost and improve latency without degrading relevance.

Key constraints & asks (verbatim/near):
- "P95 < 150ms for top-K pipeline end-to-end (embedding lookup + reranker) for web-tier queries"
- indexing scale ~ 400M active passages (sharded across 12 clusters)
- target cost-per-query <= $0.002 (avg across steady-state traffic)
- must pass our eval harness (precision@10, MRR, A/B win-rate) before any traffic migration
- security: SSO/SAML, audit logs, KMS integration, US-EAST data residency

Conversation flavour: quick bullets, lots...

normalized.cleanBody:
Account snapshot: mid-sized enterprise search vendor; builds verticalized RAG for finance and legal customers. Heavy emphasis on embedding quality + reranker performance. Currently running cross-encoder reranker on top of a dense-vector candidate set (bi-encoder) — expensive at scale. Looking to migrate to lighter reranker + evaluation-driven rollout to cut cost and improve latency without degrading relevance.

Key constraints & asks (verbatim/near):
- "P95 < 150ms for top-K pipeline end-to-end (embedding lookup + reranker) for web-tier queries"
- indexing scale ~ 400M active passages (sharded across 12 clusters)
- target cost-per-query <= $0.002 (avg across steady-state traffic)
- must pass our eval harness (precision@10, MRR, A/B win-rate) before any traffic migration
- security: SSO/SAML, audit logs, KMS integration, US-EAST data residency

Conversation flavour: quick bullets, lots...

title: Vertex AI Tools
body:
Account summary
- Vertex AI Tools is an AI tooling vendor (agent workflow + eval/monitoring) looking at a technology partnership where Redwood is a selectable inference backend inside their product. They also want guidance on how to position the combined story for marketplace-driven procurement (AWS first, GCP later).

What they said (call notes / snippets)
- "We’re getting pulled into enterprise deals where the buyer already has AWS Marketplace as the procurement path." They want a clean story: buyer buys Vertex + inference in one motion OR buyer buys inference via marketplace and Vertex plugs in.
- "Our customers complain about inference being the flaky part" — they want Redwood’s reliability / fallback narrative and operational controls to be part of the joint value prop.
- Interested in a co-branded quickstart: Vertex workflow template + Redwood API key + a baseline eval pack (lat...

title: Sagehaven Medical Retrieval Hub
body: Account summary: Sagehaven operates a cloud-connected clinical document repository serving 4 hospital systems. Primary ask: enterprise RAG search with strong PHI controls. They ingest discharge summaries, progress notes, radiology reports (~12M documents, ~2TB raw). Key requirements: low-p95 latency for clinician workflows, reranking step to demote boilerplate and promote latest diagnoses, per-request audit logging, and ability to run components inside customer VPC or on-prem.

title: Coppergate SecureApps
body:
Summary: Coppergate is a mid-sized fintech offering portfolio analytics and regulatory reporting. Very stringent controls around who can call models and where data lives.
From call: 'We cannot allow any tokens or logs to leave EU control plane — legal will kill the deal otherwise.'
AE notes: Need to map Redwood RBAC roles to Coppergate org roles (admin, security-auditor, app-service, read-only). They want SCIM group sync so we can auto-provision service accounts per application team.
Security ask: Provide KMS attestation + public key for envelope encryption. They prefer customer-managed KMS with HSM and FIPS 140-2 level 3 if possible.
Network ask: VPC peering or PrivateLink; egress limited; strict NAT gateway constraints. Netops requested example terraform and CIDR ranges.
POC scope (agreed): 4-week POC in EU region. Deliverables: SAML login flow, SCIM provisioning demo, RBAC role mat...

title: Cascade DataTrust
body:
Summary: Cascade DataTrust is a European healthcare data custodian evaluating Redwood Private (VPC) to host sensitive PHI and derived embeddings. Primary drivers: strict EU data residency, HIPAA BAA for US trials, SOC 2/ISO27001 alignment for supplier risk, and customer auditability (long-term retention + SIEM integration). They want a vendor that supports customer-managed keys (BYOK/HSM), pushable audit logs to their SIEM/S3, and a private control plane with per-route cost/latency controls.

Key requirements (from security + infra):
- Data residency: All production inference in EU regions; no staging traffic to non-EU without explicit opt-in.
- KMS: Customer-managed keys required for encryption-at-rest; support for AWS KMS & CloudHSM (BYOK) and key-rotation policies.
- Audit logging: Full request/response metadata (redacted where PHI appears) exported to customer's S3 + SIEM ingest; ...

title: Elmwood Sentinel Labs
body:
Background: Elmwood Sentinel Labs builds product analytics widgets embedded in enterprise SaaS dashboards. Heavy real-time summarization + similarity search for feature surfaces.
Timeline (short):
- 2026-01-10: Initial sign-up and hosted API sandbox trial (self-serve). Saw good quality on summarization prompts.
- 2026-01-22: Phone call (Jordan) — product team: 'need predictable latency for live widgets, can't have 300-500ms spikes'. Asked about Dedicated options.
- 2026-02-05: Demo of hosted API burst behavior; Redwood showed batching/cache options. Elmwood requests baseline P50/P95 numbers with sample traffic.
- 2026-02-25: Deep technical call (Fireflies ff_2026-02-25_elmwood-poc). Shared Fireflies transcript. Discussed KV prefix caching and tokenization patterns.
- 2026-03-04: Architecture review with SE (Priya), infra and security present. Raised VPC peering and SOC2 retention ques...

title: Aurelia LegalTech Solutions
body:
CISO (Omar J.): 'We need assurance that customer CMKs are never available to Redwood staff and that any decryption requires the customer's key.'
Identity: Okta enterprise SSO. Expectation: SAML SP initiated login and SCIM v2 provisioning. They want group-to-role mapping: 'legal-admins' -> admin, 'ml-ops' -> se, 'reviewers' -> app-user, 'compliance' -> auditor-readonly.
SCIM pain: Okta sends group members as 'members' with value 'id' while our mapping doc expects 'value' attribute. SE to propose transformation layer or mapping template.
RBAC ask: console roles must be granular. Aurelia requires a custom role that can view logs and export them but cannot change KMS bindings.
KMS details: prefer AWS KMS key in Aurelia account with grant to Redwood control plane role. Needs example trust policy, key rotation cadence notes, and HSM-backed option details.
Audit logs: retention requested 5 y...

Company runs a hybrid payments + patient-intake platform; strict EU/US residency and audit trail requirements. CTO quote: "We cannot have PHI leave our VPC or be logged in a non-audited store."

Recent timeline:
- 2026-02-12: Intro demo (ff recording: FF-2026-02-12-ceres-demo). Discussed Redwood private control plane, VPC peering, KMS options. AE (Maya) + SE (Ravi) on call.
- 2026-02-20: Security questionnaire submitted (attached drive/security/ceres_shield_security_faq_v1.md).
- 2026-02-26: Legal requested BAA draft and PCI scoping note.
- 2026-03-01: Architecture sync; customer provided network diagram and legacy DB list.
- 2026-03-08: Follow-up on KMS integration and encryption-at-rest responsibilities.

Compliance mapping (high level):
- SOC 2: Redwood privat...

title: HelioCare Retrieval Systems
body:
- Intro call 2025-11-18 w/ CTO (M. Park) + Dir Product (S. Ramirez).
- Wants a single query flow: embeddings index -> candidate retrieval -> transformer reranker, kept within customer VPC.
- Top priorities: PHI protections, immutable audit log for every query, residency in NA, signed BAA, and ability to run in on-prem air-gapped later.
- Quote from CTO: "If latency exceeds 250ms for triage queries we lose clinical utility."
- Cost target: <$0.02 per query (avg) for blended production workload (mix of short queries + long document reranks).
- Interested in Redwood Optimize recommendations (batching + quant profiles) to meet cost SLOs.
- Security team asked for sample audit log schema and KMS integration diagram (sent drive link).
- POC plan: index 45M clinical notes (de-identified set for initial test), then run 3M simulated queries to measure tail latency and cost.
- SE demo 2026-02-2...

title: Axiom Freight Intelligence
body: Key account: supply-chain + last-mile focus. Narrow ask: dedicated GPU pool to run routing/embeddings + a small production chat assistant for drivers. Workshop run 2026-03-02 with AE, SE, CTO and ops. Security: on-prem KMS + strict retention; legal asked for network egress caps. Team emphasized predictable unit cost and deterministic p95 latency for driver app (mobile). Shorthand from call: 'can't risk 1s+ tails during peak dispatch windows' — CTO. They're open to mixed-model routing: heavy model for core planning, smaller quantized fallback for degraded mode. Procurement asked for 12-month reserved commitment options and burst credits. Wants performance numbers that map directly to seat costs for pilot (50k monthly QPS budget target).

title: AegisLex Regulatory Search
body:
Account: UK-based regtech provider; product used by banks and regulators for retrospective compliance search.
Primary ask: low-latency enterprise search across 100M+ documents with strong UK/EU residency and audit trail.
Workload profile: high read-heavy search, bursts around end-of-day reporting windows; typical QPS target 1.5k-3k during peaks; avg queries/day target for POC: 50k.
Indexing scale: planning for 120M documents (policy records, email archives, filings). Concerned about vector index sharding and storage cost.
Search stack desired: dense embeddings for recall + lightweight reranker for precision (top-50 -> rerank to top-5). They want deterministic rerank latency p50 < 30ms, p95 < 120ms.
Latency requirements: EU-only interactive queries should be <50ms regional RTT for initial vector search; end-to-end (query + rerank) p95 target 200ms.
Cost sensitivity: procurement wants g...

title: Lattice Search
body:
Account summary
- Enterprise doc-search team building an internal + customer-facing knowledge search experience across tickets, docs, and Slack/Confluence exports.
- Strong preference for a "production launch checklist" they can use as an internal approval artifact (security + platform teams want a clear list of go-live requirements).

Workload / technical fit
- Primary workload: embeddings + reranking for document retrieval; secondary: summarization of retrieved passages.
- Latency: wants p95 < 500ms for rerank on typical query traffic; embeddings batch jobs can be async.
- Model posture: open to Redwood catalog defaults, but wants ability to pin model version and have a defined rollback/fallback policy.
- Observability: wants token/cost breakdown + request tracing hooks; asked specifically how to audit "who changed model settings" and "when".

Security + compliance (main theme)
- Cu...

title: InquiSight Legal Discovery
body:
Company background: mid-sized e-discovery provider focused on EU regulators and large financial institutions.
Primary problem: current search stack (Elasticsearch + off-the-shelf embeddings) has high tail latency and poor precision for legal queries; lots of manual review time.
Indexing scale: initial corpus ~20M documents (compressed), expect growth 2x in 12 months; average doc size 8k tokens, many PDFs and nested attachments.
Throughput & latency targets: 500 qps peak for internal user workflows; p95 end-to-end RAG (embed + ANN + rerank) target <= 250ms, ideal <=150ms for prioritized flows.
Cost sensitivity: procurement wants < $0.03/query average for production RAG workflows (embedding + ANN + lightweight reranker); willingness to pay more for low-latency SLAs.
Model preferences: open models preferred (Llama-family quantized); want option to run cross-encoder reranker (smaller T5-l...

title: Arcadia Capital Markets
body: Qualification notes: Arcadia is a regulated EU capital markets firm (trading + compliance). Primary ask: private VPC deployment with strict data residency (EU-only), integration with customer-managed KMS (BYOK), and audit logs forwarded to their SIEM. Latency targets: p50 < 80ms for short chat (64 token), p95 < 250ms for production reranking flows; throughput: 1200 qps for embeddings pipelines during EOD runs (batch preferred). Cost sensitivity: moderate — willing to pay premium for isolation and compliance.

title: Claimesis Discovery Systems
body:
Topline: Claimesis is an EU-based litigation support & compliance vendor (mid-enterprise legal teams) evaluating a private/VPC RAG stack for regulated customer discovery products. Key ask: high-scale embeddings + two-stage rerank with strict retention/audit controls.

Customer quotes / voice: "Need search p95 <350ms end-to-end for legal search (top10 rerank included)." Wants transparent cost per query; target <= $0.05 across average search traffic.

Requirements summary:
- Corpus: target ingest ~20M documents (~200M pages) initially, steady-state growth ~30%/yr.
- Indexing: embeddings at 1536 dims (open to quantized variants), ANN index HNSW/IVF for scale; snapshotting & incremental re-index support required.
- Reranking: bi-encoder recall + cross-encoder top-10 cross-attention rerank. Expect rerank CPU/GPU tradeoff; customer wants fallback to cheaper reranker for high QPS.
- Latency:...

title: Ironclad Vantage Labs
body:
Qualification summary:
- Vertical: EU-headquartered fintech processing payments and KYC data. High sensitivity for PII and transaction metadata.
- Primary ask: Private VPC deployment (customer-managed control plane inside their AWS account or connected VPC) with strict EU data residency.
- Workloads: low-latency chat assistant for support (p95 <= 150ms), embeddings for semantic search across docs, reranking for fraud detection pipelines.

Security / compliance status:
- Completed: NDA + initial SOC2 questionnaire review. Provided SOC2 and ISO27001 badges.
- In progress: Full security review by Ironclad Infosec (starting 2026-03-15). They requested details on network topology, KMS integration, and audit log retention.
- Outstanding: Legal requires explicit flow diagram proving no tokenized PII leaves EU. Need architecture notes for fallbacks.

title: HyperCurrent Realtime Accelerators
body:
Intro: building low-latency reranking + chat for algo-trading desk. Need sub-60ms p95 for thin client chat (avg prompt 40 tokens).
Primary ask: dedicated GPUs in region (us-east) with predictable tail latency. Interested in Redwood Optimize recommendations (batching, prefix caching, quantization guidance).
Cost sensitivity: high. Goal is unit cost < $0.0008/token for production reranking pipeline at peak. Will accept quantized 4-bit/8-bit model variants if quality delta < 2% on eval set.
Throughput: baseline 200 req/s steady, burst to 800 req/s for market open. 99th percentile latency SLO 120ms for rerank, 50-60ms p95 for chat single-turn.
Model preferences: prefer open models (Llama-2-13B, Mistral-like), but will evaluate Llama-2-70B-quantized if Redwood shows latency/cost win. Want explicit perf matrix per model variant.
Routing/fallback: need region-aware routing + lower-cost fallb...

title: Veritas Loom Dynamics
body:
Opportunity summary: mid-market B2B SaaS (product analytics) building an in-app support agent + doc search. Initially evaluated Redwood Hosted API — positive on latency and quality. Now moving to Dedicated POC to validate steady throughput and cost predictability under peak sessions.

POC goals (explicit):
- Validate Dedicated pool with 8x T4s equivalent throughput for peak: target p95 token-latency <= 200ms for short-chat flows (<=512 tokens), steady QPS 60 concurrent sessions.
- Measure unit cost delta Hosted vs Dedicated (expected 30-45% reduction at commit levels).
- Prove canary routing and rollback: start with 5% traffic to Dedicated canary, ramp to 25% then 100% over 48-72h windows if metrics steady.

Canary + fallback routing plan (agreed with SE):
- Canary: 5% of production traffic for 48h -> monitor p95 latency, error rate, token throughput, quality regressions (user satisfa...

title: Ironbark Enterprise Reserve
body:
Summary: Ironbark is an enterprise financial software provider evaluating Redwood Dedicated / Private for production inference of trading and portfolio tools. Biggest priorities are predictable throughput during market hours, strict latency SLOs, and legalized SLA credit mechanics.
Workload specifics: sustained 150-200 QPS at peak business windows; burst up to 400 QPS for market open; P50 latency target <= 50ms for small chat/rerank calls, P95 <= 250ms. For certain trading-critical endpoints they require 'fast-path' routing with P99 < 500ms SLA.
Reserved GPU planning: initial ask is 8 GPUs (A100-equivalent) dedicated, with headroom to burst 2x for market opens. They want a clear plan showing how Redwood maps GPU types to equivalent throughput and a timeline for adding capacity (within 48h for dedicated pools).
SLA & uptime: Ironbark legal is pushing for 99.95% monthly uptime for produ...

title: Lexium Compliance Analytics
body:
Summary: enterprise legal/compliance RAG use-case. Heavy emphasis on private/VPC deployment, long retention and auditable trails. Wants a repeatable on-prem pilot to validate latency and unit economics.

Key technical constraints / asks:
- Indexing: must support 12M+ docs, chunking strategy to preserve legal context (paragraph-level + metadata). Lexium wants deterministic chunk hashes for dedup/retention.
- Embeddings: prefer 1536-d float16 pipeline with KV cache enabled; willing to test quantized 8-bit variants if quality delta < 2% on QA set.
- Reranking: cross-encoder reranker for final top-10; latency budget tight — need dynamic batching and GPU pre-warming.
- Routing/fallback: primary = private cluster (VPC), fallback = Redwood dedicated pool in same region with model variant pinned.
- Security/compliance: strict retention controls (7+ years for certain matter types), immutable a...

title: Cascade Financial Group
body:
Account: Cascade Financial Group (CFG). Regulated financial services, heavy internal governance.

Current state / motivation:
- Building LLM-powered internal assistant for Tier-1 support + policy Q&A. Secondary: knowledge search across internal docs, and meeting summarization for ops.
- They tried a prototype using managed endpoints but got blocked on networking + audit requirements.

Deployment + networking constraints (VPC Private):
- Customer-managed AWS VPC. Must avoid public egress during routine ops; exceptions require security sign-off.
- Strong preference for using VPC endpoints / private links for any required services.
- Artifact distribution must work via: private ECR (or equivalent), internal chart repo mirror, and/or S3 bucket behind a gateway endpoint.
- They asked explicitly: 'Can upgrades run if we temporarily disable NAT?' -> Answer expected: yes, with internal mirror...

title: Auriga Financial Services
body:
Key ask from Auriga: deterministic tail latency for trader-facing assistant and hard blackout for maintenance during 13:30-17:30 UTC
They stressed auditability: need token-level audit logs for regulatory trace with KMS-wrapped keys — encryption at rest required
POC produced a list of 12 latency outliers; SE prioritized three kernel-level optimizations and a change to KV cache eviction policy
Auriga procurement requested breakdown: base reserved fee, per-token overage, burst pricing, and credit terms — pricing deck linked
Legal quote: 'We can accept credits but need an API to pull raw telemetry to independently verify incidents'
Customer asked for a 'black box' SLA clause: automatic fallback to a certified small-model does not count as downtime as long as result quality thresholds are met
Action item: SE (Liam) to provide latency trace examples and proposed probe definitions by 2026-03...

title: Lynchpin Compliance Solutions
body:
Summary: Lynchpin builds compliance automation for mid/large banks; looking to replace on-prem inference infra with Redwood Private (VPC) for lower ops burden and standardized audits.
Primary ask: Private VPC deployment with customer-managed KMS/HSM integration, end-to-end audit logs, and explicit data residency to US-East (and Canada for certain clients).
Security team emphasis: must map Redwood controls to Lynchpin SOC 2 / ISO 27001 control matrix; need evidence (architecture diagram, control owners, test results).
HIPAA/BAA: not core product currently, but they have a healthcare vertical pilot — legal asked for BAA language and proof of handling PHI; they want encryption-in-transit & at-rest plus KMS separation for PHI keys.
PCI considerations: they tokenize card data upstream but want confirmation that Redwood Private components will not be in PCI CDE; need guidance on scoping and...

title: Vermillion Capital, Inc.
body: Enterprise evaluation focused on Dedicated capacity. Key asks: guaranteed throughput for daytime banking windows, strict latency SLOs for chat and reranking, and iterative POC with soak tests. CTO: 'We cannot tolerate p95 > 200ms for interactive chat during peak hours' — flagged as non-negotiable. Security team requires KMS integration and audit logs sent to their Splunk instance. Finance prefers committed monthly reservation with burst capacity. Initial interest driven by cost predictability and SLA-backed latency guarantees.

title: Windward Avenue Software
body:
Account background: Windward Avenue builds a product analytics + in-product guidance layer used by enterprise SaaS customers. Heavy real-time chat for in-app help and search-based feature discovery. Currently on a mix of hosted LLM APIs for prototypes; want predictable economics and isolation for paid customers.
Initial outreach (2025-11-04): inbound via demo signup. AE Jordan Meyers assigned. Early ask = improve chat latency and consistent cost for top 20 customers.
Hosted API demo (2025-12-09): gave walkthrough of Redwood Hosted API (streaming, function calling). Product team liked per-route cost analytics and model catalog. Quote from PM: 'Hosted latency is great — but we need traffic guarantees for paying accounts.'

body:
Healthcare analytics vendor processing PHI for clinical decision support
Primary ask: Private VPC deployment with customer-managed KMS + HSM support
Data residency: must keep primary PHI in Canada (ON) and move copies to EU (FR) for research after anonymization
Quote from CTO: 'We cannot accept vendor-managed keys for PHI; need key custody and detailed audit trails.'
Also evaluating cost/perf trade-offs — target is to keep token cost ~50% of current provider
Security team flagged PCI scope for billing tokens; want confirmation on network and service isolation
