title: Riverbend Reserve Sizing Consultants
body:
Dedicated capacity sizing workshop — CRM summary and raw notes:
- Company: Riverbend Reserve, consulting firm building inference-backed routing + match/rerank layers for enterprise search products.
- Primary ask: reserved GPU pool with throughput guarantees and clear latency SLOs; must support steady-state and sustained peaks without user-visible degradation.
- Workload profile (workshop capture): steady-state baseline = 8 QPS of generation traffic (chat/rerank mix), median request = 180 tokens, peak burst profile = up to 220 QPS over 5-10 minute windows (marketing blast / nightly re-rank).
- Tokens: estimate avg tokens/response 180 -> raw token demand during steady-state ~1440 tokens/sec (~8*180); peak token demand ~39,600 tokens/minute (~660 tokens/sec) during 220 QPS if responses are similar length. Client asked for conversion to tokens/sec and GPU sizing guidance.
- Latency target...

title: Aurum Gateways
body:
Account context: mid-to-large European payments processor. Very strict EU data residency + long retention windows for transaction metadata. They run multi-account AWS (eu-west-1 primary) and use AWS KMS with customer-managed keys (CMKs) and a central SIEM. Interested in Redwood Private (VPC) — must live in their VPC, integrate with their KMS, and deliver audit logs to their S3 log-bucket via cross-account role.

Recent timeline / activity:
- 2026-01-12: Intro call (Maya). High-level requirements captured: EU-only residency, SAML SSO, KMS CMEK required.
- 2026-01-28: Technical discovery (Diego + Aurum infra). Discussed VPC topologies, subnets, and ingress options.
- 2026-02-15: Demo of Redwood Private (ff-2026-02-15-aurum-demo). POC ask: 1 week perf test, 100 qps sustained, 50ms median token latency target for short-chat flows.
- 2026-02-24: Security deep-dive with SecOps (ff-2026-02-2...

title: Lantern Harbor Search
body:
Summary: Lantern Harbor is a developer-first code search product used internally and sold to enterprises. Primary objective: replace existing Elastic-based search for code + docs with a semantic stack that supports reranking at scale.

Key technical constraints: large embedding corpus (~15M vectors now, projected 40M within 12 months), frequent re-indexing (hourly deltas for active repos, daily full rebuild), low tail latency target for interactive code search (p99 <= 120ms). Cost sensitivity: wants clear unit economics — willing to trade GPU cost for 10-20% accuracy uplift on reranker.

Preferred architecture and asks from call:
- Want private deployment inside customer VPC for regulatory customers; also evaluating Dedicated for predictable throughput on public cloud.
- Needs SSO/SAML (Okta) + SCIM for provisioning and audit logs forwarded to customer's SIEM.
- Looking for support fo...

title: Oak Hollow Inferworks
body:
Company notes and CRM log:
- Product: in-app AI assistant + search for product analytics (B2B SaaS).
- Started with Redwood Hosted API POC (Q4 2025). Hosted eval used for embeddings + chat; team impressed with prompt quality and streaming.
- Current ask: Dedicated POC to meet predictable throughput and EU data residency for a subset of customers. Mid-market procurement timeline (legal + security) typical 4-6 weeks.
- Performance targets: p95 token gen latency <120ms for short chat responses, sustained throughput target 100-150 RPS across multitenant endpoints; cost sensitivity = medium-high (aim to keep token cost ~$0.002–0.006).
- Model preferences: prefer curated open variants; interested in quantized model variants for cost (e.g., redwood-curie-q4, redwood-curie-q4-rt). Want to evaluate model-routing by workload.
- Security: must pass SOC2 security questionnaire, require KMS integr...

title: Atlas Throughput Dynamics
body:

Discovery (01/28) with infra + security: strong VPC + KMS requirement; cannot have tenant co-mingling
SLA deep dive (02/11): asked for min tokens/sec guarantee and concurrency caps — concerned about flash load from seasonal routing
POC kickoff (02/24): internal POC to validate 12x GPU baseline under synthetic traffic (Ravi running sim)
Procurement (03/02): legal flagged data residency and burst billing as primary blockers
Quote iteration v3 (03/08): committed pricing for 12 reserved GPUs, proposed burst window policy included

title: Latentis Synthetic Works
body:
Account summary: Latentis builds generative assistants for supply-chain manufacturing workflows. Primary ask = Private VPC deployment in EU (Frankfurt) with strict data residency and long-term audit retention.

Security & infra highlights:
- Wants no model data leaving EU region; model artifacts must be stored in customer S3 encrypted with customer CMK (BYOK).
- Control plane: prefers private-control-plane via AWS PrivateLink or customer VPN; explicit NO public inbound access to inference nodes.
- Network topology: prefer hub-and-spoke with central security account. Inference service should run in private subnets, internal NLB for internal service discovery, S3/ECR access via VPC endpoints.
- Outbound: only allowed to Redwood-managed telemetry endpoints via PrivateLink/Proxy; legal flagged any egress to public Internet as blocker.
- KMS: mandatory AWS KMS CMK with key policy that allo...

title: Cerulean Streaming AI
body:
Profile: mid-stage fintech focused on real-time market intelligence.
Primary app: user-facing chat assistant + immediate reranking of model-sourced signals to drive risk alerts.
Key quotes: "50ms p95 is our definition of realtime for UI flows"; "We cannot accept >2% regression in top-N rerank quality".
Perf targets: 40-80ms p95 for short chat turns; 1.2k sustained qps during market open spikes; cold-start acceptable to 200ms occasionally but aim to avoid.
Cost sensitivity: current infra ops target is <$0.0008/token equivalent for production serving (heavy constraint).
Model preferences: open-models preferred for licensing (Llama2 variants, Mistral), but will consider Redwood-verified quantized variants if quality preserved.
Optimization interests: batching policies tolerant of micro-batching, prefix/KV caching for session reuse, 8-bit/4-bit quantization profiles, per-route routing (hi...

title: Ironcrest Autonomous Inference
body:
Account snapshot and user notes:
- Vertical: autonomy stack for medium/long haul industrial vehicles. Heavy telemetry, multi-modal upstream, and safety-critical routing decisions.
- Primary ask: Dedicated pool with guaranteed throughput and clear latency SLOs for both chat-style assist and high-rate reranking pipelines.
- Performance targets (customer stated): p50 token latency <50ms for short prompts; p95 end-to-end response <350ms for 256-token completions in chat; reranking pipelines require 1,000 qps (batching ok) with p95 end-to-end <150ms for single-turn short prompts.

body:

Primary ask: predictable throughput for peak trading windows, p99 latency guarantees for chat/rerank, and strong crypto/KMS controls. Stakeholders: CTO (L. Ramesh), Head of Infrastructure (D. Park), Procurement (S. Alvarez). AE/SE motion: Ava Martinez + Rafael Chen.

title: Verdant Shield Technologies
body:
Sales notes: very security-first org. procurement cycles slow w/ vendor-risk board.
Decision maker: CTO (Elena Park) + Head of Infra (Marcus Liu). Security peer review required before procurement.
They explicitly: 'no public endpoints for PII—must run over private networking and KMS-managed keys.'
POC scope: prove model serving within their VPC overlay, measure egress patterns and audit log fidelity.
Network ask: show both AWS PrivateLink and VPC peering designs, include route tables, recommended firewall rules, egress control patterns (proxy, NAT GW, outbound CIDR allowlist).
KMS: require customer-managed CMKs in their account (HSM-backed preferred). Need clear key rotation and key-usage audit samples.
Audit logs: need immutable, tamper-evident delivery to their Splunk/ELK with at least 90 days retention then cold storage for 7 years for regulatory reasons.
Data residency: primary da...

title: Pacific Helix SoftwareWorks
body:
Topline: mid-market SaaS product team focused on embedded user workflows and in‑app help. Started with Hosted API PoC (chat + embeddings) in Jan; now evaluating Dedicated/VPC for production due to PII and residency needs.

PoC summary: hosted API trial (2 weeks) -> low friction to integrate; performance acceptable for dev tests but need predictable latency and private networking for prod. They call out cost as 'important but secondary to control & SLAs' — willing to commit to reserved capacity if latency and residency guarantees met.

Security ask (explicit): SOC2 Type II evidence, SSO/SAML for console, audit log streaming to customer SIEM, retention controls for model inputs/outputs (configurable retention windows), KMS integration for key management, and IP allowlist + private egress. Also want model isolation options (no multi-tenant cache sharing).

Quote from Director of Platform...

title: Sundial SpikeWorks
body:
POC focused on spike reliability and graceful degradation. Customer runs omnichannel CCaaS (voice + chat + SMS) — typical steady-state 1200 concurrent agents, seasonal peaks up to 32k active sessions (estimated). Key asks:
- 95th pct token latency target: < 300ms for single-turn agent-suggest; 99th pct fallback plan required.
- Throughput: sustain ~1.5k concurrent generation requests during spikes; burst to 6k for 10-30 minute windows.
- Cost sensitivity: wants predictable unit economics for seasonal events (Black Friday window). Prefer reserved/dedicated pricing but need auto-burst.
- Model preferences: open to Llama-derivative quantized variants; want ability to route to smaller quantized variant when under pressure.
- Reliability requirements: automatic fallback (model variant or region), queue backpressure handling, graceful degradation to cached suggestions.
- Security/compliance...

title: Sequoia Scribe Enterprise
body:
Profile: mid-tier investment platform building internal developer copilot for trading and compliance teams.
Primary ask: enterprise-grade IDE copilot w/ private deployment (VPC or on-prem) + full audit trail.
Use pattern: VS Code plugin for 250+ engineers, stream-first completions (first byte <50ms target), model pinning to curated ILM models, offline signing for certain requests.
POC scope agreed: 8-week POC, 10 dev seats, 1 integration with internal repo mirror, performance tests (latency/throughput), eval set for hallucination/regression checks.
Security: must support SSO (SAML + SCIM), KMS integration (customer-managed keys), immutable audit logs stored for 7 years, US-only data residency for PII and financial data.
Compliance: SOC2 Type II required; ISO27001 desired. Legal flagged retention controls and e-discovery/export controls as high priority.
Architecture preference: privat...

title: Quantum Ink Works
body:
Account background:
- Founded 2019; builds an IDE-first AI pair-programming product used by internal teams and paying customers (editor plugin + CI-based code fixes).
- Heavy emphasis on reproducible completions for CI and unit-test driven workflows.

Technical requirements (high level):
- Model/version pinning per workspace + per-branch; deterministic output for specific prompts used in tests.
- Canary/rollout control: need 0->5->25->100% staged rollout with automatic rollback on eval gate failures.
- Eval gates: run suite of repo-specific unit tests + hallucination/regression checks before promoting. Must integrate with their CI (GitHub Actions).
- Low-latency streaming: editor needs <50ms token latency (95th) for good UX; also streaming support to reduce perceived latency.
- Reproducibility knobs: fixed sampling seeds, pinned tokenizer and model weights, tighter sampling defaults f...

title: Northbridge Sentinel AI
body:
Account: mid-market B2B SaaS (product analytics + in-app help). Strong PLG motion; product teams prioritize low-latency UX.

Security: SOC2 required, must support customer-managed KMS and audit logs. Data residency for EU subset of customers.

title: Northbridge Security Labs
body:
Northbridge Security Labs is evaluating Redwood for a SOC-assistant + retrieval workloads. They are highly sensitive to cost attribution consistency: their procurement requires they can reconcile product dashboards with invoices and internal spend allocation. They explicitly asked for: (1) per-request cost breakdown w/ prefill vs decode split; (2) how cache hits reduce billed cost vs only improve latency; (3) how batching efficiency is reflected; (4) what counts as 'overhead' and whether it's allocated into per-request costs.

Open questions from their side:
- Does Redwood guarantee deterministic breakdown outputs given the same inputs (schema version pinned)?
- How does drift monitoring work (telemetry lag, backfills, reporting windows)? Can they access a drift dashboard or summary for audit?
- How are quantization profiles represented (stable IDs) and how does Optimize avoid optimis...

They are highly sensitive to cost attribution consistency: their procurement requires they can reconcile product dashboards with invoices and internal spend allocation. They explicitly asked for: (1) per-request cost breakdown w/ prefill vs decode split; (2) how cache hits reduce billed cost vs only improve latency; (3) how batching efficiency is reflected; (4) what counts as 'overhead' and whether it's allocated into per-request costs.

Open questions from their side:
- Does Redwood guarantee deterministic breakdown outputs given the same inputs (schema version pinned)?
- How does drift monitoring work (telemetry lag, backfills, reporting windows)? Can they access a drift dashboard or summary for audit?
- How are quantization profiles represented (stable IDs) and how does Optimize avoid optimis...

title: Sunward SummitWorks
body:
02/14 initial intro call — Maya + Diego; lead: Jordan Park (Head of ML); wants quick win on chat latency and a plan to reduce token unit-cost
02/21 demo (Hosted API) — showed streaming, function-calling, embedding similarity; positive reaction; asked for quantized model benchmarks
02/28 security questionnaire submitted — asked about KMS, audit logs, retention windows; infosec requested SOC2 evidence and network diagrams
03/05 cost-optimization workshop with SEs (Diego) — topics: batching, prefix/KV caching, quantization profiles, workload-level unit economics; produced concrete POC tasks

title: SableCove Enterprise Networks
body:
Account summary: SableCove is a Tier-1 telco building operator-facing AI services (NOC assistant, ticket summarization, ranked KB search). Primary constraint is strict network controls and European data residency for subscriber PII. They want Redwood Private (VPC) with a customer-managed control plane and full audit/log shipping to their SIEM.

Security & network requirements (from InfoSec + NetOps):
- Private connectivity: prefer AWS PrivateLink into a dedicated Redwood VPC endpoint for control plane traffic; explore VPC Peering for data plane to avoid cross-account NAT. NetOps biased to PrivateLink for simpler egress rules.
- Firewall rules: tight inbound: only management subnets + specific Redwood egress IPs; outbound egress controls to restrict model weight pulls and telemetry to approved buckets/endpoints.
- Egress controls: require explicit allowlist for all outbound flows from ...

title: Luminara Legal Insight
body:
Account context: Luminara builds a compliance + discovery layer for law firms and corporate legal teams. They process consolidated discovery sets (custodial data + 3rd-party docs).

Key technical requirements (from SE calls):
- Indexing scale: target 50M-120M pages (roughly 25-60M docs after chunking). Need guidance on sharding/partitioning and incremental indexing costs.
- Embedding store: prefer vector store in customer VPC or on-prem (no external vector hosting). Looking for recommendations re: quantized embeddings and cold-start cost.
- Reranking: two-stage retrieval (FAISS/approximate ANN) + learned transformer reranker for precision in top-10. Latency budget: 120-200ms p95 for single-query in UI; 20k qps batch search for bulk e-discovery jobs is also required.
- Cost targets: want <$0.03 per interactive query (embeddings + rerank combined) at steady state, and detailed cost mode...

title: Triton Harbor Financial Systems
body:
CRM notes:
- Account: large payments platform, regulatory constraints (PCI-adjacent, strict data residency).
- POC scope pinned: deploy Redwood Private control plane (customer-managed) + inference nodes in Triton's AWS VPC. Customer will provide VPC, subnets, and a Bastion for access.
- Tech ask: Terraform IaC + Helm chart to install runtime into their EKS cluster; provide minimal example values + module to create IAM roles, KMS key bindings, CloudWatch/CloudTrail audit streams.
- Security wants explicit KMS flow: customer CMK in their account, Redwood must support envelope encryption and KMS decrypt calls proxied via customer VPC endpoints. HSM mention: they want to validate HSM-backed keys (AWS CloudHSM) before full rollout.
- Data residency: training/eval and logs must stay in EU/US region separation. Need support for route-by-region and audit log segregation.
- Observability: requ...

title: Northpeak Security
body:
Account summary
- Security-focused prospect building an internal-facing “analyst copilot” + customer-facing incident narrative generation. Current stack is mixed: some workloads on Bedrock, some DIY (vLLM) for cost control.
- Primary evaluation driver: auditability + predictable unit economics. Very sensitive to observability gaps.

What they care about (verbatim-ish)
- “We need to explain why the bill moved.” They want cache efficiency telemetry to be usable for internal chargeback by product area (route) and model version.
- “Audit logs can’t be an afterthought.” Needs clear event history for config changes, access, and deployment/rollout actions.

Product interest
- Redwood Private (VPC) is the lead option. Dedicated capacity is a backup if VPC timeline slips.
- Optimize add-on is attractive if recommendations are explainable and safe (no black-box auto-tuning).

Workload details
-...

title: Archipelago Cloud Residency Ltd
body:
Overview: Archipelago is a pan-European insurance group with operations in UK, Canada (reinsurance arm) and APAC partners. Primary ask: Private VPC deployment with strict per-region data residency controls (EU-only for EU products, UK separated, Canadian subset, APAC pilot). They are wary of cross-border backups and want explicit routing and residency guarantees.

Recent activity / timeline:
- 2026-02-20: Security workshop (ff_2026_02_20_Archipelago_Workshop). Attendees: CTO (Marie Dupont), Head of Security (Tom Reynolds), Lina (SE), Maya (AE).
- 2026-02-25: Follow-up call: clarified requirement for BYOK and HSM/KMS options (prefer customer-managed keys in AWS KMS or Azure Key Vault).
- 2026-02-28: Procurement sent vendor questionnaire (waiting on SOC2 evidence + data flow diagrams).

title: Solstice Performance Systems
body:
Summary: enterprise evaluation focused on Dedicated capacity + strict latency SLO alignment. Primary driver is chat + reranking for a high-frequency trading analytics UI (low tail latency). They want clear, measurable guarantees for p50/p95/p99 across streaming vs non-streaming paths and a reproducible measurement method (server-side vs client-side).

Recent timeline (short bullets):
- 2026-02-20: discovery call (Avery) — confirmed use-cases and sensitive data residency needs.
- 2026-02-27: architecture deep-dive with Infra + Security (Ravi) — walked through VPC options, KMS, audit logging.
- 2026-03-02: metrics & SLO sync (internal + Redwood) — asked for breakdown: runtime vs network vs client overhead.
- 2026-03-05: shared Redwood Dedicated sizing draft (drive link).
- 2026-03-10: pre-POC checklist completed; POC kickoff slated for 2026-03-17 (2 week load + tail latency testing).

title: Emberfield Growth Systems
body:
Initial inbound from Growth team — evaluating for knowledge search + support agent. Looking to replace costly hybrid infra (self-hosted + API sprawl).
VP Product (Arun Mehta) demoed a prototype using embeddings + reranking; wants SLA for throughput and predictable monthlies.
CTO quote on discovery call: "We need a clear unit-econ that beats our current $0.15/query average. If Redwood's Dedicated can cut that in half with caching/batching, it's a win."
Security did preliminary review: require SSO + audit logs; retention policy question — legal prefers 90d ephemeral cache, security flagged SOC2 gap for retention controls.
AE notes: pref demo went well. SE follow-up committed to run a 2-week POC focusing on route-level batching + KV prefix cache and int8 quantization profile.
POC objective: 300 req/s sustained, P95 latency < 120ms for chat/responses; embedding throughput target 1k reqs/m...

Security did preliminary review: require SSO + audit logs; retention policy question — legal prefers 90d ephemeral cache, security flagged SOC2 gap for retention controls.
AE notes: pref demo went well. SE follow-up committed to run a 2-week POC focusing on route-level batching + KV prefix cache and int8 quantization profile.
POC objective: 300 req/s sustained, P95 latency < 120ms for chat/responses; embedding throughput target 1k reqs/m...

title: Indigo Cove Platforms
body:
Inbound lead from product team — evaluating inference economics for in-app assistant and doc search.
Primary ask: move from prototype (hosted API) -> Dedicated for predictable throughput and lower p99 latency variability.
Quoted on call: 'we need consistent sub-200ms p50 for chat flows and <400ms p95 for search reranks', emphasis on perceived snappiness.
Security: SSO (SAML) + KMS integration mandatory; retention & residency questions from legal (EU customers), want audit logs for tokens.
Cost sensitivity: moderate; willing to pay reserved capacity if ROI vs hosted API > 2x reduction in per-token cost at scale.

POC ask: dedicated GPU pool for a week with canary rollout, traffic split plan, and measured rollback conditions.

title: Halcyon Capital Solutions
body:
Opportunity summary: Halcyon is a UK-headquartered fintech offering portfolio analytics and internal research tools to asset management teams. Primary pain: existing search (self-hosted Elastic + ad-hoc vector store) is inconsistent across regions — UK users see 120-300ms tail for search; they need sub-50ms p95 for production dashboards and sub-150ms end-to-end for a two-stage dense-re-rank flow. Index scale: target initial corpus ~10M documents, with aggressive nearline updates (200k new/updated docs/day). Expect vector index to reach ~120M vectors after enrichment (per-chunk embeddings). They are cost-sensitive; finance asked for modeled cost per query (embedding + ANN retrieval + lightweight transformer rerank).

Technical preferences & constraints: prefer EU/UK data residency (must keep primary index and KMS keys in EU/UK), VPC connectivity to customer network for audit and egress...

title: Solaris Pulse Systems
body:
Account background: mid-market product analytics vendor (B2B SaaS) with ~450 employees. Started evaluation on Hosted API last quarter; saw latency variance at 99th percentile on some longer conversations. Economics: cost-sensitive on token spend for embeddings and reranking workloads, but willing to commit to Dedicated if unit economics are predictable and observability is good.

title: SummitRidge InsightWorks
body:
Summary: Mid-market product org (customer support + docs) moving from Hosted POC -> evaluation of Dedicated (VPC) for data residency + control.
Use-case detail: Support chat with context windows up to 4k tokens, prioritized low-variance latency (p95 target <= 200ms for short responses). Also embeddings for semantic search across docs (throughput ~200 QPS during business hours).
POC results: Hosted API quality OK; observed token spend higher than expected. Team wants guaranteed throughput and predictable egress for compliance reasons.
Security asks captured: Provide SOC2 report (in-scope systems), SSO/SAML SSO integration guide, proof of immutable audit logs with retention controls, KMS integration for customer keys, and region residency options (primary: us-east-1).

title: Cobalt Regulatory Solutions
body:
Account snapshot: enterprise RegTech focused on transaction monitoring and regulatory reporting. High sensitivity for EU data residency (must remain in DE region).

Conversation highlights (shorthand):
- 2026-02-26 kickoff w/ AE + SE + InfoSec (FF-20260226-315). Customer asked for private VPC deployment only — no hosted.
- Primary drivers: auditability, key management, data residency, and deterministic latency for agent flows.
- 'We cannot permit tokenized logs leaving DE region' — InfoSec quote. Need audit log ingester to Splunk in Frankfurt.
- SE asked about model family: pref open/Redwood-verified medium/large models; willing to test quantized 70B variant if latency/cost meet bar.
- Cost posture: moderate — willing to commit to Dedicated or Private with a multi-year SLA if POC validates operations.

title: TrustWell MedTransact
body:
6-week VPC POC: week1 infra + SSO; week2 model onboarding + secure tokenization; week3 agent-assist pilot with synthetic tickets; week4 load test for monthly-billing spike emulation; week5 observability validation (audit logs + tracing); week6 review + handoff.
Success metrics: reduce AHT by 18-25%, 99.9% no-PAN retention in logs, 95th percentile latency goals met for suggestions under 200ms in steady state.
Deliverables: infra IaC, security runbook, performance report, handoff checklist for Dedicated/Private deployment.

title: Aurora DevSentry
body:
Summary: Aurora DevSentry is a regulated fintech (payments + custody) looking for an enterprise code-assistant integrated into developers' IDEs. Primary ask = low-latency streaming completions inside VS Code + JetBrains, deterministic model/version pinning, and strict audit/retention controls for compliance.

Recent call highlights:
- "Wanted: 'copilot that we can trust in production' — CTO quote. Needs model pinning per repo + rollout controls for safe updates.
- Security: must support SSO (SAML + SCIM), per-API-key audit logs, and 7-year retention for certain artifacts (compliance team).
- Deployment: prefer VPC private deployment behind customer-managed peering; fallback to on-prem appliance if cloud not permitted for some teams.
- Encryption: KMS integration with HSM-backed keys required for workspace-level secrets and logs.

- Low-...

title: Cobalt Brookline Analytics
body:
Account: mid-market analytics SaaS. Product: customer-facing search + support assistant. Started with hosted API trial (Jan) to validate embeddings quality and cost profile, now moving to Dedicated/VPC for predictable latency and data isolation. AE/Ethan running evaluation; SE Priya leading POC. CSM Liam looped for contract terms if we convert.

Key quote from CTO on 2026-01-12 demo call: "We need guaranteed isolation + audit trail before legal will sign — SOC2 plus SAML is non-negotiable."

POC summary: 2-week hosted POC validated KB embeddings and reranking quality (recall + precision acceptable). Next stage: 4-week Dedicated POC in customer VPC (Redwood Dedicated) to test autoscale, KV cache warm-start, and p95 latency under 50 concurrent chat sessions.

Security ask list (provided to Redwood 2026-01-20): SOC2 Type II report, SSO/SAML metadata exchange, audit log retention policy, ...

title: Mariner Throughput Designs
body:
Summary: Mariner is evaluating Redwood Dedicated to run low-latency real-time assistants for trading desk workflows and compliance reranking. Primary focus: define unambiguous latency SLOs and align measurement across Redwood, Mariner client, and infra teams.

Key constraints / asks (shorthand):
- SLO targets: want p50/p95/p99 table per route. Initial ask: p95 <= 100ms for non-streaming short responses (<=128 tokens); p99 <=250ms. For streaming chat they want first-token p95 <= 80ms, subsequent-token p95 <= 30ms.
- Throughput: baseline 200 RPS sustained, 600 RPS burst for market open — dedicated pool sizing required.
- Cost sensitivity: prefer reserved monthly commit with overage bursts; target ARR range aligns with forecast.
- Security: VPC peering, KMS-managed keys, SOC2 attestation, audit log retention 3 years, SSO via SAML + SCIM provisioning.

Summary: Mariner is evaluating Redwood Dedicated to run low-latency real-time assistants for trading desk workflows and compliance reranking. Primary focus: define unambiguous latency SLOs and align measurement across Redwood, Mariner client, and infra teams.

Key constraints / asks (shorthand):
- SLO targets: want p50/p95/p99 table per route. Initial ask: p95 <= 100ms for non-streaming short responses (<=128 tokens); p99 <=250ms. For streaming chat they want first-token p95 <= 80ms, subsequent-token p95 <= 30ms.
- Throughput: baseline 200 RPS sustained, 600 RPS burst for market open — dedicated pool sizing required.
- Cost sensitivity: prefer reserved monthly commit with overage bursts; target ARR range aligns with forecast.
- Security: VPC peering, KMS-managed keys, SOC2 attestation, audit log retention 3 years, SSO via SAML + SCIM provisioning.

title: ApexShield Compliance Labs
body:
Summary: ApexShield is a regulated regtech vendor building a compliance automation suite that will ingest sensitive regulatory PDFs and customer-submitted evidence. They want a private VPC deployment with strict data residency (EU for EU customers, NA for US customers), BYOK KMS integration, and audit-grade logging. Primary regulatory drivers: SOC 2 + ISO 27001 as baseline, HIPAA (for a small subset of customers handling PHI) and PCI considerations for workflows that may touch payment tokens.

Customer asks / mapping (requested during security deep-dive):
- SOC 2: they want to map Redwood Private controls to common SOC 2 CC categories. title: Cobalt Banyan Solutions
body:
Summary: Mid-market SaaS (customer support platform). Started with Redwood Hosted API eval (Jan-Feb), good latency for web chat but need deterministic throughput for peak hours. Moving to Dedicated pilot to validate 99th pctl latency, cost targets, and region failover.

Key quotes: "We need predictable token economics during ticket surges" — Head of Platform. "If failover adds <50ms we can tolerate it, but tail latency unacceptable" — SRE lead.

Requirements (high level): vpc connectivity, SSO/SAML, audit logs retention 1y, KMS integration, EU data residency for certain customers. Model preference: prefer open Llama-family 33B variants for majority of traffic, fall back to smaller quantized 7B variant for burst/low-cost routing. Need embeddings for search/retrieval (512-d vector), reranking pipeline for triage.

Engagement so far: hosted API POC -> passed basic evals; now propose Dedi...

title: EmberStack AI
body:
Account summary
- Prospect evaluating Redwood Dedicated with Burst option as a way to cover predictable spikes without over-reserving GPUs year-round.
- Strong preference for a single vendor for baseline + peaks; wants to avoid stitching together “dedicated baseline + external API spillover”.

Workload / use case
- Primary: customer support agent + summarization pipeline for enterprise customers (mix of online chat + background summarization jobs).
- Secondary: embeddings + reranking for doc-search (“hybrid search” with periodic re-indexing).
- Traffic shape: relatively steady baseline during business hours + sharp spikes during customer launches and weekly batch/backfill jobs.

Peak patterns (as described)
- Baseline: steady inference for chat/summarization (24/7, but much lower overnight).
- Peaks:
 - Weekly indexing/backfill job (Thurs nights): 2–3 hour window, 1.8–2.5x baseline th...

title: Silverpine UXWorks
body:
Summary: Mid-market product analytics SaaS (B2B) evaluating Redwood. Started on Hosted API for fast validation (Q4 2025) — moved to Dedicated evaluation because of throughput predictability and VPC requirement. Primary contact: Head of ML Eng (Nina Park). AE/SE played a deep demo focused on latency and cost controls.

Key quotes from call: "Need consistent p95 under 200ms for in-app assistant or conversion drops." "We cannot send PII outside our VPC."

Highlights: short POC window (3 weeks) for Dedicated: canary traffic to a single GPU pool, automated fallback to hosted variants during overload, model quantization acceptable if quality within A/B thresholds. Security asked for SOC2 attestation + customer-managed KMS.

Buyer motion: Product + Platform sponsor (VP Product, Director of Platform). Procurement will review committed spend. Customer wants an engineering-first rollout: canary...

title: Alfred CodeWorks
body:
Summary: Alfred is a mid-market developer tools company building an IDE copilot for enterprise customers. Main ask = reproducible code completions for audits + low latency (<100ms median) for streaming inline completions.
Key quote from CTO: "We need to be able to pin exact model+config that produced a completion — auditability is non-negotiable."
Requirements (short): model/version pinning, reproducible sampling, rollout tooling (canary/gradual), eval gates before rollout, rollback/safety hooks, SSO and audit logs, optional Dedicated for p95 latency certainty.
Technical details captured on call:
- Latency targets: median <50ms for single-token streaming on warm sessions, p95 <120ms under 200 concurrent coder sessions.
- Throughput profile: ~250 active dev sessions during peak; each session streams 30-120 tokens/sec bursts.
- Reproducibility goals: ability to retrieve exact model bina...

title: Axiom Forensis Analytics
body:
- Customer: mid-size e-discovery & compliance services provider. heavy regulatory clients (financial services, pharma)
- Scale: target index ~150M documents (mixture of DIA, PDFs, PST extracts). Typical doc length varies; avg embedding token window requested ~2048 context.
- Performance targets: 95th percentile rerank+serve <250ms for SLA routes; prefer <150ms for internal analyst tier.
- Throughput: steady 60 QPS, peak 150 QPS during intake windows. P50 vs P95 latency split important.
- Cost sensitivity: aiming for <$0.05/query (embed+ann+rerank amortized). Wants clear unit economics: tokens, GPU seconds, storage.
- Model preferences: open embedding model acceptable (small/medium), but reranker must be cross-encoder (higher compute). Will consider quantized model variants; wants Redwood optimize recommendations.
- Routing/fallback: prefer primary private cluster (EU + NA regions) wit...

title: Tidecrest Realtime Intelligence
body:
Primary ask: deterministic low tail latency for live-chat and support-agent flows
Targets: p50 ~10-20ms, p95 <50ms, p99 <100ms for single-turn chat (under normal burst management). They emphasized p99 as deal breaker.
Cost sensitivity: want unit economics guidance — target blended inference cost < $0.05 per session. Open to reserving capacity for predictable pricing.
Interest in Optimize features: intelligent batching suggestions, prefix/KV caching, quantization profiles — quote: 'we need hard SLOs not averages'.
Model preference: open-weight models (Llama2 variants) plus fallbacks to smaller 3B quantized for degraded mode
Routing: multi-tier routing required (preferred region, fallback region, lighter model fallback). Must support sticky routing for sessions.
Deployment: prefer Private (VPC) behind corporate network; on-prem pilot for compliance-sensitive customers.
Security: mandato...

title: Harborline Clinical Insights
body:
- Summary: Harborline is a large healthcare analytics vendor evaluating Redwood Private for PHI workloads (clinical summarization, Q&A over patient docs, embeddings for search).
- Deployment preference: Private VPC with customer-managed KMS; primary cloud GCP (us-central1) but open to AWS if HSM import available. They asked about support for external HSM and Cloud KMS/imported key material.
- Vendor intake: Received CAIQ v2025 + Harborline vendor risk spreadsheet. They need row-level answers (not just links to SOC2). Key focus areas: key custody and BYOK, audit log immutability and chain-of-custody, encryption in-transit and at-rest details, data deletion proof on offboarding, annual pen-test report, and HIPAA BAAs.
- SIG/CAIQ specifics: 25 items flagged as high-priority by Harborline security: K2 (KMS separation), A6 (network isolation/VPC peering), R1 (retention & deletion), I4 (acc...

title: HelixCode
body:
Account summary (HelixCode):
- Devtools startup building an AI coding assistant + team knowledge search. Primary workload is streaming chat completions with tool/function calling; secondary workload is embeddings for repo/docs indexing + occasional reranking.
- Evaluating Redwood Hosted API vs Redwood Dedicated. Their hypothesis is Hosted will be fine for day-to-day, but they want Dedicated for predictable peak-hour throughput and tighter p95 control.

Current state:
- Using two external LLM API providers today with manual fallback. Complaints: cost volatility, variable tail latency during US peak hours, and inconsistent behavior across regions.
- Regions: majority NA (us-east/us-west). EU traffic starting (customers in UK/DE) but small; they asked how EU region coverage impacts latency and whether Dedicated can be region-pinned.

Key requirements / success criteria:
- Latency: p50 ma...

title: ShieldPoint Contact Ops
body:
Account overview: ShieldPoint is a payments-focused contact center operator supporting merchant disputes and high-value payments flows. Heavy compliance: PCI scope across call flows, some PHI in dispute cases, strict retention policies (7+ years for certain records).

Recent timeline:
- 2026-02-11: Intro demo with AE/SE (Maya/Diego) — Fireflies ff-20260211-35x9. Customer asked about model-level audit trails and token-level cost visibility.

title: Hamilton Stratus Systems
body:
Topline: Major regional bank. Strong interest in Redwood Dedicated + Private control plane for regulated workloads. Primary negotiation is SLA contract language. Key obs/asks: uptime target, credit schedule, maintenance windows, support RTT, reserved GPU plan.

SLA specifics proposed by Hamilton: 1) Uptime target: they want 99.95% monthly, prefer 99.99% for customer-facing endpoints but willing to accept 99.95% if credit schedule is meaningful. 2) Credits: requested tiered, e.g. <99.99 = 5% credit, <99.95 = 15% credit, <99.5 = 40% of monthly committed fees. Redwood initial counter: 99.95 target with credits starting at 99.9.

Maintenance windows: Hamilton requires maintenance to be limited to a single 2-hour window per month during 02:00-06:00 local; wants automatic 72-hour notice for planned maintenance, 24-hour notice for non-critical updates. They want an explicit policy for emerge...

title: LexQuarry Compliance
body:
Use-case summary: replace legacy keyword search for compliance requests and subpoenas. Primary flow: ingestion -> embeddings -> ANN search -> reranking -> result set for attorneys.
Indexing scale: customer expects initial production index ~3M-5M documents, plan to grow to ~20M within 12 months. Chunking strategy yields ~3-4x embeddings relative to doc count.
Latency & throughput: target p95 response < 300ms for reranked top-10 on typical queries; peak throughput 120 qps during discovery events. Cost sensitivity: want cost-per-query under $0.05 for average search, lower for high-volume batch reprocessing.
Model preferences: prefer open models for token-cost control; evaluating quantized variants. Want deterministic reranker (repeatable scoring) and ability to pin model versions for auditability.
Routing & fallbacks: must be able to failover to a cold-hosted endpoint in a different regi...

title: Ochre Guardian Technologies
body:
Summary: Enterprise procurement for Private VPC deployment. Ochre runs sensitive PII and regulated insurance claims; primary ask is a hardened VPC deployment with strong KMS and audit controls. Security wants a completed SIG + CAIQ and a shared-responsibility matrix mapping Redwood features to Ochre controls.

Key asks from SecOps/Legal: 
- Full SIG (downloaded) and completed CAIQ (partial answers provided; need vendor detail on KMS key lifecycle).
- Encryption: customer-managed keys (AWS KMS/HSM) required; need key rotation policy and support for multi-region key aliasing.
- Audit logs: immutable, tamper-evident access logs with 7+ year retention optional (default 90 days). Ability to ship logs to customer S3/CloudWatch or to an on-prem SIEM.
- Data residency: primary residency in US (NA) for PHI-adjacent data; some EU residency needed for EU-member claims. Request: confirm region pi...

title: Stonebriar Velocity Systems
body:
— Company background: mid-market analytics SaaS (product telemetry + user insights). Heavy real-time needs in product UX features.
— Current setup: single-cloud deployment on AWS, prefers VPC peering or PrivateLink for Dedicated.
— Initial interest: started with Hosted API for speed of integration, now evaluating Dedicated for predictable latency and cost.
— POC ask: 4-week Dedicated POC with canary routing, model variants and region failover testing. Must demonstrate rollback and cost targets.
— Quoted quote from CTO: 'We need predictable 95th p99 latency under load, and a clear fall-back when the new model drifts.'
— Cost sensitivity: target unit cost < 60% of current host per-token spend; procurement wants ARR maths for committed pool.
— Security: require SSO (SAML), KMS integration for customer keys, audit logging for model inputs, 12-month retention options, and EU data residency...

— Quoted quote from CTO: 'We need predictable 95th p99 latency under load, and a clear fall-back when the new model drifts.'
— Cost sensitivity: target unit cost < 60% of current host per-token spend; procurement wants ARR maths for committed pool.
— Security: require SSO (SAML), KMS integration for customer keys, audit logging for model inputs, 12-month retention options, and EU data residency...

title: LumenBridge Enterprise AI
body:
Qualification summary: LumenBridge is a European fintech (transaction processing + analytics) evaluating Redwood Private VPC for customer-facing chat assistants and internal vector search for fraud signals. Key drivers: strict EU data residency, customer-managed KMS for model artifacts & logs, and full audit trails for regulators.

Security + compliance highlights (from discovery call 2026-03-04):
- Must satisfy ISO27001 & SOC2; legal wants Redwood SOC2 scope and exposure matrix.

- KMS: customer insists on CMK in their AWS account; requirement: Redwood Private must support AWS KMS CMK for model weights at rest and encryption of audit logs.

title: Springbend Analytics
body:
Quick CRM notes (shorthand + call highlights):
- Company: Springbend Analytics — B2B product analytics platform; mid-market (series B, ~350 employees).
- Primary contact: Head of Engineering (Marco Ruiz), Security: Director IT Ops (Erin Park), Product: VP Product (Sofia Allen).
- Began w/ hosted API trial for embeddings (semantic doc search) + chat assistant for support flow. Embedded usage grew quickly; observed token costs rising on long transcripts.

- Cost sensitivity: mid-market budget, wants predictable committed pricing (Dedicated) to control unit ...

title: Stratiform Logistics AI
body:
Account: Global 3PL focused on parcel + LTL routing, high daily throughput windows (EOD batch spikes).
Primary sponsor: VP of Product (Anika Rao) — wants hard latency guarantees for SLA with enterprise customers.
Key quote from 2026-02-11 workshop: 'We need predictable tail latency during surge windows — can't have 99th jump during EOD processing.'

title: Vermillion Gateway Technologies
body:
- Intro call 2026-01-12 (AE Jamie) — high-level needs: low-latency chat for premium banking support + embedding search for KYC docs.
- Customer quote: "Latency matters for traders/ops; we need predictable p95 not 99th spikes."
- Wants dedicated GPU pool (no multitenancy) with VPC peering and HSM/KMS key management.
- Strong preference for local residency (US-east) and audit logs retained 7 years for compliance.
- POC scope: 4-week ramp, two model flavors (high-accuracy Llama3-70B quantized and low-cost Llama2-13B) with routing/fallback.

- Mentioned competitor eval: building in-house team + smaller provider for burst. Looking for clear unit economics and rollback plan.
- AE notes: emphasize Redwood Optimize recommendations (batching + quantization) and warm pools to reduce cold-starts.

body:
Customer summary: Mid-size health system (multiple hospitals + clinics). High priority on PHI controls and residency. Wants private VPC deployment or on-prem option. Key asks: BAA, KMS/HSM integration (bring-your-own-key), fine-grained audit logs retained for 7 years, SOC2 + ISO artifacts, and proof that no telemetry leaves customer region.

Sales shorthand / recent call notes:
- "can't accept telemetry out of US-East" -> insisted on regional residency for prod.
- Interested in Redwood's fallback variant routing for DR (must remain within customer VPC or dedicated private endpoints).

- Legal: wants explicit contract language for data segregation and right-to-audit.
- Security: wants KMS envelope encryption model, HSM-backed CMK, and audit trails for key usage.
- PCI question: payment token...

title: Opaline Rail Systems
body:
Top-line: Opaline Rail Systems (mid-to-large global rail operator) evaluating Redwood Private (VPC) for operations chat + maintenance workflows. Primary driver: keep PII/telemetry inside their VPC and meet EU/UK data residency for certain telemetry and maintenance logs.

Summary of needs:
- Must deploy in customer-managed VPC with private endpoints; no public internet ingress to model endpoints.
- KMS integration required: customer-managed keys (OK with AWS KMS CMK). Bring-your-own-key (BYOK) required for prod.
- Audit logging & retention: 7 years for certain incident logs; want near-real-time log export to their SIEM (Splunk).
- SSO: SAML2.0 integration for console access + RBAC.
- Network topology: prefer Transit Gateway hub-and-spoke; VPN and DirectConnect options for on-prem datacenters. Expected to use peering for EU region.
- Inbound/outbound constraints: only outbound to Redwoo...

title: Nebula Context Labs
body:
Summary: Nebula Context Labs builds a knowledge layer for regulated enterprise customers (legal and compliance teams). They currently run a hybrid search stack: dense vectors in FAISS + a lightweight cross-encoder reranker. Seeking to migrate embeddings and reranker to higher-throughput models to reduce cost-per-query while maintaining or improving relevance. Key constraints: indexing scale (~300M docs, 20-40B tokens of context), tail latency SLO 150-250ms P50 for semantic queries, P95 < 800ms for reranked results, and strict data residency (US, some EU for subsidiaries).

Evals focus: customer wants an eval-first migration. They provided ~5k labeled queries and a set of targeted hard-query prompts (legal citations, short queries with long-answer requirements). Concern: potential quality regressions when moving from their tuned SBERT->cross-encoder to a quantized embedding model + lig...

title: Canterly LogicWorks
body:
CRM notes (shorthand):
- Product team: 5 PMs/10 engs active; primary stakeholder = Head of Product (Lina Gomez).
- Use-case: internal knowledge base + support assistant for mid-market SMB customers; latency sensitive for chat (target p95 < 300ms for short replies).
- Cost sensitivity: wants to cap token spend; evaluating committed capacity to get predictable pricing.
- Models: prefer open model family (Llama/GPT-J variants) for on-prem / dedicated; will accept Redwood-curated variants if throughput beats self-hosting.
- Security: must meet SOC2 + SSO (SAML) + audit logs visible in console; retention controls for 90/180 day retention + adhoc deletion APIs. Data residency for EU customers (some customer records reside in eu-1 region).
- Network: strict VPC peering, no public egress for traffic to inference GPUs; need diagram + CIDR ranges to hand to networking team.
- Legal: requires SO...

title: EchoForge Legal Intelligence
body:
- Account: mid-stage legal platform focused on regulated compliance discovery. Need VPC/private deployment with strict retention and audit requirements.
- Quoted by client: 'We cannot have embeddings leave our VPC; audit trail must show every read/write.'
- Scale: planning to index ~45M documents (mix of PDFs, Word, email ESI). Estimated embeddings ~3M vectors initially, rolling to 15M over 12 months.
- Performance targets: rerank p50 <= 120ms, end-to-end query (embedding + ANN + rerank) <= 450-500ms for typical doc-set; peak throughput target 200 qps for search endpoints.
- Cost sensitivity: medium-high. Interested in quantization and batching recommendations to reduce per-query cost. Will compare with home-grown FAISS infra and Relativity connectors.
- Security: must support SSO/SAML, customer-managed KMS, full immutable audit logs, retention controls (legal hold), and data residenc...

title: CaseCompass Data Discovery
body:
Account snapshot:
- Vertical: enterprise legal services / compliance investigations platform. Primary customers are law firms and in-house legal teams handling regulatory discovery.
- Core ask: replace brittle boolean-search workflows with RAG-based document discovery (embeddings + reranking) for 18M+ OCR'd docs (mail, contracts, court filings).
- Indexing scale: current estimate ~18M documents, avg doc length 2.2k tokens, expect ~40M chunks after chunking + OCR cleanup. Concern: memory footprint of dense store + re-ranking cost.
- Performance targets: p50 latency <120ms, p95 <400ms for 1-2k token queries; 50 qps sustained, burst to 200 qps. Reranker must return top-10 within 300ms.
- Cost targets: target average cost per query <$0.02 including retrieval + reranking; heavily cost-sensitive for high-volume investigative workflows.
- Model preferences: open, quantizable encoders for emb...

title: Quantacore Systems
body:
Account: Quantacore Systems — corporate trading & customer support AI for retail brokerage.
Current objective: dedicated GPU pool for mixed workload: low-latency chat + high-throughput reranking for trade surveillance.
"POC ask": 90-day eval to validate latency SLOs, throughput headroom, and quantized model quality tradeoffs.
Workload details: chat (agent-assist) ~150 RPS peak, typical payload 1-2 turns, target p95 latency <200ms for token streaming; reranking pipeline: online 150 QPS, offline batch 50k qps windows (batched), embeddings pipeline ~300 RPS for similarity indexing.
Model preferences: open/quantizable models (LLaMA-2-13B, MPT-30B) for cost control; fallback 7B variants for burst routing; interested in Redwood-verified quantization profiles (int8 / Q4 variants).
Context length: primary requirement 2048 tokens for chat; eval includes extended context 8k for case escalations...

title: Venturis Compliance AI
body:
10/12 call w/ CTO + Head of Security — strong BYOK preference; legal demands customer-controlled key material.
Primary ask: private VPC deployment with explicit KMS/HSM integrations (AWS KMS + CloudHSM, Azure Key Vault + Dedicated HSM, GCP KMS, and option for on-prem HSM via PKCS#11).
Encryption scope: model artifacts at rest, KV cache, prefix cache, ephemeral token storage, and audit logs.
Preferred model routing: production traffic stays in EU region; fallback routes must not egress keys or plaintext outside customer VPC.
Audit logging: 7-year retention for key access logs; logs must forward to Splunk/SIEM and be linkable to KMS grants; retention policy will be validated by internal audit team.
Key lifecycle: require root CMK in customer's KMS, envelope keys created by Redwood runtime, monthly rotation cadence, ability to revoke access immediately (grants model).
Operational details...

title: Elmbridge Secure AI
body:
Initial qualification: regulated EU financial services firm, sensitive PII and transaction data, strong preference for in-region processing (EU-only).
Profile: global trading platform, 1200 employees, existing multi-account AWS footprint (eu-west-1 primary, eu-central-1 DR).
Primary ask: Private VPC deployment (customer-managed VPC) with optional on-prem connective path for air-gapped analytics cluster later in roadmap.
Workload: chat-based agent for trading desk support + document search across regulatory filings + reranking of trade alerts. Latency target: median <250ms for short-chat loops; p95 <700ms. Throughput target: 600 req/min with burst to 2k.
Cost sensitivity: moderate — willing to pay premium for residency & audit controls but expect Redwood Optimize suggestions for quantization/batching to manage unit costs.
Security posture: Okta SSO/SAML mandatory, SCIM provisioning, en...

title: Helios Trustbank
body:
POC scope: deploy Redwood Private control plane and inference nodes inside Helios-managed AWS VPC using our Terraform modules and Helm charts. Validate chat + embeddings workloads and verify audit log export to their Splunk cluster.
Helios requirements summary: data residency US-only (must stay in us-east-1), SSO via SAML (Okta), SOC2 evidence requested, retention controls for logs (7 years for audit entries), and customer-managed KMS (CMEK) required for token-level encryption.
Security asks: SecOps wants a live demo showing KMS integration with a customer CMK and proof that Redis/KV cache is encrypted at rest. Also requested network flow diagram and list of open ports; legal wants explicit data-flow diagrams for PII scoping.

title: Praesidio Health Systems
body:
Summary: enterprise health system evaluating a two-stage RAG pipeline (dense embeddings + cross-encoder reranker) for clinician-facing search across clinical notes, imaging reports, and pathology PDFs. High PHI sensitivity = strict audit trail, field-level redaction, and tenant isolation required.

Key scale targets: index ~120M documents (approx 6-8B tokens), expected active dataset ~25TB of raw text + attachments. Query profile: 15M queries/month peak (diurnal), 95th pct latency target <200ms for single-shot search, acceptable batch rerank window up to 500ms for deep rerank on critical queries. Cost target: <$0.002 per standard query (dense+rerank amortized), willing to trade some latency for lower cost on archive-tier queries.

Security & infra specifics: Okta SAML + SCIM required, integration with Azure Key Vault for envelope keys, detailed immutable audit logs (token-level and fi...

Key scale targets: index ~120M documents (approx 6-8B tokens), expected active dataset ~25TB of raw text + attachments. Query profile: 15M queries/month peak (diurnal), 95th pct latency target <200ms for single-shot search, acceptable batch rerank window up to 500ms for deep rerank on critical queries. Cost target: <$0.002 per standard query (dense+rerank amortized), willing to trade some latency for lower cost on archive-tier queries.

Security & infra specifics: Okta SAML + SCIM required, integration with Azure Key Vault for envelope keys, detailed immutable audit logs (token-level and fi...

title: Northbridge AI
body:
Account: Northbridge AI (northbridge.ai)

Stage: Evaluation (target close Mar 2026)
Owner: Owen Phillips | SE: Ibrahim Khan

What they’re building
- B2B SaaS "support copilot" that answers agents + drafts customer replies.
- Pipeline: embeddings + retrieval + chat completion (streaming in UI). Some tool-calling in roadmap (ticket lookups, CRM writebacks).
- Spiky usage (customer launches + incident surges). They’ve been burned by "timeouts that look like server issues" from prior provider.

What they care about (verbatim-ish)
- "If you’re overloaded, tell us. Don’t just hang until our gateway times out."
- Wants documented separation of:
 - 429 rate limit / quota / concurrency policy (client should backoff)
 - 503 overload / capacity protection (client should honor Retry-After; they want clear guidance on whether to retry vs fail fast)
- Wants stable, customer-visible error code taxon...

title: Cardinal Harvest Analytics
body:
Duration: 4 weeks (provisioning week + 3-week testing/canary window)
Phase 0 - Provision: dedicated GPU pool in us-west-2 (reserved baseline + burst), network peering and KMS integration; baseline monitoring dashboards pre-populated.
Phase 1 - Canary (week 1): 10% of production traffic routed to Dedicated pool (user-segmented - non-paying testers + internal), metrics collected: latency distribution, error rate, relevance score on synthetic prompts.
Phase 2 - Ramp (week 2-3): 10% -> 50% after health gates. Health gates: p95 latency regression < 10% vs hosted baseline, error rate < 0.5%, relevance delta < 3% on eval set.
Phase 3 - Failover & rollback drills (week 3): execute region failover (us-west-2 -> us-east-1) and simulate node failure; verify automatic fallback to smaller quantized variant if latency or cost thresholds breached.
Rollback policy: immediate rollback to hosted API if...

title: Galileo Data Residency Partners
body:
Account overview:
- Large EU-headquartered fintech (licenced payments + custody) exploring Redwood Private (VPC) for regulated inference workloads.
- Primary requirement: strict data residency enforcement per region (EU-only for EU customers, separate UK boundary, Canadian residency for CA customers). APAC out of scope for initial rollout.
- Heavy focus on auditability, KMS/BYOK, and immutable audit logs retained per regulator (requested 7 years).

Timeline / recent activity:
- 2025-11-20: Intro / discovery call (AE Maya + SE Devon) — high-level requirements captured.
- 2025-12-08: Sent initial security questionnaire (72% complete).
- 2026-01-15: Deep-dive with infra (network, peering constraints) — customer provided VPC architecture diagram.
- 2026-02-14: Security review meeting (Fireflies ff_2026-02-14_7129). Legal flagged UK DPA text and key custody proof.
- 2026-03-02: KMS capabil...

body: Security + Legal: incorporate Helios redlines into Redwood consent/BAA addendum language; schedule architecture review to confirm VPC routing + audit log export format (target week of 2025-10-28).

InfoSec requires evidence of KMS integration + key rotation policy in private deployment

title: Clepsydra EU Search Platform
body:
AE notes / call snippets:
- 10/02 intro: 'We can't move PII out of the UK/EU — must keep vectors & indexes in-region.'
- 11/05 security review: security team asked for KMS flow diagram, retention policy, breach plan (see drive).
- 01/14 infra: prefer dedicated GPU pools with autoscale; concerned about cost vs. latency tradeoff.
- 02/11 POC plan bullets:
 * POC length: 2 weeks
 * Dataset: 32M sample docs (redacted financial statements + contracts)
 * Success metric A: mean reciprocal rank improvement >= 12% vs baseline
 * Success metric B: 95th pctl query latency <60ms in eu-west-2 for top-k retrieval+rerank
 * Success metric C: warm-cold cache delta <2x for common queries
- Quote excerpt (head of eng): 'If you can prove residency and <50ms median for London, we can onboard.)'
- Risk: indexing cost estimates balloon by 2-3x if automatic re-embeddings required every 24h; explore increme...

title: Novara Regional VPC Labs
body: Summary: Large EU-headquartered insurance group evaluating Redwood Private (VPC) to replace an on-prem model serving stack. Primary constraints: strict EU-only residency for EU customers, separate UK key custody for local regulated business, Canadian data residency for claims pipeline, and APAC region for a growing client base. Customer wants per-region control plane options or tenancy isolation per region. Key technical asks: KMS integration with HSM-backed keys (customer-managed keys preferred), audit logging with 7+ years retention and exportable WORM format, SSO/SAML with Okta attribute mapping, ability to pin model versions and enforce routing policies by region/customer tier. Latency target: tail P99 < 300ms for chat-support flows (after warm-up) and high-throughput embedding batches (10k req/day) with cost sensitivity on token billing. POC scope: private VPC control plane, regi...

title: Sunstone Aperture Software
body:
Account snapshot:
- Company: Sunstone Aperture (mid-market SaaS, product analytics platform). Building user-facing insights and in-app assistants for product managers and customer success.
- Contact(s): VP Product - Maya Lind (primary), Head of Security - Aaron Becker, Platform Eng Lead - Nila Rao.
- Business driver: faster time-to-insight for customers; move from rule-based search to a natural-language assistant + reranking for analytics queries.
- Initial interest path: started with Hosted API eval (Jan 2026 demo), scoped POC for 6 weeks, then decided to evaluate Dedicated for predictable latency and VPC requirements.
POC & Tech requirements (from discovery/demos):
- Workload: conversational assistant + document search/reranking across 120M docs (indexing + embeddings).
- Latency targets: p95 generation <= 300ms for short prompts (assistive UI); p95 reranking <= 200ms for top-10 rer...

title: CipherLex Search Systems
body:
Account snapshot: enterprise legaltech vendor focusing on compliance-first document search for litigation and regulatory teams. Interested in a RAG stack: large-scale embeddings index + reranking layer to reduce noise and improve precision for top-k retrieval.

Requirements summary (from 03/01 call):
- Indexing scale: target initial corpus 40M documents, long-term plan 120M+ (contracts, filings, email archives). Need incremental ingest, dedupe, versioning.
- Latency targets: top-3 reranked response <200ms median for queries within regional VPC. Search-only (ann+faiss) acceptable <100ms on dedicated nodes, but reranker must keep under 200ms for UX.
- Cost: desirable cost-per-query <= $0.03 for avg query (includes ANN retrieval + reranker tokens); primary blocker is reranker token cost at scale.
- Models: prefer open or quantized models for reranker (fine-tune optional). Embedding model...

title: Custodia Secure Support
body:
Primary contact: VP Support - Lina Rosario (lina.rosario@custodiasupport.com). Use-case: 24/7 contact center across phone/chat/email for payment and patient support. Must surface recommended next action to agents and call internal tools (create/close/ticket-transfer) via tool-calling integrations. High priority: ensure no PHI/PCI leaves VPC control; audit log per-token; 7-year retention policy for dispute investigations.

Security constraints: strict VPC-only inference, customer-managed KMS, SAML SSO, SCIM for provisioning, explicit audit log retention & export to Splunk. Legal: BAA required plus PCI SAQ paperwork.

Quote from last call: 'We cannot accept any side-channel caching outside our subnet — logs only to our Splunk.' — Lina R.

POC ask: 2-week pilot with small set of agents (50 concurrent), simulated spike to 500c, test fallback to lower-cost quantized model, show audit log c...

title: Silverpine EdgeWorks
body:
Primary contact: CTO Samir Desai. Very hands-on, wants deterministic latency for enterprise search use-case.
Business driver: replace in-house model infra that's costly to operate; need predictable TCO and strict SLAs for customers.
Workload profile: heavy reranking + embeddings on ingestion pipeline; peak daily window 10:00-14:00 PT, bursty traffic (x4 baseline).
Latency targets: p95 < 120ms for 512-token generation (search snippets), p95 < 50ms for embedding lookups (single request).
Throughput targets: sustained 120 qps for small text rerank flows, burst to 400 qps for short windows; average context 256 tokens.
Reserved GPU planning: initial ask = capacity equivalent to 6x H100 (80GB) nodes with headroom for 20% burst; ask from infra: explicit per-GPU reservation and burst pool.
Preferred model family: open LLM (quantized) tuned for search (customer prefers ability to pin versions)...

title: Tidal Reserve Compute
body:
Account summary: Tidal Reserve Compute provides optimization and routing for containerized cargo and wants to move models off cloud-hosted endpoints into a Dedicated Reserved pool for predictable costs and SLOs. Evaluation focused on throughput guarantees for peak shipping windows and strict P95 latency for chat assistants used by operations.

-- Recent interactions:
- 2026-01-15 intro call (ff_20260115_8421) w/ CTO + infra; asked about minimum commit sizes and how burst works.
- 2026-02-08 technical deep-dive w/ SE (Maya) on batching, KV-cache reuse and quantization options.
- 2026-02-25 internal security review kickoff (ff_20260225_1284), asked about KMS integration and audit log retention.
- 2026-03-05 preliminary cost worksheet reviewed; customer requested per-token unit economics at 50%, 70%, 90% utilization.

Notes style: short fragments, quotes from CTO: "We need predictable $/...

title: HelmBridge ProductWorks
body:
Account snapshot: product-first mid-market company building an in-app AI assistant and enterprise search for customers. Heavy on session-based chat + semantic search embeddings.
Initial approach: started on Hosted API to validate prompt flows, now wants Dedicated for predictable throughput and VPC isolation.
POC objectives (customer): prove Dedicated meets latency SLOs for interactive chat (p50 < 80ms, p95 < 220ms), support 250 concurrent sessions, embedding throughput 150 qps, and show canary + fallback behaviour across regions.
Pricing sensitivity: tight unit-cost requirements; looking for guidance from Redwood Optimize on batching/quantization to hit targets. Interested in committed discount if ARR > $250k.
Canary plan agreed (high-level):
- Week 0: Provision Dedicated pool (2x A100-equivalent nodes) in us-west-2 and eu-west-1. VPC peering and KMS key exchange.
- Week 1: Run intern...

title: Saffron Rail Codex
body:
Discovery summary: enterprise dev platform for European rail operator managing signaling software and edge controllers.
Primary ask: IDE copilot integrated into developer workflow (VS Code & JetBrains) with token-level streaming for in-IDE completions.
Latency target: sub-60ms first-token for single-developer completions; streaming with <30ms token cadence desired in EU region.
Throughput: initial cohort ~120 active devs, scale plan to 800+ concurrent completions during release windows (spikes).
Model preferences: prefer pinned vetted open models; ability to pin a validated quantized variant (e.g., Llama3-70B-q8) and fallback to smaller family during maintenance windows.
Security stance: cannot send any source code or PII off their VPC; requirement for private control plane or on-prem runtime; HSM-backed KMS for keys.
Compliance: ISO27001 certified org, SOC2 Type II in progress; legal...

title: Aegis Conversational Systems
body:
Account background: Aegis builds a conversational front-end for mortgage and lending support; highly regulated, must retain audit logs and support data residency. They've built an internal vector DB and use embeddings + reranking + chat for customer-facing agents. Primary ask: committed Dedicated capacity for predictable throughput and lower unit cost vs hosted API. Also evaluating Private (VPC) because of residency/KMS requirements.

Key technical requirements (from discovery):
- Workloads: mixed (support chat ~70% reads with streaming responses, reranking ~20%, periodic bulk embeddings jobs ~10%)
- Latency target: 95th <= 150ms for short-chat turn (<=256 tokens); tail protection needed for peak concurrency
- Throughput target: sustained 200 qps for chat endpoints during peak 3-hour window; burst to 800 qps supported via autoscale/burst pool
- Context lengths: typical 1k tokens, occa...
